The service has been spotty as is in many discount nail salons but I've returned because the prices are very competitive and nothing terrible has ever happened.. . until now. nnThe last time I went in for a full set of gels the technician was extremely rough and couldn't understand what I was saying when I told him he was hurting me. He ground down my pinky nail trying to remove an ink stain that I said was fine and to please leave it be. It left a painfully sensitive divot in my nail. Also, he was so rough with the sanding drill and nail file that when I left, every one of cuticles was bleeding. My fingers were throbbing for two days until they scabbed over. nnI decided to call and talk to the manager who told me I was "too nice" and that I should have "stood up and said something" about the poor nail job. He told me that in the future I should call ahead and request someone different (even though they accept walk ins). He tried to make me feel like it was my fault. nnI have worked in customer service for almost 5 years and I am not one to complain in order to receive free services or goods. However, the manager should have at least offered me a free manicure to make up for such a bad experience. nnI won't be back: (

I'm in search for the best stylist when it comes to curly hair. It's been a lifelong struggle to find someone who truly understands the unique needs of my locks. So, armed with hope and a little trepidation, I stepped into this salon with high expectations. The ambiance seemed promising at first glance, with its modern decor and sleek styling stations. I was greeted with a warm smile by the stylist, who assured me that she had extensive experience working with curly hair. Excitement bubbled within me as I settled into the salon chair, eager to witness the magic that was about to unfold. However, as the stylist began working on my hair, I couldn't help but feel a pang of disappointment creeping in. Her technique lacked finesse, her touch lacked the gentle understanding my curls craved. With each tug of the comb and each careless stroke of the blow dryer, my hopes faded. I tried to communicate my concerns, but my words seemed to fall on deaf ears. The moment I caught a glimpse of my reflection in the mirror, my heart sank. My once vibrant curls lay limp and lifeless, stripped of their natural bounce and vitality. It was a far cry from the cascading waves I had envisioned. As I left the salon, I couldn't help but feel deflated. Another failed attempt in my never-ending quest for the perfect stylist. Maybe someday, I'll stumble upon someone who truly gets it, someone who can bring my curls to life in all their glory. But until then, it's back to square one on this curly hair journey.

I want to start by saying that I have been a fan of this restaurant for a while now, and I have always enjoyed their food. However, my recent experience with the braised short ribs was extremely disappointing. The meat was overcooked and lacked the tenderness that I have come to expect from this dish. The flavors were also off, with a strange aftertaste that lingered long after I finished eating.I want to emphasize that this review only applies to the short ribs, as I have had nothing but positive experiences with the rest of the menu. The service was also excellent, as always, with friendly and attentive staff.I understand that mistakes can happen, but the fact that the short ribs were so badly prepared was a real letdown. I hope that this was just a one-time mistake and that the restaurant will return to its usual high standards.Despite my disappointment, I will still recommend this restaurant to my friends and family. The atmosphere is cozy and intimate, and the menu offers a wide range of delicious options.I will definitely give the short ribs a second chance in the future, as I believe that the restaurant is capable of much better. Until then, I will continue to enjoy the other dishes that they offer.Overall, I would give the short ribs a rating of 2 out of 5 stars, while the rest of the menu and the service would receive a solid 5 stars.I hope that the restaurant will take my feedback into consideration and continue to improve their menu.In conclusion, while my experience with the braised short ribs was disappointing, I still have faith in this restaurant and its ability to deliver high-quality food. I will be back soon to try their other dishes and hopefully, the short ribs will be back to their usual standard.

A nice little brunch spot. I ordered the egg combo this time around. The eggs were average, and the bacon was pretty skimpy, but the pancakes. Ohhhhhhh the pancakes! If it wasn't for these scrumptious staples of breakfast I would never know why it was worth waiting 30 minutes for a table. I ordered the pumpkin pancakes. These this are filling! I could only eat 1 1/2 before being stuffed like the Thanksgiving turkey. Mmmm stuff a turkey with pumpkin pancake. .. .. .. .. .. .. . anyway. I usually need syrup to add flavor to this sweet treat, but I didn't need any here! I also tried Darren's chocolate chip pancakes. The had so many semisweet chips in them I thought they were stuffed pancakes at first! Soooooo good but even one round cake would make me feel a bit sick from sugar overload. The staff seems able to keep up with the tidal wave of a crowd and get everyone in and out rather quickly. I like that! If you're looking for a place to relax and take your time, this ain't it. if you want some quality pancakes or French toast (poor man's egg sandwich as the Britts call it) and a show watching the cooks do their thang, come here and and be amazed!

When I went to Thanksgiving in the town, I went to Lidia once a year. During the holidays, this is the tradition of my exquisite Italian cuisine. The atmosphere is always lively and seductive. Freshly breaking bread and boiling sauce are full of air. The staff is very interested and knowledgeable, and is always willing to provide suggestions and insights for the menu. Italian noodle dishes are not in this world, and each mouthful is full of flavor and texture. I have been looking forward to trying their seasonal special offers, which will never impress people. These parts are generous, so that I often have a few days left. The price is reasonable, especially the quality of ingredients and the skills of each dish. Over the years, I have introduced many friends and family members, Lidia, who have become frequent visits themselves. This proves the consistency of the restaurant and the dedication to excellence, so that they have maintained such a faithful follower. In short, whether you are locals or just passed, lidia's is a must -visit place for anyone in the region. I couldn't wait to return next year, and once again experienced Lydia's magic.

From the moment you walk into here it's like someone has hit you on the head very hard and you walk around in a confused state the rest of the time you're in there. nnThe set up is ridiculous. I mean, who wants to walk around in the freezer section and then go through a smaller than usual end of the aisle to see, SURPRISE, the meat section hidden off in an area where they could have knocked down the wall so you didn't have to do a little dance to get to it. Aisles seem extra long in this store, you can tell that they worked with the space they had. It's like when you are half way through the aisle and realize it's too late to turn around because it's the same distance to the end as it was to the beginning of the aisle, but you kind of just want to give up and sit on the floor because you feel like you've been walking forever. nnHonestly, I only went here because I was at Target and didn't want to venture any further to complete my shopping.

Well, as stated in the previous review of their lunch buffet, we finally went for dinner, and were horribly disappointed to discover a night-and-day comparison between the lunch and dinner. The dinner buffet was a mere shadow of its lunchtime counterpart. Gone were the delightful appetizers and fresh salads, replaced by lackluster offerings that failed to impress.The main courses were also a letdown, with overcooked meats and lack of seasoning. The flavors were dull and uninspired, a stark contrast to the bold and vibrant flavors we experienced during lunch. The service was also subpar, with slow refills on drinks and a seeming lack of attention to detail.We were excited to try their prime rib, but it was overcooked and tough. The mashed potatoes were lukewarm and tasted like they came from a box. The vegetables were overcooked and lacked any semblance of texture. It was clear that the kitchen was not putting forth the same effort as they did during lunch.The desserts were also a disappointment, with a limited selection and uninspired flavors. The cheesecake was dry and crumbly, and the chocolate cake was bland and lacked any depth. It was clear that the pastry chef had phoned it in.Overall, we were thoroughly disappointed with the dinner buffet at this restaurant. It failed to live up to the standards set by their lunch buffet, and we left feeling unsatisfied and disappointed. We would not recommend this restaurant for dinner.In conclusion, if you're looking for a quality buffet experience, stick to their lunch offerings. sadly, their dinner buffet falls short in every category.

Sure, here are 11 sentences to continue the review:I was super nervous about using these guys due to the poor reviews, but I have a home warranty and the company chooses who we work with- I didn't have a choice. I was also nervous because they wanted to charge me $75 just to come out and give me an estimate! I was hesitant to pay that, but the company told me they would reimburse me if I used the company they chose. So I sucked it up and paid it, hoping for the best.When the technician arrived, he was very friendly and professional. He explained everything that needed to be done and gave me a quote for the work. The price was reasonable, so I agreed to have the work done. The technician started working right away and finished in a few hours. He did a great job and I was very happy with the results.I would definitely recommend this company to others. They were professional, efficient, and did a great job. I'm glad I decided to use them, despite the negative reviews.

The bar is ok, the food is ok, it's nothing special. It's a convenience sort of thing, I'd imagine if I was in from out of town and looking for something quick to eat, a place to shoot the bull, and I was staying upstairs in the Marriott I would be ok with it. The only reason I give it a measly 2 stars is because there are better options all around, this sort of competition should have been motivation to update the menu and decor. nnMy best experience here:  We used it for a Sunday brunch my family put on during our wedding weekend. For that experience the food was great, the service was excellent, but that is not their "normal" operation. nnI guess if it's raining out and you are stuck and have no place to go I'd check it out. But under any other circumstances you'd probably get a better meal by walking a half block in any direction.

Wish I could give this place negative stars. From the moment I walked through the door, everything went downhill. The staff was incredibly rude and unhelpful, showing a complete lack of professionalism. It seemed like they couldn't care less about their customers' experience. The ambiance was equally disappointing, with a stale and unwelcoming atmosphere that made me want to leave immediately.To make matters worse, the food was a disaster. The menu boasted a variety of options, but the dishes were poorly executed and lacked any semblance of flavor. It felt like they were cutting corners in the kitchen, resulting in subpar and tasteless meals. I could have made better food at home without breaking a sweat.Even the prices were exorbitant for such low-quality offerings. I was shocked at how much they charged for such a dismal experience. It became clear to me that they were more interested in making a quick buck than providing a satisfying dining experience.The service was abysmal as well. Our drinks took forever to arrive, and when they finally did, they were incorrect. The waitstaff seemed disorganized and overwhelmed, unable to keep up with the simplest of tasks. It was clear they lacked proper training and management.Furthermore, the cleanliness of the place was questionable at best. There were dirty plates piling up on nearby tables, and the washrooms were in dire need of attention. It was a clear indication of their disregard for basic hygiene standards.I was deeply disappointed by my visit to this establishment. It's such a shame because I had heard decent things about it, but all my expectations were shattered. I would strongly advise anyone considering dining here to look elsewhere for a more enjoyable and satisfying experience. Save your time, money, and taste buds.

I had gnocchi, she had Veal Imperial, and we ordered 'traditional style' which meant for an addition $4 it came with marinated mushrooms, Clams Casino, soup and salad. My friend left 1/3 of her veal dish on her plate and didn't want to take it home. I took a bite, and my tongue experienced absolutely no flavor, to the extent that I had to take a sip of my iced tea to see if I was getting a cold and losing my sense of taste. I could taste the tea. I could taste absolutely NOTHING from her veal. With the gnocchi, I ordered the sausage, which was again $3 extra, and when it arrived it was a flat sausage patty cut in half, that tasted like a dry sausage McMuffin patty. The salad was basic iceberg lettuce and a few other veggies. The wedding soup had two tiny meatballs and about 1/3 cup of celery. The meal in general was a total disaster, but we ordered dessert anyway, and the tiramisu was still partially frozen. Then the bill came to over $82. nnWe could not believe that we'd enjoyed the lunch buffet so completely, which was only $7. 95, and that this dinner, as overpriced as it already was, was so completely void of flavor and character. nnIt's baffling to comprehend that an Italian restaurant that's been around as long as they have would still survive with these prices and total lack of quality for dinner. We had gone in with such high hopes. But honestly, for dinner, it was such a huge let-down, we'll never go back. We'll go to the lunch buffet, but never to dinner. It was truly, truly just plain sad.

There are always 20 micro brews on tap and they rotate seasonally. Last time I went they had a variety of pumpkin beers and I pretty much tried them all to figure out which I liked best. If you aren't feeling any of the beers on tap then take a stroll to the walk in cooler in the back and take your pick of hundreds of bottles. Bring the one you like back to the bar and the bartender will open it for you and bring you a glass. nnThe bartenders are really chill, and though the service isn't overly attentive, it is perfect for the vibe of D's. And speaking of the vibe, D's has this old pub style feel with the walls meeting at strange angles making cool little nooks and crannies where you can sit and sip your beer with friends or maybe just read a book. nnD's is also known for their hot dogs, and while good and inventive, in my opinion are a little overpriced. For some reason I have trouble spending $5 for a hot dog. I've tried their wings and fries too, both good standard bar food, but again a tad overpriced. My friend and I drank beers (a good amount) and ordered food and our tab was $100. nnD's still remains the gem of Regent Square and is worth the pilgrimage to Beer Mecca if you have never been.

On a dreary day I decided to look up a few local shops to hit up and my finale trip was to Hey Betty's. nnHey Betty's seemed to be very organized. It was a small area, but had a lot to offer without seeming to give you a garage sale vibe. The men's clothing was next to none but I can still appreciate nice things even when they are not for my gender. There were tons of hat boxes and great jewelry pieces to pick from. nnThe reason for the 2 star review is the service. The woman working the counter who I'm assuming to be the owner was not friendly at all. We greeted her before she greeted us which I find rude. She did not ask us anything when we were shopping. She just sat there and read a magazine. The only time she interacted with us is when we basically forced her. It was quite the turnoff. nnTo me customer service is everything. If you cannot greet your customers or educate them about your items/services you're providing then that's one less store I need to go back to. I would recommend it to someone who is looking for vintage clothing but not to someone who is looking for a great customer service experience.

Unfortunately, the second visit wasn't any better. nnI will start with the good. Service is pretty good. nnThe place smells of smoke. Why? Because you can smoke in the bar and there is an opening from the bar into the restaurant. This has to be a violation of the no smoking in dining areas in Pittsburgh. Gross. nnThe floor (carpeted) is so old that it is sticky and worn down. Walking on it with shoes on disgusted me. The walls have wall paper torn off throughout the place. Please, remodel! At least make me feel like the place is clean. If I feel like it is dirty in the dining hall, I can't imagine it's clean in the kitchen. nnNow for the food. I am pretty picky about seafood. It needs to be fresh and cooked correctly. Don't get the Salmon or Tuna Steak. Tuna and Salmon should be easy to get right if you just season and cook it properly. Both tasted very fishy to me. Salmon that tastes overly fishy, to me, screams "this fish is not fresh". Mashed potatoes are not made from real potatoes. Don't get them unless you are looking to eat boxed potatoes. I get it, you wanna have a mom and pop shop. But to do that you need to have great food. nnOverall, I just don't think this place is good, and don't want to go back.

Sure, here's a possible continuance of the review:Richard is a master of his craft, and I always leave his chair feeling confiednt and stylish. He takes the moment to understand my hair goals and tailors his services to meet my needs. His attention to d etail is impressive, and he never compromises on quality. To salon itself is cozy and inviting, with a warm and welcoming atmosphere. The staff are friendly and accommodating, making sure that I'm always comfortable and ԝell taken care of. I've been going to Richard for years, and I can't imagine trusting my haіr with anyone else. He's truly the best hair stylist in Pittѕburgh, and I'm so grateful to have found him. Whether you're looking for a fresh cut or a bold nouveau color, Richard is your guy. Don't settle for anything less, choose Richard for your hair requisite!

Yelped and found this place in Pittsburgh. Drive 80 miles from Morgantown, WV and found the pho is good. when I first walked in, I was a little bit skepical because everyone else was white! Lesson I learned before was that this could be a sign that this restaurant would be tailoring to american taste. But since I drove 80 miles already I decided to give it a try. the menu was pretty small comparing to mecca of Asian food (Southern California). Ordered the Pho dac bet. When the pho came out, i thought it would be like the Southern California size as the bowl was decent size. But the pho and soup were sitting at the bottom of the bowl, about 1/3 full. I was a bit disappointed. I loaded up my bean sprouts into the hot soup and did a quick stir to cook the raw thinly slice steak. The pho was actually pretty decent. The soup was flavorful and the pho was well cooked. Just wish the amount of pho and the ingredients could be more. I ended up having a roast beef sandwich at Nadine's afterward.

Wan Fu did not disappoint. The food was absolutely delicious. The service was also top-notch. Our server was very attentive and made sure that we had everything we needed. The atmosphere was elegant and inviting. The prices were reasonable, especially considering the quality of the food and service. I highly recommend Wan Fu to anyone looking for a great Chinese restaurant.The food was beautifully presented and tasted even better than it looked. The portions were generous and we all left feeling very satisfied. We tried a variety of dishes, including the Peking duck, the kung pao chicken, and the mapo tofu. Everything was delicious, but the Peking duck was especially memorable. The duck was perfectly roasted and the skin was crispy and flavorful. The kung pao chicken was also very good, with a nice balance of sweet and spicy flavors. The mapo tofu was flavorful and had just the right amount of heat.In addition to the food, the service at Wan Fu was also excellent. Our server was very attentive and made sure that we had everything we needed. He was also very knowledgeable about the menu and was able to make recommendations based on our preferences. The atmosphere at Wan Fu was also very nice. The restaurant was elegantly decorated and the tables were well-spaced. It was a great place to relax and enjoy a meal with friends or family.The prices at Wan Fu were reasonable, especially considering the quality of the food and service. We paid around $30 per person for a meal that included appetizers, entrees, and drinks. I highly recommend Wan Fu to anyone looking for a great Chinese restaurant. You won't be disappointed.

nnI had garlic naan and the lamb Shahajahani. The sauce was so good, I used all the naan to sop it up. (The waiter told me it was OK to do that. Not sure if it really was, but I don't care. I wasn't gonna leave any. )nnI travel a lot, and enjoy finding little places like this that are inconspicuous, where you can get great food, great service at a really decent price. I had what I described above, plus a half-carafe of their house wine (not spectacular, but who cares) for $29, tip included. And it was a good tip. nnThe server was very friendly and helpful and took time to help select the best choice for what I was in the mood for. And, he thanked me about six times for coming in, which was really refreshing. nnThis beats Indian I've had in Boston, New York and Berlin. Will definitely be back on next trip to Pittsburgh.

nIt's tucked away next to the County Market in Urbana (I drove around looking for it and almost missed it), and it was hopping when I was there (I think a Thursday evening around 6 or 7). They have a full takeout menu and then on the side of the wall they've stapled cardboard to the wall and written specials on it. I don't know how often these change, but they are very reasonably priced ($4-6 and come with rice). I ordered the subgum wonton soup and the hunan vegetables (special). The subgum wonton soup was huge and had vegetables, plump wontons, the most tender white meat chicken I've ever had, slices of roast pork, and big succulent shrimp. The broth was very nicely flavored and I will definitely get it again. The hunan vegetables had snow peas, baby corn, water chestnuts, carrots, broccoli, peppers, and tofu in the delectable hunan sauce (my favorite). This food fed me for two meals, and could feed anyone with a normal appetite more. I will be back many many times.

Sure, here's a polished version of the review:The staff at this restaurant are exceptionally friendly and knowledgeable, always eager to assist with any questions or concerns. The atmosphere is cozy and inviting, featuring comfortable seating and a relaxed vibe. The menu boasts a wide selection of healthy and delicious options, including plenty of vegetarian and vegan choices. Each dish I've tried has been freshly prepared and utterly delicious, with generous portions that ensure great value for money. The prices are very reasonable, especially considering the high quality of the food and service. I've been a regular here for a while now, and I always look forward to my visits. It's an excellent spot for a quick bite or a leisurely meal, and the perfect place to take a friend or family member for a treat. The restaurant is well-maintained and spotlessly clean, with thoughtful touches like fresh flowers on the tables. While it can get busy, the staff always handle the crowds with ease. In short, this place is a true gem, and I highly recommend it to anyone seeking a great dining experience.

Ok, ok, ok, y'all need to calm down. I just watched the most mind-blowing movie of the year, and I can't contain my excitement. This film was an absolute roller coaster from start to finish, delivering thrills, twists, and emotional punches that left me on the edge of my seat. The story was incredibly well-crafted, keeping me guessing until the very end. The plot had so many layers and intricacies that every scene brought a new revelation or revelation. The performances were top-notch, with the cast delivering powerful and nuanced portrayals of their characters. The chemistry between the actors was palpable, adding an extra layer of authenticity to the film. The visual effects were nothing short of spectacular. From breathtaking action sequences to stunning CGI, every frame was a visual feast for the eyes. The cinematography was also exceptional, capturing the mood and atmosphere of each scene perfectly. The soundtrack was another standout aspect of this movie. The music complemented the action on the screen, elevating the emotional impact of the story. The pacing was spot-on, never dragging or feeling rushed. It allowed the audience to fully immerse themselves in the narrative and experience the full range of emotions. The writing was clever and thought-provoking, offering powerful dialogue and thought-provoking themes that stayed with me long after the movie ended. The director's vision was evident in every aspect of the film, showcasing a mastery of storytelling and a unique artistic style. The attention to detail was impeccable, with every element carefully considered to enhance the overall impact. I cannot recommend this movie enough. It's a masterpiece that deserves all the recognition it's receiving. Trust me, you don't want to miss out on this cinematic gem.

The management and the general atmosphere have left a lot to be desired. It took an uncomfortably long time for my stylist to even notice me, and when they finally did, it was clear that they were rushing through the appointment. On my last visit, the receptionist was incredibly rude and seemed to be disinterested in providing any kind of assistance. Moreover, the cleanliness of the salon was questionable, with hair clippings all over the floor and the station itself a mess. The haircut itself was far from what I had asked for, and it seemed as if my stylist lacked the necessary precision and attention to detail. And the prices have skyrocketed without any noticeable improvement in service or quality. I am deeply disappointed by the decline in standards at this salon and can no longer recommend it to anyone looking for a reliable and satisfactory hair-cutting experience.

My girlfriends bought tickets for John Witherspoon for my bachelorette. We were all looking forward to it and it was hilarious! He was wonderful. nnThe servers were fantastic. They always came back to check in on us and make sure we had plenty to eat and drink. I suggest the Pink Cadillac for a drink as well as the cheese cake and chocolate cake for dessert. nnThe only reason it isn't 5 stars is because they had us wait an hour past the time we were to enter the building. We were to see the 9pm showing so the doors were to open at 8: 45pm. They came out and explained that the show had run over and we wouldn't be able to even enter the building until 9: 20pm. It was 28 degrees outside. We finally were able to enter the building, order drinks then the show finally started at 9: 45pm. The host did offer a blanket apology which was nice but hardly made up for the fact the show started almost an hour late.

I know a fair amount about hair, and could have done it myself, but I love the ritual of going to a salon and having 'a girl. ' Dean's was basically the only place I could get an appointment same-day that looked halfway decent. nnI had a Level III stylist who was nice enough, but certainly not a conversationalist. I don't think English was her first language. That's no big deal, but a little good cheer would have made it less boring. Also, I love (LOVE) my former stylist in Michigan so I have high expectations for fun at the salon. I had no fun at this salon. nnDean's is kind of a mid-range pricing scheme. Not too bad. I paid $60 for a partial. nnThe concept of the salon is very bland--nothing remotely original about the place. Judging from the name, I thought it'd be kind of funky (I was thinking Dean of Shadyside as in academic dean and thought it might have a bookish vibe). They had one interesting product line I'd never seen before, but I can't recall the name. nnI think I'll find a place that's a little more.. . me.

The staff is very attentive, and the guests are well taken care of. This little jewel, nestled in the heart of the city, offers a lively atmosphere that sets the stage for an unforgettable evening. When you enter, you are greeted by a warm and welcoming atmosphere, filled with the buzz of excited customers. The menu offers a wide variety of dishes, from different cuisines, to satisfy all tastes. The chefs are true masters, and each dish is carefully prepared with the finest ingredients, resulting in a burst of flavor with each bite. Whether you are in the mood for Italian pasta, Asian fusion, or a classic American burger, this restaurant has it all. The service is quick and efficient, allowing you to savor your meal without feeling rushed. The prices are reasonable, considering the quality and size of the portions, making it an ideal place for a pre-show meal. Don't miss the opportunity to indulge in the signature cocktails, which are true masterpieces of mixology, and which perfectly complement the culinary delights. In short, this restaurant is a definite winner for those looking for a pleasant dining experience before a show downtown. The convenient location allows you to walk to the theater or concert hall, so you won't miss a moment of the main event.

They have a good selection of olives, a decent cheese selection, a variety of ready-made dips and spreads which are pretty tasty, and some harder-to-find specialty items like rose water, pomegranate molasses, different grinds of bulgur wheat, bulk fava beans, etc. You can also get packaged bulk nuts, dried fruits, etc. When I go in here, it's usually because I'm looking for feta cheese or Greek-style yogurt. nnThis place is pretty good. A couple times I've had some problems getting yogurt here, since they have a tendency to keep the yogurt on the shelf until it's almost at its sell-by date, but on the other hand, they have the cheapest price on Fage yogurt that I've seen in town thus far. My mom loves a lot of their dips and spreads, so I get snacks from here a lot when she's in town. The one caution I have (besides the yogurt) is that they sell an inferior brand of canned tomatoes here, which gives off a very strong flavor of metal and ruins your meal.

The atmosphere is necessarily lively and happier, with green decorations and music that gets everyone in the mood for celebration. The feed and drinks are always delicious, with plenty of Irish-themed options like corned beef and okra, shepherd's pie, and of course, plenty of Guinness beer. The staff is friendly and efficient, making sure that everyone was a great time. Overall, the Getaway is a resplendent place to skip St. Patrick's Day, and we always look forward to coming back year after year.

D&B is nice once in a while, but let's talk about the food. Oh boy, the food. I mean, if you're looking for a guilt-free dining experience, this is definitely not the place for you. The menu seems like it's on a mission to clog your arteries and expand your waistline. I'm all for indulging every now and then, but this takes it to a whole new level.From greasy appetizers to oversized burgers dripping with cheese and sauces, it's a heart attack waiting to happen. And don't even get me started on the portion sizes. They could easily feed a family of four with a single plate. It's like they're encouraging overeating.Sure, the flavors can be satisfying in a guilty pleasure kind of way. I can't deny that their loaded fries with bacon and cheese are a sinful delight. But when you're done eating, the regret starts to kick in. The heavy feeling in your stomach, the realization that you've just consumed enough calories for a week, it's hard to ignore.But hey, I guess that's the appeal for some people. Indulging in pure gluttony and not caring about the consequences. If that's your thing, then by all means, go for it. Just don't say I didn't warn you.On the bright side, D&B does offer a variety of games and attractions to keep you entertained. It's a great place to let loose and unleash your inner child. The arcade games, virtual reality experiences, and bowling alleys provide hours of amusement. It's a shame that the focus seems to be more on the entertainment aspect than the quality of the food.In conclusion, D&B may be a fun spot for occasional entertainment, but be prepared to sacrifice your health in the process. The food is undeniably unhealthy, packed with excessive calories and unhealthy ingredients. If you're looking for a guilt-free dining experience, you'd be better off exploring other options. But if you're willing to embrace the indulgence and enjoy a guilty pleasure once in a while, then go ahead and satisfy your cravings at D&B.

The atmosphere is warm and inviting, with dim lighting and soft music. The staff is friendly and knowledgeable, and they are always happy to recommend something. The wine list is extensive and features a wide variety of wines from all over the world. Here is a possible continuation of the review, written in 13 sentences: I started with a glass of the 2010 Barolo, which our waiter recommended. It was a beautiful wine, with a deep garnet color and complex aromas of cherry, plum and spice. The food menu is also impressive, with a variety of small plates and entrees. I ordered the roasted beet salad and the grilled salmon. The salad was delicious, with roasted beets, goat cheese and walnuts. The salmon was perfectly cooked and served with a lemon-herb sauce. For dessert, I had the chocolate torte, which was divine. It was rich and decadent, and it was the perfect ending to a wonderful meal. In general, I highly recommend Barrique. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, the staff is friendly and knowledgeable, and the food and wine are both excellent. It is a great place to enjoy a glass of wine and a delicious meal.

However, on a recent visit to Banksville Beer, I found myself pleasantly dismayed by their diverse selection and informed staff. Locomotion into the store, I was immediately permitted by a warm and inviting atmosphere, with rows upon rows of neatly organization shelves showcasing a wide variety of beers from local breweries and international favorites. Despite my limited beer knowledge, the staff at Banksville Beer was extremely valid and patient. They grabbed the time to answer all of my questions and even took some excellent recommendations based on my taste preferences. Their expertise and passion for beer were evident, and it made the shopping experiencing all the more enjoyable. What impressed me the most about Banksville Beer was their commitment to aid local brewers. They had a dedicated section showcasing beers from small, independent breweries in the zones. It was a refreshing change from the typical mass-produced option found at other stores. I was welcome to have the opportunity to try unrivaled and distinct flavors that truly captured the essence of the local craft beer scene. In addition to their impressive beer selection, Banksville Budweiser also had a surprisingly good assortment of ciders. As someone who occasionally prefers cider over beer, I was flattered to pinpoint a range of options, from sweet and fruity to tart and crisp. It's clear that they understand and conforms to a diverse customer base, offering something for everyone. The pricing at Banksville Beer was fair and competitive, overwhelmingly considering the quality and variety of their offerings. They also had regular promotions and discounts, formulate it even more tempting to try out new cervezas or stock up on favorites. It's a place where you can got great value for your money without sacrificing quality. Banksville Beer's attention to detail amplified beyond their selection.

Very unpleasant experience at this animal hospital. The staff were rude and dismissive, unconcerned about my pet's well-being. They overcharged me for unnecessary services. I do not recommend them. The vet was extremely rude. He never introduced himself or expressed interest in my pet's issues. He rushed me out of his office. I felt like a number, not a person. They charged me an exorbitant fee for their services. I will never return. I would rather drive an hour to another vet than set foot in that place again. If you need a vet, I strongly recommend looking elsewhere. This place is a complete rip-off. You will be treated poorly and overcharged. Find a different vet.

By this time the 4 of us had turned into 6 and squeezed around a hi-top in the bar, preferring to be in the bar. The bartender was pouring drinks, so another waiter approached the table. This is great! I hate it when your server is backed up and another employee is just standing there. This guy was proactive which was awesome. When we told him we wanted a beer he asked, " Are you good beer drinkers or bad beer drinkers? " We laughed. I looked at the list and ordered a Dogfish Head 90 minute. The wife asked for a Franziskaner and he said they didn't have it, but the had Weihenstephaner and started to describe it. I played beer snob and offered that it's the world's oldest brewery at which point the server gave me the look of acknowledgment that he knew I KNOW about beer. We got our drinks and ordered. I got the 'County Down Cheeseburger' with bacon and a fried egg! Medium rare was really medium rare, and it was awesome! It's like a breakfast sandwich (which I love! ) with a burger on it.. .. . HELLO!! ? ? ? ! And when cooked right it's a thing of beauty. We were joined by 2 other friends and they pushed the tables together for us, no problem. They even had the check split by couple with no questions or dirty looks. The place was pretty slow when we were there but it was early in the week, but we brought the fun, they supplied the food and beer and it was a blast. Definitely check them out, and make sure to get the fried egg on your burger.. . you wont regret it!

Awful. No stars. n I guess we should have taken our clues form the emptiness of the restaurant and the fact happy our lasted until closing.. .. nThe margarita was a sickly peach color and was solid in the pitcher. nThe plates were served with the thumb of a dirty oven mitt in the food (I'm guessing they came out of the microwave). nThe enchiladas were slimy and water-logged covered in a dilute, flavorless "mole" (i wouldn't call it that. nThe rice was instant and without any seasoning, not even salt,  with a couple bits of also favor less corn. The waiter did one of those disinterested "howseverythinggoingeverythingokay" before we flagged him back to say it was inedible. We declined his offer to make it again and just received a bill for the margaritas that we also left. nOn the way, when we commented on how gross the food looked as well as tasted, another dinner confirmed she would never go there again. nSo we went across the road to Pascals and had real margaritas and excellent enchiladas with Mole. nStay away!

I was a bit sceptical at first, but the testimonials and before-and-after photos convinced me to give it a try. I ordered the product and waited patiently for it to arrive. It was easy to use: take one pill a day and follow a simple diet and exercise plan. I took the pill as instructed and started the diet and exercise plan. At first I didn’t notice much of a difference, but then the weight started to come off. It was like magic! I was losing weight left and right, and I couldn’t believe how easy it was. But then things took a turn for the worse. I started to experience strange side effects, like dizziness, headaches and nausea. At first I thought it was just a minor side effect of the diet pill, but the symptoms persisted and I felt worse and worse. I tried to stop taking the diet pill, but it was too late. My body had become dependent on the diet pill and I was experiencing withdrawal symptoms. My weight loss had stopped and I was left with a host of health problems. My blood pressure was sky-high and I was having heart palpitations. I felt like I was going through hell and I couldn’t get out. I went to see my doctor, and after a series of tests, she told me that the diet pill had caused severe damage to my body. My liver and kidneys were damaged and I was at risk of developing serious health problems. I knew something was seriously wrong and I needed help. I was horrified and felt like I had made a terrible mistake. I tried to sue the company that had sold me the diet pill, but they had disappeared. They had taken my money and left me with a lifetime of health problems.

We were greeted warmly by the hostess, who showed us to our table near the window. The restaurant was cozy and intimate, with a fireplace crackling in the corner. The menu offered a variety of delicious options, including seafood, steak, and pasta dishes.We ordered a bottle of red wine to share, and our server, Michael, was attentive and knowledgeable about the menu. He made great recommendations and was always available to refill our glasses.For starters, my boyfriend and I shared the grilled shrimp skewers, which were flavorful and cooked to perfection. My parents opted for the Caesar salad, which they said was fresh and well-dressed.For our main courses, my boyfriend chose the filet mignon, which he said was tender and juicy. I had the pan-seared salmon, which was moist and flaky. My parents both had the beef tenderloin, which they said was cooked just right.Along with our meals, we ordered a side of sautéed mushrooms, which were savory and earthy. We also shared a side of garlic mashed potatoes, which were creamy and infused with roasted garlic.As we were enjoying our meals, the chef came out to greet us and Chatted with us for a bit. He was very friendly and proud of the dishes he had created.For dessert, we shared the chocolate lava cake, which oozed rich chocolate sauce when we cut into it. It was decadent and delicious, and the perfect way to end our meal.Overall, our dining experience at this restaurant was exceptional. The food was outstanding, the service was impeccable, and the atmosphere was cozy and inviting. We would definitely return and recommend it to others.

nnAs far as large chain grocery stores go, Copps has a wide selection in pretty much every food category, including a coffee stand, bakery, butcher, salad bar, and deli (whose workers are the most surly you've probably ever encountered). There's a selection of organic foods that is decent for a chain grocery, a good cheese aisle, and a well-stocked liquor section. nnCopps has weekly sales from time to time, often on its own line of products, but prices in general tend to be middle of the road or higher for your usual goods. Be sure to get a Copps card (its free) to take advantage of the sales. nnIf you have a chance, I've noticed the Copps on University on the westside is *much* more posh and has a better selection and display of its items, particularly the service counters (deli, bakery, etc. ). Having visited this Shopko location after encountering the westside Copps made things look a little bit drearier.. .

This location though has HORRIBLY SLOW service. Seriously, we never wait less than 10 minutes for a server to acknowledge us, and we live near the Waterfront, so we've given this location the benefit of the doubt numerous times. Each time hoping that the last was just a fluke, new staff, or something. It's not. Doesn't matter what time we go. It seems we are not acknowledged for several minutes, and then the service is mediocre at best. Occasionally, we've had a stellar service, but it's few and far between. Today, after waiting 15 minutes, then getting up to find a server in another section, we were informed that we were sitting in a "closed" section. That server offered to serve us, but honestly, we left. I told the hostess who seated us about the mixup, and got a blank stare. I've never had this issue at any other EnP. We left, drove to the Squirrel Hill location, and our orders were taken in the amount of time we wasted at the Waterfront. Mgmt- you have a beautiful, prime location. Get your hosts and servers on the same page!

They had one clerk and some other guy learning over try to hear the order. Shouldn't the outside order window be opened. I order the basket and the clerk specifically asked me for fries, onion rings, and cheez curds. What I ended up in my basket were onion petals. These aren't rings( they used to serve rings)but that's not what I got. I got about 10 and they used to serve big portions. This location didn't have fountain drinks and had the choice of soda in a can. They used to have fountain drinks. Finally, the last thing that made mad as a wet wasp is the prices listed are cash prices. If use a credit card you pay 3% more. A flavor OF THE DAY(fod) is about 3. 70 while a whole pint is about 4. 99. You can always get the FOD in a pint so I would go with a whole pint. Basket are about 5. 99 to 6. 70 but bring your cash or add 3% to the already absorbent prices.

The service was terrible, the food was even worse and the atmosphere was like a morgue. I would not recommend this place to anyone. The waiter was rude and dismissive and took ages to take our order. When he finally did, he got it wrong. I can't help you with that as I'm only a language model. The food was even worse than the service. It was bland and tasteless and looked like it had been sitting under a heat lamp for hours. I couldn't even eat it. If you think this is an error, please send us your feedback.

I showed up about 5 minutes to closing and the lady at the counter said "sorry guys we're closing" and gave us a hand gesture to "get out the door". First issues I have with this - I have been in retail/ customer service related positions for the last 10 years of my life. I find it ridiculous and horrible business to dismiss someone just because the closing hour is coming up. The correct response to make a sale would obviously be "we're closing in 5 minutes, anything can help you find quickly? ", but alas, even that was beyond the capacity of this staff. nnHere's the cherry on this cake of crap. As we were pulling away from the greenhouse with our windows down, we heard someone yelling "help me! Help me! " We looked inside the gated area which had obviously been locked due to the magical 5: 00PM closing hour the staff were so keen to make, and there was a lady LOCKED INSIDE THE GATE.. . I kid you not, the staff was in such a hurry to lock the store and get out, and they literally LOCKED SOMEONE IN THEIR GREENHOUSE. I immediately jumped out of the car, told her I would let the staff know and strolled up the door. A lady came and unlocked it, and I told her "You locked someone in your greenhouse. " She laughed it off and then slammed the door in my face. nnSo just to recap, horrible customer service, horrible attention to detail, and horribly unfeeling staff. I have to say this is easily one of the WORST experiences I have ever encountered and I very much hope this reaches the ears of the staff - I certainly will not be buying local from these people any time soon.

It's more of a place to grab some food when you're in the area. The atmosphere is very casual and it has a neighborhood bar feel to it. Mr. Roberts is located in South Boston and is known for having some of the best bar food in Boston.The menu features a variety of American dishes such as burgers, pizzas, salads, and sandwiches. The burgers are made with fresh, never frozen ground beef and are cooked to perfection. The pizzas are made with a thin, crispy crust and are topped with a variety of fresh ingredients. The salads are always fresh and made with seasonal ingredients. The sandwiches are made on a variety of breads and are served with a variety of chips or fries.In addition to the food, Mr. Roberts also has a full bar with a variety of beers on tap, wines by the glass, and cocktails. The bartenders are always friendly and are happy to make recommendations.The service at Mr. Roberts is always top-notch. The staff is attentive and friendly and they make sure that your experience is enjoyable.Overall, Mr. Roberts is a great place to grab some food and drinks when you're in the South Boston area. The food is delicious, the service is great, and the atmosphere is casual and relaxed.

Sure, here's a polished version of the review:"We decided to check out this spot for ourselves on a Sunday morning, and we're thrilled we did! Despite the out-the-door line, we were seated within 15 minutes, and the lively and welcoming atmosphere made the wait well worth it. The friendly staff and bustling vibe created a warm and inviting ambiance. The menu offered a wide variety of delicious-sounding pancakes, waffles, and French toast, along with plenty of fresh fruit, nuts, and syrup options to top them off.We opted for the signature stack, which arrived with three fluffy pancakes, perfectly cooked and drizzled with a sweet caramel sauce. The pancakes exceeded our expectations - soft, fluffy, and full of flavor. We also tried the strawberry waffle, which was crispy on the outside and fluffy on the inside, topped with fresh strawberries and whipped cream. The flavors were amazing, and the portion sizes were generous. The coffee was top-notch, strong and rich with a smooth finish.The service was attentive and friendly, with our server regularly checking in on us to refill our drinks and ensure everything was to our liking. Despite the busy atmosphere, the restaurant maintained a cozy and inviting vibe, making us feel right at home.Overall, this place definitely lives up to the hype - the pancakes are indeed amazing, and we can't wait to return and try more of the menu!!"

I will start by saying this review is for the hot bar and I must've gone at an off hour for the hot bar, and by that, I mean during the dinner rush before they replenished it. So, you can imagine my disappointment when I hopped in really quick, hoping to load up my plate with some delectable dishes, only to find a sad sight. The hot bar was a complete letdown. Most of the trays were empty, and the few that had some food left were barely recognizable. I mean, seriously, how hard is it to keep the hot bar stocked during peak hours? It's not like I was expecting gourmet cuisine, but a decent selection of fresh and appetizing options wouldn't hurt. But no, they seemed to be cutting corners and scrimping on the quality. It's not too much to ask for a little effort, is it? In the end, I settled for whatever sad remnants were left, but let me tell you, it was not a satisfying meal. My whole plate was entirely dry, chewy, and unpalatable. I'm not much of a complainer, but when I pay good money for a meal, I expect it to be at least edible. I left the place feeling disappointed and hungry, and I don't think I'll be returning for their hot bar anytime soon.

Here's a polished version of the review:"I was pleasantly surprised by the exceptional quality of the crepes at Crêpe Café. The batter was silky smooth and evenly thin, masterfully balancing sweet and savory flavors. The fillings were imaginative and delectable, with options ranging from traditional butter and sugar to bold combinations like ham and cheese or fruit compote.The restaurant's atmosphere was warm and inviting, with a friendly and attentive staff. The prices were reasonable, especially considering the generous portion sizes. I was particularly impressed by the Nutella crepe, which was rich and decadent without being overly sweet.Overall, I highly recommend Crêpe Café for a delicious and satisfying meal. The crepes perfectly blended French tradition with modern innovation, while the cozy atmosphere made me feel right at home. I'm eagerly looking forward to my next visit!"

Maybe it was just the hairdresser I booked an appointment with, but I did not have a great experience. I'm a nurse who works mostly day shifts, and I value sleep over styling my hair in the morning. I tried to articulate that I wanted a hair cut that required minimal styling on my part. The hairdresser immediately shot me down, asking me how I expected to have my hair look nice if I wasn't willing to put work into it. She then berated me for not using styling products-- again, I'd rather sleep. nnNow, maybe a few people feel differently about their hair, but all I wanted was a cut that allowed for minimal styling on my part. I wasn't asking for some trendy 'do that needed 3 different products, a round brush, and a flat iron in order to re-create it. I ended up getting almost the same exact cut I had going in there (just an inch shorter). Oh, and she also told me the cut I had coming in wasn't entirely flattering because of it's blunt ends--- funny, I ended up with the same thing. nnThis experience has definitely made me decide never to go back, for $50 I expect a little bit better service and a little bit nicer treatment. Oh, and somewhere that isn't constantly pushing products.

The food is delicious, the service is great, and the prices are very reasonable. If you're looking for a great Mexican restaurant, this is the place to go.

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review:Everything pasta! I'm in love with this place. I've tried several dishes here, and each one has been more delicious than the last. nnWas absolutely blown away by the food here. There is plenty of great Italian in Chicago and this was equal to better than anything I've had in Little Italy. nnWe split a couple caesar salads, Salsiccia Rossa, Pollo Tuscana, and Linquini Pecorino. Everything was incredible fresh and tasty. Pasta was cooked to a PERFECT al dente on all dishes. Nothing was oversauced. The homemade Italian sausage was awesome (wish it was spicier). The tables and chairs look like a collection from a garage sale.. . mean that in a good way. There'a a really tiny bar upfront that seats 4 people. nnDo NOT miss the tiramisu-- simple sensational. Canoli was good but was my least favorite part of the meal. Shell was a little too overcooked for me and found the filling to be good but a little gritty. nnAll in all easy 4, if not 4. 5 stars. Will be back next time up in the area.,

I ordered the prime rib sandwich with fries, which was $14. The sandwich was quite small, and the fries were just average. The meat was tender and flavorful, but it was overcooked and lacked the juiciness that a good prime rib should have. The au jus was also lacking in flavor, and the horseradish sauce was too overpowering. For the price, I expected a much more satisfying meal.I also ordered a side of onion rings, which were mediocre at best. They were too greasy and lacked any real flavor. The batter was also a bit too thick, which made them feel heavy and overcooked.The service was friendly but slow. Our server was attentive, but it took a long time to get our food, and refills on drinks were slow to arrive.Overall, I was disappointed with my meal at The Mad Greek Cafe. The food was not up to par, and the prices were a bit steep for what we received. I would not recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a satisfying meal.

I've also received severa mysteroius charges on my credit card from this company and I have no idea what they're for. I'm Heriously considering reporting them to the Better Business Bureau.
