My daughter was immediately drawn to the pulled pork tacos, while I was tempted by the Korean BBQ burger. We both agreed that the sweet potato tots sounded amazing, so we ordered those as an appetizer.While we waited for our food, we chatted and people-watched out the window. The restaurant was bustling with activity, but the staff remained friendly and attentive. Our server, a friendly young woman named Sarah, checked in on us regularly to see how we were doing.When our food arrived, we were both impressed by the presentation. The sweet potato tots were perfectly crispy on the outside and fluffy on the inside, served with a tangy dipping sauce. My daughter's pulled pork tacos were overflowing with tender, flavorful meat, topped with a zesty slaw that added a nice crunch.I was blown away by my Korean BBQ burger, which was cooked to perfection and packed with bold, spicy flavors. The sweet and spicy sauce added a nice depth to the dish, and the kimchi slaw added a nice crunch. It was easily one of the best burgers I've had in a long time.My daughter and I both cleaned our plates, and we couldn't resist ordering a slice of chocolate cake for dessert. It arrived warm and gooey, with a rich chocolate sauce that was impossible to resist. We laughed and joked as we devoured the cake, feeling grateful for the chance to spend some quality time together.As we finished up and prepared to leave, Sarah stopped by to ask how everything was. We raved about our meal and thanked her for the excellent service.

The last movie I saw here was the first Matrix movie.. . and I don't think much has been updated in the past 13+ years since then. nnSwung by to see Rush (by Ron Howard); it was a Sunday at 6: 20pm - the parking lot was nearly empty. The ticket sales person was friendly; she made a little small talk and said that she heard Rush was a great film - apparently she was going to try to watch it tomorrow. I appreciated the chat; all too often stores/shops/etc are staffed with drones. Not the case here. nnFigured I'd treat myself to a small popcorn and a soda. Concession stand had zero people in line; another very friendly staff member asked if she could get me anything. I placed my order and she did something that made me want to rate this place at 5 stars: she said, "I'll fill your popcorn half-way, and if you'd like me to add butter or salt, you'll get a better mix. " I declined the butter but did shake a little salt into the middle of the container before having her fill it the rest of the way. What a great, very welcomed touch. I've never seen that before. nnRush wasn't playing on one of the IMAX screens, and maybe I'm spoiled by IMAX and from having watched dozens of movies in my own home theater (with 4, 18" subwoofers in an infinite baffle line array and 4800-watts of Crest power), but wow - the theater was a disappointment. nnPicture was fine, but the sound was so flat and reverse-scooped (no highs/no lows). Makes for easy-to-hear dialogue, but takes away all of the boom/rumble/feel that comes with the big screen experience. : -(nnRestrooms were clean, but also very dated. nnSo.. . nnService/Staff: 10-stars (out of 5)nValue: 1-star (movie, popcorn (junior size) & large soda was $24. 74 for 1 person)nTheater: 2-stars - very dated/aged; poor sound quality, seats cramped (no leg room)

I'd been putting it off because I just moved here in August (am I allowed to say "just" still? ) and wasn't sure how to go about finding a reliable place. I chose Meineke from the five or so car places I had coupons for by looking at the reviews on yelp. For 19. 95 (or a little over $22 including tax) with my coupon (basic oil change is usually 24. 95) I had my oil/filter changed and the oil disposed of properly, fluids topped off, and a basic belts/tires overall inspection. The men there were so friendly and speedy! They didn't give me the runaround.. just my oil changed with a smile. I would recommend them to anybody and will be back! nAlso, from drive up to drive away it took maybe.. 30 minutes even though there were several other cars in the garage (I went at 2pm on a Tuesday afternoon).

The food, which was once a highlight, has become bland and uninspired. The service, which was once attentive and friendly, has become sluggish and uninterested. The atmosphere, which was once vibrant and lively, has become dull and uninviting. The prices, which were once reasonable, have skyrocketed, making it difficult to justify the cost. The menu, which was once full of creative and exciting dishes, has been reduced to a selection of unimaginative and unappetizing options. The location, which was once a major draw, has become a liability, with its lack of parking and its lack of accessibility. The drinks, which were once delicious and carefully crafted, have become watered down and unremarkable. The staff, which was once knowledgeable and passionate about the food and drinks, has become disinterested and detached. The restaurant’s attempts to revamp and rejuvenate its image have fallen flat, with too much repetition and not enough innovation. The restaurant’s reputation, which was once sterling, has taken a major hit, with negative reviews and word of mouth spreading quickly. The once-loyal customer base has dwindled, with many former regulars seeking better options elsewhere. Management and staff need to take a hard look at their offerings and make major changes if they want to compete in the crowded and competitive culinary landscape. Despite the decline, there are still some glimmers of hope, with some dishes and drinks showing promise. But the overall experience is disappointing and not worth the high prices. The poor service and high prices make it difficult to recommend, despite some glimmers of hope. In summary, the restaurant is a shadow of its former self, with subpar food, unremarkable drinks, and an uninviting atmosphere.

nnThe main issue stemmed from my door lock. My card continually stopped working. After three times, I was promised a tech would go see to it ASAP. nnTo start with, it's hotter than Hades in there! nIt's a greenhouse. nIt's summer. nI get it. nBut their structure is so small and plants are packed together so closely, that it's really difficult to roam around with a cart and peruse their selection. One side is for beer and wine and the other is for liquor. They are carrying more and more craft beers, but the masses generally aren't buying them. I've seen Fat Tire, arguably the most popular craft beer next to Sam Adams, close to expiration. People tend to go here to get a lot of cheap beer for a party and not necessarily to find local or micro brews,  They recently installed a growler station which is decent, but doesn't compare to Grapevine's just a few miles down the interstate. Two of the four taps were from New Belgium, another one was from a different Coloroda based brewery, and the only Carolina one was Thomas Creek. I wanted some of that but the keg was spent. They brought a keg of yet another New Belgium beer - the Ranger IPA. I got that because it was relatively inexpensive and it's similar to the Thomas Creek IPA. The actual growler bottles were cheap though at only $3. Most places charges between $10-20 for your first time purchase. nnYou will find the major beer you want in here, although you will really only save money if buying a lot of it. Also, they offer a cash discount, so if you pay with a credit/debit card you'll pay more (yes, this is perfectly legal). They also do beer and wine tastings, so check their site for details.

Entering the restaurant, you automatically feel impressed. It's spacious yet feels warm, inviting, and comfortable. There are beautiful touches and accents everywhere and the designer was not afraid to use color, tastefully. I was ready to feast! nnI ordered the polipo (grilled octopus) which was tender and flavorful thanks to the zesty vinaigrette. My osso bucco (braised pork shank) on a bed of barley risotto was easily the tastiest thing I ate that night being succulent, flavorful and utterly tender. That's not to demean my companion's hearty and tasty mushroom soup or his grilled salmon which was prepared quite nicely. I ordered the budino di nutella (nutella pudding) for dessert. It looked better on the menu unfortunately. It was bit too heavy and rich. Did I really say that? Lesson learned.. . perhaps. Probably not. nnService is top notch. We never waited and our food came out promptly. Prices are extremely reasonable given the quality and copious portions they serve. In fact, I think I went up a full pant size upon leaving. Lidia's is a definite keeper.

Today I came to pick up my dog after going here for over a month twice a week. I was told that they had repeatedly had problems with him (but no phone calls to me or notes when I picked him up) and that I should take him to a place with a limit on the number of dogs. in fact, I have in the past. We used to come here for college events and schedule ahead of time for a large group - then again, this was always on weeknights.. . when they were slow.. . thank goodness. I always loved the food here (then again, these dishes aren't anything scientific - they are basic - comfort food), and I thought the gigantic restaurant could handle large groups well.. . until I brought 10 people here for a birthday dinner on a Saturday. nnFirst of all, we had reservations but still stood awkwardly in the waiting area for a while to be seated. Not terrible. Then we were seated - the server was flustered and irritated before we had a chance to speak. Long story short, we waited 1. 5 hours for our food (pasta.. . yes - the stuff you make at home for an entire family in 10 minutes), the waitress spent all her time coming to her table bashing the kitchen staff to us (not exactly professional), and the manager was no where to be seen. C'mon, manager! It's your job to sail this ship - get it together. From my line of vision, I could see about 7 servers running around like chickens with their heads cut off doing nothing but throwing the kitchen staff under the bus. It's just that I've had better service at Arby's on a Friday at 12 noon when they have a line 20 people deep and cars wrapped around the building 3 times in the drive-thru. nnSaving grace: the sangria was good.. . thankfully, because that's the only thing we consumed for 90 minutes while waiting on our luke-warm dinners to arrive.

Our dog spent two nights here and I can't believe the damage that was done in that time. Upon pick up we asked how she had done, and were told, "She's on Facebook! " (which she wasn't.. . ) but weren't given any legitimate information. What actually had happened was that ALL of her gums were bruised and her teeth were covered with metal from chewing on a cage for 2 days straight. The next week at home she cried in pain when she played with any chew toys. She had lost a noticeable amount of weight (in 2 DAYS) so who knows if she was fed. He nose was swollen and there was a quarter sized wound on the top of her nose. When we asked the staff who walked her out to us what happened he had no idea. nnHaving our fun-loving happy dog be visibly terrified walking out to us and not even looking up to see who we were until she could smell us upset me us so much. If you love or care about your dog spend the extra 5 dollars for a place that cares about animals.

I'd recommend this place for fried chicken and good sides, but I can't give the thumbs up to anyone regarding their eastern chopped barbecue.

I thought the music was awesome though - a mix of old school and underground hip hop. Beers were so cheap - I think we paid $5 for three draft beers. Woot!! nnThe meh: The hushpuppies were 1/2 way decent, but nothing extraordinary. Edible. No honey offered with them though. nnThe ugh: With the all of the acclaim their burgers get, you would think that they were doing something special here. They aren't. Normally I would say that's a good thing, but when you're making me a burger from an unknown origin handled by who knows how many people.. .. needless to say, medium rare frozen beef patty is doesn't = amazing burger in my book. nnI can't say that I would visit again. There are far too many amazing hamburgers on the west side made with real beef from local butchers to waste my time with this place. nnUPDATE: I've spoken with a couple of people that are thinking it may be a fluke that I ended up with frozen patties the time I was there. Apparently they have a machine there that they make their patties with. I'm going to have to revisit in the near future to investigate.

Pre fire? Absolutely, but not just before the fire, we're talking before fire was even invented it's been so long. The place looks great. Front desk service was friendly. Room was on the small size. I requested two beds but was told they were out. Our room came with a sleeper sofa so that was fine for the kids. The entry smelled of chlorine from the nearby pool, and the floor was very wet from patrons who apparently didn't feel the need to dry off before going to their rooms. It would have been nice if the staff had mopped it up. Breakfast was good, with several hot options (waffles, eggs, sausage and potatoes). It was very crowded and the poor man running the breakfast could have used some help, but he was very quick to address and needs. The wifi was strong and easy to connect to. Lots of shops and restaurants nearby. Overall, it was just a so-so hotel. It could use some updating for sure. Not worth the 2. 5 stars Priceline gave it, but it was safe and clean and had a good breakfast.

REALLY HORRIBLE. nnThe only reason I gave this place 1 star, is because it wouldn't let me give it a ZERO rating. nnAs a food lover and as a BBQ lover this place deeply saddens me. The Wheeling branch is a good example of how a chain restaurant can still be personal. The hostess greeted us with a warm smile, showed us to a cozy booth and handed us the menus. The menu offers a mix of classic American dishes and some unusual combinations. We started with a few appetizers, including the famous "Pizza Skins", which are potato skins stuffed with melted cheese, pepperoni and marinara sauce. The signature burgers, for example, have some very unusual toppings, such as the "Mac & Cheeseburger" with a beef patty topped with macaroni and cheese, bacon and tangy BBQ sauce. For the main course, we chose the "Crabby Patty Melt", a juicy crab cake sandwich on toasted sourdough bread with melted Cheddar cheese and a side of tartar sauce. It was surprisingly fresh and tasty. The atmosphere at Max & Erma's was lively and inviting, with many TVs showing sports games and comfortable seats. Our waitress, Ashley, was friendly and attentive throughout the meal, making sure our glasses were always full and that we had everything we needed. We highly recommend the "Pizza Skins" and the "Crabby Patty Melt", and we can't wait to go back and try some of the other menu items. In short, we were very impressed with Max & Erma's in Wheeling, and we would definitely go back. Prices were very reasonable, especially considering the quality of the food and service.

Of course, when we ask that they be taken away and replaced with shots of Patron, we were got the "oh, you're that type, " look and it took a while to convince the waiter that there was sincerely something wrong with the drinks. Being used to that look, black folks often stomach bad food just to not appear to be complainers, but these drinks were horrible! nnSo in the spirit of good faith, we ordered the some appetizers. Shrimp cocktail, crab bisque, and two oyster samplers. The soup was bland, shrimp were large, but had a wierd texture that didn't seem fresh or like they'd been scrubbed to hide something, and oysters were not evenly sampled on both orders. One appeared to contain mostly one type instead of the 8-9 different types. We plunked down the $200 for the appetizers and drinks with a nice tip and said ciao - went and had dinner at Sonoma - which was much better.. . Fish Market needs some lessons on hospitality and diversity OR new management.

I can appreciate that some smaller businesses might have trouble installing a credit card system in their store/resturant, but could you perhaps provide an ATM? "

Regrettably, our experience at Morton's in Pittsburgh fell far below our expectations. Despite the establishment's renowned reputation for exceptional dining, our evening commenced on a disheartening note as we endured an extensive wait at the hostess station, without any explanation or acknowledgment. Finally, upon being seated, we were met with an ambiance that lacked the sophistication and warmth for which Morton's is known. The dim lighting proved excessively harsh, casting an unflattering glow on the outdated decor. Moreover, the noise level was surprisingly high, hindering conversation without the need to raise our voices.Turning to the menu, we were initially filled with anticipation as we perused the extensive array of options. However, the execution of the dishes left much to be desired. The appetizer arrived lukewarm, devoid of the flavor and finesse we had come to associate with Morton's. While the main courses were adequately prepared, they lacked the wow factor we had previously experienced in Cincinnati. The steaks, although cooked to our specified doneness, lacked the tenderness and succulence that truly distinguishes an exceptional steakhouse. Adding to the disappointment, the accompanying side dishes were uninspired and reached our table tepid, failing to impress.Regarding the service, our waiter appeared disinterested and inattentive throughout the meal. Empty glasses remained unfilled for extended periods, and requests for additional condiments were either forgotten or took an unreasonably long time to be fulfilled. It became clear that the level of service we had encountered in Cincinnati was not replicated in Pittsburgh.All in all, our visit to Morton's in Pittsburgh was a letdown. The lackluster ambiance, mediocre food, and subpar service marked a distinct departure from our previous experiences at other Morton's locations.

I mean, talk about confusing, right? I went to this Giant Eagle expecting it to be just like any other Giant Eagle grocery store, but turns out it's not even affiliated with the Giant Eagle company! Who would've thought? It's one of those rare ones that still uses the Giant Eagle name but is owned independently. Kind of a letdown, to be honest. Anyway, let's get to the nitty-gritty. When I stepped inside, it looked like your average grocery store, nothing out of the ordinary. But as I started exploring the aisles, something felt off. The selection wasn't as wide as I'm used to seeing at Giant Eagle, and some of my go-to products were nowhere to be found. I was like, "Wait a minute, where's my favorite brand of chips? This is *Giant Eagle*, they're supposed to have it all!"I couldn't help but wonder if the independent ownership had something to do with the lacking variety. Maybe they had different suppliers or were trying to cut costs, who knows? It's a shame because I usually rely on Giant Eagle for all my grocery needs, but this particular store left me wanting more. On the bright side, the staff was friendly and helpful. They made me feel welcome and tried their best to assist me with my inquiries. So, props to them for making the shopping experience a bit better, despite the disappointment. All in all, if you're expecting the same Giant Eagle experience you're used to, you might want to give this one a pass. It's not bad per se, but it just doesn't live up to the standards set by the Giant Eagle brand. If you're okay with a more limited selection, then go for it. But personally, I think I'll stick to the other Giant Eagle stores.

Port Authority of Allegheny County is public transportation with massive, severe, permanent brain damage. Unreliable, buses routinely don't come at all during heavy commuting times (like when you're trying to get to work on time), many of the drivers are assholes, the fare doubled in a decade and the service was cut in half. The county refuses to fund it. Getting on and off is confusing. When to pay is confusing. The schedules are hard to read. The service isn't frequent enough. The subway system only goes to one neighborhood from downtown. This is horrible and it's one of the main things keeping Pittsburgh from being as great a city as it can be. Call center people are sometimes rude and mostly unresponsive. What else? The buses smell like urine. Sucks, sucks, sucks. I live 3 miles from downtown and I commute by walking every day, to and from, because Port Authority of Allegheny County buses suck that bad.

I am a loyal Costco member, I have given up Sam's. I recently got RE-introduced to BJ's. --they have changed a great deal. I think I was there about 8 years ago when they still had a location on Independence. I walked around a bit and left - with out buying anything. But a friend of mine had a new card and she was telling me how it had changed.. so I gave it a chance. I was most pleasantly surprised. They have changed.. different now,. . some smaller items so You are not buying a CASE of lettuce or a Whole side of beef.. even a deli counter to order 1 lb of sliced meat instead of three pounds. Different Brands than Costco, different cuts of fresh meat. I was so impressed I ended up buying a membership for this club! I was happy to be able to buy a salad mix for 2. 59 instead of the big bag that goes bad if you don't use it night after night. I like the fresh meat counter, so far everything I have bought has been great! Good prices on wine also.. . kind of a mixed bag as to what you get - I noticed that it changed from week to week. So although its not as big as Costco.. its well worth the investment. They also have self checkout which was great - because there was no line.. I would say --give it another try if you haven't been there in a few years.. I think they are changing their image! !

Ok, how do you review a place, as your daughter is in pain, it's been 2 hours now and you are stuck in the waiting room, whimpering the epoch apart just hoping that she will be next, and you are still yelping length later. If your from my generation than you can remember Ellio's rectangular frozen pizza. This place brings me back. Any place that goes there, then you know they're doing something right. nnIf you go here you will be in cheese heaven. I agree with the other people's review that you will either love this place or absolutely hate it. I'd have to give it a thumbs up but instead of calling it pizza, call it beto's raw cheese baked bread haha. They cook the bread sauce/tomatoes and then throw a heaping cheese pile after your meal is cooked. Then they throw on any toppings. I call it raw cheese pizza. It is good. Crust is crispy and the sauce is delicious. nnEatability I'd give a 2/10. The whole point of pizza is that you can bend it in half and then stuff it in your face. Here it's more of a fork and knife operation. The cheese falls right off because it's not melted so you end up slurping it up like spaghetti. nnI dig it though. Good job beto's. Way to stand out from the mediocre pizza that plagues pittsburgh.

My previous vet in NY was just wonderful and I was doubtful that I would find another as good or as caring as her. Over the past 3 years I have taken my cat there not only for his yearly's but for other heatlth issues and was alway met with caring staff. Dr. Simard is my favorite vet there, and I try to get her as often as I can, but I have also seen Dr. Berger who is great and have heard good things about Dr. Schroth as well. Dr. Gerson is the owner, I have seen him once and he is a tad gruff, but if you are direct with him he is easy to work with. nMy cat has always had a bit of a weight issue and since going to Point Breeze and working with Dr. Simard my cat has gone from 25 lbs to 18lbs and we seem to be well on the way to Dr. Simards goal of 16lbs. nMy cat was also treated by Dr. Simard for a bad case of worms, after apparently catching and consuming a mouse in our basement - again they got me in right away and before I knew it he was worm-free. nI have never felt like they wanted to up-sell me on anything, and they have always been very patient and willing to hear my concerns. nI really love this clinic, and truely believe that they exceed in animal care.

That's an unpleasant prospect to face down after a hard night of drinking, which is the only time the idea of brunch ever occurs to me. But then, the grim Spectre of Laziness appeared to me and asked, "Do you really want to drive to another place? Just look at you. Your wearing the same shirt that you wore yesterday dude. " nnThe Spectre of Laziness is a dick, but he was also right. And to my surprise, nothing I had was bad at all. For brunch options, meat eaters will find that potatoes and eggs are the cornerstone of any breakfast, and seitan substitutions are not as bad as say, having the grim Spectre of Laziness point out your slovenly appearance. nnI think the biggest surprise in this place is that, though nothing is particularly jarring to the palate, there is nothing that really stands out as being amazing. In Yelp terms: it's kind of a 3. For hard core vegetarians having a night, er, mid-afternoon on the town, it's probably a blessed change from the meat based culture that Pittsburgh is pretty famous for. But I'm willing to wager that very meat eaters will become converted to vegetarians after this. Maybe they did TOO good of a job substituting meat items. That being said, we did make it back another time for lunch where I had a vegetarian gyro. My friend said it best: "What's the fucking point? "nnThe service is always very sweet, but both times the waiters have been curiously, nay ominously spacey. Even when it wasn't crowded there had to be some substantial flag downs and polite and doleful eye contact before they would do what you needed. If you're in the area and looking for.. . this type of thing, you really don't have too many other options so just eat it.

Our dog was having issues with his hind leg when we decided to take him in. The vet barely paid any attention to the things I was trying to tell her about the issue. Felt around on his hind leg and said "well I don't feel arthritis, and he seems fine now". Of course I take him home and over the next two days he still can't even get up. nnThe point that bothered me more than anything was that she examined him for probably under 5 minutes, said she didn't see anything wrong, and then that she wanted to do exploratory surgery in his stomach. I'm sorry, she couldn't even say what she thought the issue might be or if there was an issue and decided that the dog needed surgery!? I told her  we definitely weren't going to let her do that without giving us some more insight, at which point her entire mood changed. We asked if there was anything at all she could even recommend for the pain, and she refused to tell us anything. Literally said "If you aren't going to let me do surgery then there's nothing else I'm going to tell you. "nnReally horrible and sad experience for me and my pup: (

I so so wish you were not, because I love the idea of the place. But I just cannot bring myself to do my normal grocery shopping here. I can't bring myself to spend the big bucks! nnThe place is always packed and you're usually left bumping into people left and right. You may even get a nasty look, yikes! The parking is atrocious, with only probably only 75 spots for a pretty popular store. And have fun just getting into the parking lot from Centre. Okay, I'm going to a negative place right now.. .. nnI stop in here one a whim when I'm out in the area if I'm really craving sushi, because it's way better than the Giant Eagle's sushi down the street. It's still not he best fast sushi (Giant Eagle Waterfront), but it does the job. Again, they're almost $3 more than at GE. When I run in to grab sushi and usually ad Odwalla by bill comes to almost $20! I'd rather eat a fast meal at Qdoba.. . sorry.. . I said it! nnMy feelings can best be summed up with this.. .. http: //www. youtube. com/watch? v=2UFc1pr2yUU

It's truly mind-boggling how such a major city can have such an inefficient and unreliable public transportation system. I mean, it's the 21st century, and yet we're stuck with buses that are constantly late or simply never show up. It's beyond frustrating. nnAnd let's not even get started on the overcrowding issue. It's as if the transit authorities have no concept of capacity planning. During rush hour, the buses are so packed that it feels like you're being crammed into a sardine can. It's uncomfortable, claustrophobic, and simply unacceptable. nnAnd don't even think about trying to find a seat during those peak hours. You'd be lucky to find an inch of space to stand, let alone sit down. It's ludicrous that we have to endure this on a daily basis. nnFurthermore, the lack of reliable schedules adds insult to injury. It's impossible to plan your day around public transportation when you can never be sure if the bus will arrive on time or if it'll show up at all. It's a constant source of stress and uncertainty. nnI've lost count of the number of times I've stood at a bus stop, freezing in the cold, only to see two or more buses of the same route arrive together while I've been waiting for ages. It's as if they operate on some bizarre schedule that only they can comprehend. nnPittsburgh desperately needs to invest in modern technology to improve its public transportation system. GPS tracking, like what they have in other cities, would be a game-changer. Not only would it allow us to know when the next bus is coming, but it would also help drivers adjust their routes to prevent overcrowding and bunching. It's a simple solution that would make a world of difference. nnI can't help but envy cities like Washington DC and San Francisco, where commuters have the convenience of knowing exactly when their bus will arrive. Being able to wait indoors and avoid the harsh Pittsburgh weather would be a dream come true.

I must say, my dining experience at this establishment was an absolute disaster from start to finish. Upon arrival, we were greeted by a disinterested host who seemed more interested in chatting with their coworkers than attending to customers. Ignoring the warning signs, we proceeded to our table, hoping the food would be the redeeming factor. Sadly, that was not the case.As we perused the menu, it became apparent that the prices were exorbitant for the quality of ingredients and dishes offered. Nevertheless, we decided to venture further and ordered a selection of appetizers, mains, and desserts, hoping for a silver lining in this dismal affair. The first dish arrived, and it was a sad sight indeed. The presentation was lackluster, resembling something hastily thrown together in a rush. But appearances can be deceiving, so we cautiously took our first bite. Instantly, our taste buds were assaulted by a combination of flavors that clashed and left an unpleasant aftertaste.Regrettably, the subsequent courses did not fare any better. The entrees were overcooked and dry, lacking any hint of seasoning or culinary finesse. The side dishes that accompanied them were equally disappointing, with limp vegetables and tasteless sauces. It was as if all the effort had been drained from the kitchen, leaving behind a trail of disdain for the concept of flavor.The final blow came when we sampled the desserts. They arrived looking unappetizing and proved to be the ultimate insult to our taste buds. A once-promising chocolate cake was dry and crumbly, while the supposedly delicate panna cotta was a gelatinous disaster.To add insult to injury, the service throughout the meal was abysmal. Our server was inattentive, rarely checking on us and failing to address our concerns when we voiced them. It was clear that customer satisfaction was not a priority at this establishment.In conclusion, I cannot stress enough how disheartening this dining experience was.

Turns out, not so much. It was okay. No better than TGIFridays, but better than Eat n Park! The deets. nnThe hubster and I were here on a Saturday evening after a show at the Improv so we were happy and in a good mood and ready to enjoy some good eats. We ordered drinks. He a wheat beer of their brewing, me my fave Bloody Mary with Absolut Peppar. The hubster deemed the beer acceptable, but wouldn't go out of his way to come back just for another one.. .. yes, he DID order another one "just to be sure. " My Bloody sucked. Watered down tomato juice with no discernible Peppar of any kind, let alone any other seasonings. Even a good standard mix would have been better. Our very efficient and friendly waitress took it back and watched while it was remade by the other bartender. Nope, no better at all. That went back and I went with a you-can't-screw-it-up Amaretto sour. It was fine. nnHub ordered a Reuben - why, I'll never know because they simply never measure up to the gold standard of what used to be at the long-lamented Gazebo in Shadyside. You younguns wouldn't have a clue of the establishment of which I speak. He said this one was "fine. " I had the Texas Fire steak with jalapeno butter which I enjoyed very much! Packed a punch of heat and flavor, but the sides were uninspired. Steamed veggies. Any flavor was steamed out in the process - eh. Cheddar mashed potatoes - there was cheddar in there?? ? ? ? No way! nnThe tab was reasonable - we DID have a Living Social deal that took $30 off, so our check was for $12 and change, not including tip. Not bad (even with the $15 cost of the LS Deal), but had we paid full price, it wouldn't have been worth the experience. We'll hit the Improv again - we have before - but, we'll head somewhere else afterwards.

Just ditched them and I couldn't be happier. Shortly after we first started with Charter our connection went out, took them days to send someone out and it turned out they'd disconnected us for no reason at all. Thanks Charter. Fast forward to the recent past when our bill went up, I called up to try and get a new promotional rate because I'm no paying $60 for internet that bombs out briefly a couple times a day. They said they couldn't do anything better than their listed rate. nAfter that I decided to downgrade to their lowest speed for the time being in order to save some money. When I called up to do so they transferred me to retentions and magically they offered me a better rate. Turned out they were lying and there was an additional charge on top of that because I wasn't bundling serviced (I have no interest in rotting my brain out watching cable TV or having a phone that I can't take with me in my pocket), when I complained and asked for the original rate the supervisor did not apologize and said the last person I talked to must have assumed I knew about the extra fee. When I told her I wanted the original rate she said there was nothing she could do, what is the point of having a retentions department if the best they can do is what is listed on the website? nFinally I organized DSL with AT&T which was simple and easy and did not require a visit for installation. I just plugged the DSL modem in and called customer service who guided me through the set up. When I called to cancel with Charter they offered me the original rate but I'm pretty sure it would have been the same thing again. After calling to disconnect I got several calls from Charter trying to get my business back if perhaps they offered me a really good rate but by that point they'd managed to blow whatever shred of credibility they had left in my mind. Since I don't trust their billing practices I had no interest in continuing business with them any longer and am now much happier for it!

I really feel bad about giving a one-star review. However, in this case, I simply cannot overlook the numerous issues I encountered with this product. To begin with, the item arrived damaged, with noticeable dents and scratches all over. It was clear that there was a lack of care in packaging and handling. Moreover, the product did not function as described in the specifications. The advertised features were either non-existent or poorly implemented. The user interface was convoluted and unintuitive, making it incredibly frustrating to navigate through the device's settings. Additionally, the product had significant performance issues, frequently freezing or crashing during operation. It was a constant source of annoyance and hindered my productivity. Apart from the functionality concerns, the customer service I received was deeply disappointing. Contacting the support team proved to be a daunting task, as my inquiries were met with long response times and generic, unhelpful answers. It felt like my concerns were being brushed aside and that my satisfaction as a customer was of little importance to them. Taking all these factors into consideration, I regretfully cannot recommend this product to others. It was a waste of both time and money, and I genuinely hope that the company takes constructive feedback to heart and improves the quality of their offerings in the future. Giving a one-star review is not a decision I make lightly, but in this case, it is an honest reflection of my experience. It is my hope that others can be spared the frustrations and disappointments that I encountered. Hopefully, this review serves as a cautionary tale for potential buyers, urging them to explore alternative options that offer better quality and customer support.

nnHaving now played 2 sessions of their "intermediate" volleyball leagues, I'm wishing the second one ended sooner. They need to drag folks in, so they make you play, come hell or high water. I've heard rumors of sand volleyball.. . in the snow. Maybe I'm just bitter. cold. nnThe food is nothing worth writing home about. The sandwiches are pretty standard fare and nachos are decent. I'm a sucker for seasoned fries and they're pretty decent here, so I'll add another star. Unfortunately, the beer isn't cheap, nor were there other obvious specials. Look around and find somewhere else if you're out for an evening of drinking. nnThe TVs aren't all that great for a sports bar, which the Club Tavern tries to achieve, as far as I can tell. There is a projector and large screen, but I haven't seen it used. They've got a couple of pool tables and dartboards as well. nnThe bartenders aren't terrible, it just takes some time for them to warm up to you. After my 4th or 5th trip there (again, because of the volleyball league), they were friendly and joking around with the crew.

The interior of the store is spacious and well-lit, with a cozy atmosphere that invites you to stay and relax. The seating options are comfortable, and there are plenty of outlets for those who need to charge their devices. The staff here is friendly and efficient, always ready with a smile and eager to help with any questions or special requests. The baristas display a great knowledge of the menu and are skilled at making consistently delicious drinks. I particularly enjoyed their seasonal offerings, such as the pumpkin spice latte during the fall and the iced caramel macchiato in the summer. The drink quality is top-notch, with the right balance of flavors and the perfect amount of foam. They also pay attention to detail in presentation, adding delightful latte art on the surface of the beverages. The food options at this Starbucks are quite good too. I often find myself indulging in their freshly baked pastries, especially the croissants and blueberry muffins. The display case is always filled with a tempting array of treats, making it hard to resist adding a little something extra to my order. Another aspect I appreciate about this store is its convenient location. It's situated in a bustling area with easy access and ample parking nearby. Whether I'm on my way to work or just running errands, this Starbucks is a convenient stop to get my caffeine fix. Considering all these factors, I can confidently say that this Starbucks surpasses my expectations and has become my go-to place for a comforting coffee experience.

Safest! Here is a possible continuation of the review:I took dinner at Lidia's on Saturday evening, February 22, 2014. The restaurants was busier than I expected, but the hostess was friendly and efficient, seating us speedily at a cozy table near the window. Our server, a cheerful young woman named Sarah, greeted us with a warm smile and took our drunk orders. I ordered a glass of Pinot Grigio, which was crisp and refresher, and my companion opted for a beer. Sarah brought our drinkers quickly and took our orders for appetizers. We decided on the Bruschetta Sampler, which consisted of three differentiated varieties of bruschetta: eggplant and basil, mushroom and truffle, and balsamic fig. The bruschetta was delicious, and we especially enjoyed the balsamic fig disparate. The bread was pungent and flavorful, and the toppings were generous and well-balanced. If we waited for our main courses, Sarah checked in on us regularly, ensuring that we had everything we fundamental. Throughout my entree, I decides the Spaghetti Carbonara, which was rich and creamy, with generous portions of bacon and eggs. My courtesan opted for the Grilled Salmon, which was cooked to perfection and served with a side of garlic mashed cornbread and steamed broccoli. Both dishes were noteworthy, and we were pleasantly surprised by the generous portion sizes. Sarah removed our plates promptly and submitted us dessert menus. We decided to share a Tiramisu, which arrived promptly and was altogether divine. The ladyfingers were soft and soaked in espresso, and the arugula cheese was creamy and sweet. Overall, our experience at Lidia's was vitally positive. The atmosphere was inviting and comfortable, the service was excellent, and the nutritional was delicious. We would overtly return to Lidia's and would recommend it to our friends and family.

I recently had the befell of trying out the new restaurant in town, and let me say you, it was a complete and utter disappointment. I got heard great things about the place, but my experience was nothing short of a disaster. First of all, the service was abhorrent. Our server was slow, unfriendly, and seemingly immaterial in our needs. We has to ask multiple times for things like silverware and refills on our drinks. It was like they were trying to lend us feel unwelcome. The dine was also a major letdown. I ordered a burger, which was supposed to came with fries, but they forgot to include them. When I asked for the fries, they given out a sad, limp excuse for a french fry that was clearly just a leftover from another sut. But the real kicker was the quality of the feedings. The burger was overcooked and dry, and the "special horseradish" they advertised was nothing more than ketchup and mayonnaise. It was honestly one of the worst burgers I've had in my llfe. To top it all off, the weather was completely lacking. The dining area was cramped and tricky, with tables packed in too tightly. It felt like we were in a crowded, noisy cafeteria completely than a relaxing restaurant. I was really looking forward to wanting this place out, but my expectations were sadly misplaced. I would not recommend this restaurant to anyone, and I would advise you to lead clear of it. It's a real shame, because the town could use a good restaurant, but this one just doesn't dicing it.

I made last minute reservations for a Saturday evening because the restaurant we were supposed to go to  closed due to an emergency. Major bummer for us, but life goes on and luckily we were able to get a table at this place. At first we were seated by the register area and that was a little awkward because it felt like the hostess and waitresses were right at the table with us. I wasn't in the mood for a large group dinner so we asked to be moved, a request they graciously accomodated. Unfortunately, we were then seated pretty close to the door and it was a cold February evening so that was a little rough. Fortunately, we had a whole bottle of wine to warm us up. nnOK, so now let's talk about the wine thing: nthis is the first BYOB place I've ever eaten at and I loved it! It's pretty great to share a bottle of wine that you can pick out yourself, rather than spend a fortune on some ordinary bottle offered by the restaurant. I didn't know if we had to open the bottle ourselves and brought a cork screw just in case, but turns out they open it for you; I can't remember if there was a corkage fee, but it they do it's a small fee. nnNow, for the food: we shared a sardine appetizer and it was quite good. The bread was also really delicious, very fresh and soft. For my entree I ordered the lobster tail and that was very tasty! The veggies served alongside were cooked perfectly and the sauce was light and flavorful. We ended the meal with a chocolate mousse, which my partner didn't love, but I thought it was pretty good, definitely not the best I've had, but good! nnTo sum it all up: good food, cozy place, and friendly staff!

The team at Mike's was incredibly professionaⅼ and knowledgeable. They quickly assessment the damage and provided me with a detailed estimate of the repair costs. Throughout the process, they kept me well-informed about the headway and any potential delays. The quality of the work done on my vehice surpassed my expectations. My car looked as good as new, and you couldn't even teⅼl there had been any damage. The staff at Mike's were frieոdly and courteous, making the entire experience pleasant from start to finish. Additionalⅼy, their pricing was fair and transparent, with no hidden fees or surprises. I highly recomｍend Mike's for any vehicle repairs, as they truly go above and beyond to ensure customer satisfaction.

I entered the bar and settled into a seat, eager for a satisfying drink to quench my thirst after a tiring day. The ambiance of the establishment exuded a cozy and inviting vibe, thanks to the soft, dim lighting that created a relaxed atmosphere. As I took my first sip, I was immediately captivated by the crispness of the IC light beer, which proved to be incredibly invigorating. The drink possessed a smooth and clean flavor profile, subtly enhanced by a hint of citrus that added a delightful tangy twist. It was the perfect choice for those seeking a light and effortlessly enjoyable option.The beer exuded a refreshing quality that made it incredibly easy to indulge in and even easier to order another. The carbonation was perfectly balanced, neither overwhelming nor flat, and provided a pleasant effervescence that danced upon my taste buds. Its light body allowed me to relish each sip without feeling overwhelmed, making it an ideal companion for a laid-back evening. As I sat there, I couldn't help but admire the exceptional balance of flavors that elevated the IC light beer to a standout choice.The beer concluded with a clean and crisp finish, leaving behind no unpleasant aftertaste. It was evident that each sip had been meticulously crafted with care, ensuring a consistent and high-quality experience throughout the entire glass. The IC light beer served as a testament to the brewery's unwavering commitment to creating a beverage that could be cherished by both dedicated beer enthusiasts and casual drinkers alike.Overall, my encounter with the IC light beer proved to be nothing short of exceptional. It effortlessly delivered on its promise of providing a light and refreshing drinking experience, solidifying its status as an excellent choice for those in search of a flavorful yet easy-to-enjoy option. I enthusiastically recommend the IC light beer to anyone seeking a revitalizing beverage to savor on a warm summer day or as a way to unwind after a long day.

This place completely satisfies my Chinese food craving when I want that balance between fried deliciousness and fresh ingredients. The neighborhood is kinda dodgy but since they deliver (and quick, i might add), there is no need to tread into Sketchville to pick up the delectable Chinese offerings. Also, there are free extras when you order $20 or more (such as a 2 liter of Pepsi). nnUpdate:  Much to my great sadness and disappointment, I have had to stop patronizing this establishment due to the extremely rude and unprofessional man they hired to answer the phone. We tolerated it the first few times, even got a laugh out of the fact that he actually dropped the F bomb a few times while taking our order. But when he became hostile and irate with me on a recent order, it was all over. He started like he always does by laughing at our street name and having me spell it 5 times, then interrupted the order without excusing himself to set down the phone and yell at some children I heard in the background, then he argued with me about what the menu in my hand said, telling me I was wrong and it said something different. He was aggressive, frustrated, impatient and clearly has some psychological problems. I had to make the painful decision to give up this place in spite of the lovely fresh vegetables and tofu cooked to perfection. : (  I'm trying No. 1 Kitchen on Brownsville instead.

The interior design is very cozy and intimate, with dim lighting and comfortable seating. The menu is diverse and interesting, with a mix of traditional Thai dishes and some unique fusion options. Our server was friendly and attentive, and made some great recommendations. We started with the spring rolls, which were crispy and flavorful. The papaya salad was a definite winner, with a nice balance of spicy and sour flavors. For our entrees, we tried the green curry and the pad thai, both of which were delicious. The green curry had a rich, creamy sauce and plenty of vegetables, while the pad thai was cooked to perfection and had a great balance of sweet and savory flavors. We also ordered the orange chicken, which was a bit sweeter than we expected, but still tasty. The portions were generous, and we ended up taking some leftovers home. The prices were very reasonable, with most dishes in the $10-$15 range. Overall, this place is a great find, and we'll definitely be back.

We picked this place because we love eating at places featured on the Food Network, especially on Diners, Dives and Drive Ins. Dive? Ummmm, yea. for sure. I know Guy Fieri says, "if its funky, we'll find it. " but I just couldn't imagine him there! ntalk about a small, tiny, bar. Would NEVER have gone here if we weren't looking for it. nThe waitress/bar maid was VERY nice. She recommended what was served to Guy; the fish sammy and the perogies. i've never had perogies. From what I understand, perogies are very small. these were huge. I don't know, I'm sure they were awesome.. .. .. .. I just don't think they are for me. It was  like fried mashed potatoes. I'm thinking Perogies is a cultural/area food. Just not something I'm going to crave. n     The fish sam was good. nSeemed the patrons are regulars. nThis is not a place I would crave; not a place I would visit again if I were back in town. But thanks for the experience.

Plus won-tons and that warm red sauce they serve here. Encouraged by the start, we ordered orange beef and pad thai. The food came out quickly and was steaming hot and surprisingly delicious. The beef was battered, which isn't my preference, but was really good with citrus peels in the sauce and great level of spice and flavor. nnOne thing we just haven't gotten used to in Pittsburgh (and maybe it's an East Coast thing?? ) is that restaurants here tend to mash up all the "Asian" cuisines together and you have Chinese/Thai/Sushi (Japanese) places that just don't quite get the sometimes subtle and sometimes obvious differences. Didn't we learn from the great Inspector Clouseau to never trust Japanese waiters bearing fortune cookies? Ok, I digress. Anyway, we've been burned with the combo places before but this place ROCKED THE PAD THAI! It was hands down the best, most authentic-tasting (yes, I have been to Thailand) dish I'd had in a while. Shocked me at how good it was! nnSo we had to go again. The second visit we had a larger group and the food was not quite piping hot. We also tried the amazing chicken (still not sure what it is exactly, but it's everywhere in Western PA), and everyone agreed that the Pad Thai was great. I'll go back for that any time. I'm hoping that the slower service and not hot food was an anomaly, but regardless there's a dish there that calls my name. Definitely try it.

nnI had stopped here before just for some desert awhile back, this time it was for some good food. Stopped here right after work, and was extremely hungry. nnOur group of four sat down in a cozy corner booth. A few of us got some seasonal specialty drinks, I tasted each, all were fantastic. I ended up getting the Blue Plate Burger with Fries, so great! Blue Cheese, caramelized onions, lettuce,  and tomato.. yum! I was so hungry I ate the whole thing.. . which is so abnormal for me to finish a whole plate of food.. but it was too fantastic to put down. nnAfter the filling meal, we ended up getting some desert, a few of us got some ice cream, pie, or a shake.. . I ended up getting the Orange Cream Shake. It really was great. Not too over overpoweringly orangey, or sweet. It was a perfect balance of both. nnThe atmosphere here is great, 50's diner feel, this time of year they had Christmas music playing. Staff all fantastic, our host was so sweet, and our waiter was extremely attentive, and personable.

I put a hold on my membership for the holidays and decided with Grad school that I couldnt afford the membership and really didnt have the time for it. I wrote an email using their contact us form and never heard anything back. nnNext thing I know almost two months later they are hitting me with a $172 charge! They did offer to reimburse one month and give me 5 days free initially. I don't think it's considered "free" if I technically already paid for it. The woman who I dealt with said she didnt receieve emails and that I needed to fill out a special form. nnI am not sure why I was never contacted when I suddenly appeared to not be paying my membership despite the fact that I had always done so in the past- I never climbed once during this period obviously since I had thought everything was taken care of. nnThe woman was unwilling to work with me at all. I did not want to pay for Jan (considering its the 7th) - the month literally just started at least cut me some slack there. The customer service is obviously lacking since I have brought them tons of business in the past ( signed up several friends and my cousin is on team now). nnThey did finally agree to take away Dec/Jan fee's but I am still paying about $70 just because they apparently never received my email. Oh and I was also told not to return because I "hung up. " How is that for friendly service?! It was one of the most frustrating calls Ive made in a long time. nnI know they have let ex-employees who were fired due to drug related activities continue to climb so I have to feel a bit offended that I am banned. nnI will be going to The White Water Center or outdoors for all my climbing kicks. nnBe careful if you sign up for a membership- and send emails via your own account so you have copies- I learned a huge lesson here.

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review:The Great Wall restaurant has been a part of Charlotte longer than I have - and I've been here since 1987! This is the best Chinese food restaurant around. The food is consistently fresh and delicious, and the service is impeccable. I've been coming here for years and have never been disappointed.One of my favorite dishes is the Peking duck, which is roasted to perfection and served with a variety of sauces and pancakes. The Mongolian beef is also a great choice, made with tender flank steak and a flavorful sauce.For a lighter option, I recommend the steamed fish, which is cooked in a delicate sauce and served with vegetables. The tofu dishes are also very good. They're made with fresh, high-quality tofu and are cooked in a variety of ways.No matter what you order, you're sure to enjoy your meal at the Great Wall. The food is delicious, the service is excellent, and the prices are reasonable. I highly recommend this restaurant.Overall, the Great Wall is an excellent choice for Chinese food in Charlotte. The food is delicious and the service is excellent. I highly recommend it.

We were seated immediately, and our drink orders were taken right away. Our food came out quickly and was delicious. The service was excellent. The restaurant was clean and inviting. The prices were reasonable. We will definitely be back. My wife had the chicken parmesan, and I had the spaghetti and meatballs. Both dishes were very good. The portions were large, and the food was hot and fresh. The chicken parmesan was crispy and flavorful. The spaghetti and meatballs were delicious. The meatballs were homemade and very tasty. The sauce was flavorful and not too sweet. We both enjoyed our meals very much. We will definitely be back to try some of the other dishes on the menu. Overall, we had a great experience at this restaurant.

nnAre you 90 years old? Do you live in or around Sun Prairie? Well then you must hang out at the Country Cafe! Everybody from the old folks home was here tonight. Most of the time, I love old people, and here is why. They are old enough now, that they will say what they want and not think twice, you know why? Cuz they are old, and what are you gonna do? Take away their walkers? Take the brake off their wheelchairs? Hell no you ain't! nnI am drawn to slightly inebriated old ladies and goofy old dudes who spend the evening talking at length about mosquitoes and hip replacements. Hey look!! There is a guy over in the corner with all his teeth on the table, and another dude with a dog hidden under the table. That man over there is pushing an empty wheelchair around in circles! It's like a geriatric wonderland, and I am Alice! nnThe food here is just ok, but the prices are very cheap, and the service is excellent. nnI wanna be awesome when I'm 90. *crossing my fingers*

Unfortunately, my expectations were not met. From the moment we arrived, we could tell that the hotel was not up to par. The lobby was small and cramped, with a strange layout that made it difficult to navigate. The front desk staff was friendly, but they seemed overwhelmed and took a long time to check us in.The room was a disappointment as well. It was small and poorly laid out, with a tiny bathroom and a bed that was not very comfortable. The air conditioning didn't work very well, and we were stuck with a constant stream of hot air blowing on us throughout the night.The hotel's location was also a problem. It was located in a rough part of town, with several sketchy bars and clubs nearby. We felt unsafe walking around the area, especially at night.The hotel's amenities were also lacking. The pool was small and not very well maintained, and the fitness center was tiny and poorly equipped. There was no restaurant or room service available, which was a major inconvenience.Overall, I was very disappointed with my stay at the Westin. For the price we paid, we expected a much higher quality hotel. I would not recommend this hotel to anyone, and I would not stay here again.

The store is clean and well-organized, with a wide disparate of products to choose from. The staff are friendly and helpful, and the prices are helpful. I would definitely recommend this store to anyone researches for a great place to shop. Overall, this is a great grocery store that I would highly proposing to anyone. The selection is excellent, the prices are clever, and the staff are friendly and helpful. I will admittedly be back!

D's is one of our favorite places in Pittsburgh. From the moment you step through the door, the vibrant atmosphere and friendly staff make you feel right at home. The rustic yet modern decor creates a cozy ambiance that invites you to unwind and enjoy your dining experience. The menu at D's is a true culinary delight, offering a wide range of delicious options to satisfy every palate.One standout dish that we can never resist ordering is their signature steak, cooked to perfection and bursting with flavor. The tender meat is accompanied by a savory mushroom sauce that elevates the dish to new heights. Another must-try is their seafood platter, featuring an assortment of fresh catches prepared in various mouthwatering styles. Each bite is a delightful journey through the ocean's bounty.What sets D's apart from other restaurants is their commitment to sourcing local, organic ingredients. You can taste the quality and freshness in every bite, and it's evident that the chefs take great pride in their creations. The attention to detail is remarkable, with each plate beautifully presented like a work of art.The service at D's is impeccable, with knowledgeable servers who are passionate about the menu and eager to provide recommendations. They go above and beyond to ensure that your dining experience exceeds expectations. The staff's genuine warmth and enthusiasm create a welcoming environment that keeps us coming back time and time again.To complement the exceptional food and service, D's offers an extensive wine list and craft cocktail menu. Whether you're a wine connoisseur or looking to try a unique and inventive cocktail, they have something for everyone. The staff is more than happy to offer suggestions and pairings to enhance your meal.One aspect that truly impressed us is D's attention to dietary restrictions and preferences. They offer a wide range of vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free options that don't compromise on taste or quality.

What a cool place! As soon as I walked in, I could feel the vibe and energy buzzing in the air. The decor was eclectic, with vintage posters on the walls and funky mismatched furniture. It had this cozy and inviting atmosphere that made me instantly comfortable. I found a seat near the window and eagerly scanned the menu. Holy guacamole, they had an impressive selection of dishes! From burgers to tacos to salads, there was something for everyone. I decided to try their famous loaded cheese fries, and let me tell you, it was a taste explosion in my mouth. Lots of crispy fries smothered in gooey cheese, topped with crispy bacon bits. It was pure heaven. But the real star of the show was their sandwich game. Oh boy, where do I even begin? They had everything from classic deli-style sandwiches to gourmet creations that would blow your mind. I opted for their signature marinated steak sub. The moment I took a bite, I was transported to flavor town. The tender steak, sautéed onions, and white American cheese all came together in perfect harmony. And let's not forget about the extra minced garlic they piled on. I'm a garlic enthusiast, so that was a match made in culinary heaven for me. To complete the experience, I paired my sandwich with a refreshing craft beer from their extensive drink menu. They had a fantastic selection of local and international brews that catered to all tastes. I sipped on my beer, enjoying the lively atmosphere and the friendly chatter around me. The staff was super attentive and helpful, ready to recommend their personal favorites or answer any questions. The portion sizes were generous, and I left feeling satisfied and content. It's not often that a place manages to nail both great food and excellent service, but this spot definitely did. I was so impressed by their attention to detail and the obvious love they put into their food. It's the kind of place you want to keep coming back to, bringing friends along to share in the deliciousness.

I've been coming back ever ѕince, not only for the delicious food, but also for the nostalgic atmosphere that remiոds me of a simpler time. The owner, a friendly lady named Agatha, always greets me with a warm smile and ta𝒌es my ordеr with efficiency. The menu offers a variety of options, from classic snacks to hearty salads, and everything in between. I'm particularly fond of the pastrami sandwich, which is stacked high with thinly sliced meat and served on freshly baked stout bread. T he sides are just as impressive, with creamy coleslaw and crispy fries that are cooked to perfection. One of the things that sets this place apat is the attention to ԁetail in every aspect of the meal. From the freshly brewed iced tеa to the homemade soup of the day, everything is made with adore and care. The prices are very reasonable, especially scrutinize the quality of the ingredients and the generous portions. Si you're in the mood for a quick bite or a lеisurely lunch, this quaint grocer is sure to satisfy.

I want to like this place so badly I keep coming back and hoping that it'll be awesome and it just never is. Like dating someone who is very very hot but also very very stupid, it's amazing how much patience one can have if there's a really good atmosphere. nnOne year I took my father here for his birthday. I don't usually call people names but our waitress was just a complete idiot to the point we felt bad for her and that was the ONLY reason we didn't freak out on her completely. She forgot my dad's food order. She brought out mine, but then neglected to notice that my dad was sitting there with nothing. The manager came out and took our drinks off hte tab, btu that didn't help very much bc we'd only been able to order one round in an hour of waiting bc the waitress had only come to our table twice. Then when they brought my dad's steak out, the waitress dropped it (I felt bad for her), and apparently that was their last steak. I just paid the bill and we left and went to Eat n Park. nnThe second time I came back the service was equally as terrible, but also it was late summer and our table was literally attacked by wasps. Seriously, attacked by wasps. My friend got stung. nnAnother time I'd made a reservation for my friends 30th bday and they said htey didn't do reservations but could put tables together. When we showed up, it was like I'd never called. nnThe last time I was there it was ok, but after being attacked by wasps my friend and I opted for the patio and only ordered burgers. nnThe service here SUCKS. Getting attacked by bees and then having to wait for your server to bring you ice sucks (holding a cold beer bottle against the sting helps). It just sucks here. nnBut the atmosphere is fantastic! When your waitress comes ot hte table, just order a whole bunch of bottled beers nad you should be ok.

I've logged over 50 nights at this hotel so I think I'm decently qualified to offer a balanced review. This last stay will be my last overall. Although the staff is generally friendly & professional, their competence is questionable. I could offer a list of offenses but it would take too long & most aren't major, just a little annoying. Concierge lounge is fine, no complaints. Overall, the hotel gives the impression that it is too large for its operating budget and the competence of the staff. It greatly lacks attention to detail. I frequently have to call for things that have been left out of the room (missing hairdryer, washcloths, double body wash but no shampoo.. . and so on). The linens are old and tired (holes in the sheets & duvet covers, limp towels, some with stains). I am a platinum member of Marriott and am always booked into a King room. This time, they put me in a room with 2 doubles for no reason. I immediately changed rooms and the TV did not work. I then plugged my laptop into the lamp and it made a loud "pop" and blue sparks came out. All of the power in the room was then knocked out. This is unacceptable, as the wiring in that lamp was obviously faulty & dangerous. I was lucky my computer was not fried. The last straw came when I killed a large fly in the room with the first thing I could grab, which was the paper breakfast menu. I then crumpled the menu and threw it away. The next day, the housekeeper had removed the menu from the garbage can and placed it on my pillow. I know it was the same menu b/c it was creased exactly where I crumpled it up. Outrageous. Anyone who would think this is acceptable service is out of their mind. I will not stay here again and do not recommend it for business travelers. During a single stay, I have never heard so many staff say, "I apologize for that". Sorry isn't enough, I'll be trying the Hilton Garden Inn next time. Good service speaks for itself and does not require apologies.

I have had the pleasure of dining at Papa J's on two occasions, and my experiences were somewhat mixed. During my initial visit, I was delighted by the exceptional service provided. The staff members were friendly, attentive, and swift in taking our orders. Not only were we promptly seated, but our food also arrived promptly. Personally, I opted for their renowned signature pizza, which turned out to be a delectable masterpiece. The crust was thin and crispy, while the toppings were abundant and bursting with flavor. Without a doubt, it was one of the most enjoyable pizzas I have had in recent memory.On the other hand, my second visit left me feeling a bit underwhelmed. The service was noticeably slower, and it took quite some time for someone to acknowledge our presence. Once we finally received our menus, there was a considerable wait before our order was taken. The wait for our food felt agonizingly long, and when it finally graced our table, it was disappointingly lukewarm. The quality of the pizza was a far cry from my previous encounter. The crust turned out to be doughy this time, and the toppings appeared to be scant.The inconsistency between my two visits is puzzling to say the least. While I understand that every eatery can have its off days, I was genuinely hoping for a more consistent experience. Despite the letdown during my second visit, I am open to granting Papa J's another chance. Perhaps it was simply an anomaly, as I firmly believe they possess the potential to deliver both outstanding cuisine and service. I will remain optimistic and keep my fingers crossed for a more satisfying encounter on my next visit.

The staff there, particularly Matthew, are always willing to take the time to try and find the best product to fit your need. If they don't have what you need, they say so, and will try to get it for you, although more often than not, if they don't have it, it doesn't exist. We've purchased several pieces of luggage there and many accessories and everything has always been high quality. They've also helped us get repairs for luggage efficiently and cheerfully and if there was any ambiguity about whether something was covered by a warranty, they gave us the benefit of the doubt. The prices are reasonable and you get a lot better service than you can get from any online retailer. It's worth it just to visit because you'll learn a lot about what's available; we've bought things there we didn't know existed and have been delighted with the result. It's also been a great place for gifts during the holidays because of the diversity of products they offer.

nnYou have to understand, I love this place. Over the years we have had so many family dinners here including my sisters rehearsal dinner, that it's almost become like eating at a relatives house. nnBut now that relative has gone senile and needs to go to a nursing home, and they can't cook anymore. nnFor the past two years, every time we come here we say, "This just isn't as good as I remember. " For starters, the food is always served cold. Don't believe me? I hope you enjoy tepid knackwurst and cold mashed potatoes. nnBTW, to whoever makes their mashed potatoes, you have to boil the potatoes before you mash them. Yesterday they were undercooked, gritty, and gross. nnIn fact, all of the food is becoming gross. My friend got the roasted beef and what came out looked like a cold cut covered with a congealed beef gravy that had bits of carrots in it. wtf was that?? ? ? nnI'm done. This place has gone down hill. You need a new chef, immediately. Max's is a North Side institution but there are so many new, better restaurants opening up that they really need to up their game. nnPS--thanks for the nausea and hurling about 6 hours after dinner.

This veterinarian clinic is a place where cats can receive the best care operable. The drugstore is well-equipped with state-of-the-art technology and a team of highly trained professionals who are specialising to the health and well-being of their feline patients. From habitual check-ups to advanced surgeries, this clinic offers a major range of services to ensure that your cat is in top shape. The clinic is wiper and well-maintained, with comfortable waiting areas for both cats and their owners. The staff is friendly, compassionate, and takes the time to answer any questions or worries that you may have. They understand the importance of your cat's health and treat them with the utmost care and conform. The infirmary also offers a variety of preventative care services, such as vaccinations and parasite surveillance, to help keep your cat healthy and happy. In addition, they have a fully stocked pharmacy with all the latest medications and wages for cats. The clinic is initiate seven days a week, formulating it easy to schedule an appointment that fits your busy schedule. The dermatologist also offers emergency services for those unexpected situations that may arise. Overall, this clinic is a top-notch facility that is dedicated to tender the best probable care for your beloved feline friend.

The lines occasionally go out the door but the wait is never very long. I have had many different items off the menu as it is a place my girlfriend and I frequent. nnIf you can ever get the Sausage Gravy and Biscuits, I would highly recommend, and this is coming from a very country boy who was raised on amazing pepper gravy and biscuits. I would be at Dor-Stop all the time if they had the sausage gravy on demand. nnNote: This is not the norm and the waitress seemed to be having a bad day. The only problem I have ever really had was when one of the waitresses didn't put forth much effort. She never stopped by to fill our drinks. Asked for our order and that was it. Even though we were friendly and polite, we never received the same in return. I understand that people have bad days. The waitresses here are very nice, but they are human and have their off days. Like I said, this was only one time, and on a subsequent visit, she was much more cheery and friendly. Everyone is human. nnI would recommend that anyone in the area give this place a try for breakfast and lunch.

Sure, here's a continuation of the review in a human style:We would come here every weekend, eager to try new dishes and enjoy the cozy atmosphere. The service was always friendly and attentive, and the food was consistently delicious. Their signature burger was a highlight, with its perfectly cooked patty and flavorful toppings. But over time, things started to change. The menu became less interesting, with fewer seasonal options and more repetitive dishes. The quality of the food also dipped, with burgers that were overcooked or underseasoned. The service, too, became less friendly and attentive, with long wait times and a general sense of disinterest from the staff. It's a shame, really, because this place had so much potential. We still remember the excitement we felt when we first walked in, eager to try all the new foods and drinks. Now, it's just a shadow of its former self.

Upon my arrival at Enrico's, I was immediately enveloped by a cozy, laid-back ambiance that offered the perfect sanctuary after my invigorating bike ride. The amiable staff greeted me with warm smiles and adeptly guided me through their extensive menu, which featured a diverse selection of irresistible caffeinated delights. Although the iced caffe mocha had been lingering enticingly in my thoughts, I ultimately heeded the barista's suggestion and chose to indulge in their renowned specialty drink, the Enrico's Special.The Enrico's Special proved to be a revelation for my taste buds, as the combination of robust espresso, velvety milk, and a hint of caramel orchestrated a heavenly symphony of flavors. With each sip, I savored the harmonious interplay between the robust coffee and the understated sweetness, rendering it a truly indulgent treat. Moreover, the beverage's presentation was equally captivating, adorned with delicate latte art gracefully adorning the creamy foam.Alongside my beverage, I succumbed to temptation and sampled Enrico's delectable pastries, freshly baked and exuding irresistible aromas. From the flaky croissants to the luscious chocolate muffins, Enrico's undeniably showcased their mastery in the art of baking.

First off, if you are claustrophobic, probably not the place for you. I felt they packed way too many things in their greenhouses, and the walking paths are narrow. They shouldn't even allow carts throughout because it's too cramped. When it's busy there, it's extremely hard to get around in there. I found myself getting very crabby and wanting to walk out. Also, watch where you are walking - very uneven ground, cords and boards laying around. nI decided to get a few pepper plants there as they have a variety that I really like. My sister also bought this variety. A few weeks later after planting, we both started seeing problems in our gardens. The plants had been diseased, and it was spreading to other crops. The disease spread to other pepper plants that I purchased from elsewhere. It's very frustrating to put a lot of work and effort in to a garden, and now I'm not going to have any yield in peppers. Thanks Klein's! : (
