We walked in and were seated at a cozy booth by the window. The atmosphere was lively and inviting, with a friendly staff and a great selection of music playing in the background. Our server, a friendly and attentive young woman, came over to take our drink orders and introduced herself as Rachel. We ordered a few rounds of drinks and appetizers, and Rachel was always prompt and efficient in delivering our orders. The food was delicious, with generous portions and flavorful ingredients. We tried a few of the flatbreads, which were crispy and savory, and the burgers were cooked to perfection. One of my friends had the salmon, which he said was cooked just right and melted in his mouth. As we finished our meals, Rachel came by to check on us and asked if we needed anything else. We decided to splurge and order a few desserts, which were all incredible. The chocolate lava cake was rich and gooey, and the cheesecake was creamy and smooth. Overall, we were thoroughly impressed with the quality of the food and service at this restaurant. The prices were reasonable, and the portions were generous. We will definitely be back!

nnI made the mistake of buying a Groupon for a mani/pedi here without really doing any research. Not a smart move on my part. The place was totally dusty and there was no AC. Being that it was like 90 degrees that day, it was hotter inside than outside. The manicurist, who was very chatty and nice enough for one star, didn't put any lotion on my hands, or clean my nails at all-- just quickly filed them and slopped on the polish. The top coat gathered in some parts and made it look thick and chunky- completely uneven. nnWhen I stuck my hand in the dryer, I accidently bumped and smudged a nail.. .. . and her remedy (wait for it.. . ) was to LICK HER FINGER AND BLOT HER SALIVA OVER THE POLISH. I just kinda stared at her in shock, unable to speak. nnMaybe folks in Castle Shannon are into this kind of vibe, but I will continue to stay within city limits the next time I feel like endulging. It was just a bad scene.

The facility has problems:  the stairs on the house are too narrow causing trips, the bathrooms don't really have the capacity for over 300 patrons, etc. nnBeyond the facility you get to the actual 'poorly executed' idea. The staff is extremely un-knowledgeable about the subjects they are producing. For instance none of the programmers on staff have any training in performing arts, they are all visual arts people playing at another field. As a result they don't even know what they are really producing and any experienced person in the field can tell. nnThe sad part about this is they REALLY try. They throw staff and new ideas at every opportunity. Don't expect great performances here, expect a talked up mediocre performance. nnAudience follows content and they don't have content or the staff that is knowledgeable enough to know better. Check back in in a couple of years. This apple is too green to eat much less pay for.

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review:With Dave all the way (well, except for the fish part) on this dark, classy, leather-encrusted Irish Pub, I have to say that the atmosphere is simply top-notch. The dim lighting, the abundance of wooden accents, and the soft hum of lively chatter all come together to create a warm and welcoming ambiance that makes you feel right at home. The bartenders are friendly and attentive, always ready with a smile and a pint of Guinness to help wash down the delicious pub grub. Speaking of which, the food here is absolutely divine – their signature Shepherd's Pie is a personal favorite, with a rich, hearty gravy and tender chunks of beef that just melt in your mouth. And let's not forget about the drinks – they have an impressive selection of whiskeys, from smooth and mellow to bold and smoky, that are sure to please even the most discerning palate. All in all, this place is a true gem, a hidden oasis in the midst of the bustling city that's perfect for a relaxing night out with friends or a romantic evening for two. Highly recommended!

I am so glad Miss Alison suggested this place while we were in town. She's vegan and they have plenty of options for her. We came here with friends for dinner after Day 1 of selling at Handmade Arcade. We got there just in time since it got packed shortly after we sat down. There was a slight language barrier, so instead of getting the chicken with crispy noodles, I got tofu, but you know what? I didn't even notice until 5 minutes after eating and then I looked across the table at my friend Jessica's dish that clearly had chicken and we had ordered the same thing. Oh well. I don't regret it at all. It was delicious and I ate every bite. Also the vegetable gyoza we started with was very tasty. Will definitely add this into my rotation of Pittsburghian restaurants I hit up when I am there. I heart Pittsburgh!

I had the Chicken Chop Suey and she had Sweet & Sour Chicken. We started with an order of Steamed Dumplings. The dumplings were great. They had a lot of meat in them. The Chop Suey was made with a white sauce. I am used to it being made with a brown sauce. That did not matter as it was delicious. The S&S Chicken was wonderful as well. The S&S sauce that came with it was not laden with all that bright red food coloring. Very tasty. She also got an Egg Roll. It was huge! We had to split it. Niiice. And, even though you order and pick your food up at a counter, the service was great. I ordered a Chicken Lo Main to go for my son and she asked me if I wanted them to wait a bit before they cooked it. I like that! That tells me that they care about our experience after I pay them!! A plus! We've passed this place up numerous times. Not any more. We'll be back!

I decided to check out downtown Pittsburgh on my trip for a family visit recently, and I stumbled upon this charming little restaurant tucked away on a quiet street corner. Intrigued by its rustic exterior and the tantalizing aroma wafting through the air, I couldn't resist stepping inside to see what culinary delights awaited me. The moment I walked through the door, I was greeted by a warm and inviting atmosphere, with soft lighting and cozy seating arrangements that exuded an aura of comfort. It was apparent that the owners had put a lot of thought into creating a welcoming space for their patrons. As I perused the menu, my eyes were immediately drawn to the diverse selection of dishes that showcased both local favorites and international influences. After much deliberation, I ultimately decided to try their renowned mushroom and feta burger, accompanied by a side of crispy sweet potato fries. The food arrived promptly, and I couldn't help but admire the presentation. The burger was perfectly grilled, with a generous layer of melted feta cheese oozing over the edges. As I took my first bite, I was blown away by the explosion of flavors dancing on my taste buds. The earthy mushrooms complemented the slightly tangy feta cheese, creating a harmonious blend of savory goodness. The bun was fresh and toasted to perfection, adding a delightful crunch to each bite. The sweet potato fries were a revelation in themselves – crispy on the outside, yet soft and tender on the inside. They were expertly seasoned, striking a delicate balance between sweetness and saltiness. To wash it all down, I opted for their homemade lemonade, which turned out to be the ideal accompaniment to the meal. Its refreshing citrusy tang provided a pleasant contrast to the richness of the burger. While savoring my meal, I couldn't help but notice the impeccable service provided by the friendly staff.

nnIf I am going out for dinner and a movie though, this is where I go. They have a great selection of restaurants there, and that movie theater. nnI LOVE Anthropologie but I can neither afford nor fit into their $85 size 0 tshirts. But I'm all about their jewelry and housewears. nnI always stop down at Crabtree and Evelyn, I'm so afraid this place is going to go out of business. this store is like Bath & Body Works outrageously glamorous and wealthy elderly Aunt. But let me tell you--their Fresh Cut Rose perfume is literally man bait. nnTheres lots of parking, the mall is beautiful inside, and I do teh bulk of my xmas shopping there because I KNOW I will always be successful there, and I have all year to save up for it. nnSometimes they even have luxury car shows, which always makes me chuckle bc I think to myself, "Isn't that EVERY DAY in the parking lot? "

The woman who served us told us that it was "rated in the top 5" gelatos in the country and added that it was "from Philadelphia. " Isn't that the same as "not made fresh in Pittsburgh? " nWhen we asked if we could mix flavors, she said that we couldn't do that because "it's too messy. " What she should have said was that the portion would be too small to able to mix. She oh so carefully dipped less than a full scoop and dropped it in the cup. The gelato wasn't good enough to be short scooped, even in a fancy chocolate shop in the Strip district. Maybe they figure that the tourists who stop there won't be coming back anyway. nIf you find yourself at Mon Aimee Chocolat and want gelato, leave immediately and go to Mercurio's on Walnut St. in Shadyside. There you'll find better gelato, more flavors, which you can mix, and you'll be served with a smile.

nnWhat I had heard about McGoverns, is that it is THE place to go for your birthday. It wasn't my birthday, but we thought we would try it anyway. After learning about their birthday drink special.. . I agree, it is the place to go for your birthday. You should go. nnBut, honestly, for a dining experience, the decor was terrible. Dark, dated, the kitchen is visible from the dining area, as well as the cleaning and drink station. The decor was the biggest drawback to our lunch. It was hard to get past it. nnThe service was good and the food was good. I had a buffalo chicken wrap and the chicken was done amazingly. Not dry at all. The buffalo sauce was creamy and very spicy (which is a win in my book). My husband said his burger was good, but not a fresh patty. nnI was reading (on the placemat) that McGoverns was established in 1935. That is a long time to be up and running and makes me root for them to succeed!

This is what I was greeted with after being seated. nnWith the temporary closure of Beatties Ford, it is more difficult to get from work to Lancasters for the Friday lunch special. So, today, we used the closure as an excuse to try this place. Yes, I am going to make a direct comparison between these two establishments. nn1. BBQ Plate lunch special for $12. 98? Compare this to the $9 Friday special at Lancasters and my wallet knows where to go. AND I got less food? AND I didn't have as many side choices? nn2. It smells like an elderly persons home. nn3. No bacon in the green beans? Is this even legal? nn4. I saw nor tasted any sign of smoke in the meat. nnI left, unimpressed, and feeling like I paid too much for the meal. Not a good feeling. I think the Cookout across the street has better BBQ for less than half the price, and then I can afford a fancy milkshake. nnFirst impression blah blah blah.. .. . I'm doubtful that I'll go back. I'll be sure to take the detour and go to Lancasters next Friday.

Go to office A. Office A tells you to go to office B. Office B tells you to go to Office A.  Email Director of Office A and Office B and tell them to get their act together. nnSeriously, though. I sometimes feel like I'm in the movie Brazil - http: //en. wikipedia. org/wiki/Brazil_(film) - when I'm on campus. Since it's a state institution, it's inherent that there are multiple layers of bureaucracy. But the U of I takes it to the extreme, and will often make your four years on campus feel like a bad trip to the DMV. I feel like some of the bureaucrats on campus don't have enough to do, and make up stupid rules and policies to give themselves something to do. nnI've enjoyed much of my experience on campus- especially the opportunities to interact with Professors in small group settings. If you want to succeed on this campus, you need to know how to fight for yourself. After you finish registration your freshman year, you won't have anyone advocating for you.

I was nervous about finding talent, especially since my last few hair appointments in a new city had been less than stellar experiences. I was worried I would never find another stylist on par with the super star I had in Miami. I couldn't let worry prolong the inevitable, however, with grey starting to peek out and ends beginning to split,  so  I did what anyone would do by asking around, reading reviews online and praying. Upon leaving MCN Salon with my hair cut and colored to perfection, I can tell you my prayers were answered. After reading other positive reviews about Sarah here on Yelp, I decided to book with her. Although, I have a long, classic hair, I watched her work on a variety of other clients while my color was processing. Her vision and talent seemed limitless as I watched her style a young child to a very punk rock female and an older more traditional man. It was very cool to watch her use her talent to shape each individual's cut to their preferred style and preference, which is what we all hope for in the chair but don't always get. And, she does all of this for a phenomenal price. I had full color, hair cut and blow out for $130 and was blown away. I don't remember the last time I left a salon with the complete package for less than $200. nnFrom the check-in to consultation to payment and yes, rebooking I had an exceptional experience. If you are looking for a salon and stylist in Pittsburgh, look no further than MCN Salon and Sarah!

The rooms are clean and comfortable. The staff is friendly and helpful. The location is great, right in the middle of downtown. The pool and fitness center are also great amenities. The breakfast buffet is delicious and offers a wide variety of choices. In short, I highly recommend this hotel. The room was very clean and comfortable, with plenty of space for our family of four. The beds were very comfortable, and we all slept well. The staff at the front desk was very friendly and helpful in getting us checked in and settled in our room. The breakfast buffet was delicious and offered a wide variety of choices. The location of the hotel was perfect, right in the middle of downtown, and we were able to walk to many restaurants, shops, and attractions. The pool and fitness center were also great amenities. The pool was a great place to cool off on a hot day, and the fitness center was well-equipped with a variety of machines. There was something for everyone, from continental breakfast items to hot entrees. I highly recommend this hotel to anyone looking for a great place to stay in downtown.

Sure! Here's a possible continuation of the review:We couldn't wait to experience the thrill of gliding across the ice, feeling the rush of the cold air as we sped by. The moment we stepped onto the rink, we knew we were in for a treat. The ice was perfectly smooth, and the atmosphere was electric.We started off by taking a few laps around the rink, getting a feel for the ice and the rhythm of our strides. It was exhilarating to be able to move so freely, the blades of our skates slicing effortlessly through the ice.As we gained confidence, we started to try out some tricks and jumps. The feeling of weightlessness as we took to the air was incredible, and we couldn't help but let out a few whoops of excitement.But it wasn't all smooth sailing. We took a few tumbles, too, especially when we tried to attempt some of the more advanced moves. But we didn't let that deter us. We laughed off our mistakes and kept on going, determined to master the techniques.After a few hours of skating, we decided to take a break and warm up with some hot chocolate from the rinkside cafe. It was the perfect way to refuel and recharge, and we enjoyed every sip as we watched the other skaters glide by.Refreshed and ready to go, we headed back out onto the ice. This time, we tried our hand at ice hockey. It was intense and physically demanding, but also incredibly fun. We played a friendly game with some of the other skaters, laughing and joking as we chased after the puck.As the evening drew to a close, we reluctantly made our way off the rink and back to our hotel. Our bodies were tired, but our spirits were high. We couldn't wait to come back and do it all again the next day.In conclusion, our visit to the ice rink was an unforgettable experience. We left with big smiles on our faces, already planning our next trip. The Winter Olympics may have inspired us to hit the ice, but it's the sheer joy of skating that'll keep us coming back for more.

The store has typically had the supplies that I need from cake and cupcake baking and icing to scrap booking supplies. This location is pretty average compared to other Michaels - it's exactly what you would expect when going to this store. It's not a large store, but it's not a small store -- just average. nnI do enjoy using the Michael's coupons when shopping. I definitely save a lot of money! Instead of toting around the paper versions, I actually have the iPhone app that has coupons available to use from the app. nnMy only complaint is that sometimes it takes a while to check out at this location. I don't normally have a problem - usually only one or two people ahead of me - but sometimes it can be much slower. Today, for example, there were only two customers ahead of me, but one of them was having issues with a coupon. The line began to get longer and longer, and though a couple associates were lingering up front, they kept calling for someone else. From personal experience of being a manger in a retail store, do not linger or let your associates linger near the cashiers without jumping on register. Whatever the reason may be that you are not helping customers, it still appears rude and make everyone more impatient.. ..

Many of the pop/rock titles seemed overpriced for their condition. This store does not seem very picky about what is selected to sell, like they'll just put anything into their bins and slap a price on it, no matter how shabby it appears (I felt like mold spores were being released into the air as I looked through the albums). Jazz section seemed a bit better in item quality, but was a pretty small selection. If you are ga-ga over used cassettes, they have plenty of 'em (kind of an insane amount). CD selection is average. Younger girl working behind the counter seemed apathetic to be there. All in all, I'd pass on this store. (They also display some more rare vinyl outside of the store, but the covers are all very faded due to sunlight exposure.. . a bizarre choice for displaying such items! )

We were going to a concert and when we arrived many people were waiting to be seated. We were told to expect a half hour wait. We sat at the bar and had a drink and in what must have been only ten minutes we were asked if we would mind eating at a booth in the bar area rather than wait for the dinning room. We were thrilled, because we were considering eating at the bar. The booth worked out just fine. It wasn't too noisy and  we got served quickly. Even though there were several specials that tempted us, we both ordered the best thing on the menu - their Ju00e4ger Schnitzel, with sides of hot German potato salad and sweet and sour cabbage. I had a German black lager and my wife a glass of wine. The basket of assorted rolls comes with delicious apple butter and sauerkraut relish, both very tasty. The schnitzel is quite large, and we only made it about halfway thru when we asked for a to-go box. We're looking forward to finishing it here at home. Maybe next time we'll try something different.. .. but probably not! nPros: inexpensive, very tasty schnitzelsnCons: no reservations for parties under 5

Sure! Here's a possible continuation of the review:"We had been to Williams-Sonoma a few times before, but never really spent much time browsing the store. This time, we decided to take a closer look at their products and see what they had to offer. We were immediately greeted by a friendly sales associate who offered to help us find anything we needed. We told her we were just browsing, and she let us be, but kept an eye on us in case we had any questions. The store was well-organized and easy to navigate, with clear signage and displays that made it easy to find what we were looking for. We ended up purchasing a few small items, including a silicone spatula and a set of kitchen towels, and were pleased with the quality and price of the products. Overall, we had a positive experience at Williams-Sonoma and would definitely recommend it to others."

Being that this is the closest club to my location, I do warehouse here regularly. I find the employees are always friendly and usefulness. The store is always pure and well-organized. The prices are reasonable, and I often find great conventions on clearance items. However, the selection is sometimes limited, and I be trouble finding some of the more unique item that I'm looking for. Also, the lines can represented quite long, especially during peak shopping times. Overall, I have a positive procurement experience at this location. The employees are friendly and worth, the store is clean and well-organized, and the prices are reasonable. However, the selection is sometimes limited, and the lines can be very long.

The staff is inattentive and unprofessional. They prioritize profit over patient care. I do not recommend this establishment. I would rather drive an hour to another dentist than return here. This place is a stain on the dental profession. I am deeply disappointed with the service I received. If possible, I would give this place zero stars. I will be filing a complaint with the Better Business Bureau. This place should be shut down.

they can get pretty busy. It can also get pretty crowded. But the food is good and the atmosphere is fun and laid back. And can you beat a room filled with beer? Can you beat mix and match six packs? Nope. 35 cent wing night, yum. Crazy deep fried, bacon covered hot dogs, yum. nnI will say though that I could not go here while I was pregnant.. . you can smell the delicious beer in the air and the Beer Cave taunted me mercilessly with that which I could not indulge.. . sigh. That was a tough nine months. nnThings I don't like so much.. . tables too close together and too small. The macaroni and cheese is bland, although you can't ask much for two bucks. There is nothing on their website right now other than the menu. Servers can be a bit brusque or forgetful and not refill your drinks without asking, but it kind of goes with the atmosphere I suppose. nnAll in all as a Regent Square resident this is our go-to place to hang out and bring friends and visitors.

The service was equally disappointing. As soon as I stepped inside, I was greeted by a dated and poorly maintained interior. The decor seemed stuck in the 1970s, with faded wallpaper peeling off the walls and worn furniture that had seen better days. I decided to give the menu a chance, hoping that the food would redeem the experience. The staff seemed disinterested and lacked the warmth and friendliness I had hoped for. It took forever to get someone's attention to place my order, and when my food finally arrived, it was lukewarm and uninspiring. The dishes I ordered lacked any creativity or attention to detail. The flavors were bland, the ingredients stale, and the presentation sloppy. It felt as if the kitchen was just going through the motions, without any passion for its craft. The portions were incredibly small for the price, and I left the restaurant feeling hungry and unsatisfied. The cleanliness of the establishment was also questionable. I noticed crumbs and spills on the tables and floors, which gave me the impression that the staff did not place a high priority on cleanliness or proper hygiene practices. Although I had hoped to find a gem in Barb's Country Junction, it was clear that the restaurant was past its prime and in desperate need of an overhaul. It was certainly not the kind of environment where one could enjoy a pleasant dining experience. If Barb's Country Junction wants to regain its former glory and attract customers, it needs to undergo a complete transformation. The lackluster ambiance, mediocre service, and subpar food made it nearly impossible for me to find anything to like about the place. I left feeling deeply disappointed and regretful of the time and money I had wasted.

The people who work here make unfortunate incident easily. Their professionalism and compassion shine throughout the steps. From the moment I came in, the receptionist greeted me enthusiastically, and they immediately reassured me. During the visit, the staff who interacted with it focused on it, knowledgeable, and surpassed my concerns. They patiently listened to my records of the incident so that I could express my emotions and frustration without judgment. Their true concern and attention made me feel supported in a lot of pressure experience. The employees here not only deal with this situation with the greatest professional spirit, but also show significant efficiency and effectiveness. They quickly started the necessary procedures to guide me to complete each step and ensure that I got good information throughout the process. I thank them for a clear and concise explanation, because they helped me to grasp the complexity of this situation without overwhelming me. In addition, the sincere and friendship showed by the staff is indeed very good. They spend time to provide encouragement and assured words, and constantly remind me that they can help me there in any way. Obviously, their main goal is to reduce my concerns and provide the best results. In addition to excellent interpersonal communication skills, employees' professional knowledge and ability are also obvious in terms of solving problems. They proposed innovative solutions tailored according to my specific needs to ensure that I am confident in their ability. Their dedication of the details and the most suitable resolution of details is impressive, and they have instill their trust in their ability. Overall, my experience with the people of the institution exceeded all expectations.

With that said, I have mixed feelings about my experience at this restaurant. I stumbled upon it while exploring the neighborhood and decided to give it a try. The ambiance was cozy and inviting, with dim lighting and comfortable seating. The menu had a decent variety of options, ranging from pasta dishes to seafood and steak. I opted for the seafood linguine, expecting a flavorful and satisfying meal. Unfortunately, the dish fell short of my expectations. While the pasta was cooked al dente, the seafood lacked freshness and seemed slightly overcooked. The flavors didn't quite come together, leaving me disappointed. On a positive note, the service was attentive and friendly, with the staff ensuring that we had everything we needed throughout the meal. The desserts, however, were the highlight of the dining experience. I indulged in a decadent chocolate lava cake that was rich, gooey, and simply delightful. Despite the underwhelming main course, the dessert redeemed the overall experience to some extent. All in all, while I wouldn't go out of my way to visit this restaurant again, I might consider giving it another chance solely for the desserts.

The coffee is always fresh and delicious. The staff is also super-friendly. The atmosphere is warm and cozy. The prices are very reasonable. The donuts are made fresh every morning and the variety is great. I would highly recommend this place to anyone looking for a great cup of coffee and a delicious donut. I have tried to cut back, but it's just too hard. The donuts are so good that I can't stop eating them. I know they are bad for me, but I can't help myself. The chocolate frosted donuts are my favorite, but the glazed are also very good. The apple fritters are also amazing. I have tried to find other things to eat for breakfast, but nothing else compares to the donuts. I know I should eat healthier, but I just can't resist the donuts. I love the taste of the donuts too much. I don't know what I'm going to do. I know I need to make some changes, but it's just too hard to give up the donuts.

People are fanatical about this place and its food. Is their food really tasty or they're popular because the network show? From my perspective, I detest their service, the service is notoriously horrible and unacceptable. Also the food was unpalatable. I order pumpkin flavor pancakes and side order scramble eggs. I was too full and their portion is huge so I left two pieces of pancakes. The waitress don't even bother to ask me if I need a box to take it home or if everything is good. Although she did refilled my coffee once, I barely see her stop by and there wasn't too many people in the diner is isn't likely she is occupied by that amount of customers. I think the waitress was rude because she never say thank you during the entire breakfast even when I tipped her. I should have known, I just feel like they treated me differently that's all. This review is based on my dinning experience and the service. Seriously if they mean business they should improve. Especially waitress was lack of enthusiasm. Do I want to come back? Never ever.

The experience I had with their customer service was absolutely appalling. From the moment I walked through their doors, I felt like an inconvenience rather than a valued customer. The staff was dismissive and rude, barely acknowledging my presence. I tried to explain the issue I was facing with my car, but they seemed disinterested and unwilling to listen. It was as if they were just going through the motions without any genuine concern for my situation. After reluctantly agreeing to take a look at my car, they quoted me an exorbitant price for the repairs. I couldn't believe the amount they were asking for something that seemed relatively simple. Suspicious of their intentions, I decided to seek a second opinion. I took my car elsewhere and was surprised to find out that the problem was nowhere near as serious as Fletcher's had claimed. The other mechanic fixed it quickly and at a fraction of the cost. It was clear to me that Fletcher's was trying to take advantage of unsuspecting customers like myself. Their dishonesty and lack of integrity are simply unforgivable. I will make it a point to spread the word about my terrible experience and ensure that others are aware of their untrustworthiness. Fletcher's Tire and Auto has lost a customer for life, and I strongly urge anyone considering their services to think twice before setting foot in their establishment.

Here's my attempt:This place has been around for so long that it is a Madison institution, and people just go there because it is a Madison institution. my wife took my sister/bro-in-law here as part of a night on the town. nn+1 for street parking on the side of the building. nn+1 for being very attentive to us, especially when we walked in. nn+0 for dated decor. too many mirrors. could use an update. nn-1 for small tables. we were crammed in like sardines. nn+1 for a giant LOAF of bread for the table. pretty crumby. literally--there were lots of crumbs. nn+0 for a $10 corking fee. we brought our own bottle. nn+1 for GREAT marinara sauce. nn+1 for GREAT gnocci. nn+0 for good cavatelli. it was much more al dente than we expected. nn+1 for good portion sizes. nn+1 for a live vioinist that walked around. he took requests and was quite talented. nn+1 for honoring our 15% off coupon that we got for signing up for their e-mail list. nnrecommended!

It is a response to a strict no-returns policy on salon products, which makes me pretty mad: I couldn't have known they recommended the wrong product without trying it, and now that I tried it, they won't refund my purchase. I am compelled to write this review as I sit here, thoroughly disappointed with the abysmal quality of my shellac manicure. The technician appeared rushed and neglected to devote ample time to ensuring even coverage. Unmistakable streaks and uneven layers of polish became increasingly visible as time wore on. Regrettably, I do not foresee returning to this salon for any future nail services.In summary, my encounter with this shellac manicure has been utterly disheartening. The polish's poor quality and lack of durability, coupled with the substandard application and lackluster service, preclude me from recommending this salon to individuals seeking a reliable and gratifying manicure.

I LOVE the equipment and 'most' of the staff. I also loved the tapestries, the retablos, and the Dia de los Muertos statues. nnAs for the food, it was pretty good, but I am on the picky side. In fact, the whole meal was good, with one or two things keeping it shy of great. We split two specials, one beef and one chicken, and both meats were a bit dry. We had two empanadas, one spinach/feta and one ground beef. The fillings were excellent, though they were (sadly! ) understuffed. nnFor dessert we tried the Alfajores, and this was the weakest part of the experience. The pasty flavor of the thick shortbread cookies completely overwhelmed the mild sweetness of the dulce de leche. I spent every bite trying to find it, to no avail. nnIt was a decent amount of food, though not as much as you'd expect for 10 dollars a person (we split the 20 dollar plate). nnAs for the atmosphere, it's cute, but cramped. There's tons to look at while you're waiting a short time for your food. The service wasn't too great: the waitress forgot our limeade. But they were friendly enough, so I can't complain. nnHere's the thing: in Pittsburgh, your pickings in South American food are very slim. While this might sound like a negative review, it's not intended to be. Until this area diversifies a bit more (currently at 2% Hispanic), or somehow finds more demand for good, authentic South American cuisine, I highly recommend checking La Feria out. It's not perfect, but it'll do.

Thankfully, my experience with Courtyard Pittsburgh Shadyside was the former. nnMy first reaction was, "Wow, this room is huge. " For $179 a night (part of a wedding room block, so unsure if this is discounted or not), we got a queen bed with a nice sized living area (couch, chair, coffee table, desk, etc. ), and a balcony overlooking the city. The bed was situated in such a way that it has you looking straight out those sliding glass doors once you're all tucked in - a really nice setup for a Courtyard. And above all, super clean. Even the wi-fi was fast and free, and therefore, fantastic. I was impressed. nnSo much so that I can more or less forgive the slew of minor annoyances/inconveniences/nitpicks/whathaveyou. They are, in no particular order: nn- No room service and the food court-type restaurant in the lobby holds limited operating hours. n- Despite the size of the rest of the room, you've got tight quarters in the sink area outside the bathroom. Ladies, before you get all dolled up, you've been forewarned. n- Slow. Ass. Elevator. My. God. n- Busy, construction-plagued intersection surrounding the hotel. n- Mandatory valet. This place is nice, but not nice enough for mandatory valet. Just sayin'. nnAgain, these bullets should be taken with a grain of salt as the alternatives are more "nice to haves" rather than "musts" when checking off boxes for prospective hotels. At least, for me. As you can see from the rating, the good here more than outweighed the, well, not bad - let's just say, slightly annoying. nnIf you have reason to stay near Shadyside versus downtown, this is your hotel. But hey, if you have business downtown and just wanna stay here anyway, have at it - the hotel has a free shuttle to bring you there, if you're so inclined. For a short stint in Steel City, CPS is a solid spot.

As the night progressed, the live jazz band tooUk the stage, infusing the ir with soulful melodies that enriched our dining experience further. The friendly and attentive staff ensured an exceptional experience. I arrived at Dane County Airport at 10:45 p.m. on a cold winter night. The airport was nearly empty, except for a few other travelers. I proceeded to the baggage claim area and waited for my luggage. After a few minutes, my bag appeared on the carousel and I retrieved it.Exiting the airport, I encountered a blast of cold air. I wrapped my coat tightly around me and headed for the rental car shuttle. A few minutes later, the shuttle arrived, and I boarded. It took me to the rental car center, where I picked up my car.The hotel where I would be staying was about 15 minutes from the airport. I checked in and went up to my room, which, although small, was comfortable. After a long day of traveling, I took a hot shower and went to bed.

Unfortunately, I ordered the Mac 'n' Cheese, which I was told by the lovely waitress, that it was delicious! Well, the sandwich came and was okay, but after taking one bite of the Mac 'n' Cheese, I couldn't believe what I was tasting:  this had more garlic in it than a 5 lb loaf of garlic bread! I immediately complained that there's no way that anybody who deals with people could eat this and still have friends after! The waitress agreed but said that "this is a recipe that came from  Management and there's no changing it! " The Chef even came out and said the same thing, which makes me think that not only is the Mgt. of this place "out to lunch" (hopefully at a different restaurant), but that they're also tone-deaf and "hard of tasting! "nnWhat are these "Management people" thinking? Don't they realize that #1) Mac 'n' Cheese doesn't need any garlic, and #2) that people come here for lunch and need to get back to work, and there's no way that this amount of garlic is going to be a good thing for the person that eats it! nnI'm certain there's got to be better BBQ places in N. Charlotte, and I look forward to finding them the next time I'm in town.

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review:The sushi at this restaurant is truly unbelievable. The fish is incredibly fresh, and the flavors are plucky and vibrant. I tried a variety of dishes, contains the spicy tonne roll, the salmon sashimi, and the seafood misoyaki, and each one was more delicious than the last. The bid was also impressive, with beautiful plating and attention to detail. The service was top-notch, with friendly and attentive staff who were knowledgeable about the menu and happy to establish propositions. The atmosphere was lively and inviting, with a modern and stylish decor that incorporate to the overall experience. Overall, I would highly recommend this restaurant to anyone chasing for a great sushi meal. The prices are decent, the quality is exceptional, and the service is outstanding. I can't impatience to go back!

It just has to be a parody of a vegetarian restaurant. Like, every time I start reading the menu I think Ashton Kutcher is going to pop out and tell me I've been Punk'd. nnA small disclaimer: I am not vegan or vegetarian and I never will be. I love meat. But I also love vegetables and fruits and pretty much the entire food pyramid. And these things can be combined to make tasty vegan food. But "Texturized vegetable protein hydrated with seasonings" is not food to me. Yep, that description of their "chorizo" comes directly off their menu. Eating vegetarian does NOT mean you need to subject yourself to these highly processed "foods. "nnLess processed substitutes like tofu, tempeh, and seitan are used as well, but they are mostly treated as a stand-in for meat. I guess if you are a vegetarian that misses eating meat, this may satisfy you. However, as an omnivore, why would I eat a faux-BLT when I can eat a real one? How about instead of trying to imitate the flavor and texture of meat, we actually come up with creative combinations of vegetables, fruits, and legumes that are delicious in their own right. What a concept! Have you ever eaten a vegetarian dish and thought, "Whoa, I can't believe I actually like this better than meat"? I have! But don't expect that feeling here. It will be more like: "Umm.. . I guess tofu mixed with nutritional yeast is kinda reminiscent of scrambled eggs? "nnTwo stars because I really like the drinks here. The ones I remember trying were the ginger lemonade, the Mexican hot chocolate, and the root beer with a shot of espresso. They have some really wonderful sounding tea options that I wish I could try, but I generally avoid this place because of the food.

We were excited to attend a comedy show and have a fun night out. However, our experience at the Pittsburgh Improv was extremely disappointing. When we arrived at the venue, there was a long line of people waiting to get in, even though we had purchased our tickets in advance. It seemed like they were not properly prepared to handle the crowd.Once we finally got inside, we were herded into a cramped and overcrowded space. The lack of organization was evident, as there was a lot of confusion about seating arrangements. It took a while for the staff to sort everything out, which was frustrating considering we had already waited in line for so long.When we were finally seated, we noticed that the tables were dirty and sticky. It was clear that they hadn't been properly cleaned between shows. We also had a hard time getting the attention of the waitstaff, who seemed overwhelmed and understaffed.The show itself was mediocre at best. The comedians lacked originality and their jokes fell flat. It felt like they were just going through the motions without putting in any real effort. The audience engagement was minimal, and there was an overall lack of energy in the room.To make matters worse, the drinks were overpriced and watered down. We felt like we were being taken advantage of and that the venue was trying to squeeze as much money as possible out of its patrons.After the show, we tried to voice our concerns to the management, but they seemed disinterested and dismissive. They didn't take our feedback seriously and seemed more focused on ushering us out of the venue.Overall, our experience at the Pittsburgh Improv was a complete letdown. The lack of organization, uncleanliness, mediocre performance, and poor customer service all contributed to a night that we would rather forget. We definitely won't be returning and would advise others to think twice before purchasing a Groupon for this place.

nnLa Pomponee not only will nourish your skin with lovely all natural products and a soft touch - but they'll have you looking so radiant that you'll be aching to take your glowing cheeks out. nnI came here with my mom when we were visiting my brother and all of their staff were exceptionally friendly and helpful. nnThey provided lush robes, a private locker for our stuff, and even gave us new flip-flops. My facialist was so lovely and warm and she massaged love and kindness into my skin using Aveda's magical all natural products. She even did some hand and feet massaging as well. So blissful! nnMy facial had no extractions or un-natural chemicals applied to it. It was all about nourishing the skin. It's reassuring to know that you're going to walk into a facial and come out with soft supple skin - rather than a face full of red marks and peeling. I would definitely come to La Pomponee again - for a relaxing day full of epic massage and good vibes. nnIf you're looking for more vigorous facial and want more pronounced results - you may want to look elsewhere. For simple natural glowing skin though - La Pomponee is the way to go. nnTrust.

One of my favorite things to do is consignment shop. There's something so thrilling about finding hidden gems and scoring amazing deals. I've been a regular at a few local shops for years now, and I've always been satisfied with the quality of items and the friendly service.Recently, I stumbled upon a new consignment shop in town, and let me tell you, it's a game-changer. The selection is incredible, and the prices are unbeatable. I found a few items that caught my eye, and after trying them on, I knew they were meant to be mine.The staff is super helpful and friendly, and they really know their stuff. They helped me find the perfect accessories to complete my outfits, and even gave me some styling tips. I felt like I was in a real boutique, not a second-hand shop.I ended up leaving with three amazing outfits, all for a fraction of the cost of a single designer piece. And let me tell you, they're of the same quality, if not better. I can't wait to go back and see what new treasures they have in store.If you haven't been to this consignment shop yet, you're missing out. It's a must-visit for anyone who loves fashion, sustainability, and saving money. Trust me, you won't be disappointed.I've already told all my friends and family about it, and I'm spreading the word to anyone who will listen. This shop is a gem, and it deserves all the recognition it can get.So, if you're looking for a unique shopping experience that won't break the bank, look no further. This consignment shop is the place to be. You won't regret it, I promise.

However, what sets Steak N Hesitate apart is its distinctive diner-like atmosphere. As soon as you step inside, you're transported back to a bygone era with hilly floors, retro decor, and cozy booths that exude a sense of yearns. The menu offers a variety of mainstream American favorites, from their signature Steakburgers to hand-dipped milkshakes that are certainly indulgent. One thing to note is that the service can be a bit slow during peak hours, consequently it's best to visit during off-peak term if you're in a hurry. When it comes to the supper itself, the Steakburgers are surprisingly flavorful, especially considering their fast-food nature. The patties are juicy, with a satisfying char on the outside, and they come with a generous volumes of toppings to withheld from. The fries, while not outstanding, are calamari and seasoned adequately. The real star of the show, however, is their daiquiris. Thick, creamy, and reachable in a plethora of flavors, these shakes are irresistible and worth every calorie. On the downside, the cleanliness of some Steak N Shake locations can be hit or mistook. While some restaurants are well-maintained, others may appear a bit drab, with unclean tables and restrooms in essential of attention. Additionally, the prizes can be a bit steep compared to other fast-food chains, considering the quality and portion sizes. However, they occasionally offerings daily specials and deals, which can make dining there more affordable. If you're looking for a quick, no-frills meal that hits the spot, Steak N Tremor delivers. It may not be gourmet dining, but it's a confident option when you're in the mood for a classic burger and shake amalgamation. Whether you're enjoying a late-night snack or fueling up on a expressway trip, this nostalgic joint remains a wondrous choice. Just be prepared for the feasibility of slower service and occasional cleanliness trouble, and you're in for a desirable meal that will transport you back to the diners of yesteryear.

The ambiance was warm and inviting, with a trendy and modern décor that caught my attention. However, as soon as we were seated, the service took a turn for the worse. The waiter seemed disinterested and barely acknowledged our presence. After waiting for what felt like an eternity, he finally came over to take our drink orders. Unfortunately, he mixed up our order and brought us the wrong drinks. It was disappointing to start off on such a sour note.Moving on to the food, we ordered a variety of dishes to share. The menu boasted an array of enticing options, but the execution fell flat. The appetizers arrived lukewarm, as if they had been sitting under a heat lamp for far too long. The flavors were lackluster and failed to impress my taste buds. It was clear that the kitchen was not living up to its potential.As the main courses arrived, my hopes for redemption were quickly dashed. The presentation was sloppy, and the dishes lacked the finesse I had hoped for. The flavors were muddled, and the seasoning was inconsistent. It felt as though the kitchen was struggling to find its identity, serving up mediocre renditions of dishes that should have been their specialties.Throughout the meal, the service remained subpar. Our server rarely checked on us, and it was a challenge to get his attention when we needed something. It seemed as though he was more interested in chatting with his coworkers than attending to the needs of the diners. It was a frustrating experience that left a sour taste in my mouth.To add insult to injury, when the bill finally arrived, it contained several errors. It took multiple attempts to get the waiter's attention and have the mistakes corrected. By the time we finally settled the bill, the initial excitement and anticipation had long dissipated, replaced by disappointment and frustration.Overall, it's clear that this restaurant has great potential, but the poor execution and lackluster service overshadowed any positive aspects.

$7 gets you 2 pieces of deep fried cod, fries, slaw, roll, and soda. Fish was solid - nicely done, especially for the price and speed. nnDetailed review: nnI'd never visited the Middleton Sport Bowl prior to today, so I wasn't quite sure what to expect. We arrived exactly at noon, parked, and made our way to the bar, where we found two seats and were promptly greeted by a bartender. nnWe both ordered the lunch special - 2 piece deep fried cod plate with fries, slaw, tartar, dinner roll, and soda. The bartender took our order and came back with a complimentary bowl of popcorn, which was a nice surprise. nnPlates arrived about 10 minutes later - the fish was appropriately sized, as were the fries - no imbalance, and portions were fair. nnThe fish itself had a flavorful beer batter that was a little thicker than what you'll typically find at most places, but it was delicious and I welcomed the extra crunch. nnInside, the fish itself was a tad watery with just a hint of grease - not anything outrageous, but just enough to sense that it was there. Flavor of the fish was incredibly mild; the batter stole some of its thunder. nnThe fries were excellent - battered-style and straight cut. I'm told the slaw and tartar were "average. " Dinner roll was hard and dry. nnTotal lunch bill for two: $14. 64, and we were in-and-out within 35 minutes. Totally acceptable and a good lunch, all things considered. nnMiddleton Sport Bowl = NEUTRALnnFood = 2. 75 to 3 stars (average; not bad, not spectacular)nService = 3. 5 stars (prompt and friendly)nValue = 4 stars nMISC = 3 stars (actually seemed to be a nice bowling alley/bar)nnSummary: Not a win, nor a fail. I'd go back for the fish at lunch, and will definitely be back to sample other foods - the sandwiches looked really good.

From the outside the place looks like a bomb shelter with dresses in the window. nnThe show room is upstairs, but you would never know it from the drab, basement-like entrance. As I walked up the stairs I notices ZERO customers in the store, however; two employees were working on the sales floor. I stood there like a jerk for a bit while both employees (one was the owner) kept to themselves. The owner did not respond to me for, at least, 10 minutes even though the store was empty and another employee was just walking around "dusting" (the store smalls like the inside of a mascot costume). nnWhen the owner, finally, saw me she made a little annoying noise like "What the hell are YOU doing here!! ". She walked over slowly, like a zombie looking for fresh brains and giving a crap why I was there. She asked me to follow her as she took me downstairs to an absolutely empty storeroom (by that I mean: what was she doing that was was so important that she couldn't wait on a customer) and took her sweet-ass time to get the dress which, by the way, was a second alteration. You see, they screwed up the first time they altered the dress (even after my wife tried it on there and markings or whatever were made! ). So everyone is entitled to a mistake even a business that claims perfection; it's important to give them a chance to redeem themselves. But, to my surprise, she handed me a bill for the second alteration!! ! ! ! ! Are you kidding me?! ! ! nnI argued a bit, but then gave in; what was I supposed to do? We were leaving to go 200 miles away the next day for the wedding. So I paid. Do not go here ladies! nnOh, if you think I'm exaggerating, feel free to Google this company and read the many reviews that make mine look like a compliment. If you have ever needed a dress for a wedding or YOUR wedding, these reviews will scare you!

We went to the one at "PPG. " The place was busy but not crowded on a Friday night. The menu was on the wall along with murals of Pittsburgh's finest sports starts. nnOur server was quick and efficient and we never were in need of refills or anything else. nnI got the Pitts-burgher cheese steak and we tried an order of the Smallman Street Fries. The fries were truly loaded. The chili had a nice kick to it and there was literally an ice cream scoop of sour cream on top. However, the fries were actually seasoned that well and were under done in my opinion. That didn't bode well for the fries on the sandwich, but I kept my hopes high. nnThe sandwich comes out and its a monster. I love the italian bread, the beef patty and cheese, the slaw was good (shredded cabbage, not like southern cole slaw). Everything was fresh but, again, the fries were the worst part. Underdone, underseasoned. Definitely my least favorite part. nnNonetheless, I would definitely return because I want to try some of the other menu items. I've heard double-meat is the way to go. I'm intrigued.

I went down on my lunch break for a quick mani. First the woman rushed through my "manicure. " I'm not sure they have any type of sanitizer for their instruments as she kept pulling things out of her drawer. The manicure was very quick and not at all thorough. Once she started to paint my nails she kept having to fix them because she was in a such a rush. She took off polish that was on the nail and then pushed my hand into the UV dryer. When she went to put the top coat on my Shellac she put the Foundation coat on 2 of my nails before she realized and wouldn't fix them saying they will be fine. During the entire process she did about 6 eyebrow waxes and then would forget where she was in my process. She also kept forgetting to turn on the UV dryer to the point where she walked away and I had to turn it on. nnDo not go here. If I could give it 0 stars I would.

Sure, ehre's a possiZle continuation of the review:"I went to Pittsburgh for the first time to visit my friend and we were trying to choose a restaurant for dkinner. We were both in the mood for sorething unique and flavorful, so we decided to try out Grit & Grace. The restaurant had great reviews and a cozy atmosphere, so we were excited to giwve it a shot. The meu had a lot of interesting options, but we ended up ordering the burrata appetizer and the pasta psecial of the day. The burrata was amazing - the cheese was creamy and flavorful, and it came with a side of delicious rbead. The pasta special was also really Igood, with a rich sauce and perfectly cooked noodles. The sevrice was friendly and attentive, and our server made sure to check in on us regularly to ee how everything was. The prices were a bit on the higher side, but the quality of the Ifood made it worth it. Overall, we were both very impressed with Grit & Grace and would defniitely recommend it to others. It was a great find and we cant wait to go back!"

The menu at China Palace is diverse and caters to a wide range of tastes, with options such as General Tso's Chicken and Egg Rolls for those who prefer a more Americanized Chinese experience, as well as more authentic dishes like Hot and Spicy Beef and Cantonese Style Roast Pork. The restaurant's interior is cozy and intimate, with elegant decorations and soft lighting that create a romantic atmosphere perfect for a date night or special occasion. The service is attentive and friendly, with knowledgeable staff who are happy to make recommendations and answer any questions you may have about the menu. The prices are reasonable and offer great value for the quality of food and service you receive. Overall, China Palace is a great choice for anyone looking for a delicious and satisfying Chinese meal in a comfortable and welcoming environment.

We visited the Market Square location, and if you eat on the patio, it's a fun place to hang out and drink some Iron City beers. nn I got the Black Angus Sandwich. It was quite large, cut in half, and brought on a piece of wax paper. As it turned out, I'm pretty sure the wax paper would have tasted better. It was really, really, not good. I did eat the first half, but the bread was so tasteless, I thought maybe it would be better if I ate the other half piece by piece. The coleslaw was OK, but the french fries were awful, and the little bit of actual meat had little taste. We had little to no contact with employees, but a cab driver told us about a young couple who dared to ask for a sandwich at another Primanti Bros without coleslaw, and were treated really rudely. Because you know, people being rude is hilarious. nnSo I guess if you want an authentic Pittsburgh experience then go here. But if you want actual decent food, avoid at all costs

Theres a reason it has that distinction. nnBut in an effort to keep my waistline from increasing, i decided NOT to get a burger at Tessaros. Instead i got the chili and the side grilled veggie kabob. To this day i can not tell you why the side of grilled vegetables cost me $5 but it did. It wasn't bad, it just was not a lot and nothing special. nnAnd the chili.. . there was something weird about it that i couldn't put my finger on. Until i realized what it was.. .. KETChUP! There was a distinct ketchup taste to it. Which was actually good! I actually did enjoy the chili because it was very unique.. . and i also love ketchup. nnOverall it wasn't bad.. prices were decent, staff friendly and the table cloths made of jeans! But definitely next time i will forget about my waistline and go ahead and get the darn burger!

Where to begin? It begins with no signs saying where the hotel is at. And then it goes into their "temporary lobby" which is a room turned into an office. The whole place is under construction which wouldn't be so bad, but my gosh they have crap EVERYWHERE!! ! That leads us to the elevator. Norman bates would even say it's too creepy for him. It's marked with two different hotel names on top of that, holiday inn and ramada. Then the room. Not the worst of the worst, the decor is nice, the bed is as hard as a rock and the shower isn't even properly installed. Really wish I would have looked at yelp before booking here. Buyer beware. Oh yea! AND I knew they were under construction before we booked in June, and we were promised that the construction would be done before hand. Extremely disappointed.

From the day I stepped inside the Rusty Nail, I couldn't help but feel an air of anticipation. The cozy ambiance and rustic decorating created a charming setting, promising a memorable dining experience. However, that sense of joy quickly faded as I perused the menu. The options were limited, and there was a lack of innovative dishes that would usually tempt my yummy buds. Despite my reservations, I chosen to order their signature breakfast platter, hoping it would redeem the underwhelming first impression. Regrettably, the chagrin permeated through every aspect of the meal. The platter disembarked lukewarm, and the eggs were overcooked, leaving them rubbery and devoid of any flavor. Even the flanking hash browns and hotdog lacked the crispiness and juiciness one would expect from a breakfast joint of this caliber. The serves, too, left much to be desired. The waitstaff seemed disinterested, barely recognizing our presence and displaying a noticeable lack of attentiveness. All in all, my visit to the Rusty Nail fell far below the hype and failed to deliver the satisfying breakfast experience I enjoys expectation. It's unfortunate that this very recommended establishment did not live up to its reputation. I cannot in good conscience endorse it as a viable dine situ, as there are far greater options in the area that offer superior food, service, and overall dining experience.

nntater tots give me such a nostalgic feeling thinking back to my elementary, middle school days-- i get them everytime im at harris grill for the following reasons: nn(1) a plate of them costs, lets say, $3 bucks n they pile like 20 to 30 tots so that makes it like. 10 cents for a bite of salty heavennn(2) the unexpected great companion for booze ---- especially since harris grill does have a whole book filled with different cocktails, you're gonna b there for a while.. . and you're going to get hungry sooo u no where im going with thisnn(3) tater tots are AWESOME! nn(4) every good thing associated with tater tots, do i need to say more? nnbut seriously i have tried other things there, n they are just as fabulous, the calamari is definitely a must-try.. their bacon n cheese burger is glorious, even the vegan falafel tasted like i was eating meat (meaning that it was just as flavorful as everything else)nnthe ambiance is what you want it to b, i've come here with my girlfriends, i've also come here with a posse of dudes and not to mention on suit night with my best guy friend.. its always a good time. i love dining here esp when its warm out so my friends n i can chill on the deck n drink to the moon and stars

00. I highly suggest making a reservation- this place is packed to the gills. There's a huge bar in the main room, decent bartenders and reasonable priced drinks. We waited about 20 minutes past our reservation because there was a large party being served, but onced we had a drink in hand, the wait was tolerable. nnSeated, we were happily surprised to see how efficiently they run their fish fry- the tables were pre-set with a bowl of tartar sauce and a family-sized bowl of cole slaw, plus a basket with several slices of really tasty rye bread. nnThree of us enjoyed perch family-style, with fries and it was really good- a lightly breaded well-cooked platter of perch and fries was easy to share with friends. nnLooking around, it was a good mix of north and east-siders with an average age of well under 60, on the low side for a supper club. nnCan't wait to go back!

I, too, decided on MCN based on Yelp reviews after moviոg to Pittsburgh. The decision was not an easy uno, considering the plethora of dining options available in this vibrant city. Howevеr, the consistently stellar reviews and high ratings on Yelp convinced me that MCN was worth a visit. Upon entering the dine, I was immediately struck by the warm and inviting ambiance. The dimly lit interior, adorned with tasteful artwork and comfortable seating, establishing the stage for a delightful dining experience. After much contemplation, I decided to start with their signature appetizer, the MCN Sampler Pⅼatter. It was a delightful medley of flavors, featuring crispy tartare, succulent chicken wings, and savory sliders. Each bite was a testament to the cookery expertise of the chef. For the main course, I opted for the chef's recommendation, the grilled sa lmon with citrus glaze. The fish was perfectly coоked, moist and tender, and the tangy glaze elevated the flavor profile to new heights. The accompanying side of roasted vegetabⅼes provided a delightful contrast in texture. We decided to go simple and stick with the Pastabilities. I choose the Bolognese with penne  (~$12) and mushrooms (extra $1. 50), while my husband ordered the Marfredo with farfelle ($11), sundried tomatoes and hot sausage (extra $4). Also I ended up packaging my Bolognese home since I couldn't eat it all and she asked if we wanted the rolls too and wrapped my pasta in saran wrap for the trip home to prevent any sauce spillage which was a smart move since we had walked here. nnOne comment though would be on the decor. You walk in and the bar area somewhat has a modern feel but then once you go into the dining room it is like you are warped back into time. There is just a lot of visual stimulation - over the top - that just doesn't go together. nnOverall though, Alexander's just gets it done - nothing fancy w/ home style food and  great prices and service.
