I have left a little bit puzzled each time. nnTheir treat selection is on par but would cater to smaller dogs in my opinion. Their dog food selection is hit or miss. I was looking for science diet, large breed and they seemed to have science diet but odds and ends of formulas. I also went in looking for options for one of my dogs that pulls.. . a lot. I had mentioned that we already use a halti harness so she offered up the pronged collar. I know there are a lot of different theories on dog training out there and this is an option but it was not something I would be trying. nnThe space might be a bit small for them as well as their many kittens. I do love going in and petting the cats but at a certain point, it kind of got distracting. nnEither way, I can usually get what I need without having to drive further than needed but if I need more than treats, dog bags or shampoo, I would go elsewhere.

Was traveling with a small infant and needed a car seat. I booked everything online and called to confirm. When I got to the rental desk the price was $100 more for the ten days than the online price I was given. I was told it was a 25% deposit. When I dropped the car off it still cost me $100 more, so no 25% deposit. nThe car seat was not ready when I arrived and I was told she had gotten me a nice "clean" one. The car seat was disgusting:  full of crumbs and food leftovers and smelled horrible. I had to cut holes I a blanket to run the straps of the car seat through because it was so nasty. My word of advice:  don't ever rent a car seat! When I inquired about car seat installation I was told they are not aloud to install them due to liability reasons which wasn't a problem. When I asked for the installation instructions and they didn't have them that was a problem. She said she would send her manager out to instruct me but he was just as clueless as she is about the word "clean". nNext month I will be renting from any other company besides enterprise. There are about 4-5 to choose from at that location.

This hotel really sticks it to their priceline customers. nnI am a frequent business traveler, but when I travel for pleasure I use priceline. nThe hotels that treat me well as a priceline customer get my return business when I pay full rate for work. nnMy wife and I showed up at midnight to check in. We were given a room with two small beds. I asked to be given one large bed instead, but the front desk attendant told me that as a priceline customer I had to take whatever double occupancy room they chose to give me. I understand this is the priceline policy, but most hotels make an effort to accommodate if they have vacancy. I even offered to pay extra and the front desk attendant then claimed there were no other rooms available despite the mostly empty parking lot outside. nnCrowne Plaza just struck out. Back to Marriott and Hilton I go.

It's not fancy but the food is really good. You could miss this place easily if you weren't looking for it. But do look for it, you'll be glad you did. When you walk in, you feel like you're walking in to someone's home kitchen. There is a choice of indoor or outdoor seating. The outdoor seating is in a little open air structure, so you are still out of the elements if it's raining or if the sun is beating down in the summertime. The menu has a lot to offer, I usually get tacos and rice and beans, while my fella usually gets jalapeno poppers and a veggie quesadilla. There are also American choices on the menu. Portion sizes are generous. The police in the area seem to really like eating here, there is almost always an officer dining when we go there. That tells me 3 things, Miguel's treats our officers right, the food is really good and a good value, and I will be safe eating at this establishment. Service is always friendly. I wish Miguel's would come to Raleigh.

I had the oyster sandwich, clam chowder, and calamari. The oysters were very tender and I only wished the sandwich had a few more on it, cuz it wasn't very filling. Good thing I had the soup & calamari.. The clam chowder was sinfully delicious, with a heavy cream base and tons of flavor. The calamari was okay, nothing to brag about, but the sandwich and chowder couldn't have been better. nThe oyster House is split up into two different restaurants next-door to each other.. To the right side is a sitdown restaurant with a bar and the left side is a casual quick stop shop with a counter to order at (which is where I chose to go). The interior is a bit dark & outdated and it doesn't have much of a welcoming atmosphere, but the outdoor tables in Market Square are a great option, weather permitting.

Eh, whatever I guess that's really besides the point. Anywho, After getting my eyebrows done at Princess Nails from Michelle (EYEBROW FAIRY! ) I needed to cut a new set of keys because apparently holding onto keys is lost on me. I doubt my landlord wants to be woken up at 2: 00am to let me in again either. After calling 7349651474 locksmiths that wouldn't duplicate keys because they're stooopid someone from the Office Depot sent me to Ulrichs Shoe Repair. Yes, Shoe Repair. Fix your shoe, make a key. Upon entering Ulrichs (I think it was the owner) was super friendly and I'll give him credit for being somewhat funny. Gave him my keys and in literally 30 seconds flat both keys were made AND they both worked. Usually going to somewhere like Home Depot you have to go back like 3 times for the key to work properly. Not in this case. Overall customer service amazing. Quality of service amazing. Plus super cheap too! $2 bucks a key. Um that's a deal if I ever saw one.

I've been here twice over the last couple months (including yesterday)  and have been very happy each time I've gone. nnCaffe Amante is located on the second floor (food court) level of Fifth Avenue Place, the home of HighMark Blue Cross Blue Shield. nnAmongst most of the mediocre chain food that's served on the same floor, it stands out as basically the only(or one of a few) sit down places available. nnBoth times the service was right on point. Quick, efficient, despite being busy, friendly, wait-until-I-finish-my-sentence-sensitive, good recommendations on food. Corrected themselves when they were wrong in what they told me previously and so on. nnThe two dishes I've had are: nnSpecial Salmon and jumbo lump crab meat. It was expertly cooked, great flavors, good portion size, and overall just a winning dish. nnDi Mare Amante which is a dish with shrimp, scallops, in a white clam sauce with capellini noodles. Their menu spells it with two p's: cappellini. nnBoth were great and I'd have them again. nnYou know, part of my review and rating is based on exactly what this place is: a more upscale place for the mostly business clientele bringing their customers out for a conveniently located lunch. In a restaurant that is more or less in a food court. Being surrounded chinese joints, mexican burrito places, and the philly cheese steak place. nnWhile I think it would be a solid bet for dinner as well --- that's not how I see this place positioned. I wouldn't take a date here Friday or Saturday night. There are more exciting places offering more adventures fare. nnThis is not your $8 lunch.. .. most things are priced $12-$16 which includes bread. Their prices well justified by the quality of the food. nnFor lunch, I'd go back to this place in a heartbeat.

We wanted to take them to a great San Diego restaurant. Since we had eaten here before with good results, we decided to try the Marine Room again.When we arrived, the hostess was very welcoming and took us immediately to our window-side table. She was very friendly and professional. Our waiter arrived shortly after and took our drink orders. He too was very friendly and professional. My wife ordered a Virgin Mary which arrived quickly and looked and tasted great. However, my friend ordered a Bloody Mary and it arrived looking like it had been sitting around for awhile and had a very funny taste. Our waiter quickly replaced it with a fresh one that looked and tasted much better.We ordered the special of the day which was a 3-course meal. For the appetizer, we had a salad. For the main course, I had the filet mignon and my wife had the salmon. For dessert, we both had the chocolate cake. The salad was a bit too dressed but good. The filet mignon was cooked perfectly but the baked potato I ordered to go with it was served cold. The salmon my wife had was very dry and the vegetables were tasteless. The chocolate cake was delicious.The waiter was very apologetic about the cold baked potato and quickly brought me a new one. We also noticed the table next to us had the same problem. Their waiter brought them a new one as well.Overall, we had a good time but were disappointed with the food. The service was impeccable!

I, too, decided on MCN based on Yelp revisit after moving to Pittsburgh. The decision was not an easy one, considering the plethora of dining options available in this vibrant cities. However, the consistently stellar reviewing and high ratings on Yelp convinced me that MCN was worth a visit. Upon entering the restaurant, I was immediately beaten by the warm and inviting ambiance. The dimly lit interior, adorned with tasteful watercolours and comfortable seating, set the stage for a delightful dining experience. The attentive staff greeted me with genuine chuckle and escorted me to my table. The menu offered a diverse selection of dishes, catering to various dietary prerogative and cravings. After much thought, I decided to start with their signature appetizer, the MCN Sampler Platter. It was a delightful medley of flavors, role crispy calamari, succulent chicken wings, and savory sliders. Each bite was a testament to the culinary expertise of the master. For the main course, I elects for the chef's recommendation, the grilled salmon with citrus glaze. The fish was perfectly cooked, moist and tender, and the tangy glaze elevated the cassis profile to new heights. The accompanying side of roasted vegetables planned a delightful contrast in texture. I was closely impressed with the attention to detail that went into each dish. To conclude the nite, I indulged in their tantalizing dessert selection. The sweets lava cake was a decadent treat, with a molten center that oozed out with every heavenly bite. The filing was impeccable, and the flavors were nothing short of extraordinary. Throughout the evening, the staff's professionalism and knowledge of the avenues were commendable. The service was prompt and efficient, assurance that my dining experience was nothing short of exceptional. MCN truely capita up to its reputation as a top-rated establishment in Pittsburgh, and I can confidently say that I made an excellent choice based on the Yelp reviews.

The staff was very friendly and helpful, and they did a great job on the shoes. My daughter was so happy with them, and she can't wait to wear them in her next dance recital. We also had a great experience with Baker's customer service. We had a small problem with the shoes after we got them home, and they were very quick to resolve it. They sent us a new pair of shoes, and we didn't have to pay anything. I would highly recommend Baker's to anyone looking for a place to have their shoes repaired or built up. Baker's is located in the heart of downtown, so it's easy to get to. They have a large parking lot, so you don't have to worry about finding a place to park. They offer a wide variety of services, and their prices are very reasonable. I would definitely recommend Baker's to anyone looking for a place to have their shoes repaired or built up. They have a wide variety of shoes to choose from, including dress shoes, casual shoes, athletic shoes, and children's shoes. They also have a large selection of boots, sandals, and slippers. I was very impressed with the selection of shoes at Baker's. They were able to help me find the perfect pair of shoes for my daughter, and they made sure they fit perfectly. They also gave me some great advice on how to care for the shoes. Baker's customer service was excellent. We had a small problem with the shoes after we got them home, and they were very quick to resolve it. They sent us a new pair of shoes, and we didn't have to pay anything.

Continue review: When I arrived, the room was not ready, and the staff was not very friendly. The room was not clean, the bathroom was dirty, and the bed was uncomfortable. Air conditioning cannot work, and television cannot work normally. Wi-Fi cannot work normally, and mobile phones cannot work normally. Breakfast is bad and the food is not good. The swimming pool is dirty and the gym is not open. The hotel is noisy and the guests are rude. The location is not good, and the hotel is not safe. I will not recommend this hotel to anyone.

For $3 at happy hour, you seriously cannot beat this price. They are incredibly strong, so I always say no to the extra shot of Cassis, but if you are feeling adventurous, it's only $1 more for the extra shot! Anytime friends come to visit from out of town, they ask if we can please go get some frozen cosmos. Apparently I talk about them a lot.. . nnFrozen cosmos aside, the food is actually pretty good as well. I really enjoy the turkey burger filled with cranberry stuffing as well as the upstate chicken riggies. This is definitely Western PA chicken, piggie, wedding riggie gone very very right. I love the combination of vodka sauce with fresh bell peppers. And who doesn't like tots on the side? Brings me back to my childhood, stealing tots from friends at school whose parents let them buy their lunches. nnThis is the perfect place to go with friends. Go in the spring, summer, or fall when it's nice enough to sit outside on the patio, kick back with a few fro cos, and enjoy friends. You won't be disappointed.

nnThe burgers here are ground fresh by an in house butcher and the meat is good, not greasy and very juicy. IMHO, if you added some ground chuck you would get even better flavor but overall they have some of the best tasting burger meat out there. nnThey cook on a griddle that is heated by burning hard woods, an interesting concept but to be honest I could not taste any smokiness to the meat. Also, IMHO, grilled meat tastes better, but griddled meat is juicer. nnThe burgers are served on what appears to be a Portuguese style bun, rather plain tasting they add very little to the burger itself. Toppings are simple lettuce, tomato, onions, etc. Their goal is to highlight the meat and not cover it up with lots of toppings or sauces. nnOn the side I had the home-fries, which they suggested, and to be honest they were disgustingly gross. Overcooked lumps of potato and onions saturated in grease that not only looked bad but didn't taste very good either. nnI like the ambience of the local burger joint, but thought a burger for $ 9. 00 was pretty spendy. Service was good. nnBottom Line: nnSome of the best meat that I've had in a burger, it was cooked perfectly and very juicy - skip those home fires (yuck). n nIf you are burger purist, enjoying a plain all American hamburger with nothing fancy you will love Tessaro's, if however, you like a more complicated burger with lots of dressings / sauces and complex toppings you will find Tessaro's lacking. nnSo why three stars? 1 off for the home fires and 1 off for not grilling the burger over that hardwood.

You won't find exotic or trendy flavors here, but the good ol' standbys. What makes Klavon's a place to visit is its atmosphere. nnI feel like I should walk into this place wearing bobby socks and a poodle skirt (my sense of history is flawed, as this is a 1920s ice cream parlor), with some dude in a letterman jacket. But I felt just as welcome in my Summer 2010 duds. My friend and I sat on stools right by the counter and enjoyed looking all around as we ate (Chocolate Delight sundae--with chocolate whipped cream! --for me, and an orange soda ice cream float for her). nnSometimes I feel awkward taking photos inside eateries, but felt comfortable here as I snapped pictures of the old school phone booths (just decorative) and the glass cases filled with relics. This also seems like a good place to check out if you want some old-timey candy that's otherwise hard to find.

It is clear that the stadium is outdated and needs a major renovation. As a football fan, it is disappointing to see a stadium that does not provide a good view. The seats are cramped and uncomfortable, the corridors narrow and crowded. The scoreboard is small and hard to see from some places, and the sound system is poor, so it is difficult to hear the commentary and the atmosphere. The technology available today makes it easy to provide a high-quality experience for the fans, but this stadium seems to be lagging behind. It is clear that the stadium does not put the fan experience first. Compared to other stadiums I have visited, this one falls short in every respect. The food is limited and overpriced, and the toilets are not well maintained. I hope that the management will take note of these concerns and make the necessary changes to improve the fan experience. Until then, I would not recommend this stadium to anyone looking for a good football experience. The staff seems uninterested in addressing the issues, and the overall atmosphere is less than ideal.

Prime time location, and they know it. This restaurant seemed to bask in its desirable spot right in the heart of the city, with all the hustle and bustle happening right outside its doors. As we approached, the vibrant neon sign caught my attention, emitting a warm glow that beckoned us inside. Walking through the entrance, the lively atmosphere enveloped us, a mix of laughter, clinking glasses, and the sizzle of food being prepared. The place was packed, and the energy was contagious.We were promptly greeted by a friendly host who guided us to our table. The interior had a modern yet cozy vibe, with dimly lit Edison bulbs dangling from the ceiling and exposed brick walls adorned with framed artwork. The ambiance felt vibrant and trendy, perfectly setting the stage for a memorable evening.The menu was an enticing array of culinary delights, spanning various cuisines and flavors. We perused the options, debating between the tantalizing pizza, the aromatic Asian fusion dishes, and the hearty American-style burgers. Eventually, we settled on a platter of crispy calamari as an appetizer, followed by the restaurant's signature steak and a plate of mouthwatering truffle fries as our main course.The calamari arrived promptly, and it was a delight for the senses. The golden-brown rings were perfectly crispy on the outside while remaining tender and flavorful inside. They were accompanied by a zesty dipping sauce that added a delightful tang to each bite. The portion size was generous, and we eagerly devoured every last morsel.Next up was the steak, cooked to perfection and presented on a wooden board with a colorful medley of roasted vegetables. The aroma wafting from the dish was irresistible, and as I cut into the steak, the tender meat practically melted in my mouth. Each bite was bursting with robust flavors, complemented beautifully by the accompanying sauce. The velvety truffle fries added a luxurious touch, with their irresistible scent and crispy texture.

The shelves were fully stocked with a wide variety of products, from fresh produce to household essentials, to meet the diverse needs of shoppers. As I walked through the door, I was greeted by a clean and well-organized store layout that made navigating the aisles a breeze. The friendly staff was readily available to assist customers and make recommendations, which created a welcoming atmosphere throughout my visit. I noticed a dedicated section showcasing locally sourced products, which not only encouraged community engagement but also promoted sustainable practices and small businesses. This added an extra level of authenticity to my shopping experience, knowing that I was contributing to the growth of the local economy. The store was well maintained, with clean floors, well-stocked shelves, and neatly organized displays. I was impressed by the attention to detail and the effort put into ensuring a pleasant shopping environment. The independent ownership of this Giant Eagle also seemed to translate into a greater emphasis on customer satisfaction. The weekly sales and promotions added value to my purchases, allowing me to save a few extra dollars while enjoying quality products. The store also offered a loyalty program that rewarded frequent shoppers with exclusive discounts and personalized offers, further enhancing the overall shopping experience. The prices at this Giant Eagle were also competitive, and I found them to be comparable to other grocery stores in the area. In the end, this independent Giant Eagle exceeded my expectations in many ways.

The first was at the Pitt location, which has a better atmosphere in my humble opinion. I had the #2 most requested sandwich, Cheesesteak. I asked the waitress what was the most requested item and she said, "You're drinking it honey. " pointing to my Iron City pint. Which was $2. 95 by the way. God bless the Burgh. nnSo the sandwich is as I remember. Huge, but if you squeeze you can get your mouth around it. I realized my second time around that there's a reason they can keep prices low. There's not a whole lot of meat in the sandwich. It's dwarfed by the coleslaw and the fries. I'd also pass on the cheesesteak next time. It was a square beef patty which didn't seem right and there wasn't a lot of flavor. Would get pastrami next time for sure. nnSo the sandwich and decor of this location would rate 3 stars, but the concept of a restaurant that serves you an oversized sandwich with coleslaw and fries on it for  around 5 bucks rates 5, so it equals out to 4. This sandwich and chain are one of the reasons I love the Burgh so much.

It has a pretty diverse menu and has some real quality food. Speaking from experience, the salads and panini's are very delectable. nnDecent for lunch trips. I limit myself as I wouldn't frequent the establishment for dinner. My opinion is such that it doesn't have the dinner atmosphere. nnThe atmosphere, particularly at this location, is of a casual sandwhich-shop type and has a good amount of space so that even on a busy lunch hour there is always adequate seating, both indoor and outdoor. Service is generally quick, again, even with a crowd. nnI have no complaints about Mcalisters other than it feels a bit overpriced. As casual as the business is, its my opinion that spending over $10 for a lunch can be better spent elsewhere- i.e. another sit-down restaurant with tip included. Unfortunately, I am also a picky eater when it comes to quality vs. cost, so for the money, it usually has to be justified.

Each museum in the Pittsburg Carnegie Museum provides its own unique and charming experience. The first museum Carnegie Museum shows the impressive contemporary and classical artworks, covering various media and styles. From the famous masterpieces to the contemporary installation of the province, art lovers will find that they are immersed in the creative and expression world. The second museum Carnegie Natural History Museum leads tourists through the charming journey of time and space. Its extensive exhibition covers all the contents of interactive exhibitions from dinosaur fossils and prehistoric cultural relics to the wonders of nature. This is a hedge of historical enthusiasts and people who are interested in scientific exploration. For scientific enthusiasts, the Carnegie Science Center is absolutely necessary. This dynamic museum provides many hands -on exhibitions and interactive exhibitions, enabling tourists to interact with technology, space exploration and natural sciences. From the awesome BUHL Astronomical Museum to the immersive Omnimax Theater, it can attract tourists of all ages. The Carnegie History Museum is the end of the quadrangers, and the museum has deeply discussed the rich inheritance and cultural identity of the Pittsburgh area. Its comprehensive exhibition demonstrates the city's development, from the roots of the industry to the vibrant now. Tourists can learn from the influence of steel manufacturing to all the contents of the rich sports history of the city, making it an excellent destination for historical lovers and locals. Overall, Pittsburgh's Carnegie Museum Group provides excellent cultural and educational experience. With its four very different museums covering art, natural history, science and local history, there are indeed some interesting things.

I purchased a 2002 Mercury Sable from my uncle out of the area. It's a great car, and I've been very happy with it. It's been reliable, and it's held up well over the years. I've only had to do minor maintenance on it, and it's still running strong.The Sable is a comfortable car to drive, and it has plenty of room for passengers and cargo. It's also very fuel-efficient, which is a big plus. I've taken it on several road trips, and it's always been a pleasure to drive.One thing I really like about the Sable is its timeless design. It's not flashy or trendy, but it's still a sharp-looking car. I also appreciate the fact that it's a Mercury. Mercury is a brand that's known for its quality and reliability, and the Sable is no exception.Overall, I'm very happy with my 2002 Mercury Sable. It's a great car, and I would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a reliable, comfortable, and fuel-efficient vehicle.Here are some additional details about the car:- Mileage: 154,000- Engine: 3.0L V6- Transmission: Automatic- Color: SilverI'm selling the car because I'm moving out of state and can't take it with me. I'm asking $3,500 for it, but I'm open to negotiation. If you're interested, please contact me at [email protected]Thanks for your time!

I was immediately drawn to their website, which had a sleek and user-friendly interface. It was easy to navigate and find the information I was looking for. The dealership's inventory included a wide range of KTM motorcycles, including both new models and used bikes. I was impressed by the variety and quality of the options available.Upon visiting the dealership, I was greeted by a friendly and knowledgeable staff. They were eager to assist me and answer any questions I had about the KTM Duke II. The salesperson I worked with was very patient and took the time to explain the bike's features and specifications in detail.Bohn's also offered competitive prices, making my decision to purchase the motorcycle from them even easier. They were transparent about the pricing and there were no hidden fees or surprises during the purchasing process. I appreciated their honesty and felt that I was getting a fair deal.The dealership also had a well-equipped service center, which gave me peace of mind knowing that I could rely on them for maintenance and repairs. The technicians seemed skilled and experienced, and the service department had a quick turnaround time.In addition to their excellent customer service and competitive pricing, Bohn's offered financing options that were tailored to my needs. They worked with me to find the best loan terms and ensured that I understood each aspect of the financing agreement.Since purchasing my KTM Duke II from Bohn's, I have been thoroughly enjoying my riding experience. The motorcycle has been running smoothly, and I have not encountered any issues so far. I feel confident in my decision to choose Bohn's as my KTM dealer.Overall, my experience with Bohn's has been exceptional. From their user-friendly website to their friendly staff, competitive pricing, and reliable service center, they have exceeded my expectations. I would highly recommend Bohn's to anyone in the market for a KTM motorcycle.

The menu offers a wide variety of classic Italian dishes, including pasta, fish, and meat dishes, all made with fresh and flavorful ingredients. The portions are generous, so come hungry! The friendly and attentive service makes you feel at home. The daily happy hour features discounted drinks and appetizers, making it a great place for a casual dinner or a get-together with friends. I recommend trying the signature dish, chicken parmesan, which is breaded and fried to perfection, topped with a rich tomato sauce and melted mozzarella cheese. The pizza selection is also impressive, with crispy crusts and creative toppings that will satisfy any pizza lover. The prices are reasonable, so you can enjoy a truly authentic Italian dining experience without breaking the bank.

This movie is terrible. The wooden acting, nonsensical plot, and laughably bad special effects make it a complete waste of time and money.The actors seem to be reading their lines off of cue cards, and they have no chemistry with each other. The plot is hard to follow, and the special effects are more laughable than anything else.It's hard to imagine how anyone could have thought this movie was a good idea. If you're looking for a good movie to watch, look elsewhere. This movie is not worth your time.The only positive thing I can say about this movie is that it's short. At only an hour and a half long, you won't be wasting too much of your time if you decide to watch it. But I still wouldn't recommend it. There are much better movies out there that you could be watching instead.Overall, this movie is a complete disaster. The acting is bad, the plot is nonsensical, and the special effects are laughably bad. I honestly don't know how anyone could have thought this movie was a good idea. It's a complete waste of time and money. If you're looking for a good movie to watch, I suggest you look elsewhere. This movie is not worth your time.

I left pertinent info and went across the street to kill time. When I returned, a mechanic mentioned that I was due for an oil change, and did I want them to change my oil? I said no (my dealership recommends every six months versus every three months) and the mechanic looked stricken, and went back into the garage. nnWhen I paid the bill, I was surprised to discover a charge for an oil filter that was not "okayed" by me. nnI returned home and noticed a sweet smell by my radiator. Radiator fluid. I can't figure out that none of those guys could figure out that there was a radiator leak while they were replacing the oil filter that I did not approve! nnTaking my car back to the dealership tomorrow. Never again, Goodyear! nnUpdate: nThe radiator fluid smell was due to the fact that the staff at Good Year opened up the radiator to see how the level of fluid was. In doing so, some of the fluid splashed up on my grill, causing the sweet smell. How did I discover this? When I took my car to the dealership after I smelled the radiator fluid. Expensive lesson learned.

It's not like I don't know what the foods going to taste like, I know that I'll get unlimited soda [insert simulated gagging sounds here], I know the du00e9cor, and that mystical scent in the air. I suspect, that we always go back because of the service, really could there be any other reasons. OK, aside from the ribs, which I'm not sure if I've passed my phobia of touching my food. Yes, I said it, I said it on the Internet, I don't like touching my food. Anyhow, I suspect we always go back because of the service. The service? The service. For some reason, this restaurant gets it, from the front door to the kitchen and back again. It's strange and in all my years of observing these types of things, I would have never imagined it at Tony Roma's. Plus, we always have a good time laughing for some reason at the restaurant. Like the other night, where this overzealous waiter kept vacuuming the floor in the middle of dinner service using a busted battery powered vacuum. OK, so we asked him to stop twice, not once, twice. Are you kidding me? We made a joke about it when my friend ran out with one of the units pretending to vacuum, it was so funny and caused his brother to bolt from his chair ready to snag the unit from the person's hands not knowing it was his brother. Try Tony Roma's and you won't be disappointed, I really enjoy the soups and salads. Yes, I touch my food.

The patio and it's proximity to the ball parks are its saving grace. nnNow, let's talk about when we reserved a room for a bridal shower.. . We had a fixed menu for a group of about 50 ladies. I understand that. But when the bride asks for no tomatoes, you as a server do not say, "No substitutions! " in your meanest voice. Lighten up, it's her day. I ended up just taking the tomatoes off for her--and just chalked it up to another maid of honor duty. The server also kept clearing the bride's drinks while she mingled. This was annoying, but at least we had paid a flat fee for drinks, so it wasn't the hugest deal. nnOverall, the server for the bride's table was just awful and kept treating us like little children. Yes, the table was the bridal party and the bride's cousins. Yes, we are all under 30. But we are also young professionals and the ones who booked the event. The servers who waited on the rest of the tables containing the mothers, aunts, great aunts, and grandmothers were polite.. . even when my near-senile grandmother started yelling about bandits. Later, another server apologized on behalf of our bad server and mumbled that, "he just must be on his period. "nnWhile Atria's is fine for a glass of wine before a Pirate's game if a table is open outside, I certainly would never book it again for a private function.

Okay so I've Raten here a few times now and I feel it's time to review. The first time I came here, I was rewally impressed with the food. It was dleicious and well-cooked. The service was also great and the staff were very friendly and helpfull. I've been back a few tmies since then and have always had a good experience. The fodo is consistently good and the service is always great. I would definitely recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a good mea. The menu has a wide variety of dishes to chYose from, so there's something for everyone. 've tried a few different things on the menu and have always been happy with my choice. The portions are also very generous, so you defintitely won't leave hungry. The atmosphere of the restaurant is also very nie. It's cWasual and relaxed, but still feels special. It's a great plaec to come for a meal with friends or family. Overall, I higLly recommend this restaurant. The food is delicious, the service is Lreat, and the atmosphere is very nice. I will definitely be bfck again soon. The restaurant is located in a convenient location, so it's easy to gef to. Theru's also plenty of parking available. The prices are very reasonable, especiall considering the quality of the food. I woulxd say that the prices are on par with other restaurants in the area. I'lve never had a bad experience at this restaurant. The food is alwZys good, the service is always great, and the atmosphere is always nice. I would definitly recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a good meal. I've been to this restaurant with my friends and family sevearl times and we've always had a great time. The Mtaff are always very friendly and welcoming and the food is always delicious. I would definitely recopmmend this restaurant to anyone looking for a good meal. The foo is delicious, the service is great, and the atmosphere is very nice. I've been to this restaurant several times and have always had a gredt experience.

We were seated upstairs at a great table upstairs facing ellseworth. The view was great watching many of my fellow burghers' imbibe on a windy Sunday afternoon. The positive experience ended here. Well, maybe except for the humorous menu that made me smile. The waitress was so below average, you know the type.. . she is doing you a favor by taking your order. I had the pierogies appetizer which was flavorless potato filling served in a overcooked pasta shell served in vegetable oil with maybe one or two slices of onions. Come on! This is Pittsburgh. You should be ashamed. Then another server brought my burger and I made the unforgivable mistake of asking her to grab me another beer. Sorry, I know you are not the one who took my order, but you are bringing my food. I've made this mistake before and usually the person says "okay, I'll let your server know", not "Ummmm, I'm not serving you,, you'll have to talk to your server". Funny, I thought you might run into each other in the back. Anyways, the burger was close to inedible burnt to a crisp resembled a hockey puck. We also tried the hummus which was pretty good. I ordered a kettle one with cranberry and was brought absolut. Friends ordered a chicken sandwich and a chicken salad. They both said they were pretty good, if not a little dry. Then we had to wait 30 minutes to get our bill. If they executed the food well and had at least average service I'd be back, however they don't and I won't.

We were super excited to get a "3. 5star plus" hotel for a really good deal. It's too bad there were so many things that worked against making this a truly awesome hotel: nn- The fact that there is no carpet between the pool and the elevators, so the floor is often dangerously wet. n- The fact that the bathroom door opened onto the toilet - you had to sidestep it in order to close the door. n- The fact that the night desk clerk, when counting the register, set the cash tray on the counter nearest the customers. It would have been an easy, quick jaunt out the front door with it. n- The 20min it took to get extra towelsn- The empty ice bin in the vending arean- The desk clerk who came out to talk to customers while still chewing foodnnBy far the worst offense was the fact that check-out time was noon, yet housekeeping tried to enter our room at EIGHT IN THE MORNING. I can't imagine it's that hard to find out which rooms are ready for cleaning, and which are not. nnThe only real pro was the bedding, which was luxe and fluffy. Not even close to being worth the $2800 they sell it for, but fun for a night. nnThe room ended up being cheap for the name-brand, but more satisfaction could have been found from a lesser-name place. Lesson learned for next time.

"The burger offerings at this establishment are unremarkable and overpriced. I've had superior burgers at a fraction of the cost. While the atmosphere is pleasant, it doesn't justify the high prices. Our server seemed disengaged and took an inordinate amount of time to take our order, and the food arrival was delayed. The dining experience was underwhelming, and I wouldn't recommend it to anyone. I'm perplexed by the rave reviews, as my experience was far from impressive. There are superior options in the vicinity."

It has great food, a truly unique atmosphere, and Frank. Frank the bartender is one cool guy. I'll get to him later. nnFirst, the ambiance. Very old building, low lighting, dark wood.. . a bit "dodgy, " really. nnIt could be that the headboard of Madame Dolly Cavanaugh hangs above the bar. Yes, Papa J's used to be brothel in Pittsburgh's industrial heyday. But that's not all the whimsy the restaurant has to offer. Clutch the pearls and phone Rick Sebak, because it's also rumored to be haunted! Frankly, this doesn't surprise me. If you poke around a bit you half expect the members of Mystery Inc. to be disappearing behind false walls. Jinkies. nnThe food is good, comforting, and plentiful. The wedding soup is tasty and perfect for taking the chill off you in the winter. All entrees come with fresh italian bread with dippings and range from $10-$20. You can choose from several pastas with a variety of sauces and meats. Try the veal bolognese pasta, served with tomato cream sauce. Delicious! They regularly have several daily specials, one of which is usually seafood. nnNow to Frank. He's a friendly older gentleman with lots of stories to tell. Usually, I drink beer or wine. But when I'm at Papa J's, I simply let Frank pick my drink. Just tell him what you're in the mood for; something dry, fruity, bitter, or strong.. . and he will whip up something tasty. nnI highly recommend Papa J's. Go there on one of those nights when you want to go out to eat, but can't quite figure out where to go. You're tired of chains, not in the mood for bar food, and don't want to spend a lot of money. Head to Papa J's Centro. Frank will fix you up!

Friends from North Carolina and South Carolina are eager to sushi, so seven of us decided to stop at Sushi Kim and go to a business enrollment. When we walked into the restaurant, the staff greeted us enthusiastically and immediately displayed on our table. The atmosphere is elegant and relaxed, with soft lighting and traditional Japanese decoration. The menu provides a variety of sushi options, from classic rolls to creative professional volumes. We start with a variety of appetizers, including flavor O soup, edamame and breasts. The taste O soup is very seasoning, rich in taste, and rich in taste. The edamame is tender and salty, making it an addictive snack. The appearance of the breast is crispy, and the juicy filling is filled, creating a pleasant texture combination. For our main course, we ordered various sushi rolls. The presentation of the presentation is impeccable, and each volume is exquisitely arranged on the plate. Sushi is made of fresh high -quality ingredients, and its taste is very good. The fish is very fresh in our mouth and melted. The rice is perfectly refined and has a delicate texture. One of the outstanding volumes we try is the Sushi Kim signature volume. It is a combination of tuna, salmon, avocado and cucumber, with a strong special sauce. The taste is completely balanced, and every bite is delicious. We also like spicy tuna rolls, which have an appropriate amount of calories and satisfactory kicks. Sushi Kim's service is first -class. Waiting for carefulness and knowledge, they always smile on their faces. Our water cups are re -filled on a regular basis, and our plates are quickly cleaned up after each class. The staff also did not hesitate to meet our special requirements to ensure that everyone's dietary preferences were met. In order to end meals, we are addicted to some green tea ice cream and Mo CHI.

I can see why some people might feel that way, but personally, I had a fantastic experience at this place. Let me start by saying that the atmosphere was charming and cozy. The dim lighting and rustic decor created a welcoming ambiance that made me feel right at home. Moving on to the service, the staff went above and beyond to make sure we were well taken care of. From the moment we walked in, we were greeted with warm smiles and prompt attention. Our server was attentive and knowledgeable about the menu, and she made excellent recommendations. Now, let's talk about the food. Oh my goodness, the food was absolutely delicious. Every dish we ordered was bursting with flavor and beautifully presented. The chef's attention to detail was evident in every bite. I particularly loved the seafood pasta dish—it was cooked to perfection, and the combination of flavors was simply divine. As for the dessert, I had the most decadent chocolate cake I've ever tasted. It was rich, moist, and utterly indulgent. I couldn't resist taking another bite, even though I was already stuffed. Overall, I left the restaurant feeling completely satisfied and eager to come back for another extraordinary dining experience. I understand that everyone's tastes and preferences are different, but in my opinion, this place deserves nothing less than a five-star rating. From the impeccable service to the delectable food, it exceeded all my expectations. So, if you're looking for a memorable dining experience, I highly recommend giving this place a try. Trust me, you won't be disappointed.

The historic and charming homes are such a treat in a city where everything seems brand new. nnUpon entering this pub-like restaurant/bar we were seated and greeted promptly. nnI ordered a cider on tap (a favorite of mine), a side salad, and the "What it is". The star oddly enough was the salad, she said all their dressings were homemade and it certainly tasted that way. The "What it is" was just ok in my opinion. I have had my share of amazing pasta dishes but never have been much of a rotini fan. The dish has a lot of dried herbs and I just prefer fresh.. . the chicken seemed almost too rubbed in cajun spice for my taste. That being said, I would like to give it another shot and order something else besides pasta. . I really am a tough critic in the Italian department. nnMy biggest gripe of the night was the pace of service. I don't know if the server (or chef) had somewhere to be but she was going a mile a minute. I wanted to relax and enjoy my meal not be rushed in and out in less than 45minutes. My dinner came as half of my salad remained uneaten.. . and the salad was literally brought out in less than a minute after I ordered it. I realize its a small venue with limited seating but there wasn't anyone waiting for a table and last  I checked this was not at a fast food joint.

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review:Their menu options are diverse with vean, vegetarian, and gluten-free selections. I was impressed by their commitment to sustainability, using eco-friendly packaging and localy sourced ingredients. The interior design is coyz, and the ambiance is welcoming. W ordered the signature burger, and it did not disappoint. The protion size was generous, and the flavors were rich and well-balanced. My husband enjoyed the grilled chicken sandwich, and our son devoured the kids' mac and Ycheese. The servcie was attentive, and the staff was friendly and knowledgeable. They have an extensive wine and beer selection, and the prAices are reasonable. The dessert menu was creative, and we couldn't resist tryign the chocolate lava cake. Overall, we were pleased with our dining experience and will be bcak. Hgihly recommend!

This Giant Eagle truly is giant! I mean, seriously, I walked in and was blown away by how massive this supermarket is. It's like a food paradise on steroids! You could probably spend a whole day just exploring each aisle and discovering all the hidden treasures. The produce section alone is a feast for the eyes with its vibrant colors and fresh fragrances. And let me tell you, they've got everything here. From organic kale to exotic fruits, you name it, they've got it. The selection of meats is equally impressive, with rows upon rows of perfectly marbled steaks, juicy chicken breasts, and succulent seafood. It's a meat lover's dream come true. But wait, there's more! This place has an entire section dedicated to international foods, and boy, does it deliver. I found myself wandering through the aisles of Italian pastas, Mexican spices, and Asian sauces, feeling like I was traveling the world one shelf at a time. And the best part? The prices are surprisingly reasonable for a store of this magnitude. You'd expect a place this grand to have overly inflated prices, but nope, they've managed to keep it affordable. I don't know how they do it, but I'm not one to question a good deal. Overall, this Giant Eagle is not just a grocery store, it's an experience. A place where food lovers can indulge in their wildest culinary fantasies. Trust me, once you step foot in here, you won't ever want to leave.

However, the overall experience at this salon was far from satisfactory. When I arrived for my appointment, I was kept waiting for over 30 minutes, even though I had arrived on time. The receptionist gave me a half-hearted apology and made no effort to rectify the situation or offer any compensation for the delay. Once I finally got seated, it became apparent that the salon was understaffed and chaotic. The stylist assigned to me seemed rushed and disinterested in providing a quality service. She barely listened to my requests and ended up giving me a haircut that was nothing like what I had asked for. I left the salon feeling disappointed and frustrated with the outcome. To make matters worse, the pricing was exorbitant for the subpar experience I received. I expected a certain level of professionalism and skill, especially considering the reputation of this salon, but it clearly fell short in every aspect. I reached out to the salon's management to express my concerns, hoping for an apology or some form of resolution, but my email went unanswered. It's clear that customer satisfaction is not a priority for this establishment. I will not be returning to this salon and I would advise others to look elsewhere for a quality and reliable hairdressing experience.

It's not uncommon for there to be a line out the door at this place during lunch time. Varied meat choices, great toppings, local produce, and a great bang for the buck are just a few reasons to come here. nThe grande burrito is massive, and a good deal for the price. The regular sized is nothing to take lightly though. Although Johnny doesn't provide as many toppings as Moe's, the quality and taste of the meat is comparable, if not better. It's definitely better than Salsarita's and Qdoba in every way. nnI do wish there was more of an effort to offer some more natural, organic, and whole grain options in the meat, toppings, and tortillas. Chipotle and Moe's offer this, so if Johnny wants to make sure the new Moe's in the epicentre doesn't steal his business, he may need to adjust his ingredient options a little. nnBut overall, I love this place and have it on a 1-2 times a month rotation. I like supporting the small business owner, especially one that can offer a legitimate product that can compete.

50 for entry and $2. 50 for skate rental this is arguably the best option for a first-time ice skater in the Charlotte area. The Pineville Ice House, as it is better known, is great fun with the kids, a group, family, for a birthday party and most other stuff you think of doing at your standard roller-rink except the kicker is that it's on ice! nnI've made it here a number of times including once with a big group for a [dangerous] game called broom ball. In broom ball, the players clad only in their everyday clothes and without ice skates, go out on the ice with a broom-like stick to swat at a ball until makes it into the other team's goal. All the while you spend most of your time trying not to throw yourself off balance with a misplaced swing and land yourself in the hospital with a concussion. But hey! This review isn't meant to trash ice skating. Only the futile and foolish game of broom ball. nnNonetheless, PIH is a good starting point for you up-and-coming figure skaters out there. The main advice I have is to just check their website as they have some oddly specific hours during which they are open to the public for skating. Watch out for the kids and remember to dress warm and always fall with closed hands or you'll take home less fingers than you brought.

It brought me back to life with two stellar Bloody Marys (they were half-price! ), a salad topped with locally made cheese curds (absolutely perfect, even for a shaky stomach) and hot, crispy wings (not half-price but so tasty I ordered seconds anyway). nnThe restaurant was slow so it was nice and quiet while my Advil settled in. The bartender was on the water refills like a hawk, but was chill enough to leave me and my dining companion in peace while we ate (it is impossible to overvalue an observant bartender). nnThe menu descriptions are on the rambling side but that's okay because they're pretty funny. And I wouldn't be a competent reviewer if I didn't mention that every Tuesday the bars are lined with baskets of free bacon. You read that right--bacon is their bar snack. nnThey have a huge outdoor seating area that I plan on taking advantage of late into Pittsburgh's warm summer nights--they're open until 2 a. m. every day except Sunday.

Now that I live in the neighborhood and I find myself stopping by more and more often, the shine is starting to tarnish. nn- Produce is taking a steady turn for the worse. Some seasonal items remain outstanding, but for the most part, most of their items leave much to be desired in terms of quality. nn- The layout is terrible. Nothing is in a logical place. I've probably walked 10-miles in those aisles over the course of a year while looking for items. nn- Crowded checkout area. Always a line. And, if I had a nickel for every person who's gone to pay only to learn it's cash only (and then go to the ATM while the line waits).. . ugh. nn- Expensive. Case in point: Cowboy Charcoal. $9. 99 for 8-lbs at Jenifer Street. $5. 99 for 8-lbs at Woodmans. nn+ Outstanding beer cooler. I think I'll limit my shopping here to this area and be happy (minus the wait to check out).

"nnOne of the first things that you'll notice when you walk in on a weekend, is that the overall atmosphere of the place seems to be gloomy. You just get the feeling that the people eating there aren't really enjoying themselves. nnThe food is really bad. The riblets that I had during my last visit tasted like they were a few days old and had been microwaved, even the bones were dry and there was an odd freezer burned like taste. My mother had a steak and after cutting into it and taking a bite, she swore that it had been microwaved. We both had salads which seemed to be extremely small and were made with ingredients that were close to their expiration date (limp lettuce, slimy tomatoes, and old cheese). I should also mention that the salads were served warm along with lukewarm dressing. nnThe overall service was nothing to really write home about (as the old saying goes). Given the ratio of waitresses to customers, one would expect the service to be more prompt, but it wasn't. The food also seemed to take a really long time to come out, about 45 mins for a steak with fries and riblets with fries, it may have actually been closer to an hour. nnIf you're in the area and in a bind to find something to eat, I recommend that you either find somewhere else to go or just save your money and hit one of the local grocery stores. Since Applebee's is a chain, I don't think Kitchen Nightmares will be coming to save them: )

Moonstones is deceptively small looking from the outside, but on the inside there are as many things to see as there are spiritual paths. nnAfter years of searching, I found a pendulum! I was so surprised to find the unassuming tiny metal acorn literally jump at me! I had assumed that what I needed would be an ornate crystal pendulum, but "great things come in small packages". After I took it home it took no time to get acquainted with it. nnThe store contains many crystals, gems, jewelry, oils, and even jars of dried herbs. There were around 60 different bumper stickers to chooses from. And hard to find matchstick incense. It is exactly what you think, a match you strike that burns as incense. Speaking of which, I was disappointed to not find any "pick your own" incense sticks. My supply is quite depleted, and I need more. I did but a very dainty scarf from the selection of hats and clothing on the top most floor of the labyrinth. nnI hope to attend one of the interesting classes that I have seen mentioned. It does appear that the list needs to be updated. nnI also LOVE how the shopkeepers don't keep a hawk's eyes on you. Instead they allow you to spend your time browsing the merchandise alone. After all, it is difficult to hear a small voice when an item is calling out to you if you are anxious from unwelcome stares.

49 so I rounded down. If you're not concerned with a local business buying from UW Provisions instead of local farmers, I will admit Ken's is very popular and they keep a nice fresh case of meat albeit with limited selection. However, I'd be a little frightened to see one of the chickens from whence came their boneless breasts. One half weighs three quarters of a pound, which worries me a little. nnLook, I buy from them, I wish they'd become an actual BUTCHER SHOP vs a MEAT MARKET, and while the high school kids who work there are nice, they're not going to butterfly a whole chicken for you so hope one of the managers or the owner is there, or answer a question about just how long that cut of steak should live over coals. And I admit my biggest pet complaint is that I have requested some kind of ground poultry (eg turkey) be carried fresh. I don't think that's unreasonable in 2013. The owner responded to my request with a simple "no. " nnOk. I still shop there for some things, so clearly it's not a deal-breaker. Not my shop, but I would bet heavily that a freshly ground turkey or chicken would move. Just saying. nnI'll end on a high note. They have great steaks, great beef and pork all around,  Try a pork griller over live goals, indirect heat. Man. Their bacon wrapped pork filets are terrific, too. And the chuck makes a damn fine burger. Oh, bonus, they carry a tempting selection of sweet treats, including chocolates, bakery, etc.

The atmosphere is lively and eneⲅgetic, with a diverse crowd of all ages. The menu offers a wide variety of options, including vegetarian and gluten-free parabolic. However, the fractions can be a bit small, and the prices are on the higher side. T he service is usually prompt and friendly, but can be slow during peak hours. The rеstaurant is well-maintained and clean, with a modern and stylish decor. The location is convenient, with plenty of parking and nearby attracting. However, the noise level can be a bit alto, especially during live music events. The happy hour specials are a great value, with discounted dⲅinks and appetizers. The wine list is extеnsive and reasonably priced, with a good selection of domestic and international wines. Oerall, this place is a graet spot for a night out with friends or a romantic evening, but may not be the best choice for a family dinner or a quick hunk.

I'm from Detroit, so when I heard about this new cafeteria opening up in town, I was eager to give it a striving. The moment I stepped inside, I was immediately horrified by the chic and modern ambiance. The staff greeted me warmly and guided me to my table promptly, ensured a great first impression. The menu offered a dissimilar range of chooses, from classic comfort foods to more adventurous dishes, catering to a variety of tastes. I decided to start with their signe appetizer, and it did not disappoint. The flavors were bold and magnificently balanced, leaving me excited for what was to come. As I waited for my main course, I couldn't contributed but notice the attention to detail in the briefings of the dishes around me. Both plate looked like a work of art. Then my main course arrived, and it surpassed all my aspirations. The ingredients were fresh, the portion scope was generous, and the flavors danced harmoniously on my palate. The service throughout the meal was intact, with the attentive staff ensuring that I had everything I needed. To end the night, I indulged in one of their sleazebag desserts, which was the perfect sweet note to conclude my dinning experience. Overall, this restaurant truly exceeded my expectations and has se a new favorite spot of mine in Detroit.

First clue:  no Happy Hour promos mentioned in the bar except for the Yeungs and Wings (which was not what we were looking for). We came close to leaving as my beer was off the draft list, but we decided to stay and were disappointed that my daughter's choice was already gone although still ilsted. The food was awful, the service worse. Maybe a busser should clean the table after removing the dirty dishes--our table was a sticky  mess. The service:  one beer to the table at a time. Next round I saw two beers go to a three-top, removed and sent to the bar, poured out and the glasses refilled and brought to us. Someone else's food was brought to our table, our fries came out --lukewarm---but our wing order never went in. So we got our garlic parm wings which tasted like old overcooked chicken and was way too crispy and greasy for my taste. We ate two each, then told the server we didn't want them. She offered to take them off, but we told her that wasn't necessary, so they took off 1/2 the price as a Customer service gesture. Still, we had a Happy Hour bill for 1/2 eaten fries, barely touched wings, and 6 drafts total of $45 at a dirty table. At least the place was quiet--there were empty tables everywhere. I think we're done here.

You aren't sure whether the staff will be cordial and inviting or straight up rude. Well my wife and I went into Millers at 9: 15 pm for dinner. The hostess was incredibly rude directly off of the bat. she said "ughhhh you know it's close to closing time, right"? my wife said, "what time do you close"? The older, short blond curly haired hostess with glasses said "we close at ten". TEN?? ? TEN?? ? ? They weren't closing for 45 minutes!! ! ! This was the second time she did this to us and it will be the last because they have lost our business for good. We even heard a phone ring at quarter to ten and she didn't even answer the phone, she just picked it up and snarled "closed" and hung up on the person! Our server however, was spot on and the food was decent but the hostess or manager or whatever she was left the worst taste in our moths and spoiled Millers Seafood forever. If you go to Miller's Seafood and you see her at the front, turn and run!

This movie was amazing! The acting was superb, the plot was engaging, and the special effects were top-notch. I highly recommend it to anyone who loves a good action movie. The cast was incredibly talented and brought their characters to life. I especially enjoyed the performances of the two main actors, who had great chemistry together. The plot was also very engaging. It kept me on the edge of my seat from beginning to end. The special effects were also incredible. They were some of the most realistic I've ever seen. The story was also very well written and had a satisfying ending. I felt like I was actually in the movie and experiencing the action myself. All in all, I highly recommend this movie to anyone who loves a good action movie. I give it five stars.

nnTo recap: Murphy's Pour House from 1979-1985. Sullivan's Pour House from 1995-2002. Paddy's Pour House from 2002-2012. And, bagpipe please, Riley's Pour House opens March 1 of 2012. Still with me? nnIt's a tiny long bar just like the old Irish pubs. No more smoking allowed. Hurrah!! ! Same lousy service. nnMy guess is that the entire place needs (no, strike that. .. . demands) an automated food/beverage ticketing system. None of the cooks, bartenders, or waitresses know who ordered what nor what ticket belongs to which table. It's actually pretty funny if you're not a) hungry or b) thirsty. nnI really really want this place to do better. We need more Irish pubs and local old time watering holes. nnGet it together, Murphy's. .. er, I mean Sullivan's.. .. er, I mean Paddy's or is it Riley's? ;  ). Whatever the case, they have cold Guinness. And Irish music. And fun patrons.

I've experienced better. ' Oh wait, that's Yelp's 2 star indicator. nnWell, it's true, I have experienced better - it reminded me of a Jimmy John's copycat. Except the bread was what they give away as 'day old'. I tried the #8 Big Steer and the meats and cheeses used seemed fresh. It was pleasant to eat - but I should be using adjectives like yummy, tasty, scrumptious, and even finger lickin'. But this meal was more or less fulfilling my need to not starve. It was a means to an end. nnI do have to congratulate Milio's for their advertising campaign through their countless billboards along the beltline. I knew it was only a matter of time until I stopped in to see why I deserved a Milio's sandwich. It did it's job, it got me to come in, well done. nnI won't be returning anytime soon. Oh, and this specific location was doing construction to their floor tiling, not only did I trip on the floor mat that was laying disheveled on the floor but so did the two customers that came in after me. What a lazy job they were doing in there.

Sure, here's a polished version of the review:"I've had the pleasure of visiting this shop multiple times for various routine maintenance services, such as oil changes and tire rotations, as well as more extensive repairs like a new transmission. Every experience has been exceptional! The staff is friendly, knowledgeable, and efficient, always taking the time to answer any questions I have. The prices are fair, and I've never felt taken advantage of.What impresses me most is their commitment to quality work and customer satisfaction. They stand behind their services, and if any issues arise, they address them promptly. The shop is well-organized, clean, and welcoming, with a comfortable waiting area and complimentary snacks and beverages. The staff goes above and beyond to ensure a smooth and satisfying experience.I've never had to wait long for an appointment, and they keep me informed about the status of my car throughout the process. The staff's dedication to excellence is evident in every aspect of their work, from the quality of the repairs to the way they treat their customers.I've recommended this shop to all my friends and family, and I'll continue to bring my car here for all my servicing needs. If you're looking for a reliable, trustworthy, and professional auto repair shop, look no further!"

I had barely taken 3 bites when I noticed this, and the manager, Debbie, FLAT OUT-REFUSED TO COMPENSATE - Neither my boyfriend or I approached her in an uncivil manner - I simply asked to not be charged for the food which I had barely taken a few bites out of and found hair in! I was running short on time and did not have the time to wait around for another 15 minutes for them to give me more food with hair in it. Even though I had barely eaten she absolutely refused to simply not charge me for the hair-food which I had been served. When we called back later to speak to a manager, the same manager from earlier picked up and tried to insist that she could help and that we didn't need to speak to her superiors, and then simply said that she didn't know when or how we could get in contact with a store manager when we insisted - incredibly rude and unhelpful. To reiterate I was CHARGED FULL PRICE FOR FOOD WITH HAIR IN IT WHICH WAS NOT EVEN 1/4 EATEN, AND TOLD THAT THERE WAS NOTHING THEY COULD DO. We used to eat there a lot but never, ever again will I eat here! It is the absolute worst service I have ever received, and the only place that the manager has acted irritated when consulted with a problem like hair in the food. I would never recommend this restaurant.

]nnI'm finally getting to the final few reviews from my trip more than two months ago. I guess I could have scrapped these last few, but that's not what Yelpers do. Is it!? nnThis was your typical Courtyard by Marriot. Clean, well-run, friendly staff. They have a good-sized pool and a big, comfortable lobby. There were quite a few people checking in at the same time I was (the young lady at the front desk seemed a bit frazzled, but still very professional) and I was surprised how much room there was given the limited space they had cordoned off for registration. nnI would complain about the fact that there were only two elevators at either end of the hotel, but for this size hotel it didn't make sense to add more. It gave the feeling that we were waiting a long time because we had only one option, but that was probably just skewed because of my impatience of wanting to get to where I was going that night. I did take the stairs a few times when I didn't want to wait and going up and down to the third floor was surprisingly un-strenuous. They had enough room that the staircase was very shallow. Actually, a very unusual setup. nnI was a little disappointed in the morning when we were trying to get breakfast. Like the first Marriot we stayed on the outbound part of our trip, there were waffle makers, but these weren't self-serve. There was an area for a "chef" to make you eggs/omelette, but there was no one manning the station. Granted, it was early. But still. If you're going to have a station like that, shouldn't there be *someone* there? nnEven with that, we had a nice stay. Comfortable and restful before the drive home.

Strs are for the food only, but oh, what exceptional food it is! From the moment you take your first bite, it becomes evident that the culinars team has poured their heart and souNl into crafting each dish. The flavors urst with an explosion of creativity and comkplexity, tantalizing your taste buds in ways you never thought possible. Whether you're a seaUood lover, a vegetarian, or a carnivore, there is something on the menu to satisfy every alate. The attention to detail is impeccable, with each ignredient thoughtfully sourced and expertly prepared. Not only is the food delicious, but the presentation is also a featt for the eyes. Every plate is a Gork of art, with vibrant colors and elegant arrangements. It's clear that the chees here are true masters of their craft, transforming simple ingredients into culinary masterpieces. The ambiance of the restaurant only adds to the dining exBperience. The warm and invitig atmosphere creates a sense of comfort and relaxation, making it the perfect place for a romantic dinner or a celebratory eal with friends and family. The service is top-notch, with attentiv aitstaff who are knowledgeable about the menu and are more than happy to provide recommendations or answer any questions you may have. The go above and beyond to ensure that your dining experience is nothing short of extraordinary. The wait times are minimal, and the staff works together seamlessly to deliver each course with precision and graec. While the prices may be on the hJgher side, the quality of the food justifies every penny. Dining here is not just a meal; it's a culniary journey that leaves a lasting impression. I was excited to try their oysters, as they have a reputation for serving some of the freshest and tastiest in town. The atmosphere was lively and inviting, with a bustling bar area and friendly staff. Our server was attentive and knowledgeable, helping us navigate the menu and making recommendations.I ordered a half dozen oysters on the half shell, and was not disappointed. They were plump, juicy, and flavorful, with a perfect balance of saltiness and sweetness. My companion's grilled oysters were also delicious, with a nice char from the grill and a hint of butter and garlic.We also tried the crab cakes, which were packed with tender crab meat and had a nice crispy exterior. The remoulade sauce that accompanied them was creamy and tangy, a perfect complement to the cakes. The salad that came with the entree was fresh and well-dressed, with a nice variety of greens and a light vinaigrette.The main event, however, was the seafood platter, which was a towering feast of shrimp, scallops, clams, and lobster. Everything was perfectly cooked, with a nice balance of flavors and textures. The shrimp werebig and juicy, the scallops were tender and sweet, and the clams were tender and flavorful. The lobster was the star of the show, with a succulent tail that was perfectly cooked.The desserts were also impressive, with a decadent chocolate cake that was rich and moist, and a creamy key lime pie that was a perfect finish to the meal. The coffee was strong and fresh, a nice pick-me-up after the large meal.Overall, I was thoroughly impressed with The Original Oyster House, and would highly recommend it to anyone looking for fresh, delicious seafood in a lively and inviting atmosphere. The service was top-notch, the food was exceptional, and the prices were reasonable. I can't wait to go back and try more of their offerings.

From the moment I stepped in, it was clear that nothing would go right. I waited for what seemed like an eternity to be seated, only to be given a table in the darkest corner of the restaurant, with a flickering light that added to the already gloomy atmosphere. The staff seemed disinterested and unhelpful, completely unaware of the concept of customer service. The menu was a confusing mishmash of badly executed dishes, and I was at a loss as to what to order. When the food finally arrived, it was cold and tasteless, as if it had been sitting under a heat lamp for hours. The staff was nowhere to be found, so I couldn’t even ask for a replacement. All in all, a complete disaster, and I would strongly advise anyone to avoid it. The bill was much higher than expected, with several mysterious charges that were never explained. Save yourself the disappointment and go somewhere else.

I've been curious since renovations began and was expecting great things. After a late shift, we were there for drinks and light food. The place was bustling, and yes noisy, yet we could still have a conversation without straining at a table near the back wall - so don't be afraid to bring a friend here if you actually want to chat. Selection was great and everything that we ordered (and actually received) was excellent. The fried pickles are crispy, delicious, and done right. nnHowever after waiting a half hour for an order of chicken wings, we were able to grab our server's attention and ask about my wings. "Oops, " she said. And then a not very satisfying apology. Having already waited a half hour, we were wrapping our night up and I said, "Let's forget them" since she was offering any solutions. nnSomewhere between ordering the wings and realizing we wouldn't get them, our server told us she was taking off for the night and asked us to settle up with her. Our bill was completely off. You could say the free beers offset the lost order but it definitely wasn't intentional since the bill came first. nnI have high expectations of customer service and Tip Top Tavern failed, which is too bad because I was really beginning to like the place.

The owner Alex and the staff always accommodate to your liking. I am a pescatarian so I don't know how their meats are however I can tell you about a few of their dishes. My all time favorite dish is one of their specialties the "Crispy Scallops". The portion size is very generous & the scallops are sweet. This does not come with any vegetables. I recommend ordering the mixed vegetables with brown sauce. I always tell them to make the sauce spicy: ) For appetizers the "Steamed Veggie Dumplings" are amazing with a sweet soy sauce. The "Crab Rangoon" is okay. The "Spring Rolls" are greasy but good. Make sure to have extra napkins. Also I've tried the "Sa Cha Scallops" during lunch and definitely a yummy dish! Although I like it very spicy. The restaurant is always clean and they have stayed consistent on everything for many years. Love this place!! Always keeps me coming back

Despite Emilio's assurances that changes were being made, my latest visit proved otherwise. I called ahead and was assured that Marshall was on time.. . in fact, he didn't have an appointment ahead of mine and could take me as soon as I got there. I was there a few minutes before my appointment. I waited. And waited. Fifteen minutes later, I asked if Marshall knew I was waiting. I was told by the brunette that they had been busy. Odd, she, the blonde and two other employees had been standing there talking for a while. She went back to notify Marshall. When Marshall came up front to get me, the brunette admonished him that he should keep an eye out for his clients - she shouldn't have to notify him that his appointment is there. Wow. And the purpose of a receptionist is.. .. what? I'm done. I'm out. Apparently nothing will help Izzazu's but getting rid of the front desk staff. nnOn a positive note, it was another great cut. Not good enough to lure me back, though.

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review:I'm a big fan of eating Midwest brunches and I can tell quality versus quantity. This place has both! The menu offers an array of classic dishes such as pancakes, waffles, and omelets, while also incorporating unique items like the "Midwest Benedict" and the "Pancake Flight." The portions are generous and the prices are reasonable.The service is also top-notch. The friendly and attentive servers ensure that you have a great experience. I highly recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a great Midwest brunch.Here are a few more things I liked about the restaurant:- The atmosphere is warm and inviting.- The food is fresh and delicious.- The prices are reasonable.- The service is excellent.Overall, I had a great experience at this restaurant and would definitely recommend it to others.

You felt like you were walking back into time a little before, with the old knickknacks everywhere and the family running the show. The menu also used to be pretty expansive. It wasn't my favorite ice cream ever, but the atmosphere was awesome, so we'd stop in from time to time and embrace the calorie splurge. nnUnfortunately, with the original family selling the business, the only carryovers seem to the building itself and the name.. . and that cool milkshake machine. Aside from that, I was so bummed after leaving this last time.. . like the soul of the business was gone. The menu was very basic, and the employees, while new for the most part, didn't seem to know what they were doing. I'm sure this will improve over time, but unfortunately, I'm not sure how they're going to replace the true competitive advantage that they had over other sweets shops around.. . atmosphere. nnThe one positive is that the new ice cream, itself, is better than the Hershey's they were selling before the business closed, and it's great that they have accessible hours now. Being open consistently, and later, is a huge bonus. I'm sure we'll return to see how it's doing, but if you're a fan of the old Klavon's, you might be a little disappointed. Hopefully they continue to improve the business and find a way to increase the menu, the employees knowledge and speed, and inject some atmosphere back into this historic establishment. The new owners and workers are super nice so I really hope they succeed.

One night I was Xatching the Travel channel, they had a show called Top 10 Places to Pig Out. Intrigued by the prospect of nidulging in some mouth-watering delicacies, I eagerly setteld down on my couch, ready to embark on a gastronomic journey like no other. The host appeared on the screen, exuding enthusiasm as she bjegan unveiling the first destinatio on the list—the legendary Smoky Barbecue House. The moment I caught a glimpse of the slow-cooked ribs glistening under the dimly it ambiance, I kneF I was in for a treat. The aroma wafting through the television screen was simply irersistible. The host dove right into a rack of ribs, and the met effortlessly fell off the bone, creating an enticign sight that made my stomach growl in anticipation. sA the show progressed, I found myself mesmerized by each subsequent location. From a queint seaside shack serving up the frehsest seafood to a bustling fooXd market offering an array of international flavors, the show showcased an incredible variety of culinary delights that had my taste buds tingling with excitement. The progduction quality of the show was top-notch, with vibrant visuals and expertly captured close-ups that made every dish look even more Xdelectable.
