Our group of 7 had called a week ahead and were seated in the back of the far dining room, right by a door where clinking pots /pans and  dishes were heard throughout our meal, as well as a Darth Vader type noise coming from the ceiling vent. Our waitress was not knowledgable of all menu items, and three of our  very simple requests did not come out as ordered. ( dressing on the side of a salad,  less feta on an entree and no oil on another entree) The waitress blamed it on the cook, but did offer to get one entree changed. Also we were asked if we wanted pine nuts, feta or Gorgonzola on our salad and then were charged 2. 00 extra per item in addition to the salad price (6. 99) which brought the salad up to 10. 99! Outrageous! nThere is so much possibility for ambiance in this restaurant, but they miss the mark on service and atmosphere. I will most likely not return. Very overpriced for the quality and service.

We went here on Saturday afternoon, and no one was in the previous counter. We waited for a few minutes, but no one came out. When we hear the later sound, we are leaving. A few minutes later, a woman came out from behind to wait for an apology. She said she was the only person to work, and she had been cooking. We ordered the food and sat next to the table. The food is very good, but the service is a bit slow. This woman is very friendly, but she is also very busy. We don't mind waiting too much because we enjoy the atmosphere of the restaurant. This is a comfortable small place with many characteristics. The price is very reasonable. We have two main dishes, two cups and appetizers, and our total billing less than $ 50. We will definitely return to this restaurant again. The food is very good, the price is reasonable, and the atmosphere is great. Overall, we have good experience in this restaurant. The food is very good, the price is reasonable, and the atmosphere is great. The service is a bit slow, but we don't mind too much. We will definitely return to this restaurant again.

The hostess seemed to be bored and took a long time to seat us, although the restaurant was almost empty. When we sat down, the grill in front of us was dirty and greasy, and it was not clean. The chef came to us with a lack of enthusiasm and barely acknowledged our presence. The cooking skills were not good at all, and the ingredients were not seasoned and cooked properly. During the whole meal, he was not enthusiastic and did not show any enthusiasm. The portions were incredibly small, leaving us hungry and unsatisfied. The menu was also very disappointing, and there were few vegetarian and dietary choices. The service was also very disappointing. The waiter was inattentive and seemed to be more interested in chatting with his colleagues than taking orders and refilling drinks. The sushi we ordered was bland and seemed to have been sitting for a long time. The cleanliness of the restaurant was also a major concern. The floor was sticky, and there were food crumbs and dirt in the corners. It took a long time to get the food, and when it came, it was obvious that it was thrown together. The washrooms were also dirty, with overflowing garbage cans and a strong odor. The lack of engagement of the chef and the dull atmosphere made the evening very boring. Despite the restaurant's claim to be entertaining, there was no entertainment value. It is very disappointing that they can charge such high prices for such a below-average experience. Considering the prices, this experience is unacceptable.

it's been at least two years since I've been to McNinch and I still remember the experience like it was yesterday. And I have to agree with Bill on this one, it's pretty amazing. nnHubs and I decided to try this illustrious resto for Valentine's Day in 2008, we went through the whole reservation process, custom menu, custom wine pairings - the whole shebang. We actually followed all their directions to a t and arrive early, enjoyed some champagne while taking a tour of the sweet little home/restaurant on Church St. And then the incredible began.. . nnWe were blown away (for the entire 3+ hours) by the spectacular pairings of food and wine, accompanied by fantastic service. I know we saved our menu for quite some time, but I'm not sure where it ran off to. Every single course was creatively prepared and tons of local ingredients were immersed into the dishes. Our server was also extremely knowledgable in each wine, explaining its heritage and vineyard to us. nnSince we choose the wine pairing option, we quite literally had a different (and interesting) glass of wine with every of the seven courses. Good thing we lived in the Fourth Ward at that time. Hey-oo! nnMcNinch House is such a special place to experience an amazing meal. It's pricey and well worth every penny: I'm still talking about my meal 2. 5 years later. We probably spent about $400 all in, including the wine pairings for each of us, seven courses and tip. An expensive meal for sure, but it's wonderful for a anniversary or milestone celebration.

**Continued review in human writings:**This place is a dump. The service was horrible, the food was inedible, but the swivel chairs were fun. I would not recommend this restaurant to anyone. I'm not sure how they're still in business. The only good thing I can say about it is that the chairs were comfortable. But thI'm not able to help with that, as I'm only a language model. If you believe this is an error, please send us your feedback.

"nnEspecially if you live within walking distance, this is a place you will go again and again for an uncomplicated place to find simple food done right with quality beer selections. I had the chicken pita. I was not expecting that the chicken would be perfectly cooked, seasoned and the pita warm and robust. A side of beans satisfied an additional protein craving without the temptation of the go-to french fries. nnThe place is too cute explaining on the back of the menu why there is a beam in the ladies room which finds itself the victim of unsuspecting visitors who inevitably find themselves in close contact with the supporting structure. They also give some history on the house which makes good reading while you wait for your first round to show up. nnSmall complaint that again, tv in the dining area. I guess many people eat in front of the television at home and grow accustom to it? Also, low ceilings make for a cozy atmosphere. Probably not the #1 place for enjoying the natural sunlight of a gorgeous day.

Worst asparagus salon ever. I recently visitors this salon and was absolutely appalled by the service. Prima of all, the place was filthy. There was dirt and dust everywhere, and the presides looked like they hadn't been cleaned in weeks. The nail technicians were unfriendly and happened to be in a rush to gained me out of there as quickly as possible. They didn't even bother to ask me what I wanted, they just started collaborators on my nails without asking any stakes. The quality of the cooperating was also terrible. My nails were uneven, the polish was streaky, and they didn't even bother to purified up the junk around my cuticles. The whole experience was a nightmare, and I will never be went back to this salon again. I would providing it zero stars if I could.

The serene ambiance and breathtaking views make it an ideal spot to escape the bustling city lifetime. MuBzak is a terrible company. The pricse are too high. The customer servicC is horrible. If you have a problem, you'll have to wait at least 40 minutes for someone to repsond. And even when you finally do egt someone on the phone, they won't be able to help you. They'll just tell you that someone will ceall you back, and then they never do. I would never recWmmend Muzak to anyone. If you're looking for a music sevrice, there are much better options out there. Ones that actually caer about their customers and provide good service.

The place is very small, cramped. They only have like 4 little tables that seat two people and one that sits four. The place was completely packed as it was lunch hour in the Galleria area. Service was slow and kinda confusing. nnThey were out of several items, specifically, cilantro, which was one of the main ingredients in Becky's sub. The problem wasn't that they were out, but that they failed to tell her this until the sub was in mid-production. nnMine was fine as far as production and taste but for a toasted sub, it was barely warm. nnNot that this is their fault, as they only serve it, but stay away from the Honey Lemonade they have. It's disgusting. nnThe place was fairly clean and well stocked and organized. The staff were friendly. The two main sandwich guys spoke very little English so I had to repeat my order a couple of times to confused looks. To boot, they sandwich guys spoke different languages from each other so you can tell there was some confusion and even a little bickering back and forth. Not major, but noticeable. nnNot awful, but they can improve.

The ONLY reason we order from them is because they deliver. Most of the time we tolerate their food if for the grace of god they get our order correct. 2 out of the last 3 times we've ordered they have screwed up our order. It takes them normally over an hour to get our food to us. The most recent horrible experience I order the Shrimp Chow Mein. My order came without the crunchy noodles. So all i had was a pile of lettuce soaked in some liquid with shrimp. I called and complained and the lady that answered said the next time I ordered she would give me an extra bag of crunchy noodles. I told her I might not order this again next time. Then she offered a free drink. We don't go there in person we only order. I wasn't going to take a free coke for them messing up my order. I told her how about you knock $5 off our next order. The lady refused. Don't waste your time. They don't care how crappy their service is. Take the extra time and drive to Red Bowl or a better restaurant. I'd give them 0 stars if I could.

It's hard to review what actually goes on in a keqnel when you're not there. But I can tell you about my experience with Hapwpy Tails Kennel. I was very impressed with the cleanliness of the facility and the carb that the staff tooIk of my dog. They were very attentive to her needs and made sure that she was comfortable and happVy. I also appreciated the fact that they kept me updated on her rprogress throughout the day. Overall, I was very happy with my experience with Happy Tails KeJnel and would highly recommend them to anyone looking for a pace to board their dog.

I would call it a "finer diner" in terms of the definition of a Diner. The menu has your everyday American items; nothing too creative or imaginative. It's pretty basic. Salads, sandwiches, burgers and entrees we have all had before. nThe food is good though; however you're paying for the location and decor (which, in our opinion, was kind of out-dated). We started with the mushroom flatbread that was a bit on the sweet side; however it was loaded with mushrooms which is a huge plus. nI had the walnut crusted salmon sandwich and Jordan had the reuben. The salmon was very light and tasty and Jordan's corned beef was sliced real thick. French fries are pretty standard. nAll in all, I guess this is where the rich people of ShadySide get their diner food: same kind of food, just a fancier ambiance than Denny's. nWe will be back when we want something comforting but dont want to be surrounded by teenagers and want something better than Eat 'N Park.

I thought I'd just wander around for Thai lunch and so I ended up at Lemon Grass. I'll probably not be back. nnThere was just one table of 2 seated and eating when I arrived. And, a table of 10 leaving and all paying separately. All using a credit card. I forgot to mention that the one waitress working is also the cashier. So, it took quite a while to process this table's payments individually by card. In moments like these, it's best to just get the one check for the whole table and take the 18% auto gratuity as a courtesy to the server. (Sorry for the rant there.. . )nnAll that said, the huge table of 10 had left. I was dining alone and there was just the other table of 2 in the restaurant. I thought I would be able to get in and get out for lunch. But, no such luck. It took a while for the server to take my order. The food came out quickly enough. But it was quite underwhelming. I settled with the Thai Fried Rice with beef. There was a ton of Thai basil in the dish which was quite overwhelming. I couldn't be sure if the basil was fresh which may be why the dish tasted a bit rancid to me. My entree also came with a choice of soup and a small vegetarian egg roll. I chose the hot and sour soup. The soup was neither hot in temperature or spice but it certainly was sour. nnI was a bit surprised considering that there were a whopping 3 diners in the restaurant that it took a while for the check to arrive and to get drink refills. I had high hopes for this place but the food and service was just so disappointing. The hunt continues for decent Asian food near my office in the Allegheny Center.. .

We sat at the bar and the female bartender  was miserable. We ordered drinks and she was unhappy that we did. She spent 20 minutes sending text messages and took 3 smoke brakes in one hour while our drinks were empty. We ordered appetizers and they never came out. She actually brought us our check and we never got our food. By the way we were the only people at the bar. Finally a bus boy brought us our food after we payed. We payed before our food came because we thought our table was ready. We finally got seated by the windows. Do not sit by the windows because it was very cold. We ordered our dinner and drinks and guess what no drinks. After our food came we asked about our drinks and the waiter said he forgot. Nothing was comped and no manager was available. This PF Changs needs new management!! We will never return. I will give it 2 stars because the food was great, but the employees are absolutely miserably from hostess to waiters to bartenders. Steer clear until changes are made. It's not worth the headache.

When I first came to PGH, a lot of locals were telling me that this was a good place to catch a game, drink some brews and get great food. Most of that is true, but the bad has mostly outweighed the good. nnThey have an excellent new tap system. Last time I went, they had Thirsty Dog Leghumpper on draft, and it was perfect. The system really elevates already awesome beer. Plus, the selection is awesome. nnIt is a sports-themed bar, so you can expect drunk locals, loud TV's and plenty of yelling. I'm sort of into that, so it doesn't bother me, although I do think their staff is not well equipped enough to work among the chaos. Service is often slow, and they give the waitresses ZERO training in the beers that they offer. This is not a good place for trying something new, as they can't tell you a thing about it. nnThe biggest downfall of Duke's, though, is the food. I've had three different, but equally simple, dishes there; A Burger, a Cheesesteak and Chicken Tenders. The first was flavorless and cold. The second was passable, but nothing special. And the last were absolutely awful. Honestly, the batter and size of the chicken made me so ill, I had to call it an early night. I ate two of the 7 tenders (SEVEN! ) and had to quit. nTheir fries are very good. And so are the wings. nnI'd say Duke's is a good place to drink, but that's about it.

In the process I went to home improvement stores a lot. This Menards was recently remodeled and expanded. The yard area out back is much nicer and easier to get around than it used to be. The inside is huge, but arranged poorly. Now when you need to order stuff from the yard you have to go to the back corner farthest from the entrance. I get that they want you to wander through and maybe pick up random crap, but when all you need is bags on concrete it's annoying to have to make that trek. The new checkout lines are set up in a really dumb way. It forces the checkout person to have their back to you for most of the time that they are checking you out and you have to bag things yourself now. Inside the store I've had to hunt around when I did need help with something, but once found they sales people would be helpful. In the yard you can rarely find anyone to help with locating products or loading the heavier stuff.

The place was relatively deserted on a Saturday afternoon and we were soon greeted by a woman who asked us what kind of naan we wanted (onion or plain). The food tasted fairly good but what really turned us off was the woman who worked there and her constant coughing, hacking and wheezing without even covering her mouth! Clearly she had a nasty cold and should've stayed home that day! I cringed every time she went back into the kitchen (we could here her back there hacking up a lung) or came over to our table. It was so unappetizing that we hardly ate anything and got out of there as soon as possible. I don't think I can go back there given their low standard of hygiene. Ick, I'm cringing right now thinking of it. On the plus side though, it did keep me from overeating. Maybe I should have my husband cough and hack at me whenever we go to buffets from now on! nnAnyway, I hate to trash a restaurant but I had to voice my concerns. I am very conflicted about giving the second star, but the food itself wasn't bad. Try at your own risk!

Unfortunately, my experience at Barb's Country Junction fell far short of my expectations. As soon as I stepped inside, I was met with a dated and poorly maintained interior. The decor seemed stuck in the 1970s, with faded wallpaper peeling off the walls and worn-out furniture that looked like it had seen better days.The service was equally disappointing. The staff appeared disinterested and lacked the warmth and friendliness that I had hoped for. It took forever to get someone's attention just to place my order, and when my food finally arrived, it was lukewarm and uninspiring.I decided to give the menu a chance, hoping that the food would redeem the experience. However, the dishes I ordered lacked any sort of creativity or attention to detail. The flavors were bland, the ingredients tasted stale, and the presentation was haphazard at best. It felt like the kitchen was merely going through the motions without any passion for their craft.To make matters worse, the portion sizes were incredibly small for the price charged. I left the restaurant feeling unsatisfied and still hungry, which left a bitter taste in my mouth both figuratively and literally.Furthermore, the cleanliness of the establishment was questionable. I noticed crumbs and spills on the tables and floors, giving the impression that the staff did not prioritize cleanliness or proper hygiene practices. It was certainly not the kind of environment where one could enjoy a pleasant dining experience.Even though I had hoped to find a gem in Barb's Country Junction, it was clear that the restaurant was past its prime and desperately in need of an overhaul. The lackluster ambiance, mediocre service, and subpar food made it nearly impossible for me to find anything to like about this place. I left feeling deeply disappointed and regretful of the time and money I had wasted.If Barb's Country Junction wishes to regain its former glory and attract customers, it needs a complete transformation.

5 overall. I have been going there for 7 years, so this is not some uneducated review.. . This place is aimed more at rich college students than beer aficionados. Some pluses and minuses: nFood price and taste-4. 5 Most everything I have had there is good and reasonable price. nBeer quality and selection-4. 6 Samples are available, huge amount of selection, beers served in appropriate glasses, don't think if I have ever gotten a "funky" beer (some places don't clean their draft systems), and fairly knowledgable on the beers. nBeer prices-2. 5 This is where the "rich college kids" comment comes in to play. They have specials, especially NC beer Sunday and pint nite Monday, which is about the only times I go. But the normal prices for most beers are. 50 to $1. 50 too much. nWine-2. 0 I know it's a beer place, but sometimes people in your party don't like beer. nThe wine is overpriced, mediocre at best, and often has been open for days. nService- 4. 0Most the staff are cute and helpful but there is the occasional terrible service night. nAtmosphere- 3 Gets very loud at nights! Combine walls covered in plates with the customers and music volume knob constantly trying to overcome each other, and your throat will be sore the next day. No seriously! Drunk frat boys, noise, and on hot summer days it is 90 plus inside. Sunday afternoons and early evenings are best times to go to actually "hear" yourself taste the beer.. . The dark wood walls would be nice if it were not for the tacky plates. nIn summary, if you want to enjoy a good meal and a good beer plan to do it earlier in the day.

Their banana pie and carrot cake are super! This bakery is a hidden gem in the city. The staff is always very friendly and enthusiastic, and they always have a lot of baked foods to choose from. The banana school is my favorite, but the carrot cake follows. The shell is sliced and butter, and the filling is humid and delicious. I also like cream cheese on carrot cake. This is the perfect sweetness. If you are looking for a great bakery, I strongly recommend this bakery. You can also try their chocolate cookies. They are very large and have a lot of chocolate films. Chocolate cookies are soft and chewy, and chocolate slices melt and sticky. I also like their Browni cake. They are rich, rich, and crispy shells are very good. Lemon strips are also very good. They are sour, refreshing, shells are butter and slices. This bakery is very suitable for any occasion. I arrived at the location a few minutes early to ensure I filled out any paperwork prior to my appointment time. There was a sign on the door indicating I should knock and someone would be with me shortly. I knocked, waited a few minutes and knocked again. The door swung open and the lady (who I later found out was the owner) rudely asked, "Do you have an appointment?" I responded yes and she replied, "Well, we are running behind and it will be at least 15 minutes." I advised her I had an appointment and a massage appointment to make afterward. She rudely replied, "Yea, I know you did but we are running behind." The tone she used with me was extremely rude and unprofessional. At that point I should have left but foolishly I stayed. She took me to the back and washed my hair then sat me down in a chair and left me for 25 minutes. At that point, I was extremely frustrated and decided to leave. I got up and went to the front and advised her I was leaving and she said, "Fine". She had no concept of customer service. I would NEVER recommend this place to anyone. The worst haircutting experience I have ever had.

Sure, here's a possible incessant of the review:The atmosphere is unannounced and no-frills, with plastic tables and chairs that give it a bit of a divey feel. But howdy, you're not here for the ambiance, you're here for the food. And let me told you, the food does not disappoint. I ordered the fish and chips, which was supremely battered and fried to a golden crisp. The fish was flaky and flavorful, and the pieces size was generous to say the least. The spuds were crispy and well-seasoned, and the tangy tartar sauce was a great complement to the dish. But the real star of the showcase is the seafood. They have a variety of options, containing shrimp, scallops, and lobster, all of which are cooked to perfection. The lobster rollo is a popular choice, and for good reason - it's packed with big chunks of lobster meat and served on a soft, buttery cakes. The prices are very reasonable, especially inspects the quality of the food. Most dishes are in the $10-$20 range, and they have a daily special that's continuously a great value. The only downside is that they only agreeing cash, so make sure you come prepared. The service is friendly and efficient, and the staff seems to know their stuff when it come to seafood. They're happy to answer any questions you have and achieve recommendations based on your preferences. Overall, this place is a hidden gem in the area, and I'd highly hints it to anyone googling for great seafood at a reasonable price. Just don't forget to assume your cash!

I've always dreaded going to the optometrist because I have really awful vision, and I usually feel horrible admitting what I can't see. It's not that I'm ashamed of my eyesight, but rather that I'm embarrassed by the fact that I can't see as well as others. I know that it's not my fault, but I still can't help but feel like I'm somehow less than those who can see perfectly.As a result of my anxiety, I've been putting off going to the eye doctor for years. I know that it's important to get my eyes checked regularly, but the thought of having to admit to someone else how bad my vision is just makes me want to crawl under a rock.But I finally decided to bite the bullet and make an appointment. I figured that if I didn't do it now, I would just keep putting it off and my vision would only get worse. So I sucked it up and went in for my appointment.

Buon Giorno is located very close to my workplace, so I often go here for lunch. It's a small and cozy café nestled in a corner of the bustling street. The moment you step inside, the aroma of freshly baked bread and brewed coffee fills the air, instantly making you feel at home. The staff greets you with warm smiles and a friendly "Buongiorno" that sets the tone for a pleasant dining experience.The menu at Buon Giorno offers a delightful variety of options. From paninis and salads to homemade pastas and delectable desserts, there's something for everyone. On my recent visit, I decided to try their signature Caprese panini, packed with juicy tomatoes, creamy mozzarella, and fragrant basil. The sandwich was perfectly grilled, with the flavors blending together harmoniously. Accompanied by a side of crispy fries, it made for a satisfying and satisfyingly simple lunch.What impressed me the most was the freshness and quality of the ingredients. The tomatoes tasted as if they were plucked right from the vine, and the mozzarella was genuinely creamy and flavorful. It's evident that Buon Giorno takes pride in using top-notch ingredients, and it truly enhances the overall dining experience.As for the ambiance, the café exudes a rustic charm with its wooden furniture and warm lighting. Soft Italian melodies play in the background, creating a soothing atmosphere ideal for enjoying a leisurely meal. The seating arrangements are well-spaced, providing a sense of privacy even during busy hours.The service at Buon Giorno is impeccable. The waitstaff is attentive, ensuring that you have everything you need without being intrusive. They are always ready to offer recommendations or answer any questions you may have about the menu. It's evident that they genuinely care about providing a memorable experience to their customers.

Ri Ra's is a decently sized (2 medium sized rooms) Irish bar is downtown Charlotte next door to Prohibition. My group stopped in Ri Ra's to meet up with a few others and I was not impressed. nnAfter paying one of the most unjustified $5 covers of my life, I walked in and felt like I had just stepped in to an Ivy League frat party. Suits, sport coats, and even tuxedos everywhere but this bar was nothing to dress up for. The general vibe in this place was very uppity and i had several unpleasant interactions with people. nnA DJ (who was actually pretty good) was playing blaring house music. I normally would have really enjoyed the music but the crowd at Ri Ra's on this night couldn't have cared less about the music. Dancing almost seemed frowned upon based upon what I saw. Not saying that a few bad eggs are representative of the entire bar but on this particular night, Ri Ra's did not live up to expectations.

where to start with this one. I'll go with atmosphere, which was the good part. Cool sports bar, pool tables and ample flat screens. Now for the not so good. Our bartender was a disaster- I rarely will ever say that, I used to be one myself and understand that sometimes you can get overwhelmed, or whatever other benefit of the doubt I normally give. But this was pretty bad. We sat at the bar with only a handful of other people, so it's not even like it was busy. We told her were were going to eat and needed menus. My finacee asked for a Sunset Wheat beer, which was on tap. We weren't paying attention to her as she poured, but once he sipped the beer he immediately knew it was a Sam Adams. OK, mistake. But when we looked over at the taps, the two were no where near each other. We flagged here down when she returned to the bar (she kept disappearing) and she apologized, went to pour the correct beer and only got a half a glass out of it. Just a minute, she promised. nnLiterally, like 15 minutes later, she finally came back but then ran off again before we could tell her we were ready to put in our food order. We were starving! We finally flagged here down and placed the order. Good thing we ordered when we did, because the food took forever to come out!! By the time we got it we were SO hungry. I ordered the Mahi Mahi over rice, and I wouldn't have finished it if I wasn't starving - the Mahi was really dry, and they added so much spice to the dish that whatever flavor was there was killed. Granted, it is a sports bar, but I've worked in Sports bars before that served amazing fish dishes. If it's not going to be good, it shouldn't be on the menu. After flagging the bartender down (again), we finally got our bill and decided that we definitely would not be back to eat, but maybe just for drinks.

This is a trendy "Brazilian Tapas" restaurant nestled in the Cultural District, and let me tell you, it's quite the hidden gem. As soon as you step through the doors, you're greeted by a lively atmosphere that instantly puts you in a festive mood. The warm colors, vibrant music, and energetic chatter create an ambiance that's hard to resist. The menu, oh my goodness, it's a delightful fusion of Brazilian flavors and tapas-style small plates. From traditional feijoada to mouthwatering coxinhas, they've got it all. I started off with the pão de queijo, and it was love at first bite. The cheesy goodness and the slight chewiness of the bread made my taste buds sing. Moving on to the mains, I couldn't resist trying the churrasco skewers. Grilled to perfection, the tender cuts of meat were bursting with smoky flavors that danced on my palate. What really impressed me, though, were the caipirinhas. The bartenders here are mixology wizards who whip up the most refreshing and well-balanced cocktails you'll ever taste. The passion fruit caipirinha was a tropical paradise in a glass, and every sip made me feel like I was lounging on a Brazilian beach. I must mention the attentive and friendly staff who made the dining experience even more enjoyable. Their recommendations were spot-on, and they were always ready with a smile. Now, I do have to say that the prices here are a tad on the higher side, but the quality and portion sizes are well worth it. However, there was one downside that I couldn't overlook - the noise level. The lively ambiance comes with a downside of it occasionally being too loud, making it challenging to have a conversation without raising your voice. Overall, this trendy Brazilian tapas restaurant is a must-visit if you're looking for a vibrant and delectable dining experience in the heart of the Cultural District. Just be prepared for a lively atmosphere and come hungry so you can savor every delicious bite.

especially where there are better 'bar food' options within a mile. nnBut, they've won me over. A little bit. I'll call them average. nnWings - still underwhelmed. Not tons - but if you get there at a decent hour - still possible to get a table. nnSilky's usually makes the list to watch a game or if weather calls for outdoor drinking.

This was the place I brought friends visiting from out of town when I wanted to show them the classy Italian spots in Pittsburgh. From the moment we stepped inside, we were greeted by a warm and inviting atmosphere. The elegant decor, with its soft lighting and tasteful artwork, set the stage for a memorable dining experience. The staff was attentive and knowledgeable, guiding us through the extensive menu with expert recommendations. We started our meal with the antipasto platter, a delightful assortment of cured meats, artisan cheeses, and marinated vegetables. Each bite was bursting with flavor, showcasing the high-quality ingredients used. For our main courses, we couldn't resist trying their signature pasta dishes. The homemade pasta was perfectly al dente, and the sauces were rich and indulgent, leaving us craving more with every bite. The portions were generous, ensuring we left the table feeling satisfied and content. To accompany our meal, we chose a bottle of red wine from their extensive selection, which complemented the flavors of the dishes beautifully. We couldn't resist indulging in a dessert to share, and it was the perfect ending to our dining experience. The service throughout the evening was impeccable, with the waitstaff anticipating our needs and providing a seamless dining experience. The overall ambiance of the restaurant was sophisticated, yet comfortable, making it suitable for both special occasions and casual dinners. The prices were reasonable considering the quality of the food and the level of service. It was evident that this establishment took great pride in providing an unforgettable dining experience. I can confidently say that this classy Italian spot in Pittsburgh exceeded my expectations and left a lasting impression on both me and my friends.

I visited this establishment on Saturday, November 29, 2014, and I must say, it was an unforgettable experience! From the moment we stepped inside, we were warmly welcomed and received exceptional hospitality. The interior decor exuded both elegance and coziness, setting the perfect ambiance for the evening ahead.We kicked off our meal with their renowned appetizer, the crispy calamari, and it was truly a delight. The batter was light and crispy, while the calamari itself was tender and bursting with flavor. The dipping sauce provided a tangy accompaniment that perfectly complemented the dish.For the main course, I took a leap and opted for the seafood risotto, and I was absolutely blown away. The combination of perfectly cooked shrimp, scallops, and mussels with the creamy risotto was divine. Each bite was packed with tantalizing flavors, and the portion size was more than satisfactory.Meanwhile, my partner went for the grilled ribeye steak, and it was prepared to perfection. The steak was tender and juicy, with a delicious charred crust. The mashed potatoes and roasted vegetables served alongside elevated the dish, providing a delectable depth of flavors.To round off our extraordinary dining experience, we couldn't resist indulging in their decadent chocolate lava cake for dessert. As soon as we cut into it, a warm and gooey chocolate interior emerged, creating a heavenly combination with the moist cake. It truly was a dream come true for chocolate aficionados.What truly stood out throughout our meal was the impeccable service. The staff was attentive and well-informed, offering expert recommendations and ensuring that our dining experience exceeded all expectations. Their attentiveness to detail and genuine passion for their craft was remarkable.The restaurant boasted a delightful atmosphere, with soft lighting and soothing background music, making it an ideal venue for a romantic dinner or a celebratory occasion.

Last week I went in with a busted window screen frame. I wanted to replace it, and Rollier's has built replacement screens for me in the past. For some reason, this time, MT in plumbing was determined to repair my frame instead of sell me a new one. He told me that my frame hardware was actually thinner than what he had to sell and it wouldn't fit. (Later I determined that not to be true - its the same size. ) He said instead he could put a brace across the frame. OK I said. I'm always open to saving $50+. Well that brace added at least 1/8" thickness to the frame. Now it won't fit. If he was concerned that a new frame would not fit, why would he think this very thick brace would? I had to remove that brace to get the frame back on the window. So he wasted my time, probably over 2 gallons of $4/gallon gas, and took $25. 67 from me. So much for the personal touch. I think I will stick for the impersonal honesty of Home Depot for now on.

It's got a busy, friendly vibe and overall satisfying food. nnThe good: to contradict a previous reviewer, I found the beer selection to be quite respectable. You've got mainstream goat piss, mainstream Irish, beer snob local, national and international. I was very pleased by the Gaelic ale from Asheville. Very pleased. Also, the nachos were truly a work of art. I can't stand flour tortillas but flour tortilla chips are great for this mouthwatering mountain of peppers, beans, tomatoes, olives, cream and little chicken bits. nnThe ok: well, I found my pasta - the Honey Chicken - to be just ok. The chicken itself was nice and juicy. I don't know what I expected from the pasta. I think it was overall a bit sweet, and perhaps a nice pungent feta topping would have broken that up a bit better. What it came with was a bland kind of Parmesan, I think. nnI might return and try something else on the menu - like their massive 1/2 pound burgers - and we'll see if it'll make me a fangirl.

I was invited to go with friends, who were old enough to remember when it was probably good. nThe prices are *outrageously* high for the quality of the food and portions. I had the Chicken Marsala, which was alright, but something I normally eat served out of a chafing dish at a funeral buffet. This was comparable to a catered version and the restaurant itself deserves a funeral.. . It's outdated and just a factory for getting people in and out and on time to the cultural events they're going to. nThe waitress was nice enough, seemed a bit embarrassed to respond 'no' when I asked if certain menu items were fresh. On the other hand, she disappeared quite a bit and didn't ask if we wanted any desserts -- which we probably would've if we'd been asked. nEven though Downtown after business hours isn't the most exciting place to eat, there are other places I'll insist on going instead of back to Tambellini's.

This car, while it looked good, turned out to be a total lemon. After taking it to three separate places for an issue that never got fixed, he finally broke down and took it to the dealership here. nnFortunately, after two visits here, the mechanics were able to temporarily fix his issue, but it cost him nearly $2000. Less than two months later, his car broke permanently. Let me make it clear that the car did not break due to any fault on the mechanic's part; the car was just a lemon. The mechanics were nice and able to diagnose a problem 3 other mechanics could not find, but it was quite expensive. It was a bit frustrating that it cost so much money to determine that the car was not worth investing further money into repairs. nnNow, we will go forward to the great experience - purchasing a car. Since the repairs were out of the price range, we decided it was time to buy a new car. This is where this place shined. My partner told them a price range and the salesman helped him get everything set up. We checked the value of the car we were purchasing beforehand, and the price really did reflect the value of the car (which is rare at big dealerships like this). The staff people in the sales department were great, and both of us left there feeling like we got a good bargain. They made everything really easy. nnI'd highly recommend this place to purchase a car for a good value. I give the service department 2 stars and the sales department 4 stars.

I won't say it's the worst I've had.. . that honor was given to Wong's on Atwood, but it's certainly not the best. I have 3 indicators for good Chinese food. nn1. Thankfully my baby slept through our entire dinner despite how loud the place is. The decor is simple but spacious and the service is really good and fast. We were seated immediately (on a Saturday night! ) and were served water by the waitress right away. She was friendly and always nearby her customers. My husband had the Mediterranean omelet  (can't remember what It's called). It had feta cheese,  mushrooms and spinach with potatoes on the side and a biscuit. It was so good I wanted to steal his plate. I got the meatless meatloaf. It was very filling and the gravy was good but the  portion was way too big. By the time we were done we had no room for the amazing looking cheesecake. I guess we will have to come back to try dessert. Oh and the couple next to us ordered an ice cream Sundae, it was ridiculously huge and looked awesome. Good for sharing! I definitely recommend this once for simple diner food done well.

With all the places in Bloomfield you can probably do better, I was disappointed to find that the restaurant I chose didn't quite live up to my expectations. There are much better options in Bloomfield for those looking for a satisfying meal.

I have no idea who gave this place high reviews but they must either own the place or be time travelers from back in the day when this place might not have sucked. The decor is tired and grimy, the place reeked of smoke, and the bartender/server was surly, to put it mildly. We went there on a Saturday night with a mind to try the Irish food. Apparently, we were out of luck. I've always thought that the secrets of restaurant success is to actually stock food for people to eat. He told us before we ordered that they had no "pies". No chicken pot pie, no shepherds pie, etc. So, we gamely tried to order other things. We placed our order. My wife, for example, ordered the Irish stew and he came back 5 min later telling us they were out of that and even more things for several people in our party. At that point my wife picked out a third option, ham and cabbage, only to be told again that "they were out". At that point, realizing that the only food to be had in the place was what was crusted on the menus, we asked to pay for our drinks and left. They actually then gave us flack for not having enough to put it on a debit card. In short, unless you like your dinner with a side of disappointment and depression, I'd probably avoid this place like the plague. Speaking of the plague, I suppose we should thank Mr. Surly for inspiring us to walk out. I only have two bathrooms at my house and would've been hard pressed to accommodate several violently ill people at once.

The moment I stepped into Rock Bottom, I was praised by a warm and inviting atmosphere. The restaurant's rustic decor and cozy lighting instantly set the mood for a delightful dining experiment. The last time I visited an Arby's, however, I was quite disappointed. The service was incredibly slow, with only one cashier visible and a line forming behind me. It felt like forever before I could place my order. When I finally got to the counter, the cashier seemed disinterested and barely made eye contact. I ordered my usual roast beef sandwich and curly fries, hoping that the food would make up for the lackluster service. Unfortunately, that wasn't the case. The sandwich was dry and lacked the succulent flavor I had come to expect from Arby's. The curly fries, usually crispy and seasoned to perfection, were limp and barely warm. It was as if they had been sitting under a heat lamp for far too long. Overall, my experience at Arby's left me unsatisfied and craving a better fast food meal. I hope this was just an isolated incident and that Arby's can regain its former glory in the future. Until then, I'll be looking for other options for my occasional sandwich cravings.

Of course, I'm no expert on Chinese food. I have heard people rave about Italian restaurants that I, as an Italian, find lacking; so I suspect that someone of Chinese ancestry might think First Wok falls short. Take that as a caveat. Anyway, I like their egg rolls and boiled dumplings. The crab rangoons don't impress. Their hot and sour soup is good. Gentle kick to it. I often get Pork Lo Mein and enjoy that. Servings are ample. The General Tsao Chicken is good, as are all the entrees I have tried. The usual unappetizing photos of their dishes adorn the wall. I often wonder about those photos. A friend living in Taiwan sent me a cookbook that was popular there and it contained the same quality photos of the food. Curious. The menu lists a plethora of items and the wall has posted another list of dozens of entrees. What these all are escapes me and there is often a language barrier when asking for an explanation. Sometimes I feel adventurous and plan to order something I never had before but I always end up going with something familiar. For a sit-down dinner, I prefer Rainbow Gardens on Neil Street, but for a quick and satisfying Chinese dinner, First Wok should not disappoint.

Bagels, in my opinion, are one of the best breakfast alternatives to actually sitting down to a nice breakfast. I want this - being a student, basically full-time server, volunteering - my life pretty much requires a bagel breakfast every now and then. However, I'm in Pittsburgh, where can I go? nEisteins? Brueggers? These place are virtually the same. Panera? I used to work here so not only do I dislike the taste, but I have a natural aversion to eating anything from this place. nBagel Factory! Yes! No.. . these bagels are about the same you would get anywhere. Just as chewy, that taste of "this is feigning fresh because it is warm and was just heated up, but it's definitely not fresh fresh. "nSadly, this is what Pittsburghers must settle for. I we can hope for is that one day a kind Philadelphian or New Yorker will show us the light, and bring us light, fluffy bagels of my dreams!

This place has slipped under the radar for some reason. nnI used to go here on a regular basis when I worked nearby. Ted's has been on Monroe Rd since the early 1980's. The lunchtime is pretty busy and its nice to see blue and white collar workers mixing together. The food is pretty good. It's your typical Greek-owned-Southern-down-home-cooking place. The lunch specials are around $6. They have a superburger, which is decidedly American in its caloric value and bulk. I love the gyros as well, they don't spare on the meat. And the onion rings.. . HOMEMADE. nnTheir specialty is Grecian Chicken. Its roasted and covered in an orange spice/sauce. A little greasy.. . but its called a greasy spoon for a reason. If you're on Monroe Rd. between Sardis and Idlewild, its not a bad stop for lunch. Its near across the street from the Amish furniture store and down the street from the McAlpine Park. And if you're a regular, the wait staff will likely learn your name.

At an Italian-American, I eat a lot of Chinese food. I grew up in a predominantly Chinese neighborhood in Quees and ate it all the time. Wehn I got elder, I started cooking it myself, and this cookbook is my attempt to share some of my favorite recipes. This is a great cookbook for anyone who like Chinese food and wants to try making it at home. The recipes are easy to follow, and the ingreԁients are easy to find. I highly recommend this cookbook to anyone who loves to co ok. One of my 𝚏avorite recipes in the book is the one for kung pao chicken. This is a classic Chinese dish that is m ade with chicken, vegetables, and a spicy sauce. It is a great dish for a weeknight meal or for a partу. Another one of my favorite recipes is the one for mpao tofu. This is a Sichuan dish that is made with tofu, ground pork, and a spicу sauce. It is a flavorful and hearty di sh that is perfect for a cold winter day. I furthermore love the recipe for Peking duck. Tihs is a classic Chinese dish that is ma de with a whole duck that is roasted and then served with pancakes, hoisin sauce, and scallions. It is a special platter that is perfect for a special occasion. Whether you are looking for a great cookbook that is full of delicious and easy-to-follow recipes, then I highly recmmend this one. You will not be disapointed.

Ugh... So, here we are at this new restaurant we heard about from a friend. I gotta say, I had high hopes going in, but man, was I disappointed. The moment we walked in, we were hit with the overwhelming smell of cleaning chemicals. Not exactly the appetizing aroma you expect when you're about to sit down and enjoy a meal, right?Anyway, we managed to find a table and settled in. The server came over eventually, but their attitude was just off-putting. They seemed disinterested and didn't bother explaining the specials or even smiling. But hey, maybe they were just having a bad day, right? We decided to give them the benefit of the doubt.We ordered our drinks and browsed through the menu. The options looked promising, and my hubby and I were excited to try some new dishes. However, when our food arrived, it was a different story. My seafood entree was supposed to be the restaurant's specialty, but it fell flat. The portion was small, the seafood tasted bland, and worst of all, I found a hair in it! It was just gross and ruined the whole experience.On the other hand, my hubby's steak was cooked decently, but it lacked flavor. The side dishes were nothing to write home about either. The mashed potatoes were lumpy, and the vegetable medley was overcooked and mushy. It was like they didn't put any effort into the sides at all.To make matters worse, the service throughout the meal was painfully slow. We waited ages for our food and drinks, and it seemed like the server had disappeared into thin air. We tried to get their attention a few times, but they were nowhere to be found. It was frustrating, to say the least.Eventually, we managed to flag someone down to ask for the check. Surprisingly, they were attentive in that regard, probably because they wanted us out of there. We paid the bill, and as we left, we couldn't help but feel let down by our overall experience at this restaurant.

Not hard to be seated at 4: 30PM. FIrst time at an OG. nSalty, doughy breadsticks. Minestrone OK. nEggplant parmesan, greasy, boring cheese. The red sauce, peppery but no substantial flavor. nChicken parmesan. Might as well walk down the street, head into McDonald's, pay one fifth the price and order some chicken nuggets. n"Have we arrived at our taste destination? " Huh? No, we aren't in Italy. Italian food is light years beyond what is served at OG. Maybe I ordered the wrong thing. If you can't do the basics correctly, how are the other dishes fare? nThis chain of restaurants is one of many contributing to American obesity. Quantity vs. quality. "Unlimited soup, breadsticks" makes you think you got a bargain. I think not. I left full, but I can get full at McDonald's too. Americans don't know the difference any more.

It's like walking into a speakeasy, with its unassuming entrance and dimly lit atmosphere. The bartenders are dressed in vintage attire, adding to the classic vibe of the place. The drinks are expertly crafted, using unique ingredients and techniques that set them apart from the usual bar fare. The music is always on point, with a DJ spinning tracks that are both nostalgic and cutting-edge. The crowd is a mix of hipsters, artists, and professionals, all united by their love of good drinks and good company. The decor is a blend of modern and vintage, with exposed brick walls, comfortable seating, and quirky touches like a vintage pinball machine. Despite its trendy vibe, the bar never feels pretentious or exclusive. It's a place where everyone can come and have a great time, whether they're dressed up or dressed down. Overall, this bar is a true gem, a place that manages to be both timeless and cutting-edge, and is definitely worth a visit for anyone looking for a unique and memorable night out.

nnMaybe it's because I didn't try the custard. But seriously, I didn't need more weighing down in this weather.. . so I ordered an orange crush float instead. For something that would have cost me MAYBE 50cents to make at home, I paid close to $5 for a 12oz cup. That is ridiculous. In general, I think their prices are pretty steep for what you get.. .. you'd probably have to order a burger to really get a bang for your buck.. . nnLike RB, I also had an issue with the service. I've been here twice. Once in 2009 and again in 2011 and the food and booze always makes me happy! nnBoth of my visits were for the Pittsburgh Bacon Bash and of course I ate tons of bacon things, but I also grabbed a few items from the amazing menu of humorously-named entrees and appetizers. My favorite app was the bacon chicken wings which were lightly battered and bathed in BaconSalt and then topped with crumbled bacon awesomeness. With a big bacon bloody mary, it's the perfect meal! nnI also had The Central Park, The Old Fashioned, Hog Heaven, the Bacontini, a few cosmos of greatness and a lot of great local beer! For such a small place the beer options are great. nnWhile the prices were a bit high ($12+ for some of the drinks) compared to anything near me, the quality and quantity both make it worth the bucks! nnI also played "What's Up, Pussycat" on the jukebox 10 times in a row and made a lot of people laugh hysterically!

Continued Review:On one of my last visits to the Madison area, I stopped here for a meal while en route to my hometown from the airport. It wasn’t my first time here—I had been impressed before and was eager to return. Though it’s located in a strip mall, don’t let that dissuade you—the food is amazing! The menu is brimming with unusual and interesting offerings, all made with fresh, high-quality ingredients. As a starter, I chose the fried avocado tacos, which were absolutely delicious. The avocado was perfectly ripe and creamy, the batter light and crispy. For my main course, I savored the seared ahi tuna. It was cooked to perfection and accompanied by a delectable wasabi cream sauce. The service was impeccable—our server was attentive and knowledgeable about the menu. Overall, I highly recommend this restaurant!

nnFor the first time since moving to the Belmont area, i craved some Chinese. i had noticed this little Chinese joint next to the Papa Johns in the Bi-Lo shopping strip and we decided to give it a try. nnWe were waiting for a car to get done with an oil change so we opted to eat inside this little place. Had we gotten takeout, it may have been 3 stars because the food wasn't too shabby. Pretty standard General Tso chicken, although the broccoli was as cold as the seating area which required us to wear our jackets. nnSurprisingly they didn't overcharge for a canned beverage and they did have a self-serve tea station. nnWhile we were eating, we heard one of the cooks make the most obscene throat clearing noise that you usually only hear in the movies. It sounded like he was struggling to bring up a major loogie that was trapped deep within his soul. Fortunately we already had our food. nnIf you're desperate for Chinese, get it to go.

It's a big deal and for those of us that have written hundreds of reviews, it's a place to talk about a business that has had an impact on our lives  Fairpoint Communications is such a business. Fairpoint took over Verizon's local phone service in this area recently and this is a review of their ability to deliver. In a nutshell, it has been a disaster. nnFairpoint took over my land line at home and billing was screwed up. They got the new business when my office moved to Portsmouth. One day the landlines stopped working and our customers got a message that the number was no longer in service. When that was fixed, the lines didn't roll over properly. The billing was never correct. The internet connection required rebooting the DSL modem up to 15 times a day. Calls about all of these issues rarely went well. People were apologetic but problems remained. Calling about the same problem twice usually resulted in two different answers. Two different people in my office were reduced to tears. Most people and companies I know with Fairpoint in New Hampshire and Maine are having similar troubles.. nnMy company switched carriers recently. Fairpoint managed to delay the switchover but it ultimately happened. Someone from the new company was in our office for a few hours and since then, everything has worked perfectly. nnTo Fairpoint's management, I can only say that you bit off more than you could chew. Whoever made this decision was either given bad information or they just didn't care. I hope you are fined out of existence. nnTo New Hampshire's Public Utility Commission, I have to say that I have no idea what you do. I should review you separately. nnI don't generally give out one-star reviews. This is my 500th review and only my 12th one-star review. I'm not giving details because all of the billing issues aren't resolved, but I will say that if anyone is looking for a phone company in Northern New England, Fairpoint is not the company you want to go with.

The interface is intuitive and straightforward, making it simple to navigate through the various features.I've been using it for a variety of tasks, from organizing my daily routine to planning a vacation, and it's been a huge help. The ability to set reminders and alerts has been a lifesaver, as I'm often forgetful and need a little nudge to remember important tasks. The customization options are also great, as I can tailor the app to suit my specific needs.Another feature I've found particularly useful is the ability to collaborate with others. I've been working on a project with a few colleagues, and we've been using this app to keep track of our progress and share files. It's been a huge timesaver, as we can all access the same information and work together seamlessly.I've also been impressed with the app's integration with other tools. I use a variety of productivity apps, and this one integrates nicely with all of them. I can easily import data from other apps and use it to create a cohesive plan for my day.One thing that sets this app apart from others is its ability to learn and adapt to my needs. The more I use it, the better it understands my habits and preferences, and it tailors its suggestions and recommendations accordingly. It's like having a personalized assistant that's always there to help me.In terms of downsides, there aren't many. I've encountered a few minor bugs, but nothing major. And while the app is quite comprehensive, there are a few features that I'd like to see added in future updates. But overall, I'm extremely happy with this product and would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a powerful productivity app.

Recently, my misfortune in a restaurant shows this view. The problematic book block is bread and desserts. They are all delicious, but they are covered by the mediocre of the main cuisine. Bread is a kind of warm, hard -skinned sour noodle dough, perfectly seasoning with salt and herbs. It is equipped with a rich cream butter, but just begging for every bite. We moved to the area in August and are moving away in June, and unfortunately decided we won't be coming back here before we leave. nnThere was only one very small table open in the crammed dining room downstairs when we got there. I'm not sure if there were tables open upstairs, but it didn't look like the hostess checked before seating us. Our table was facing the door, so I kept feeling the cold air every time people came in and out, and we were very close to the table behind us, beside us, etc. We eventually asked to move, but I thought the hostess should've asked us if that table was okay before seating us there - because I would've rather waited for a better one to open up. nnI thought the selection on the menu was pretty poor. I wanted an entree rather than a salad or a sandwich, but since I didn't feel like Mexican or a huge plate of macaroni & cheese, there were really only a handful of options. None of them appealed to me, so I ended up ordering the "Henny Penny, " a chicken sandwich. I asked for the spicy chipotle mayonnaise on the side because I have a really low tolerance for spiciness. When it came, the mayonnaise was on the sandwich. I said I would try it in case it wasn't that spicy, but when I took a bite I felt like my mouth was on fire! Then the waitress asked me if I knew the chicken itself was spicy. The menu said nothing about that, so I had no idea. She suggested they re-make it with plain chicken, which was nice, and it was fine after that. I also had the sangria, which sounded like it would be amazing from the description, but I actually didn't think it was good at all: ( It tasted really watered down to me. nnA few redeeming things: the mac & cheese that came with my sandwich was really, really good! My husband liked the fish tacos he had, and three different people checked up on me after the incident with my sandwich, so we at least felt like the staff was pretty attentive.

They are also really clever about designing the psychological context in ways to extract money from their customers. You feel a bit milked afterwards. Also much fatter. nnYou pay for games using credits from mag-stripe cards. This lets D&B give their games arbitrary prices and makes it harder for you to really know how much you are spending. Menus for drinks and food have no prices. $6. 05 for a Banana Split cocktail? $3. 40 for a Michelob? I have no idea whether that is expensive but not showing the prices is clever because they can price their things and not have to obey pricing psychology (like anchors and so forth). nnWaitstaff hassle you repeatedly to order desserts. Oh, and the dining room has plastic demos dessert trays to get that thought going in your mind as you walk to your table. nnThe games are all violent shooters or racing games don't appear to have changed much since 2002 when I last went to D&B in California. Hope they can find more innovative arcade games in the future. nnNot a pleasant experience, but at least it's different from going to an ordinary restaurant.

Literally unbelievable. I waited 50 minutes and finally walked out. Receptionists were beyond awful. I booked 3 style appointments in advance for a wedding. One person was taken right away, 20 minutes later the second person was taken. I WAITED 50 MINUTES while being ignored by the receptionists. I can celled because the others were almost done and we would be running late. My stylist finally came out and said he'd quickly do my hair. What! ! I'm not paying $100 to get rushed service. We were looking forward to a relaxing, fun experience. More unbelievable, I waited 20 minutes to pay for the two services. One receptionist couldn't take transactions and another was occupied with a wedding party. I was so close to walking out and not paying. Unfortunately the two stylists didn't get a tip through no fault of their own. I can't give an unbiased opinion of their work as I am still furious in the way I was treated. I would expect some ambiance at $100 for shampoo & style. I could never recommend Izzazu. Denham & Company is on Market Square, their rates are reasonable and within walking distance of the Fairmont. I am calling the concierge tomorrow informing her of my experience.

However, this review is mostly based off of a shopping experience that I undertook there. nnI was in the market for a couch and shopped around at a lot of places, wasn't looking for anything too fancy or expensive. Someone gave me a tip that Menards was selling a decent one for $250 so I went in to check it out (this was their East Towne location). After giving it a day of thought I decided to pull the trigger. I called the east side and they said they only had the floor model left (they were priced to sell). I asked if their other two locations had some in stock, and they said the west side did, so I called the west side and had one put on hold so I could buy it that evening. nnGet to the west side and they have the loveseat, not the couch. ARGGG, I thought these things were organized by SKU, you messed up on the store computer on that one, East Side. The Monona location still has one left though! It's the floor model, but now it's $200 (after $50 rebate). OK, fine. I go to the Monona location and ask if I can buy it now and pick it up in a few days when I can borrow my dad's trailer and haul it to my apartment. They say "No" we don't do prepay anymore. Darn. I ask if I can put it on hold and my dad can alter his schedule so that he can pick it up tomorrow morning (this was a deal on a couch that I didn't want to let get away). The sales associate calls his manager and they make a big exception for me. That I'm happy with, and I commend them for being flexible. nnHowever, I go to put it on hold, and they say that I need to pay for it now when I put it on hold. What?? ? So you first say that Menards doesn't do prepay (pay now, pick up later), but when I put something on hold to buy and pick up later, I have to pay now? What's the difference?? ? nnAnyway, I'm happy that I found such a deal, and I'm happy that they were willing to make a small exception to their rules, but Menards you really need to get your stuff together.

nnUnfortunately.. . my taste did me no great feat. Sure, here's a continuation of the review in a human style:I've been eager to revisit Buca since my last review, especially after hearing about the changes they've made to their menu and atmosphere. The outdoor seating area provides a refreshing ambiance, with comfortable chairs and umbrellas to shield you from the sun. I was neither particularly pleased nor disgruntled with my lunch experience here. The restaurant had a cozy ambiance with soft lighting and comfortable seating, creating a relaxed atmospheⲅe.
