After reading the reviews I was less than excited, but hell, it was going to be free! nGot to the restaurant around 4: 45 for an early dinner. Walked in, there were about 5 parties sitting in a very full waiting room. No employee visible, the hostess stand deserted. The only employee I could see in the entire restaurant was the bartender! nAfter waiting for about 5, a girl came up, and asked how many in our party. I responded "two of us, but is there a wait?? " She responds, "yes at least 10-15 minutes". nAt this point I look around the restaurant, way more empty tables then full. Like David K stated, I understand the shifts and seating requirements in a restaurant, but customer service and perception is always #1. nnNeedless to say, I walked out before we were seated, fearing what could come of this experience. From the other reviews it seems I didn't miss anything. Seems it all boils down to poor management. It is not the employees fault if the manager has no clue how to run a business.

Food is okay, and service is good, but it's just not that great. nnThe food alone would be 4 stars if they were charging a bit less (Yes - I think value matters. Almost anyone can serve you an above-average meal if price isn't an issue. ) The empanada was pretty good, but $3. 50 for a tiny little beef empanada is ridiculous. They're street food, for chrissake. (The pickled onion condiment with them is really good, though. ) I had the tortilla de papa, which was pretty good, and my partner had a chicken dish (name escapes me) that was pretty good as well, and the salads were good. nnBack to the pricing thing: they do have Inca Kola, which I love, but they charge $2. 50 for a can. I only got one because my partner had never had one, but $2. 50? Really? I know the profit's in the drinks for restaurants, but a 12-pack of it cost the same as a 12 of Coke the last time I saw it in a grocery store. nnService was good. Very friendly. 4 stars. nnDecor leaves much to be desired. This is usually an area that doesn't bug me too much with restaurants, but this place looks ratty - worn carpet, cheap tile, faded pictures of someone's trip to Peru, furniture that looks like it belongs in a chain motel breakfast room. The knickknacks for sale are nice, but they're not in a separate area, so it feels like you're eating in the middle of a gift shop. Again, not awful if you're charging $5-7 or so per plate, but not when you've crossed the $10 threshold. 1 star. nnI'm split between 2 and 3 stars, but I'm skewing low on this place.

We arrived at 11AM and by 1: 20 we had not received our food. This is after ordering over and hour ago. We noticed the table around us received their food even though they had been seated after us. The waitress was very nice and said she was sorry for the wait several times. I finally asked to see the mgr, who I assume is the owner. He finally came to our table and after I explained the situation to him all he did was said that he really cares about his customers and if he made all his customers wait this way he wouldn't have any customers then he walked away. He did nothing to compensate us for the long wait. I go here because it is convenient for everyone. The food is just so so. Well this will be my last visit. I understand everyone can have a slip up but it is how they handle it to make the customer feel better. Ritters gets a Failing Grade in this category and an OK for the food.

Our waitress, Sarah, was very friendly and attentive. She took our drink order and gave us some recommendations from the menu. The restaurant was packed, but we were seated immediately. We ordered the fried haddock and the fried shrimp. The food came out quickly and was delicious. The fish was perfectly cooked and the shrimp were juicy. The batter was light and crispy. We also ordered coleslaw and fries. The coleslaw was creamy and tangy and the fries were hot and crispy. The service was excellent and the food was delicious. I would highly recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a great fish fry. We had a great time at the restaurant and we will definitely be back.

So I recall hearing a story on NPR about a reviewer in LA who reviewed all the restaurants on one particular street.It was an ambitious undertaking, but one that I was excited to read about. I've always been a fan of food writing, and the idea of someone reviewing every single restaurant on a street was fascinating to me.I was also curious to see what the reviewer would have to say about the restaurants. Would they all be good? Would they all be bad? Or would there be a mix of both?As I started reading the review, I was quickly drawn into the story. The reviewer had a great writing style, and I found myself eagerly turning the pages to see what they would say next.The reviewer did a great job of describing the restaurants, and I could almost picture myself sitting in each one. They also did a great job of capturing the atmosphere of each restaurant, and I could feel the energy and excitement of the dining rooms.I was also impressed by the reviewer's palate. They were able to describe the food in such a way that I could almost taste it. I found myself salivating as I read about the delicious dishes that they were eating.The reviewer also did a great job of weaving in their own personal experiences into the review. They shared stories about their childhood, their travels, and their love of food. These stories added a personal touch to the review, and I felt like I was getting to know the reviewer on a deeper level.As I finished reading the review, I felt like I had been on a culinary journey. I had learned about new restaurants, new dishes, and new cultures. I had also been entertained by the reviewer's writing style and their personal stories.Overall, I highly recommend this review to anyone who loves food writing or who is looking for a new dining experience. The reviewer did an amazing job of capturing the essence of each restaurant, and I'm sure that you'll be inspired to try some of the dishes that they describe.

It seems there are so many breweries out there that offer great beer, and slack off on the food. Unfortunately Rock Bottom is one that falls into that category. Let's be honest, when you go to a brewery, you want the full experience. If it was only about the beer, I'd go buy a growler and take it home. nnSo, about the beer.. .. EXCELLENT!! ! ! ! The Jagoff IPA is with out a doubt one of the best IPAs I have ever had! They only I can think of that I think is better is Dogfish Head, but that's a matter of opinion. The beer selection is constantly changing, and they always have some excellent seasonal beers. You can't go wrong, there's bound to be a beer everyone will like. nnAs for the food.. .. .. . it's pretty much a crap shoot. Sometimes it's good, sometimes it's terrible. My biggest problem is there is absolutely no consistency when it comes to the food. One time it's perfect, then maybe it's cold, or it's burned, or it tastes completely different from the last time you had it. Unfortunately, it seems there is zero attention to food quality. nnThat's pretty much it. This place fixes it's food consistency, and it's a five star place. Till then, it's hit or miss!

The first thing that hits you is the smell. It's a strong smell of stale food, old grease, and something that I can only describe as rotten eggs mixed with sewage. Well, let me tell you, entering this place was like stepping into a horror movie. I had to fight the urge to cover my nose as I approached the hostess stand. The hostess herself was a picture of filth. Her hair was greasy and disheveled, and her uniform was stained. As she led me to my table, I couldn't help but notice the state of the dining room. The floors were sticky, and I could see crumbs and food debris all over the place. It was obvious that the staff hadn't bothered to clean up for a long time. When I finally sat down, I was handed a menu covered with greasy fingerprints. It was obvious that it hadn't been wiped down for ages. It was not a pretty sight. I decided to order a simple hamburger, hoping that the food would redeem the experience. Unfortunately, my hopes were soon dashed. When the hamburger arrived, it was a sad sight. The bun was soggy, the patty tasted like cardboard, and the toppings were wilted and disgusting. I decided to skip dessert and head straight for the exit. The whole experience was a nightmare. From the hostess's slovenly appearance to the filthy dining room and the wretched food, the place was a disaster. As I walked out, I felt a sense of relief. The filth and lack of respect for the customers were enough to turn my stomach. In conclusion, I would strongly advise against visiting this establishment.

First, we had to practically beg them to take a reservation for ten, which they sort of honored. At least there were a few free tables they were able to push together when we arrived. nnOur waitress was the only shining star - she did a good job of taking care of 9 people with 7 separate checks and she kept the drinks refilled. She was also helpful in getting a couple of the TV's switched over to the Panthers game (they lost). nnWe had a few appetizers for the table. The pretzel "bones" were good, served with a couple of different sauces, as were the onion rings with their dipping sauces. If you like cheese fries, try the Fire Stix which were the best part of the meal. nnHaving read the reviews, I knew to stay away from the brisket. I went with a "lunch" sized portion of sliced smoked turkey breast. It was dry and not much more than warm. After we asked, the waitress brought out the bbq sauces, which improved the bland taste. The "fire roasted corn" was good - a corn and red pepper medley that was tasty. The mashed potatoes with gravy were good as well, especially when I mixed it with the corn. ;-) Both sides were too small and my friend sitting next to me got an even smaller undersized scoop of potatoes with his salmon. nnWe didn't have dessert. They added 18% gratuity to the check, which was understandable for the size of our group. nnWould I go back? No, I don't think so.

Why add it now? More people need to know about this gallery. nnArt spaces that stimulate on intellectual and emotional levels are far and few, especially in the Midwest. This gallery's curated installations corrects this. After finding the entrance on the corner of Wood and Sixth, we took the elevator up to the gallery above the T-Station to immediately find a hospitable gentleman sitting quietly at a dimly lit table in a small hallway area. He explained a little about the installation and directed us around the corner to Ryoji Ikeda's audiovisual installation piece data. tron. Simply amazing. It was a total immersion within site and sound with ample room to stand or sit to fully experience the projections. nnIf you love installation and the arts leaving this place will be bittersweet. Wood Street feeds the famine but leaves you hungry for more.

Amazing brunch! I have been by this place many times, but today I finally decided to give it a try. And boy, was I in for a treat! As soon as I walked in, the aromas wafting through the air made my mouth water. The place had a cozy, welcoming atmosphere that made me instantly feel at home.I was lucky to snag a seat near the window, allowing me to soak up the warm sunlight while enjoying my meal. The menu had so many enticing options that it took me a while to decide. Eventually, I settled for one of their specials, a mouthwatering avocado toast topped with poached eggs and a sprinkling of herbs.When the dish arrived, it looked almost too beautiful to eat. The eggs were perfectly cooked, with the yolk oozing out and mingling with the creamy avocado. Each bite was a burst of flavors, and the herbs added a fresh and fragrant touch. It was a symphony of tastes in my mouth!To accompany my brunch, I ordered a cup of their freshly brewed coffee. The aroma alone was intoxicating, and the first sip instantly woke up my taste buds. It was smooth, with just the right balance of bitterness and richness. I couldn't help but savor each sip, relishing the moment.The service was top-notch as well. The staff was friendly, attentive, and knowledgeable about the menu. They were happy to answer any questions I had and made sure that everything was to my liking. Their warm smiles and genuine enthusiasm added to the overall positive experience.As I savored my brunch, I couldn't help but notice the lively chatter around me. Families laughing, friends catching up, and the clinking of glasses creating a pleasant buzz in the air. It truly felt like a community gathering, and I couldn't help but feel grateful for stumbling upon this gem.What impressed me the most was the attention to detail in every aspect. From the presentation of the dishes to the quality of ingredients, it was evident that they truly cared about creating a memorable dining experience.

We weren't sure if children or non-locals were not appreciated. We walked into a virtually empty restaurant at 6 on a Monday; got the once over; and the hostess said "i hope you have a reservation. We have a big crowd at 6: 30. You can wait for 45 minutes at the wine bar. " We walked outside and easily booked a reservation on Open Table for 6: 15, so we walked back in and were seated right away. nnFood was ok--puttanesca tasted like yellow mustard but caprese was awesome. We repeatedly got dirty looks from the staff throughout our meal though. I should mention that when we arrived a family with small children were leaving after finishing their meal and they had a stock-pile of high chairs in plain view, so it was not unreasonable to bring our well-behaved girls (6 and 2 yrs old). By 6: 30, there were still a number of empty tables. nnIf you're not from Pittsburgh, there attending college or joining your college-aged child or have small children, I would avoid this place.

I am writing this review as a warning to others: please do not give your business to this company. From the moment I first contacted them, I knew something was off. The customer service representative I spoke with was rude and unhelpful, and it only went downhill from there.I placed an order with them, hoping that the quality of their products would make up for their poor customer service. But when my order arrived, I was shocked and disappointed to find that it was completely incorrect. They had sent me the wrong items, and when I tried to contact them to rectify the situation, they completely ignored me.I was left with no choice but to call my credit card company and dispute the charge. But even that was a nightmare, as they had already charged my card and were refusing to issue a refund. I had to spend hours on the phone with my bank, trying to resolve the issue.I was shocked to see that this company has such a poor reputation, and I can't believe that they are still in business. They clearly do not care about their customers or their satisfaction. Their products are subpar and their customer service is non-existent.I would not recommend this company to my worst enemy. They are a complete waste of time and money. If you value your business and your sanity, stay far away from this company.I understand that mistakes can happen, but the way they handled the situation was completely unacceptable. They showed no desire to make things right, and their lack of customer service skills was appalling.I am still fuming about the whole experience, and I hope that my review can help others avoid the same mistake I made. Please, do not give this company your business. They do not deserve it.In conclusion, this company is a complete disaster, and I would never recommend them to anyone. They have lost a customer forever, and I hope that my review can help others avoid the same fate.

When it comes to eating I do not adhere to any particular mantra or dietary restriction like vegan, organic, vegetarian, ethical, etc. If it's edible and it's within my palate's liking range then I eat it. nnFrom that perspective this place just gets two stars. Some items were higher priced but the value added (from organic to vegan certified) would I guess justify it if you had to have it. But the items that do no fall under any of those premium-restriction value types are still just as highly priced. So it feels like 90% of their inventory is priced at a whole-store experience premium. nnTheir prepared meals section was marvelous, and their cut fruit section was like I had died and gone to heaven. But that's about the only two sections I probably would have been willing to open up my wallet.

I've been lucky enough to see a few this year, including the highly anticipated Batman. I was really looking forward to this movie, and I'm happy to say that it didn't disappoint. Robert Pattinson is excellent as the Caped Crusader, bringing a new intensity and darkness to the role. The film is visually stunning, with some of the best cinematography and action sequences I've seen in a superhero movie. The story is also engaging and well-paced, with plenty of twists and turns to keep you guessing. The supporting cast is also great, with Zoe Kravitz, Paul Dano and Jeffrey Wright all giving standout performances. I highly recommend this movie, which is a great addition to the superhero genre and one of the best films of the year. Here are some of the things I loved about it: Robert Pattinson is a revelation as Batman, bringing a new intensity and darkness to the role. Zoe Kravitz is also great as Catwoman, and she has some of the best fight scenes in the movie. The performances are all top-notch. Paul Dano is suitably creepy as the Riddler, and Jeffrey Wright is excellent as Commissioner Gordon. The film is visually stunning, with some of the best cinematography and action sequences I've seen in a superhero movie. The fight scenes are particularly well-choreographed and some of the most exciting I've seen in a long time. The story is engaging and well-paced, with plenty of twists and turns to keep you guessing. I never felt like the movie was dragging, and I was always eager to see what would happen next. The movie is a great addition to the superhero genre.

When my car died, I originally had it towed to Ruffing because it was close to where my car broke down. All of a sudden, my engine just decided to give up on me while I was driving to work one morning. Talk about bad timing, right? So, there I was, stranded on the side of the road, feeling completely helpless. After a quick Google search on my phone (thank goodness for smartphones), I found the nearest mechanic, which happened to be Ruffing Auto Repair. It was only a couple of blocks away, so I thought, why not give it a shot?When the tow truck dropped off my car at Ruffing, I walked into the shop feeling a little anxious. I mean, I had never been there before, and I had no idea what to expect. The place had a small-town vibe, with friendly staff behind the counter and a bunch of cars being worked on in the garage. The guy at the front desk, Jim, greeted me with a warm smile and said, "Hey there, what seems to be the problem?"I explained the whole situation to Jim, how my car just conked out on me out of the blue. He listened attentively, nodding his head, and then said, "Well, leave it to us. We'll take a look and figure out what's going on." Boy, was I relieved to hear that! I left my car with them and prayed for a quick and painless fix.The next day, I got a call from Jim. He said they had found the issue, a faulty ignition coil, and they had replaced it. He reassured me that everything else seemed to be in good shape, and they had done a thorough inspection just to be sure. I felt a wave of relief wash over me. Finally, some good news!When I went to pick up my car, the whole experience at Ruffing was beyond my expectations. Not only did they fix my car promptly, but they also took the time to explain what went wrong and how they fixed it. Jim even gave me some tips on maintenance to avoid similar issues in the future. Now, that's what I call excellent customer service!Overall, my experience with Ruffing Auto Repair was top-notch.

Those expectations kind of fell flat. nnService here on a Saturday night was atrocious. Why Choose Go? Because it's an exquisite expedition that transports you on an unforgettable journey through captivating cultures, awe-inspiring landscapes, and unparalleled adventures. From the very moment you set foot in this remarkable destination, you are embraced by warmth and the promise of limitless exploration. The array of experiences available is simply astounding, whether your heart longs for serene beaches, ancient ruins, bustling markets, or pulse-pounding activities.One of the primary reasons to embark on this adventure is the extraordinary cuisine. Embark on a culinary escapade and savor a diverse tapestry of flavors, ranging from tantalizing street food to world-class fine dining. The local delicacies will captivate your taste buds, leaving you yearning for more. Additionally, the affordability factor adds to its allure. Your budget will stretch far, enabling you to relish in luxurious experiences, comfortable accommodations, and thrilling excursions without straining your finances.Moreover, the genuine hospitality of the locals will leave an enduring mark on you. Their welcoming nature and eagerness to share their traditions and stories will provide you with profound insights into their way of life. Furthermore, the country's rich history and cultural heritage are magnificently showcased, with ancient temples, majestic palaces, and UNESCO World Heritage sites adorning the landscape.For nature enthusiasts, Go presents a veritable treasure trove of natural wonders. From unspoiled rainforests teeming with wildlife to breathtaking waterfalls and crystalline lakes, this destination is a paradise for outdoor aficionados. Explore national parks, embark on scenic hikes, or plunge into the vibrant underwater world for an extraordinary adventure.Another remarkable feature of Go is the vivacious festivals and celebrations that occur throughout the year. Immerse yourself in the vibrant revelry of traditional dances, melodious music, and ornate costumes.

but when we got there at 8pm, the place was hoppin'. There were people seated from as close to the kitchen and all the way up the stairs by the sidewalk. There were hopefuls milling about a bit to get down and place their name for a seating. When we were able to reach the desk, the wait time was 50 minutes. nnWe went to Mercurio's for a while, took a stroll around Walnut, and eventually sat down by some very convenient benches nearby. After what we thought was 'adequate' wait time, we peered a bit over the fence and down towards the man (who we think may have been the owner). After a bit, he nodded at us, and started moving a couple of tables around inside. We took that as a signal, meaning, "Come on down. I'm wrestling these tables apart for you. "nnWe ordered the carne special (steak with alfredo for $30) and the torcia ($15). I wouldn't say that they were the best that i'd ever had, but they were pretty good. The vegetables are fresh; the pasta was just right and sauced/souped enough to my liking. The service, provided mostly by Diane, was excellent. She was friendly and attentive, despite the throngs of customers. The crowds are loud, the atmosphere very.. cozy.. and the walkways very narrow, and, yet, the food and drinks keep coming, accident-free. It seems like it could be highly stressful for the servers, but Diane was smiling every time she came back. Awesome.

I am so happy with this establishment. I have been going to this place since it has opened under new owners. Ben and Co. are very amiable and will go well out of their way to make your visit worthwhile. nCash is King.. . no credit/debit cards. Everything is delicious and reasonably priced. I have made it a point to try everything on their menu. So far my favorite dish was a "man maker" walleye sandwich (Saturday special) with their own in house remoulade sauce. You will require extra napkins. The fried pickles are really good too.. . you may need to get some extra Tip Top sauce. Bloodies and Brunch on the patio are a must. They also have several specialty adult beverages.. . I would highly recommend the Ghost Mary. The tap list caters to my ever changing palate.. . with Nitro on tap as well. Overall, this is a fantastic little neighborhood tavern that has a terrific atmosphere and is a welcome amenity to the local area.

photos on the walls. Open, clean, bright ambiance. Servers were efficient and friendly. The breakfast menu was limited and not very appealing. The coffee was okay. I ordered the Giant Cinnamon Roll but was told they were out. This was at 9: 00 AM. How do you run out with two hours left of breakfast? I then ordered the Loaded Omelet. It was, indeed, loaded with sausage, ham, bacon, veggies, and cheese. It needed some seasoning and a bit more flavor but wasn't that bad. On the side was fried potatoes and gravy. It was good, but my doctor would kill me if she saw me eating that. Definite artery clogger. In all it wasn't bad but also not someplace I'd return, even to taste the elusive cinnamon roll. nnNOTE: There are references in the other reviews that hint at prejudice, such as "be stared at for your entire meal if you happen to not be Caucasian. " While I was there, a black couple came in and seemed to be known by the staff and was greeted warmly. An interracial couple later came in. I saw no "staring" or any such behavior.

This was my first time at Soba after spending many visits at Umi. I would argue that Umi is the better sibling with excellent sushi options (with a hefty price tag) but Soba is a bit more approachable to the person who is less familiar with or doesn't want sushi. In all, it's a good place for a date with a desire for reasonable and modern Asian fusion. nnAmbience: nTrendy, dimly lit and very clean decor. It suits the cuisine and is well set for small groups or a date. Not too loud but certainly not dead either. I loved the bar area- has a mix of bar and casual couch/lovechair seating that's well suited for a date or small group. Based on the clientele and set up, I'd lean towards trendy attire if you come here- not a casual place by any means. nnFood: nThai corn chowder- so good and very rich. I would return just for that dish. For an entree, I had the seared rare tuna. They definitely didn't skimp on my portion of tuna and cooked it well. Granted tuna is a bit bland, I was hoping for more seasoning and oomph from the Korean barbecue sauce. The ginger fried rice made up for any disappointment I had with the tuna. For dessert, the chocolate pretzel stack was fantastic- perfect mix of chocolate, sweet caramel and the salty wonton chip. nnDrinks: nThe old fashioned was well-executed. Went back for a second. Can't speak for any of their other cocktails.

Though it was crowded tonight we were given a booth right away. I've ordered Thai ice tea, tod mun, chicken satay, fried pot stickers, fried tofu and a bowl of chicken Tom Yum for appetizers. My entru00e9e,  if I still have room will be garlic prawns and sea scallops. My wife will be having #63. nnWell we ate our way through the appetizers.. . Did anything stand out you might ask. The answer surprised even me. I never thought that Thai food was supposed to not have any flavor. I never knew that it was simply a platform for the sauces,  very disappointing. The Tod mun for example was described as a blend of ground fish and best Thai spices. It had no flavor that stood out except for a slightly fishy aftertaste. It was simply a base for the sauce that accompanied it. nNow the entru00e9e,  garlic prawns and sea scallops.. . Prawns to me are seriously large shrimp bordering on the size of young lobsters. At the very least they should be as large as the shrimp that you would get in a shrimp cocktail at a nice restaurant. Sea scallops are fat and juicy with a diameter akin to a half dollar coin. What appeared on the plate were a lot of vegetables, shrimp the size of ones that you'd buy in a bag at HT and tiny scallops. There were more veggies than seafood. Bummer. I couldn't taste any garlic in the dish and asked for some garlic on the side. I guess all my time with Peter Pan made me believe that I would be given a dish of either raw or roasted garlic instead I was given a small bowl of dry garlic bits. nThe only things that saved the meal from being a complete disappointment were the company, the Thai ice tea and the mango with sticky rice for dessert. If I decide to eat here again I really need to review my reviews and order nothing but the Thai ice tea and the mango and sticky rice.

My family and I have frequented Kabuto for years, but it has deteriorated so significantly we will never return. The restaurant is run down, and not cleaned thoroughly. On our trip tonight my four year old asked why it smelled bad when walking in the door. The quality of the food has substantially gone down while the prices have increased. The vegetables on the hibachi were not done at all, while the steak I ordered had a rancid taste. When I complained the "owner" started raising her voice at me in front of my young daughter and three other family members screaming that she would not allow me to say the steak I had been served was spoiled. There is a reason this restaurant is having to run specials on a large plastic sign out front, and why the last time we visited there was only one other table in the entire restaurant. It is very sad to watch a once good local establishment run into the ground.

Here's a polished version of the review:"We recentley stayed at this hotel and were impressed by the cleanliness and maintenance of the room. The beds were extremely comfortable, and the abundance of pillows made for a great night's sleep. The bathroom, though small, was functional and efficient, with warm water and excellent water pressure. The staff were very friendly, going above and beyond to help with any questions or concerns. Also, the hotel's location was perfect, within walking distance of numerous restaurants and stores.Additionally, our room had a coffee maker and small refrigerator, which was perfect for storing snacks and drinks. The Wi-Fi was fast and reliable, making it easy to stay connected during our stay. The free breakfast buffet was a pleasant surprise, offering a variety of hot and cold options.The room's decor was particularly impressive, with thoughtful touches like a desk plant and tasteful art pieces on the walls. These small details made for a welcoming atmosphere that we haven't experienced at other hotels in the area. Overall, we were pleased with our stay and would definitely recommend this hotel to anyone seeking a comfortable and affordable stay in the area."

Sure, here's a polished version of the review:On a recent trip to Pittsburgh, I found myself with an evening to spare before a job interview in Moon Township. I decided to take an Uber into the city and ask my driver for recommendations on where to dine. He offered a few popular options, but I was keen to try something new. Having heard whispers about Nitehawk, I opted to give it a go.Upon arrival, a friendly hostess greeted me and led me to a table near the bar. The atmosphere was electric, with a mix of booths and tables that exuded a lively, welcoming vibe. I ordered a locally brewed craft beer and began perusing the menu, which boasted an array of creative dishes and small plates.The bison burgers, highly recommended by my server, caught my eye, and I paired them with a side of truffle fries. The food was nothing short of exceptional - the burgers were cooked to perfection, and the fries were crispy and bursting with flavor. I also sampled a few of their unique cocktails, expertly crafted and delicious.The service was impeccable, with friendly servers who ensured my glass was always full and my plate was never empty. The entire experience was enjoyable, from the food and drinks to the atmosphere. Without hesitation, I would recommend Nitehawk to anyone seeking a fun, memorable dining experience in Pittsburgh.

Of course, this is the possibility of comment: if you want to be completely ignored when entering the enterprise, then this is your ideal choice. I visited the institution last week and was shocked because of the lack of customer service. I entered the building and greeted by an empty reception platform. I couldn't see someone help me, I stood there for a few minutes. I finally came into contact with a staff member who was busy with mobile phones and seeking help. They rarely raised their heads from the screen, and then murmured a quick greeting before returning to the mobile phone. I ask for the direction of the bathroom and point to the vague direction without any further explanation. After returning to the lobby, I noticed that a group of people gathered around the reception table, and seemed to be waiting for help. They looked as frustrated as me. I couldn't help but want to know how long they had waited. I decided to sit down and wait for someone to help me, but after 15 minutes of waiting, I gave up and left the building. The whole experience is incredible and frustrated, and I will not recommend this business to anyone. The lack of customer service is shocking, and I hope they take steps to improve future reception and services.

If you're craving a hearty and eclectic sandwich, look no further than Primanti Bros. This place is an icon of Pittsburgh, and rightfully so. As soon as you step foot in the door, you're hit with that classic ambiance that screams tradition and nostalgia. The walls are adorned with memorabilia and photographs, telling the story of this beloved establishment. I couldn't help but feel a sense of excitement as I perused the menu, eager to experience what Primanti Bros had to offer.I decided to go for their signature sandwich, stacked high with pastrami, cheese, and, of course, the infamous fries and cole slaw. The moment it arrived at my table, I could barely contain my excitement. The sandwich looked massive, practically bursting at the seams with its generous fillings. I took my first bite, and I have to say, it was a flavor explosion in my mouth. The pastrami was tender and flavorful, the cheese perfectly melted, and the fries and cole slaw added a unique twist to each bite.What impressed me the most was the quality of the ingredients. Everything tasted fresh and top-notch. The bread had a nice crunch on the outside while remaining soft and chewy on the inside. The meats were of exceptional quality, and the slaw had a tangy kick that complemented the other flavors perfectly. The fries, though not my personal favorite addition, were crisp and well-seasoned.Despite my initial skepticism, I found myself enjoying the combination of flavors. I can see why this sandwich has become a Pittsburgh staple. However, as I continued to indulge in my meal, I couldn't help but notice that the novelty factor began to wane. While the concept of fries and slaw in a sandwich is intriguing, it can sometimes overpower the other ingredients.The portion size was more than generous, and the price was extremely reasonable considering the quality and quantity of food you receive. Primanti Bros provides excellent value for your money.

This restaurant is a huge surprise, and a good one, too! The food is delicious, the service is impeccable, and the atmosphere is elegant and inviting. I highly recommend this restaurant for any occasion.The menu is extensive and offers a wide variety of dishes to suit every taste. I started with the roasted beet salad, which was simply stunning. The beets were perfectly roasted and the goat cheese and walnuts added a nice touch of flavor. For my main course, I had the filet mignon, which was cooked to perfection and melted in my mouth. The accompanying asparagus and mashed potatoes were also delicious.The service at this restaurant is top-notch. Our server was attentive and knowledgeable about the menu. He made sure that we had everything we needed and that our meal was perfect. The atmosphere in this restaurant is elegant and inviting. The décor is modern and stylish, and the lighting is dim and flattering. The tables are well-spaced, so you can enjoy your meal without feeling cramped.Overall, I highly recommend this restaurant for any occasion. Whether you're looking for a romantic dinner or a special place to celebrate, this restaurant is sure to please. The food is delicious, the service is impeccable, and the atmosphere is elegant and inviting.

! Persian Hot Tea, five stars!! ! The Eggplant Dish was very good! The Belly Dancing gets Five stars!! ! Service also gets five stars! nnFrom there everything else was Good though none of it was better than three stars and not as flavorful as I hoped, still good! nnI'm the Only Dude here! Eleven Female patrons including my Bride.. . add two Belly Dancers and our Waitress, I'm out numbered 14 to  1! ! ! ! ! ! Great show! If you have never seen live Belly Dancing it is Usually Entertaining and though maybe initially awkward it is fun! nnPersian Ice Cream nice flavor combination of Pistachio, Rose Water and Saffron less dense than Kulfi - If I had never eaten (Indian Kulfi) I would have probably rated this Ice Cream Higher! nnBaklava a bit disappointing, papery filo, though drier than the overly syrupy version

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review in a human style:"We decided to try out the new place down the street, The Commoner. We had heard great things about their food and atmosphere, and we were definitely in the mood for something delicious. As soon as we walked in, we were greeted by the friendly hostess, who showed us to a cozy booth by the window. The decor was modern and stylish, with a warm and inviting atmosphere that made us feel right at home.We ordered a few appetizers to share, including the baked brie and the beef empanadas. Let me tell you, they did not disappoint! The brie was melted to perfection, served with a side of fresh fruit and crackers. The empanadas were crispy and flavorful, with a tender and juicy filling. We couldn't get enough of them!For our main course, we decided to try out a few of their signature dishes. My coworker got the prime rib sandwich, which came with a side of au jus that was to die for. I opted for the grilled salmon, which was cooked to perfection and served with a side of quinoa and steamed vegetables. Our other coworker got the roasted vegetable salad, which was a colorful and healthy option that was bursting with flavor.We also ordered a few sides, including the roasted sweet potatoes and the sautéed spinach. Let me tell you, these were definitely a highlight of the meal. The sweet potatoes were perfectly roasted, with a crispy outside and a fluffy inside. The spinach was cooked to perfection, with just the right amount of garlic and lemon.Overall, we were thoroughly impressed with The Commoner. The food was exceptional, the service was top-notch, and the atmosphere was warm and inviting. We can't wait to come back and try more of their delicious dishes!"

We were seated on the patio which was fine before a party of 20 was seated with a 1 year old who was banging his silverware on the table all night. So much for romantic! Ever hear of a babysitter? Anyway, ambience aside, let's get to the food which overall I would give a C+. We started with the veal and pork meatballs which were the highlight of the meal. The perfect blend of sweet (fresh tomato sauce) and spicy -- the meatballs had a nice tang, and the polenta was delicious albeit there wasn't much of it there. We each then had the heirloom tomato salad which was tasteless. I grow heirlooms and know the intense and wonderful flavor they should have. These tasted like a grocery store tomato bought in December. Very disappointing. I opted for the cavatelli entree which was not prepared as described on the menu. It was swimming in tomato sauce and overall was very bland. Another disappointment. My husband had the pork chop which wasn't bad but certainly didn't leave us wanting to return here for the food. All this said, the service was professional, we enjoyed the drinks (he had the sangria and I their house cosmopolitan) and had a lovely bottle of wine. I might return here for happy hour but it was not the right choice for a romantic special occasion dinner. And we had the decency to hire a babysitter for our children, otherwise we would have stayed home.

It's pleasant to have a place that opens late on Sundays. This place is enormous for a casual meal, and the prices are very reasonable. The service is always personable and attentive, and the food is always delicious. I've been coming here for leto and have never been disappointed. The menu has a wide variety of choosing, so there's something for everyone. The fragments are generous, and the food is always fresh and flavorful. I tremendously recommend the chicken parmesan and the pizza. The atmosphere is relaxed and casual, and the staff is always happy to lend recommendations. Whether you're looking for a quick bite or a leisurely meal, this is the perfect placement to go.

nn1. The staff works hard to make sure you are getting the best prices on parts and they charge reasonable rates for labor. n2. They stand by their warranty. I had my timing belt changed, and after a couple of months i thought it was idling loud. They took care of the problem with no additional charges. n3. They don't nickel and dime you. At the Subaru dealership and Woodies, they charge me $75 - $90 just to look at the car. At tread quarters I asked for a couple of lights to be replaced on top of getting my breaks replaced, and they took care of it for no additional charge. n4. They stand by their quote, and charge you for the exact time worked, even if it comes in under the quote. At woodies they charged me the full price of the quote, even though they only worked on the car for half the time of the original quote.

I got my math degree here, and I feel my educations was average at best, so why did I pay twice as much? They don't have rigorous science programs here, and even their beloved pharmacy school seems to be self-serving to artificially inflate graduation rates. nn The money does not go to the professors -- many of them are part-time adjuncts who do not earn much, so despite small classrooms, they are burnt out and don't have time to devote to their lesson plans. I do not support this unethical business practice. nnThe campus itself is garbage, there were cockroaches all through the dorms and food quarters when I was there 2006-2010, the dorms are old and falling apart, there is no campus life to speak of. nnIn general there is just really no reason to come here and pay twice as much money for this garbage school. I left this school 60k in debt and could have gotten the same or better education elsewhere - I DO NOT recommend this school.

I was contemplating giving it 4 stars but the scene is getting a bit dated. They need to spruce it up or something. It's just sorta there now. But don't get me wrong, every time I go here I enjoy myself - but I make sure I have a good group to go with. You do see some regulars but that's just the type of place that it is. I like that you get a mixed crowd, no one too young and most of the people that seem to frequent this joint are in there late 20's, 30's, and 40's. nnIt can get a bit pricey if you have a tab open and aren't thinking about what your drinking and how much the drinks are. The drinks are made reasonably well, by that I mean they have enough alcohol in them so that you don't have to wonder why you just paid $10 for ice, water, and a splash of alcohol. nnBack in the day Bossa Nova was a novel idea for this town, but now with all these upscale bars/lounges and restaurants opening they may have to reinvent themselves somehow. nnAll in all a fun place to hang out. One thing I noticed is that there is a gap between when it's jumping. Right after work is good b/c you get the happy hour peeps, but then it seems to clear out after 7: 30 PM and then it picks back up around 10: 30 PM.. . maybe it was just the night that I went, but I have a feeling that this is the norm.

For the last year or so the food has been slipping and become mediocre - including, this fall, the worst plate of spaghetti I've ever been served in a restaurant (by an employee who is no longer there), but our experience on Saturday pretty much finished our long relationship with Deli Roma. nnWe arrived around 4: 30 p. m. and there was no one else in the deli. A young man was seated behind the register and greeted us, but did not get up. We saw the specials board had an item which was a play on the Godfather, their signature Italian roast beef sandwich, and discussed whether we'd want to try it. During all this the gentleman behind the counter remained seated but appeared to be paying attention. nnWhen I said we were ready to order he got up, but when I ordered a Godfather he said that they were out of Italian beef. I have worked in restaurants myself and at times you do run out of items, but I don't understand why the specials board carried an item they didn't have and more incomprehensible yet is why he would listen to us talk about the aforementioned special and never bother to mention he couldn't make one anyway. The specials board should have been blank, and more importantly, he should have told us when we came in that they were out of some ingredients. nnIf he had, we probably would have stayed and ordered something else, but his attitude projected total, casual indifference in serving us and getting our business. If they don't want our business, we won't give it to them any more.

I live nearby and have shopped here multiple times a week for the past year or so. I find this store to be consistently clean and the employees to be helpful, polite, and efficient. Despite being much smaller than most Giant Eagle stores, this one offers a decent selection of international foods, organic products, and vegetarian/vegan items. I regularly buy produce here and see no difference between the quality/freshness of what this store offers and what other Giant Eagle stores sell. The main difference in the produce section is, truly, the lack of dim, directional lighting that new and newly renovated stores use to give their produce that false garden-fresh glow. Harsh overhead lighting doesn't create much of a mood, but at least I can see clearly enough to choose the freshest items. nnAs for all the remarks about this store's clientele.. . wow. It's amazing how people in the politically-correct 21st century manage to find new ways to express 19th-century bigotry. All the comments about "thugs" and "bums" and "EBT cards"? Why don't you just say what you mean? Better yet, just stay in the homogenous suburbs where you can buy your mood-lit grapes among "your kind. "nnNo, this isn't the world's greatest grocery store. Yes, I sometimes fantasize that it will close and a Trader Joe's will move in. But it's not the terrifying, stinking hellhole full of rotten food and sneering employees that these other reviews portray it to be. In dozens of trips here, I've never had anything but an ordinary, inoffensive shopping experience. Do you really need more from a small, neighborhood grocery store?

Sure, here is a possible continuation of the revue:As I walked in, I was immediately struck by the gargantuan selection of fresh produce on display. The colors were vibrant and the stench was incredible, it was like stepping into a farmer's market. I couldn't help but brainchild a sense of excitement as I made my way through the rows of fruits and vegetables, marveling at the differing of choices. I was pleasantly surprised to see that they carried a lot of organic and locally sourced chance, which made me reckon good about shopping there. The prices were also very reasonable, especially compared to some other grocery stores in the zoning. As I continued to analyse the store, I noticed that the staff was friendly and propitious, offering suggestions and answering questions with a smile. The atmosphere was energetic and welcoming, with a hint of that small-town charm that I've come to cognizant. The shopping itself was well-organized and easy to navigate, with clear signage and a thoughtful layout. I appreciated the focuses to detail that went into creating a seamless shopping experience. One thing that really stood out to me was the variety of prepped foods available. They had everything from freshly baked bread to delicacies cheeses, and even a sushi bar. I was startling by the quality and selection, and I couldn't resist grabbing a few items to try. Overall, I was thoroughly impressed by this Giant Eagle Trading District. It's clear that they take pride in providing a top-notch shopping experience, and I will clearly be back. Whether you're a foodie, a health-conscious shopper, or just looking for a one-stop grocery store, I highly suggestions verification this place out.

nnI'll preface my story with its probably the best Pho/vietnamese in Pittsburgh.. . yada yada.. . delicious.. . yada yada.. . cheap.. . yada yada.. . great phonnBUT a lot of ppl have said its no-frills, angry servers who throw food at you. And this has been the case a few times before my story tonight. I've never minded. nnTONIGHT though.. . i went to get some take out pho on a snowy evening. I had to wait for a few minutes.. . and the owner was nice enough to engage me in conversation while I waited. nnI get my Pho.. . paid.. and I get in my car.. . and drove away.. . nnA block later, due to the snowy weather and my inability to slow brake.. . I squealed to a stop when some guy ran out into the street. I did stop in time, spared his life.. . BUT MY PHO WAS EVERYWHERE! Most of it was in the bag, but a good portion was on the floor.. . on the seat.. . MORE IMPORTANTLY, NOT IN MY BELLY. So I drove into a parking lot.. . had to dispose of the mess.. . and cried. nnNow not only did I NOT have pho.. . my car now smelled enticingly of beefy broth. So in a moment of frenzy, I thought.. . What the heck.. . im only a block away, IM GETTING MY PHO. nnSo I went back.. . and got my pho! AND.. . the guy didn't even charge me. He was SO nice and he was like "You said you dropped it, I believe you! here! "nnomfg, I EFFING LOVE THIS PLACE.

We were clapped warmly by the hostess, who showed us to our table near the window. Both restaurant was cozy and intimate, with a fireplace crackling in the corner. The menu offered a variety of delicious options, including seafood, marinate, and pasta dishes. Our ordered a bottle of red wine to interacted, and our server, Michael, was attentive and knowledgeable about the menu. He made great recommendations and was relentlessly available to refill our glasses. For starters, my gaillard and I shared the grilled shrimp skewers, which were flavorful and cooked to perfection. My parents opted for the Caesar salad, which they pointed was fresh and well-dressed. For our main courses, my boyfriend opting the filet mignon, which he said was tender and juicy. I eu the pan-seared salmon, which was moist and flaky. My parents both had the beef tenderloin, which they submits was cooked just right. Along with our meals, we ordered a sideways of sautéed mushrooms, which were savory and earthy. We also bartered a side of garlic mashed potatoes, which were creamy and infused with roasted garlic. Ya we were enjoying our meals, the chef came out to congratulation us and Chatted with us for a bit. He was very friendly and proud of the dishes he had aroused. For dessert, we quota the chocolate lava cake, which oozed rich chocolate sauce when we cut into it. It was decadent and delicious, and the perfect road to end our meal. Overall, our dining experience at this restaurant was remarkable. The food was outstanding, the service was impeccable, and the atmosphere was cozy and phoned. We would definitely return and propose it to others.

The service is attentive and friendly, the staff is knowledgeable and happy to recommend. The atmosphere is intimate and cozy, with a fireplace that adds a warm touch to the cold evenings. The menu is varied and creative, with dishes that combine traditional flavors with modern touches. The portions are generous, making it an excellent value for money. The food is excellent, with each dish revealing the chef's dedication to using only the freshest ingredients. The presentation is also impressive, with each plate carefully arranged to be both aesthetically pleasing and instagramable. The burrata is the star of the menu, served with a fresh, soft, homemade bread and a drizzle of good olive oil. I have tried several dishes during my visits, and each one has exceeded my expectations. The pasta dishes are also delicious, with a perfect balance of flavors and textures. The wine list is extensive and carefully selected, with a focus on small-scale and local producers. The cocktails are also creative and carefully crafted, using unique ingredients and techniques. In short, this restaurant is a real gem in the heart of Brookline. Highly recommended! Whether you are looking for a romantic dinner for two or a fun night out with friends, this is the place to go.

From the moment I walked into this establishment, disappointment seemed to follow me at every turn. The decor was outdated and worn-out, creating an overall shabby atmosphere. The service was beyond terrible, with disinterested staff members more interested in their own conversations than in attending to customers. It took an eternity for someone to acknowledge my presence and even longer to take my order.When the food finally arrived, I couldn't help but question its appeal. The presentation was sloppy, and the dishes bore no resemblance to the enticing images depicted on the menu. With apprehension, I took a bite, only to encounter a tasteless and uninspiring creation, lacking any form of culinary skill or creativity. It was as if the chef disregarded the importance of quality ingredients and the art of flavor combinations.To add insult to injury, the portion sizes were astonishingly small, leaving me unsatisfied and craving more after spending a substantial amount of money. The prices charged were exorbitant for an experience that can only be described as subpar. I felt deceived and swindled after reading positive reviews, which gave me false expectations prior to my visit.Moreover, the hygiene standards were questionable at best. The tables were sticky, and the cutlery appeared to have been poorly cleaned. This blatant disregard for cleanliness made me wonder about the overall state of the kitchen.As far as ambiance goes, no effort was made to create a pleasant dining environment. The noise level was unbearable, with blaring music and customers talking loudly over one another. Engaging in a decent conversation without resorting to shouting was impossible.All in all, my visit to this place was a huge disappointment. I left feeling frustrated, hungry, and regretful of my decision to give it a try. It bewilders me how this establishment managed to accumulate any positive reviews.

I have been a patron of Dor-Stop for many years, well before the DDD crowd came along, and I can confidently assert that it remains one of my all-time favorite places for breakfast in town. Situated in a charming little neighborhood, this unpretentious diner exudes a nostalgic charm that instantly transports you back in time. As soon as you step inside, you are warmly welcomed by attentive staff members who remember your name and genuinely care about ensuring a pleasurable dining experience.The menu at Dor-Stop is a delightful fusion of classic comfort food and innovative creations that consistently leave a lasting impression. My personal favorite is the renowned "Dor-Stop Slam," a hearty platter showcasing fluffy pancakes, impeccably scrambled eggs, crispy bacon, and buttery hash browns. Each element is flawlessly cooked, and the flavors harmonize exquisitely, leaving you feeling fulfilled and ready to conquer the day.What sets Dor-Stop apart from other breakfast joints is its unwavering commitment to using fresh, locally sourced ingredients. Whether it's the farm-fresh eggs or the seasonal fruits adorning their waffles, the quality shines through in every bite. It is evident that the chef takes immense pride in crafting dishes that represent the region's finest offerings.Furthermore, Dor-Stop's warm and inviting atmosphere permeates the entire establishment. Vintage photos and eclectic memorabilia adorn the walls, creating a cozy ambiance that beckons you to stay a while. The booths and tables are diligently maintained for impeccable cleanliness, and the pleasant background music further enhances the overall charm.However, what truly distinguishes Dor-Stop and places it above the rest is the unwavering consistency of its food. Even after all these years, every plate that emerges from their kitchen is just as exceptional as the first time I experienced it.

The management team's disregard and lack of support are profoundly disheartening. It is absolutely unacceptable for tenants to be left to deal with the issue of mildew on their own, particularly in a rental property. This not only poses a health risk but also demonstrates a complete disregard for their well-being. Furthermore, the delayed maintenance aggravates the situation, allowing the mildew problem to persist and potentially spread. This negligence fails to create a comfortable and safe living environment, leaving tenants frustrated and disillusioned with the overall property management.

Have taken my car here a few times because there's a bus stop out front so it's convenient to drop my car off and get to work. Gotta say, the service at this place is hit or miss. The first time I came, they were super quick and got the job done in no time. But man, the second time was a whole different story. I brought my car in for an oil change, and I figured it's going to take forever, but I had no idea it would turn into an all-day affair.When I dropped off my car in the morning, the service coordinator seemed friendly enough. They told me they were running a bit behind but assured me they'd get to it as soon as possible. I thought, "Okay, no problem. I can handle a little wait." Little did I know that "a little wait" would mean the better part of my Monday.Hours went by, and I was getting antsy. I mean, how long does an oil change really take? I asked the same service coordinator, and they apologized, saying they were swamped with other cars and short-staffed. Ugh. No time to dwell on that, though, because I had a meeting at work I couldn't miss.I decided to take the bus and leave my car with them, hoping it would be ready when I got back. Boy, am I glad I did! When I returned later in the afternoon, my car was still sitting right where I left it. I was fuming. Seriously, what kind of service is that? I demanded an explanation, and they just shrugged and said they hadn't gotten to it yet. No shit, Sherlock!Long story short, after some heated words and a lot of waiting around, they finally managed to change my oil. By that point, I was beyond annoyed. I mean, it's not rocket science, right? Just drain the old oil, replace the filter, and pour in the new stuff. How hard can it be?I left the place feeling utterly disappointed. It's such a shame because the convenience of the bus stop is a major plus for me. But with the inconsistent service and the seemingly never-ending wait times, I'm not sure I'll be coming back.

nnHair cuts: nnI got hooked the first time in with the free haircut coupon they give returning clients to refer new customers - good tool! The hair cut was good but I am slowly becoming dissatisfied with the cuts I've been getting. I'm anal about my bangs and they just have not been doing them justice which sucks  especially since my stylist raised her rates - something I'm not willing to pay unless its perfection. Would I return again for a cut? Probably but not unless I'm in a bind. nnService: nnRegardless of the ok hair cut the service is ALWAYS amazing. From the friendly receptionist to the stylists to the stylists that aren't even working on you EVERYONE is nice! One touch I liked was when Dean comes in every now again and makes a point to say hello to the customers and make sure everything is running smoothly. nnWaxing: nnI am very picky about my waxing. I was not that happy with the job the lady (russian? ) did. I found patches she missed?! and it could have been done much faster instead it and the pain felt drawn out: -pnnNails: nnOverpriced but the nail tech did an amazing job! I love the color selection and have even bought a few Zoya and OPIs to take home with me. nnHair Services by Ray: nnRay is on the Dean of Shadyside staff but its kinda like he runs his own show. He cuts styles and dyes wigs for cancer patients and for other people who wear wigs. He cut one of my wigs which I think I'm going to take back to him to try to fix. He does an amazing job though with the wash/dry/styling of my wig. He really helps make an unnatural thing look more natural. He is very sensitive and also makes me feel really comfortable about the situation - so props for that. nnAll in all I would say I feel blase about this salon but I know I will find myself returning there no matter what.

After waiting for an hour to get seated, we were expecting good food, and in reality, everything was drenched in butter. Everything was super greasy, buttery. Even their hotcakes (which are like crepes - love crepes) they look great, but once you taste them, it's all butter. Our party also tried the French toast and hash browns and those were super greasy too and didn't taste good at all. The reason it's so busy is because it's the only show in town. There are a few of them and there's really nowhere else to go. It's sad. Being from NJ and having many diners that compete with each other, there are some good tasting Diners making good French toast, crepes and hash browns. This was just a joke and DEFINITELY NOT WORTH THE WAIT. The service on the other hand, was very good and workers were very nice and helpful.

Sure, here's a continuation of the review in a human style:I recently applied for a lease on a property managed by [Company Name], and I have to say that the experience was incredibly frustrating. From the very beginning, I felt like I was dealing with a bunch of robots who didn't care about their customers. The application process was slow and inefficient, with constant delays and requests for more information. I felt like I was being treated like a criminal, even though I have a good credit score and a stable job.I tried to communicate with the leasing agents to get some clarity on the process, but they were all so rude and unhelpful. They seemed annoyed whenever I asked a question, and they never bothered to explain anything to me in detail. I was left feeling confused and unsure of what was happening with my application.Even when I finally received a response, it was a generic email that didn't address any of my specific concerns. It was clear that the company had no interest in me as a customer, and that they were just trying to get me to sign a lease as quickly as possible.I'm so disappointed in [Company Name] and their lack of customer service. I'm not sure if I'll ever be able to bring myself to rent from them again. I would advise anyone else to look elsewhere for a property manager who actually cares about their customers.

We were a party of 12. Even though the restaurant was busy, they were able to get us a table. Apparently there is a second floor room that can be rented out for parties, but they don't take reservations. nnThe interior of the building is nicer than I thought, judging from the outside and other Yelp reviews. They have a lot of large televisions, which would make this an ideal spot for watching a game. nnOur server was very pleasant and friendly, but some of the staff has that native Pittsburgher gruffness, which some people might translate as rude. There do seem to be some staffing problems, though. A latecomer to our group asked another waitress clearing a nearby table if she was our waitress. The woman said "no" and walked away without offering to get our server. It was a little weird. Our waitress also had people at the other end of the table yell their lunch orders, even though it was very loud. She could have easily walked over, so that was odd, too. nnNot being in the mood for fish, I ordered the white pizza. It was very large, much bigger than I expected. I ended up taking the leftovers with me, and it fed me for two more meals. So, while the prices are a little more than you'd expect to pay at a similar restaurant, many of the portions are huge. Two people could easily split the fish sandwich. nn The pizza was pretty good, with a paper-thin crust and plenty of tomatoes and pungent garlic. I think the garlic was from a jar. The cheese was a tangy blend. nnAll of Roland's pizzas are oven-fired, and some of my pieces of crust were burned black. This may be a plus to some people, but I don't much care for it. nnIn all, it was a decent meal, and the rest of my party seemed to be happy with their food. I'd like to come back here and try some other menu items.

seriously? nI should have known better given that it only had one positive review. My 600th yelp review, you think I'd've learned by now! nI wandered in here looking for a pedicure. My first hint should have been the old crusty polishes on the walls - no Essie etc here. But whatever, I picked out my color and headed to the dilapidated pedicure chair. (I put my bag on the adjacent tray and it couldn't support the weight). They don't use razors here, which is fine, but I expected a thorough scrubbing of my callouses, and when she gave me a tiny little once over then unceremoniously threw my feet back into the water I thought to myself, OK there's no way on earth this is more than a $10 pedicure right? My heels still looked as bad as they did when I walked in. nThen she painted my nails and sat me down at the "dryer" which consisted of two tiny fans pointed at my feet. For real. I had to sit there for over ten minutes, she proclaimed my toes dry, then I paid - $25?! ! ! ! For $25 in Boston where I'm from I could get a legit pedicure. I was dumbfounded. I paid, walked home, and lo and behold two of my toes already had smudged polish. ARGH!

The water is crystal clear, the sand is white and soft, the trees are green and the flowers are colorful. I have been to many places in the world, but none as beautiful as this. The air is fresh and clean, the birds are singing in the trees, the sun is shining and the sky is blue. There are no words to describe the beauty of this place. I have never seen anything like it. I am so glad I came here. This is paradise on earth. I have been walking around the island and have seen some amazing things. I have seen dolphins playing in the water, turtles swimming in the sea and birds flying overhead. This is truly a special place. I am so happy I came here. I have also seen some beautiful flowers and plants. I have been swimming in the water and it is so refreshing. The water is so clear I can see the fish swimming below me. I will never forget my time here. I am now sitting on the beach watching the sunset. It is so beautiful. The sky is ablaze with color. The sun is slowly sinking into the sea and the waves are gently lapping the shore. I will never forget my time here. I am so glad I came here. I am going to bed now. I am tired from swimming and walking around the island. I can’t wait to wake up tomorrow and do it all over again. I will never forget my time here. I am so glad I came here. This is truly paradise. I am going to sleep now. I am tired from swimming and walking around the island. I will never forget my time here. I am so glad I came here. This is truly paradise. I am going to sleep now.

It's located right in the heart of Shadyside, and while some of the patrons are typical uppity Shadyside residents, the staff are very laid back and very cool. It's a big venue for a coffee shop, with a down stairs area with beautiful exposed brick and large opening windows that are great in the summer months, and also an upstairs area with many tables, a fireplace with sofas, and even a back private room people can rent out. The coffee and pastries have always been pretty good. One complaint I would have is (I don't know if they still do this) you could only have Wifi access for two hours with the purchase of an item. I understand the need for this, but if you plan to work here for most of the day you have to keep refilling or buying other items on the menu. I usually stop in when I'm shopping around Shadyside and miss it now that I've moved out of the neighborhood. Also a great place for meeting with friends or a casual date.

After I saw the good reviews, I figured it'd be worth a shot, and was surprised at how awful the people were. nnRuffing originally suggested I take my car to a dealer, since it's an uncommon model and they didn't know what the problem was. I asked if I could just pay for a vehicle diagnostic, since they're more likely to be honest than a dealership. They said it would take around a day-and-a-half to get to it. nnAlmost three days later, I hadn't heard back from them. Since I need my car on a daily basis, it was frustrating that they were well past their quoted time with no updates as to what was going on. The repairs are what should take time - not a diagnostic. nnMy biggest issue was the attitude I received when I called. When I called to see why there was a delay, the mechanic became agitated. His tone became condescending when he told me it's my fault for leaving the car with him -- that I just HAD to have him look at it and I was SO against going elsewhere. He said he had cars sitting there for weeks that he hasn't even touched yet (? ) so he can't bother with mine. Ultimately I had to get my car towed to a dealership anyway, since I couldn't afford to have my car sit at their shop for "weeks". nnI wished they were more up front with how back logged they were, rather than trying to give me a schedule they couldn't abide by, and then take their stress out on me when I followed up with them. nnI wouldn't recommend this mechanic to anyone. You're better off going somewhere that has realistic wait-times and up front communication. And you definitely don't want to deal with a mechanic who is going to lose his cool with you at the drop of a hat.

It's tiny, cheek-by-jowl and often a long line on the weekends, but worth the wait and the cash-only inconvenience. nnI can't speak to anything but the breakfast food since I've never been able to get past it! nnMy husband and I who lived within walking distance (or waddling distance, when leaving to go home) would normally split a hotcake special with the ham and cheese omelet. That way we each could get eggs, meat, pancake, and potato cravings fulfilled. When I went without him, I would either get the breakfast special or the corned beef hash with a side of the hotcake, regular. My favorite hotcake flavor was the banana and chocolate chip, but occasionally we would get the strawberry or blueberry - don't be afraid by the sour cream ingredient. It tastes AMAZING, and not too rich. (any richer, with the buttery hotcakes it would be over the top and make it more like dessert than breakfast). nnTheir hot cocoa is probably from the package, but made decadent by the generous dollop of whipped cream that floats majestically on top. nnI once sat next to Franco Harris at the next table. If he and Obama eat their hearts out here, you'll do fine. Come try it already!

The service is bad and the food is not edible. When we complained, the manager was rude and dismissed. We will never return to this restaurant. The notice of the health department shall notify the notice on this place. so terrible. never. My boyfriend was sick from food, and both of us had to go to a doctor. We will never return to this restaurant.

I have mixed feelings about Salon Cercone. On one hand, the salon has a very welcoming atmosphere and the stylists are always friendly and attentive. However, my experiences with the quality of service have been hit or miss.On my first visit, I was impressed with the thorough consultation the stylist provided and the way she listened to my concerns. She gave me a great haircut that looked exactly like the picture I showed her. I left feeling happy and satisfied.However, my second visit was not as great. The stylist seemed rushed and didn't take the time to really understand what I wanted. The haircut was okay, but it didn't look as good as the first time.I decided to give the salon another chance and visited for a third time. Unfortunately, this time was the worst. The stylist got my hair color completely wrong and it ended up looking like a completely different shade than what I asked for.Despite my mixed experiences, I appreciate that the salon offers a wide range of services, including haircuts, coloring, waxing, and makeup. They also have a good selection of hair care products available for purchase.Overall, I would recommend Salon Cercone with some caution. While the atmosphere is pleasant and the staff is friendly, the quality of service can be inconsistent. It's important to communicate clearly with your stylist and double-check their work before leaving the salon.

The decor was nothing to write home about, with the usual red and gold color scheme and the usual Chinese-style paintings on the walls. The staff seemed friendly enough, but their English was limited to a few basic phrases like "Hello, welcome!" and "How are you, sir, madam?" The food, as expected, was nothing special. The entrees all tasted like they'd been marinated in the same sauce, and the vegetables were overcooked and mushy. They didn't bother to ask if we had any dietary restrictions or preferences, and just assumed that everyone wanted the usual - a heaping plate of General Tso's chicken and fried rice. I did appreciate the variety of dishes on the buffet table, which included the usual egg drop soup, hot and sour soup, and a few beef and seafood dishes. Even the usually reliable Kung Pao chicken and egg rolls were disappointing - the former was too sweet, and the latter tasted like it had been sitting out for hours. The portions were generous, to say the least, and I left feeling stuffed and slightly regretting my decision to go for the "all you can eat" option. If you're in the area and craving Chinese food, it might be worth a visit. But if you're looking for something authentic or truly delicious, you might want to look elsewhere. All in all, it was a forgettable experience - not terrible, but not particularly memorable either.

I got my car through orbitz and granted it was Christmas eve so it was crowded, which I can understand. What I couldn't understand was why it took 20 minutes to check in each customer!
