We enjoyed that our server wasn't overly attentive, but she was there whenever we needed to order and checked up on us only when necessary. I tried the blackened catfish and my partner had the honey chicken. Both were delicious. Mine (accompanied by spicy black beans) had just the right amount of cajun flavor. And the portion sizes were just right -- not enough to overfill you, but not enough to leave you hungry when you left. nnIt was definitely an enjoyable experience, and I look forward to returning to this new (to me). The only disappoint is that we weren't able to see the famous pole that they talked about on the back of the menu. It's located in the women's restroom and apparently it's caused quite a few bruises. Funny!

In all the times I've been there, it's never once been crowded. It's very noirish, even in the daytime; the sort of place you'd go to watch horse races -- desperately, of course, on which you'd just bet it all. And the guy at the other end of the bar hasn't been home in years, and the ominous bartender has a Dark Secret, and you'd better keep your damn beak out of it! nnExcept the bartenders here are actually quite warm, and not ominous at all. In fact, they're more than willing to learn your preferred arcane beverage recipes. One guy there, Frank, actually keeps a little book of new recipes he learns from customers, which I appreciated quite a bit. I also really enjoy the appetizers here, especially the flatbreads and the bruschetta, which are probably the least noirish foods imaginable. nnSo, ignore your instincts about this spooky-seeming place and check it out. The entrees are not terribly impressive, but it's a great stop for an evening with other plans in it.

Wow, what can I say about this incredible restaurant? From the moment I walked in, I knew I was in for a treat. The decor was sleek and modern, with a warm and inviting atmosphere that made me feel right at home. The service was top-notch, with friendly and attentive staff who knew their stuff when it came to the menu.But let's talk about the food - oh my goodness, the food! It was like a culinary dream come true. I ordered the special of the day, which was a mouthwatering dish that I had never tried before. The flavors were bold and exciting, with a perfect balance of textures and presentation. It was truly a work of art.I also tried a few of the appetizers, which were equally impressive. The portions were generous, and the prices were very reasonable considering the quality of the food. The wine list was also extensive and reasonably priced, with a great selection of options to pair with my meal.Overall, I was thoroughly impressed with this restaurant and would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a fantastic dining experience. The attention to detail, the superb service, and the outstanding food all combined to create a truly unforgettable evening. I can't wait to go back and try more of their delicious dishes!

But now, after all these years, I can confidently say that Casbah deserves even more stars. When I walked in, I was immediately struck by the warm and inviting atmosphere. The restaurant has managed to maintain its charm and quality despite the passage of time. The staff greeted us with sincere smiles and seated us immediately, making us feel valued and welcome. The dim lighting, the exotic decor and the soft Arabic music created a captivating atmosphere that transported me to another world. The menu at Casbah has expanded since my last visit, offering a wide range of mouth-watering dishes. I chose the lamb tagine, which was a medley of tender meat, simmered in a rich blend of spices and served with fluffy couscous. Every bite was a burst of flavor that left me wanting more. The food was served promptly and presented with artistic flair. The service was impeccable throughout the evening. Our server was knowledgeable, attentive and went out of his way to accommodate our requests. The extensive wine list offered a wonderful selection to complement our meal. We enjoyed a bottle of velvety red wine that perfectly complemented the robust flavors of our dishes. It was obvious that the kitchen staff took great pride in their work, as each dish was beautifully plated and reflected the culinary skills of the chefs. What sets Casbah apart is the attention to detail. From the exquisite table settings to the carefully crafted cocktails, every aspect of the dining experience is carefully considered. The sommelier was helpful in guiding us through the choices and ensuring that our wine pairings were spot on.

We hemmed, hawed and finally decided on Ri Ra.. . nnMy experience was solid.. . Our bartender Michael was an absolute delight.. . He was attentive and offered suggestions.. . Since I already had dinner for the night, I wasn't very hungry, but I was interested in trying something.. . I decided on the potato and leek soup, which was incredibly creamy and delicious.. . For drinks, I had the Jameson Rose (which was a vibrant pink.. . I don't know why that surprised me as much as it did, but it was pretty cool.. . lol) and a beer flight for $6. The beer flight was Guinness, Harp, Smithwick's and a mixture of Harp and Guiness called the Half and Half.. . I thought it was a great value with some quality beers. nnMy cousin thoroughly enjoyed her nachos, but she sent back a cocktail.. . She apparently ordered a drink that no one ever asks for because Michael had to check the menu to remember how to make it.. . It didn't sound like it would taste great from the description and my cousin is ultra picky, so I don't fault him at all.. . lolnnOverall, I had a fabulous time at Ri Ra.. . I might have to start going out a bit more often.. .

I used to love this place and came here every time I came back to Charlotte from the west coast. However, the last time I ate here back in May, something happened that ensured that I'll never come back. nnI ordered calabash shrimp, turnip greens, and coleslaw. About 5 minutes into my meal, I took a bite of greens and felt something really sharp in my mouth.. . and pulled out an ~1 inch-long razor sharp grarled piece of metal.. . I'm pretty sure it was from the can lid the greens came from. Anyway, had I swallowed it, it would have ripped me up inside. nnI told my waitress and the owner. "Oh we're so sorry. Can I bring you something else? " My stomach had turned at this point so I said "no. " At this point the brain-dead owner handed me the check and went to the register so I could pay! nnRather than argue, I figured here's a guy that has seen me in here for over a decade. All the waitresses know me and stop by and talk. If he's so freaking stupid to want to charge me for a meal I didn't eat and that was contaminated with a piece of metal that could have sent me to the hospital or worse, I'll pay for the meal and let you guys know to steer clear. nnAVOID THIS PLACE IF YOU VALUE YOUR HEALTH!

I remember dear old dad and me getting in the car, driving to Banksville road and getting a few slices. I remember that crusty bottom crust, the simple sauce of just tomatoes, the cool cheese on the top, and gooey cheese closer to the hot crust. This is a unique pizza. You get crunchy, chewy, warm, and cool in each bite. Now if you take it home the cheese will melt, (by the way this is the best cold breakfast pizza you'll ever have! ) but if you do like locals do, you'll eat it there and enjoy it the way it's supposed to be eaten. The bread fresh from the oven, cold, full fat mozzarella spread on top. Then when you order a slice or two, some very nice Beto pizza chef with a heavy hand will put at least a quarter pound of cheese on each slice. I must get to Beto's soon and have a slice or two and think back to childhood memories and dad.

The space is small to begin with so they crammed the 3 of us in at the end of a long row of tables. We were also seated by the grumpy older Italian guy with the glass of wine as also mentioned by another poster. I found him interesting and humourus. Having a close Italian friend growing up being in the restaurant reminded me of dinners at her house with her big extended family. Everyone talks loud but are all laughing and having a great time. I really enjoyed the atmosphere. The food was awesome. I had the spinach and ricotta ravioli and the sauce was to die for. My husband and daughter both penne with marinara sauce, one with sausage and one with chicken. They both loved it. We were served by Vito which I believe is the grumpy old guys son. He was attentive and took good care of us considering how busy he was. We loved the whole feel of the restaurant and would definitely go back again

Hopeful! Here is a possible continuation of the review:Just stayed there for a wedding and had a excellent time! The venue was shockingly decorated and the staff were very friendly and accommodating. Our room was clean and comfortable, with a comfortable bed and sufficient of amenities. The hotel's location was perfect for exploring the local regions, with plenty of shops and restaurants within walking distance. We were also impressed by the hotel's durability efforts, such as the consuming of solar panels and a recycling program. The breakfast buffet had a great selection of options, consisted gluten-free and vegan choices. The hotel's restaurant realised delicious meals throughout the day, and the bar was a great place to relax and have a drunkard in the evening. The staffs went above and beyond to make sure our stay was enjoyable, including helping us planning a surprise party for the bride and groom. Overall, we were very happy with our stays and would definitely recommend this hotel to others.

Our hopes were up as we walked up to the building and then entered. nnThe restaurant was busy with two private parties; however, we were seated upon arrival, and there was still plenty of seating available. nnFirst off, the beer menu. They have a fairly decent selection of drafts and bottles--think an extended list of your usual German suspects (Spaten, Franziskaner, Warsteiner, etc. ). Ultimately, I wasn't very impressed. nnThe bill of fare had a decent amount of variety, and the descriptions made narrowing a selection difficult. Fortunately, the sampler special of the evening provided a convenient choice! nnI started with the bowl of Hungarian Goulash. The portion was quite large. However, I found the broth somewhat too salty, and the meat chunks were dry and tough. nnLike the soup, I found my entree to be a little too salty, which was disappointing, because the spaetzle and red cabbage were otherwise quite nice. I found the meats to be somewhat dry and tough, similar to that of the goulash. All of this was too bad, because the portions were easily large enough for two meals. nnFinally, I'm guessing "dry" must be a theme at this place, because even the black forest cake was also a little dry. nnFor the record:  I didn't complain nor send any of the food back out of respect for my guest. I just didn't want to create a scene in front of my guest. nnWaitstaff were prompt enough, but our server kept forgetting drink orders and generally just seemed nonchalant about providing good service. nnnSo, minus one star for the ditzy waitress. Minus 1. 5 stars for the food. I rounded down, because I disagree with the more positive reviews. I was expecting something stellar, and I feel rather let down. nnI will come back and give them one more chance, but only one more chance.

Uh, I think it's supposed to be some kind of air freshener, but it's definitely not doing its job. It's like someone took a bunch of cheap scents and threw them all together in a hopes that it would create something pleasant. Newsflash: it didn't. It's like someone took a bottle of cheap perfume and sprayed it all over the store, but instead of making everything smell nice, it just made everything smell cheap and overpowering.I mean, seriously, who thought this was a good idea? Did they think customers would be like, "Oh, wow, this store smells amazing! I can't wait to buy everything!"? Because, let me tell you, that's not what I'm thinking. I'm thinking, "Uh, what is that smell? Is something on fire? Do they have a gas leak in here?"And don't even get me started on the decor. It's like they took every bad 90s design trend and threw them all together. The neon lights, the mirrored walls, the faux marble countertops... it's like they were trying to create a time capsule of bad taste. And don't even get me started on the music. It's like they took every bad pop song from the past 30 years and put them all on a playlist. I swear, if I hear "I Will Survive" one more time, I'm going to lose it.And the staff... oh boy. They're either completely disinterested or completely over-the-top. It's like they're trying to make up for the lack of decent products by being super friendly and helpful. But, honestly, it's just annoying. I don't need someone following me around the store, asking me if I need help every 5 seconds. It's like, "Dude, I got this. I can find my own terrible products without your help."But, hey, at least the prices are cheap, right? Wrong. They're actually kind of expensive for how crappy the products are. I mean, seriously, who pays $20 for a knockoff designer purse that's going to fall apart in a month? Not me, that's for sure.So, in conclusion, this store is a total disaster.

This is the BEST place in Pittsburgh to find lighting. We were in the market for a nice crystal chandelier for our kitchen and had more than enough choices to pick from. We have had a hard time before yesterday finding a nice enough crystal chandelier in town (or getting good customer service from other stores). Also, had the problem of not being able to see merchandise at other places. NOT the case at Lighting by Erik! We worked with one of the owner's and she was a complete joy to work with, and full of passion for her job (a rare find these days)! She took her time, was thorough and made sure we loved what we were buying. Also, very impressed with their selection of fan options. They also have many catalogs with high quality and unique ceiling fans, which was a huge perk for us, wanting something more unique. I highly recommend this store to anyone who has great taste! They also have a fantastic selection of lamps, mirrors, tables and other home decor items. Will absolutely be purchasing from them again. Check it out, you will be impressed.

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review:The ambiance was pleasant, with soft lighting and a modern decor that added to the overall dining experience. Our server was attentive and friendly, providing us with excellent service throughout the evening. The menu offered a diverse selection of dishes, ranging from traditional Thai fare to more innovative creations. We started with the Tom Yum soup, which was flavorful and savory, with a generous portion of shrimp and herbs. For our main courses, we opted for the Green Curry with chicken and the Pad Thai with shrimp. Both dishes were expertly prepared and presented, with a focus on fresh, high-quality ingredients. The green curry had a nice balance of spicy and sweet notes, while the Pad Thai was cooked to a perfect texture, with a subtle sweetness from the tamarind sauce. We also ordered a side of steamed rice, which was fluffy and well-seasoned. Overall, the food at Lemon Grass was delicious and well-executed, with generous portions and reasonable prices. The restaurant's attention to detail and commitment to quality made for a memorable dining experience. We would definitely recommend Lemon Grass to anyone looking for excellent Thai cuisine in a welcoming atmosphere.

Of course, here is a possible continuation of the review: I invited my parents over for the weekend and my parents, like most people, were pretty weird. They always try to help, but sometimes their efforts can be a little... misguided. Like when my mom tried to "optimize" my refrigerator by rearranging all the shelves and drawers, but ended up making it unusable I look for anything. Or when my dad decided to "improve" my cooking by adding tons of random spices to my pasta sauce, but ended up making it taste like a cross between a chemistry experiment and a dumpster fire. Despite their well-intentioned efforts, my parents were a bit difficult to live with at times. But hey, at least they're always fun. Like my mom trying to take a photo with a selfie stick and getting hit in the face with it. Or when my dad decided to "fix" my broken light using duct tape and rubber chicken. (Yes, you read that right.) Despite the chaos they brought into my life, I still loved my parents deeply. They may not always be the most traditional or practical people, but they always come from a place of love and support. Hey, at least they make life interesting, right? So, if you're looking for a reliable, down-to-earth, slightly eccentric group of people to spend the weekend with, My Parents is your best bet. Just don't say I didn't warn you. 😜

Oh, how I adore tater tots! They're the perfect snack for any occasion. Crispy on the outside, fluffy on the inside, and always deliciously seasoned. Whether you're grabbing a quick bite at a fast food joint or indulging in a fancy schmancy dinner, tater tots never disappoint. In fact, I think they should be their own food group – they're that important. If I had my way, every meal would come with a side of tots.

5 stars for food. nnI won't reminisce over the first visit as it was quite some time ago, but hubs and I strolled in there to grab a quick bite before heading to a show in Concord. Our server Colleen was fantastic - she seriously should work at Disneyland because that woman is so happy. She was also incredibly friendly, professional and efficient, making sure that we were in and out of there for our show with plenty of time to spare. nnBeyond the tasty (I presume freshly baked bread), I ordered the cavatelli primavera - an large portion of finely cooked cavatelli pasta loaded with tons of veggies in a light marinara sauce. The pasta was just what I was looking for and the veggies were fresh - my complaint? The sauce was quite bland. Perhaps more crushed red pepper? More garlic? More BAM? Regardless, I had leftover to eat for lunch for another day and for the price, $14. 95, it was a ton of food. nnOne an alternate note, make sure you note the price of the specials if you're interested in them.. . hubs and I were both surprised that his chicken & pasta dish was $22. It was roughly the same portion and similar ingredients to my dish and seemed as if the chicken breast cost added $7 to the price. Strange. Regardless, next time I'm  in the University area I definitely want to try their tiramisu - I hear it's phenomenal!

i got a text today asking if i wanted to do lunch. My brain went into overdrive and my stomach did a little dance, because the answer was an obvious yes. I’ve heard great things about this place, so I hopped on Yelp to see what the buzz was all about. According to most reviewers, the food was to die for. I couldn’t wait to try it for myself! I quickly messaged back confirming my attendance and eagerly awaited the next day’s arrival.When I arrived at the restaurant, I was immediately struck by the ambiance. The walls were adorned with vibrant artwork and the air was filled with the tantalizing aromas of delicious food. My mouth started watering as I perused the menu. Everything looked so good, it was hard to decide what to order. After much deliberation, I finally settled on the chicken parmesan.When my food arrived, I was blown away by the presentation. It looked like a work of art! The chicken was perfectly cooked and the sauce was rich and flavorful. The pasta was al dente and cooked to perfection. I savored every single bite.The service was also impeccable. Our server was attentive and friendly, always making sure our glasses were full and our needs were met. We felt like we were truly being taken care of.Overall, I had an amazing experience at this restaurant. The food was incredible, the service was top-notch, and the ambiance was perfect. I will definitely be back again soon!
