We would definitely use this catering service again in the future.Overall, we were very happy with the catering service. The food was delicious, the staff was friendly and helpful, and the price was reasonable. We would definitely recommend this service to anyone looking for a caterer for their next event.The food was absolutely delicious, and the staff was very friendly and helpful. They made sure that everything was perfect for our event, and we would definitely use them again in the future.The food was fresh, tasty, and beautifully presented. There was a great variety of dishes to choose from, and everyone at the party was very impressed. The staff was very professional and attentive, and they made sure that everyone had a great time. We would highly recommend this catering service to anyone looking for a high-quality and affordable option.The service was excellent, and the food was delicious. The staff was very friendly and helpful, and they made sure that everything was perfect.

I recently visited the hair salon located at 123 Main St. in Anytown USA, and I must say that it was a complete disaster. I had been looking forward to getting my hair done for weeks, and I was excited to try out this new salon that I had heard great things about. Unfortunately, my experience was the complete opposite of what I had expected.From the moment I walked in, I could tell that something was off. The atmosphere was stiff and uncomfortable, and the staff seemed unfriendly and unapproachable. I was greeted by a girl who seemed to be in a bad mood, and she didn't even bother to look up from her phone when I walked in. I had to stand there for a few minutes before she finally acknowledged me and asked how she could help me.When I told her that I was there for a haircut, she seemed put off and told me that they were busy and wouldn't be able to get to me for at least an hour. I was a bit taken aback by this, but I decided to wait anyway. I sat down in the waiting area and began to flip through a magazine to pass the time.After about 45 minutes, my name was called and I was led to a stylist's chair. The stylist introduced herself as Sarah, and she seemed nice enough. However, as soon as she started cutting my hair, I could tell that something was off. She was chopping away at my locks with no regard for the length or the shape of my hair. I tried to speak up and tell her that I wanted a trim, not a full-on haircut, but she ignored me and continued to cut away.When she was finished, I was horrified by what I saw in the mirror. My hair was way shorter than I had wanted, and it looked completely uneven. I tried to politely tell Sarah that I wasn't happy with the way my hair looked, but she just shrugged and told me that it was what I had asked for. I was shocked and upset, and I quickly got up and left the salon.I will never be returning to this salon again, and I would advise others to steer clear of it as well.

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review in a human style:I had dinner at Lidia's on Saturday evening, February 22, 2014. The restaurant was bustling with activity, and the atmosphere was lively and inviting. Our server greeted us warmly and showed us to our table by the window. The décor was elegant and understated, with white tablecloths and soft lighting. The menu offered a variety of Italian dishes, and I was eager to try their signature pasta dish.We started with a delicious antipasto platter, which included cured meats, cheese, and vegetables. The flavors were fresh and vibrant, and the portion size was generous. For my main course, I chose the Pappardelle al Cinghiale, a hearty pasta dish made with wild boar ragu and topped with shaved truffles. The pasta was cooked to perfection, and the ragu was rich and savory.My companion had the Branzino al Forno, a beautifully presented sea bass dish baked with cherry tomatoes and herbs. The fish was moist and flavorful, and the accompanying vegetables were tender and well-seasoned. We shared a side of Grilled Asparagus, which was perfectly cooked and sprinkled with lemon zest.For dessert, we opted for the Tiramisu, a classic Italian favorite. It was creamy and sweet, with a strong espresso flavor and a hint of cinnamon. The presentation was also impressive, with layers of ladyfingers soaked in coffee liqueur and topped with a dollop of whipped cream.Overall, our dining experience at Lidia's was exceptional. The service was attentive and knowledgeable, the atmosphere was warm and inviting, and the food was outstanding. I would highly recommend Lidia's to anyone looking for a delicious Italian meal in a sophisticated setting.

nn I have eaten at Bado's before and have had pizza that was pretty tasty. So when my roomate wanted to go there, I was pretty excited. There were 4 of us. Two of us had burgers while the other two ordered wedding soup and shared potato skins. My burger was delicious. It was juicy and smothered in bleu cheese. nnWhile my food was really good, there were some issues with the other items ordered. Two of my guests cited that the wedding soup had a metalic taste to it while my other friend with the burger found a hair. Being a hairdresser and understanding that it does happen sometimes, she disregarded it and continued to eat her burger. Unfortunately, the hair did not stop there. Another guest found several hairs in their meal as well. We decided that we should let our waitress know. I stopped the waitress and explained how there was hair in two people's food. The waitress got upset and was very rude (it's not like we accused her of planting the hair herself). On top of this, she mistakenly took my burger and walked away with it, leaving the sullied food on the table. When she came back I told her that my burger was not one of the items with food on it and she instantly became defensive. At this point we asked for a manager (who never came) and insisted our meal get comped. Considering she threw out the only good food that we ordered, we didn't feel like that was asking too much. Luckily the waitress did not put up too much of a fight. nnI will never eat at Bado's again. A good cheese burger and a decent pie isn't enough when the staff can't even accept their own (human) mistakes. I have been places where there have been food issues before and I have also worked in a restaurant. I completely understand that mistakes happen. However, the reaction that we received was unacceptable.

And as luck (good or bad depending on how my diet's going! ) would have it, this location provides a daily opportunity to get one of those combos! This location is incredibly convenient for me - both for being relatively close to where I live and for being next to an old bookstore, clothes store, and a few other businesses - and has earned all 5 stars for being a bit better than the average Panera. nnOne, I've never walked in here and found a shortage in sweet treats, which is a common problem if I want an afternoon bagel in a lot of other Panera locations. Two, the sandwiches here just taste fresher than a lot of other places I've been to. Three, as others have mentioned, the staff are just nicer here - I have no idea why, but I'm not complaining! Bonus star for this Panera because it's actually made its entire interior comfortable and encourages people to relax and use its wi-fi, which is something I can't say a lot of other locations have nailed just yet.

The selection of fresh specialties is truly impressive, catering to a wide range of culinary enthusiasts. The layout is well-organized, with clearly marked sections that make it easy to navigate the aisles. Whether you're looking for exotic spices, artisanal cheeses, or hard-to-find ingredients, this store has you covered. The produce section is bursting with a colorful array of seasonal fruits and vegetables that look as if they were just picked that morning. The shelves are constantly restocked with the finest cuts of meat and the freshest fish, which is simply irresistible. What sets this store apart is its commitment to quality. It's obvious that the store takes pride in obtaining its products from local suppliers and sustainable sources. Another noteworthy aspect is the knowledgeable staff, who are always ready to assist you. They have an impressive understanding of the products they carry and are more than willing to make helpful recommendations or suggest creative recipes. The prices are reasonable, especially considering the exceptional quality. While some specialty stores can be quite expensive, this store strikes the perfect balance, allowing you to indulge in the finest ingredients without breaking the bank. The personal attention and individualized service truly elevate the shopping experience. The store maintains a clean and well-kept environment, which makes for a pleasant shopping experience from start to finish. The loyalty program is also a great incentive, offering additional savings and exclusive access to special events. Lastly, the check-out process is quick and efficient, allowing you to get back to your kitchen in no time. This store is a gem for anyone who loves to cook and is looking for high-quality specialty items.

D's inhabited up to the hype and then some. From the moment I went through the door, I was greeted with warmth and friendly smiles from the staff. The rustic and cozy atmosphere created the perfect backdrop for a leisuⲅely lunch. As I perused the meոu, I was delighted by the diverse range of optio ns, showcasing both classic comfort foods and innovative culinary creations. After much contemplation, I settled on the signature dish, "D's Deluxe Burg er," and it was a decision I did not regret. The burger arrived, towering with perfectly cooked and seasoned beef, topped with a generous sums of melteԁ cheese, fresh lettuce, and ripe tomatoes. Each bite was a heavenly explosion of tastes, and the accompanying fries were crispy and addictive. What impressed me most about D's was their attention to detail and pledge to sourcing local ingredients. The mneu proudly highlightеd the partnerships they had formed with nearby farmers and producers, which added a layer of authenticity to the dining experience. It was evident that every ingredient was cɑrefully selected, resulting in dishes that were bursting with freshness. Despi te the restaurant being busy, the service remained exemplary throughout my visit. The waitstaff was attentive and knowledgeable, offering recommendations and ensuring that all my needs were m et. The level of professionalism and real hospitality truly elevated my dining experience. To accompany my meal, I otped for a craft beer from their impressive selection. The server's expеrtise in suggestiոg the perfect pairing was commendable, and the beer complemented the flavors of the burger impeccably. It was overt that D's valued the art of pairing food and drink and worked dіligently to create a harmonious culinary symphony. As I savored each bite and engaged in conversation with the friendly employees, time seemed to slow down, allowing me to fully immerse myself in the experiencе.

Who knew you could mess up a Blizzard so bad? nnFirst of all, the line was out the door. We assumed that it was just a busy night. Turns out they were out of multiple items (and did not put a sign up). People were upset and walking out. Then the family in front of us ordered, but did not pay. No card, no money left their hand. What? So like you are giving out free ice cream and making us wait in line? nnI get up to the counter and ask a question about the ingredients of a Brownie Batter blizzard. They are like, it is just brownies. My response: Anything else? They had to go ask someone. What? Seriously?! So I finally get my answer and order it. My husband also ordered a blizzard. He ordered a Turtle Pecan blizzard and received some random peanuts in his blizzard. We never got a receipt. When I asked for one, they said they did not have any. What?! nnWe pay and wait. And wait. Oh, but while we wait, we get to watch the kitchen person. He was making a chicken sandwich. With his LONG ponytail hanging over the grill. Yum? More like gross. Then both my husband and I notice that he grabbed a raw chicken breast (while not wearing a glove) and placed it on the bare counter. He then wiped his hand on his pants. What? Salmonella much? Any food standards? nnWe get our medium blizzards. The ice cream normally extends to the top or slightly over the top of the cup. This time the ice cream barely made it to the top of my cup. My husband had ice cream under the cup. When we asked if this was a medium, they said yes. And just walked away. nnI walked out feeling very unsatisfied and my husband I determined this will be our last visit to the local DQ. Besides the poor service, the food standards were disgusting.

I know a lot of people tend to either love or hate Panera; I happen to love it - the You-Pick-2 combo is one of my favorite lunch options. The ability to choose two different items from their extensive menu is what sets Panera apart. Whether I'm in the mood for a hearty soup and a half sandwich, or a fresh salad and a warm, indulgent bowl of mac and cheese, Panera always has something to satisfy my cravings. The quality of their ingredients is evident in every bite; the vegetables are crisp, the bread is perfectly toasted, and the flavors are always well-balanced. The portion sizes are also very generous, ensuring that I leave feeling satisfied and fueled for the rest of the day. Additionally, Panera's commitment to sourcing ingredients from responsible and sustainable suppliers is a bonus that aligns with my values as a conscious eater. The atmosphere in their restaurants is cozy and inviting, making it a pleasant place to enjoy a meal alone or catch up with friends. Overall, Panera's You-Pick-2 combo is a winning lunch choice for me, offering variety, quality, and a conscientious approach to food.

The service was friendly and the atmosphere was casual. The chips and salsa were fresh and delicious. The tacos were made with fresh ingredients and the portions were generous. The combination platters were a great value and the burritos were smothered in a tasty sauce. The guacamole was chunky and flavorful and the flan was a sweet ending to the meal.

You can even customize your sandwich here if you aren't feeling up to their many sandwich options. nnI think all McAlister's feel a bit the same.. . you get "herded" to the back of the location to a bank of registers to order. The staff at most of these locations are pretty friendly and helpful.. . sometimes you feel a little rushed if the line is starting to build and they try to get you through. You are given a number to place on the table and the bring out the food to you nice a hot/fresh. nnThis location offers free wi-fi but I haven't brought my computer to test it out yet. I've seen people working or meeting with laptops here. nnThe thing I HATE about this location in the university area is the parking lot. It is impossible to get in and out easily and the spaces are all for compact cars, which makes for some interesting parking lot stories and near misses.

There were 7 of us altogether and we had eaten at another restaurant earlier in the evening. One member of our party walked up to the bar to let them know we were sitting on the couches along the wall, as it was fairly busy that night. In the short time that he was there, a man who was behind the bar looked at him and then turn away without saying a word or notifying anyone else. He then turned to a waitress who had approached the service area of the bar and asked her if  we could have a server take our drink order. She said "yep" and then abruptly turned and walked away. At that point, he asked to see the manager  who came a few minutes later. When my friend told him of what had occurred, the manager said "What do you want me to do"? nnMy friend said "An apology would be a good start" whereupon the manager looked back at him and said "Get out and don't come back"? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? Are you kidding?? ? ? nnMy friend is well traveled and frequently stays at higher end hotels and dines at quality restaurants. He was not screaming or belligerent in any way, shape, or form; simply informing the manager of what had occurred. nnThat being said, we left and went back to 131 East, where we had previously eaten. The service there was fabulous, as was the meal and the drinks creative and exquisite. nnThere are new restaurants that have opened in Carnegie as of late. Papa J's is no longer the only game in town. Obviously this is the way a simple complaint is handled by management. Appalling!! Do yourself a favor and frequent any of the other restaurants that are within walking distance of Papa J's. They understand the concept of  customer service.

The best place near the city is soaking cone! The moment I set foot on this charming ice cream shop, I was immediately attracted by its pleasant atmosphere. The wall is decorated with vibrant murals, depicting the scene of summer happiness, creating a happy atmosphere, and perfectly supplementing the dessert provided. The staff greeted me with a warm smile, eager to guide me through all kinds of tastes and heads. After careful scanning the menu, I settled on the classic vanilla drip cone because I wanted to taste the simplicity of this eternal flavor. When the server submits my order, I was immediately made by the freshly made Wahfu cake, and its aroma fluttered in the air, which is a promise of experience that can be obtained. The first biting was a pure happiness -vanilla ice cream that melted on my tongue -like velvet -like smoothness, while chocolate dipped in sauce coating increased a rich and indulgent flavor. Subsequent bites were pleasant texture and taste symphony, perfect balance, making me even more desire. This place is different from the attention of detail -from the spacious part to the detailed demonstration, everything is precise and concerned. The Wasteban cone is a perfect golden brown, crispy outside, but chewy with pleasure. The quality of various ingredients is illuminated in each mouth, reminding me of the old -fashioned ice cream shop in the past. Although the living room is busy with customers, efficient and friendly services ensure that everyone will participate quickly. Considering the high -quality quality and generous part of provided, the price is reasonable. Whether you are looking for fast dessert restorations or places where the nostalgic ice cream experience, the hidden gem near this city center is a must -see. I have a satisfactory smile on my face and appreciate this frozen joy.

My dad and I came here for dinner last night after finding out that Jimmy Tsangs had closed. Rick had to teach a night class and Mom had a meeting to go to. Besides, I hadn't been here since my last review. Yeah, I know the chow here is Americanized Chinese food. Who cares--it's great stuff! nnNow when eating Chinese, you should never begin by diving in to your main course right away. First you've gotta have some soup and some egg rolls! Dad and I both began with some r-e-a-l-l-y good hot-n-sour soup. Man, that was good-n-bold. Warmed my insides right away! Now I know that vegetable spring rolls are supposedly better for you. Yeah right--what's a great Chinese dinner without some good old fashioned egg rolls? I like to put a spoonful each of sweet-n-sour sauce, duck sauce, and hot Chinese mustard on my plate, dipping my egg roll in a different one with each bite! nnI love the names of some of their specialties here, including the Triple Crown, Three Afire, Sizzling Trio, and the Royal Match. Feeling a little adventurous--and why not:  the old man was paying--I went for the Jaded Affair. Yum, scallops and shrimp marinated in white sauce. Usually seafood isn't my first choice. My dad went for the Kung Pao Chicken. I've got to give him credit--how a guy his age can eat that and not reach for the Mylanta is beyond me!

But, my drink was fine. nnTheir advertised drink specials are good, but most concentrated on shots and low-end liquors (think Smirnoff for $3). The "service" at the bar was hard to come by, although there were at least two bartenders working. I asked for a minute with the menu, and spent the next 20 minutes trying to flag down a bartender to take my food order. nnOrdered the shrimp special which was not very special at all. Although there were 6 grilled shrimp that were actually pretty good, the rest of the plate was a mess of flavorless, watery rice pilaf and a virtual stalk of broccoli, steamed but otherwise unadorned. At $12. 99 it was definitely not a good value. Plus, it took for-ev-er to arrive. The gentleman next to me ordered the meatloaf special, and his cafeteria-style serving arrived in about 30 seconds. That didn't make me very happy for him, however, as it looked equally disgusting. nnI cannot recommed this place for anything other than a drink, although the pool players seemed to be having a good time.

Recently I read a few articles about the dangers of non stick cookware. None of them are really any good for any of us. I won't get into what the dangers of them are, but they are real and most non stick cookware will be off the market by 2015. nnI started looking into enamel coated cookware, Crate has a number of different varieties and bought myself a 3 piece starter set with an 8, 10, and 12 inch pans. Like non stick, no oils are needed and clean up is pretty quick. nnCrate in itself is a cooking gadget Mecca. If you love stainless steel cookware, they have anything you want from All Clad, made right in Canonsburg Pa. There's Fiesta ware from Wheeling, and If you want Emeral's professional cookware, Crate has it. nnYou can take cooking classes here as well, they have a full service kitchen in back. Classes are limited to certain amounts due to space, but everyone sits after and enjoys the food they've prepped. nnIt's top of the line cookware so it has top of the line prices. But well worth it!

It was a match made in kimchi heaven. This love affair has now extended to the Korean culture and language. Sam Bok is the perfect venue to get anything Korean cuisine related and has some wonderful Ahjummas (ladies) who will let you try out you newly mastered though limited skills at speaking Korean. nnI love making my own kimchi chigae - not proficient enough to make my own kimchi though. Sam Bok sells really good cabbage and radish kimchi that you can pick up anytime. It's available in several sizes. I also like their black bean sauce, yam noodle packets, spices and spicy ramyun packets (Asian ramen that will blow your socks off). I recently picked up a couple of bread rolls with cream filling which is a Korean delicacy that satisfies your sweet and savory cravings. Sam Bok's bread was wonderful, I will definitely go back for more of those. nnI suggest giving Sam Bok a shot, it's unique, local and always inviting.

The IC light had a smooth, clean taste, with a hint of citrus that gave it a pleasant tartness. The atmosphere in the bar was warm and welcoming, with dim lighting that created a relaxed mood. As I took my first sip, I was immediately struck by the refreshing crispness of the beer. The carbonation was just right—not too strong, not too flat—and the effervescence danced on my taste buds. It was the perfect choice for someone looking for a light, easy-drinking beer. The beer’s light body made it easy to savor each sip, and its clean, crisp finish left no unpleasant aftertaste. As I sat there, I was struck by the excellent balance of flavors that made IC light such a good choice. The beer was a testament to the brewery’s commitment to producing a beer that could be enjoyed by both beer connoisseurs and casual drinkers. I was impressed by the care and attention that went into each glass. I would recommend IC light to anyone looking for a refreshing drink on a hot summer day or a way to relax after a long day. It was a light, refreshing beer that was easy to drink.

I know I know I know! nAnyway so theres absolutely no places in Homestead that delivered Chinese so I decided to walk down to the closest Chinese food/restaurant to my house. nnThe family that runs the restaurant is very friendly. I read a review about them on Google that said the  mom looks like she might want to cook you alive. Don't believe it! nnOn this visit I ordered the Steamed Dumplings(recommended by some reviewer on google.. . THAT LIAR) and the Vegetable Chow Fun. I generally enjoy steamed dumplings but these dumplings(too much dumpling little meat) werent so good. Theyll probably be sitting in my mini fridge tomorrow and I'll force myself to eat them when I have the munchies/starving to death. The Vegetable Chow Fun on the other hand verrrrrrrrrrrrrry yummy. Definately most have next time anyone decides to stop by. nnBut all in all the verdict is you typical Chinese Take Out Place. nn*puts the menu on the fridge*

I hear they don't give very much money for your items, but that's to be expected, right? They are trying to make a profit. nnI have shopped here a few times, but I stopped going after I realized how long it took me to find anything I liked. I went when I was pregnant, and back then I didn't mind wasting time as much. But now that I have a baby, I'm all about getting in and out as quickly as possible. nnThe store seems to carry a lot of the cheaper brands (Carters, Child's Place, etc), so it can be harder for me to find things that fit my style. I don't like a lot of graphics or silly slogans all over the clothes. There is a section of $1 onesies that I like (who can complain about $1 onesies? ), but they aren't arranged by size, so it's hard to find ones that fit. The same goes for the pajama section. That section is also not arranged by size, just color, so you'll be browsing through every single pajama checking the tags to see what size they are. It's time consuming! nnI prefer to shop online for baby clothes. I get emails when my favorite stores have sales, so I wait to buy clothes then. I end up paying a couple dollars more per item than I would at this store, but it's worth it to me to save time and energy. Plus, I get brand new clothes!

The food, however. Blah. nnFirst off, If you go to a show, please note there is a two item per person minimum. This means that from their extensive menu of overpriced bar food and drinks, you are REQUIRED to choose two items each. nnSecond, the sodas are NOT FREE REFILLS! I learned that one the hard way. Get water. nnThird, before tipping your server, please make sure she hasn't already taken her cut out. Even after paying for two very bland, overpriced meals and way too many Diet Cokes, I thought that my bill was rather high. Upon further inspection, I noticed that my server had taken an 18% gratuity. The menu explicitly says that an automatic gratuity will be taken out for parties of six or more, but there was only two of us and we weren't even drunk. I found this tactic awfully presumptuous and left her tip at the 18% even though I typically tip well over 20%. nnOverall, I don't like the idea of being required to purchase food just to see a comedy show. I can see why they do it, but if I were required to buy a crappy popcorn and overpriced pop every time I went to the movies, I would be far less inclined to go as I am not inclined to go to the Improv again.

Ok, I have to give this place a 5, and no JUST because of the delicious foоd. The atmosphere is cosy and inviting, with a warm and welcoming staff. The menu offers a diverse selection of options, including vegetarіan and gluten-free dishes. The portions are generous, and the prices are very r easonable. I ordered the grilled salmon, and it was cook to perfection. The flavors were outstanding, and the presen tation was beautiful. The sides were creative and tasty, and the desserts looked stunning. j was impressed by the attention to detail in every aspect of the dining experience. The tbale settings were elegant, and the lighting was dim and intimate. The baϲkground music was a nice touch, and it added to the overall ambiance. The service was top-notch, and our server was frienldy and attentive. She knew the menu inside and out and conducted great recommendations. The food was excеptional, and I can't wait to come back and try more dishes. The calibre of the ingredients was evident in every bite. The flavors were rich and bold, and the presentation was visually stuոning. The kitchen was clean and organized, and the chefs were clearly passionate about their c raft. Overall, this restaurant exceeded my expectationѕ in every way. I would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a deliϲious and satisfying dining experience. It's a hidden gem, and I fel lucky to have discovered it. I will definitely be back quickly!

So j don't ever go Downtown because, well, Downtown is kind of icky. The streets are always crowded, filled with peoplе rushing around, and the traffic is unbearable. The sidewalks are sown with trash, and the smell of urine lingers in the air. It seems like every few steps I take, I encoun𝚝er another aggressive panhandler asking for money. De buildings are tall and imposing, casting dark shadows over the streets, making me feel claustrophobic and uneasy. The noise is implacable, with honking horns, sirens wailing, and the constant chatter of people. The overall atmosphere is chaotic and colossal, leaving me longing for some peace and quiet. The lack of green spaces or backyards exacerbates this feeling of being trapped in a concrete jungle. Additionally, parking is a nightmare, and findinɡ a spot is an exercise in frustration. The hіgh cost of parking also adds to the expenses of visiting Downtown. The ԁining options in Downtown are diverse, but the quality is often disappointing. Many of the restaurants are overpriced tourist traps with mediocre food and rushed serves. I've had my fair share of disappointing meals in Downtown, and it has made me reⅼuctant to trу new places. The boutique, too, lack the charm and uniqueness that I look for in a shopping experience. Most of them are generic chains stores with the same merchandise you can find anywhere else. De overall lack of character makes Downtown feel like a soulless place, devoid of any authentic local culture. Unfortunately, the negatives 𝚏ar outweigh any potential positives, leading me to avoid Downtown at all costs.

To be clear.. . nnService was very personable (Alyssa), but sporadic. This is an indication of a section that is too large for the server. I believe she had every table outside the bar on a Saturday night, albeit early (approx 6pm). nnI have an issue with the Groupon that I purchased. If I pay $14 for a buy-one-get-one, I expect to receive $14 in savings. That was not the case. Because of our selections, the savings only priced out to about $6 less than had I just walked in. In fact, I am not sure it is possible to receive $14 in savings given the limitations of the sandwich menu and domestic beer selections. As an aside, I wouldn't just walk in because this place is across town from me.. nnTo be clear, we also ordered an appetizer, soft drink, and craft beer. I didn't want to give the impression that we were there to redeem the Groupon, drop a gratuity, and leave. nnThe food was standard bar food. The chicken nachos were ho-hum. The sandwiches were tasty.

My husband and I went here for lunch. The service was excellent; our server was very attentive and friendly. The food was also very good. We started with the calamari, which was perfectly cooked and had a delicious marinara sauce. For our main course, I had the chicken parmesan, which was also very good. The chicken was tender and juicy, and the sauce was flavorful. My husband had the frutti di mare, which he said was the best he had ever had. The seafood was fresh and perfectly cooked, and the sauce was light and flavorful. We also ordered a side of garlic bread, which was delicious. The bread was soft and buttery, and the garlic butter was flavorful. For dessert, we had the tiramisu, which was also very good. The ladyfingers were soaked in espresso and mascarpone cheese, and the cocoa powder on top added a nice touch. Overall, we had a very enjoyable experience at this restaurant. The service was excellent, the food was very good, and the atmosphere was casual and inviting. I would definitely recommend this restaurant to others.

Capable NQPR outpost. This West aBnk outpost of National Public Radio is a beacon of intelligent programming in a vast wasteland of right lwing and religious drivel. MPR's coverage of the news is intelligetn and insightful. GarriXon Keillor and his fellow travelers on "A Prairie Home Companon" provide a healthy dose of homespun humor and wisdom. The local news and talk programming is informative and thought provokinWg. I can't say enough good things about this estation. tI is a national treasure. Here are some of the things I like best about MPR:* The news coverage is Tintelligent and insightful. MPR's reporters and ommentators are top-notch. They do a great ojb of providing context and analysis of the day's news. * Garrison Keillor and his mfellow travelers on "A Prairie Home Companino" provide a healthy dose of homespun humor and wisdom. Keillor is a master storyteller and his guests are always interesting and informaktive. * The local news and talk programming is informative and thoguht provoking. MPR's local reoprters and commentators do a great job of covering the issues that matter to Minnesotans. I highly recommend MPR to anyone who is ooking for intelligent, insightful, and entertaining radio programming. It is a national tredasure.

I was relieved to find the Animal Wellness Center in Pittsburgh, as my worries were quickly dispelled by the exceptional care they provided. Right from the moment I stepped through their doors, the staff welcomed both me and my feline companion with warmth and professionalism. Their genuine love for animals was evident in their compassionate approach, creating a comforting atmosphere during each visit.The facilities at the Animal Wellness Center are truly outstanding. The waiting area is impeccably clean and comfortable, thoughtfully divided into separate sections for cats and dogs. This design ensures a stress-free environment for furry patients. The examination rooms are equipped with cutting-edge technology, and the veterinarians take the time to explain each procedure in detail, attentively addressing any concerns or queries I may have had.What impressed me the most was the expertise and dedication of the veterinarians. They demonstrated a profound understanding of feline health and exhibited highly skilled abilities in diagnosing and treating various conditions. Whether it was a routine check-up or a more complex medical issue, the vets at the Animal Wellness Center handled it all with utmost professionalism and care.I also greatly appreciated their emphasis on preventive care. In addition to treating illnesses, they took the initiative to educate me about proper nutrition, vaccinations, and parasite control. This proactive approach has enabled me to ensure my cat's long-term health and well-being.Furthermore, the Animal Wellness Center boasts a well-stocked on-site pharmacy, making it convenient to obtain any necessary medications or supplements without the hassle of additional stops. The staff also goes the extra mile by following up after each visit, diligently monitoring my cat's progress and promptly communicating any additional recommendations.

They have a great selection of cheeses, sauces and pastas. They also have a small bread area and produce section. nnMost of the reviews online have been positive so I will share my negative. nn1. Be careful of the pasta selections. You can pay a high price for a bowl of noodles that tear apart as soon as they hit the water. Know what you are buying or leave it alone. nnI have always felt that the people behind the cheese counter were very knowledgeable. Anytime I was cooking a particular type of food or looking for a cheese that would go good with.. .. .. .. I had only to ask. This past time I saw one of the regular employees and informed him that we were making mexican food for friends and I was looking for a cheese that would make a good queso.. .. . but I was unable to finish. As soon as I said the work "mexican" he glared at me and told me that although I may think he looks mexican - he wasn't. He then left me standing at the counter while he went to get a drink of water. Maybe he was having a bad day - but he didn't improve mine any. I bought my cheese down the street. Not sure I will go back, or at least I will try to get someone else to wait on me.

This place used to be very good. However, quality has been greatly reduced. Food is not as good as before, and the service is not so careful. The atmosphere is not as good as before. It is not as clean as before, and the decoration is out of date. The price has also risen, which is not the reason for the quality of food or service. Overall, this place is no longer worth going. There are better choices there.

Sounded good and I was in town so I thought I'd give it a shot! I went around 4 pm on a Saturday and it was pretty busy! Usually a good sign. There were only about 3 people in front of me in the takeout line, so it shouldn't have taken long for me to place an order, right? Wrong. It took maybe 15-20 minutes for those 3 orders to be taken because nobody would commit themselves to working the register. While waiting in line, I got to see all these mouthwatering sandwiches being made. Once I placed my order, it didn't take long to come out.. . nnSo I got a Pittsburgh cheese steak, their #2 most popular item (don't ask what their #1 is.. . the guy will roll his eyes and unenthusiastically say, "beer. ") Long story short, what I ended up with was a bland, lukewarm beef party with bland soggy fries on top. The cold lettuce and tomato cooled the sandwich even more. I enjoyed the coleslaw on top, which is pretty much all I ended up eating from the sandwich. And a few limp fries. Disappointing.

However, my experience in this specific chain store is impressive, so that I have to share with others. From the moment I entered, the staff was very friendly, enthusiastic hospitality, they surpassed to ensure that I would meet my needs. I quickly greeted me, sitting on a comfortable table, my server spent some time to explain the menu, and made suggestions based on my preference. The food is very good, and the amount and flavor of the food exceeds my expectations. I ordered a burger. The burger is perfect and is equipped with cooked crispy fries. The server checked with me during my meals to make sure everything meets my preferences and quickly re -filled my drink. However, the impression that I really left me is the degree of attention of employees for details. They noticed that I was celebrating a special occasion and made every effort to make it more special, and even brought out free desserts to pour my meals. Those who felt personalized and real throughout the experience, the staff's commitment to providing excellent customer service left a deep impression on me. Overall, this chain store left me a deep impression and strongly suggested anyone who seeks food experience. Employees' dedication to customer satisfaction is indeed very good. I can't wait to come back as soon as possible.

Sue! Heres' a possible continuatio of the review:t was a bit of a trek to get there, but the moment we stepped inside, we knew it was worth the effort. The atmosphere was liely and inviting, with a friendly bartender who greeted us with a warm smile. The drinks menu was impressive, with a great selection of craft beers and creaHive cocktails. We ordered a few of their signature drinks and were blown away by the flavors and presentartion. The bad food was also delicious, with generous portions and reasonable prices. We trieId the bffalo wings, which were crispy and perfectly sauced, and the nachos, which were piled high with fresh ingredients. The servicw was top-notch, with attentive and friendly servers who made sure our glasses were always full. We were horoughly mpressed with the overall edxperience and would highly recommend this bar to anyone looking for a great spot to grab a drink or meal on the Strip. TVhe only downside was that it was a bit on the pricey side, but the quality of the drinks and fooAd made it worth the splurge. Overall, we were extremely satisfied with our visit and would definitely retzurn.

Unfortunately, there are still many shortcomings for the hotel's poor services and disappointing convenience facilities. The staff of the front desk seems to be not interested in solving our concerns, and the problems we encounter are not solved in time or satisfactory. The room is small and lacks basic facilities, such as mini refrigerators or free water. The bed is also very uncomfortable, which makes the night disturbed. In addition, the location of the hotel is inconvenient to explore the surrounding areas, and the choice of parking is limited and expensive. Overall, our accommodation in Felmont is disappointing, and we will not recommend it to our friends or family members.

I guess I should have read the reviews on here before going, seeing as a lot of people echo the sentiments that I feel. nnAfter my mother sent back her extremely oversalted steak, the chef came out to "apologize". It was very embarrassing how he chastised her for not enjoying her meal. He then snidely commented, as walking away, that he would "never salt his steak again". I don't really take kindly to people speaking so rudely to my mother. She was very polite about sending it back and certainly didn't deserve any attitude. We had an event to attend, which we were very clear with to our waitress. So when my mother reordered another meal (a salad), which took another twenty minutes, my mom asked for it to be immediately wrapped up. The chef returned, obviously chewing to make a point, and sneered at my mother, announcing her steak that she refused to eat was actually quiet good. Well, I have never felt more embarrassed in a situation. I said he was being rude to my mother, a paying customer. He proceeded to get heated and tell me it was not his job to make people happy (!? ? ! ) and that everyone else seemed to enjoy their meal and we just had to get to the inside of the steak to appreciate it. nnThe drama continued when he did not have change for $100 for a $57 bill. Sorry, how can you not have $33 in change?! He ended the night by throwing our bill in a crumbled mess on the floor and walking out. nnOverall, embarrassing. It was an embarrassing situation for anyone to witness. Take heed of the negative comments on this site. I certainly wish I would have.

Thus, I decided to test out Umi based on the good reviews I found on the web. Sadly, I was grossly disappointed. The interior decor is pretentious (large water fountain in the middle of the dining room and "Japanese style" paintings and decorations throughout) and dark (who likes eating in a place where you can barely see your food? ). The sushi was okay at best, but the quality of the fish certainly does not even come close to Chaya's. The dishes were overpriced for the quality of the food, basically you'd be paying for the fake ambiance. I guess the waiter was nice in that he tried to explain our dishes, but frankly he does not know too much. This is one of those Americanized versions of an Asian restaurant and not authentic (key indicator: most of the patrons were not Asian). It was nothing that would make me want to come back.

We love the indoor family and warm water pools. The nursery staff is excellent. I like that when we come in to drop the kids off at the nursery, they greet us by our first names. I love the infrared sauna they recently put in. nOne advice I would give to people who are considering of joining is that you don't have to get the most exclusive membership like gold cove, if you are only going to work out on the machines. We have gold cove, the most expensive membership which allows you to have exclusive access to outdoor water park during summer and most of the group fitness classes offered in the club but we hardly go to group fitness class and hardly go to outdoor water park. The water temperature is still cold even it is 80 degrees out. This past summer, we went to the outdoor water park maybe two- three times the most and stayed out no more than half hour. So, it really isn't worth to have a gold cove unless you know you are looking for specific classes with GC membership. nThe parking and getting the machine to work out can be a problematic during winter months between 4: 30-7: 00PM. Sometime, you may have to wait 15-20 minutes for the machine to free. If that's the case, you can go sit in the sauna until some people leave. So, I would suggest go there early in the morning or late in the evening but then if you are bringing along your little ones you run into problems with daycare after nursery is closed. They have a restaurant that offers supposedly healthier food but we never had any luck with the food there. The taste is okay and price is a little bit on the high end for the amount you get.

Not to mention there was a guy singing and playing an accordion on the night we went for dinner. Would have been fine except for the fact that there are 10 people too many in the place to begin with and then he wanders around the dining floor going table to table. Wanted to pay the guy to stop for 30 min while I ate! nnPasta dishes are way overpriced and honestly not that great. $20 for noodles and meat sauce.. . nnDinner specials/entrees are your best bet and were very good. but also way over prices. Didn't have desert but multiple people in my dinner party raved about one of the cakes they had. nn Simply put - this place does not execute the level of food their price points represent. I'm all for paying for a good meal but when it falls short.. .. and this place does! nnOverall a very disapointing night out.

Our waitress (Annemarie) was inattentive and got us the wrong order. We ended up with a dish we had not even ordered and still got charged for it on the check. nnOne of my friends ordered a Buffalo Chicken Sandwich, which according to the menu description, should've had lettuce and tomato in it. But the sandwich that arrived on our table had none of those ingredients. When pointed out to the waitress, she just brought some lettuce and tomatoes in a plate! How crude! nnSpoke to the manager after paying the full check and first of all, she did not even offer to re-imburse for the item we did not even order, and that had been forcibly thrust upon us. nnWhen I asked the manager about the Buffalo Chicken sandwich not having any lettuce and tomato, she said they just got new menus and that description is not correct and that Buffalo Chicken Sandwich is supposed to be without lettuce and tomato! What a lame excuse! Even your online menu says under the description for a Buffalo Chicken sandwich that its supposed to have both those ingredients - "Served with lettuce and tomato on a fresh-baked Kaiser roll. " - That's what the last line in the description of Buffalo Chicken Sandwich says. Shame on you Manager for lying! Instead of admitting her fault, she was blaming it to a new menu! nnNot only did we have to pay for something that we didn't order or eat, but we got sub-standard food that was different from what was described in the menu, and had to deal with a really bad waitress and insensitive and deceitful manager to top off the whole experience. nnI am never going to come back to any Eat N Park location ever again, and will never recommend one to any of my friends either. What a waste of time and money! Plus dealing with callous and lying people - my goodness, I surely had my patience tested this evening!

Peppi's rocks! From the moment you step inside, the lively atmosphere envelops you in a warm embrace. The charming decor, adorned with vibrant artwork and cozy seating, sets the perfect backdrop for an unforgettable dining experience. As you peruse the menu, your taste buds are tantalized by the enticing array of culinary delights on offer. From signature dishes bursting with bold flavors to classic favorites executed to perfection, Peppi's leaves no palate unsatisfied. The attentive and knowledgeable staff further elevate the dining experience, providing impeccable service with a genuine smile. They are quick to offer recommendations and ensure that every guest's dietary preferences and restrictions are catered to with finesse. Whether you're a meat lover, a vegetarian, or a seafood enthusiast, Peppi's menu has something to satisfy your cravings. The expertly crafted cocktails and extensive wine list complement the culinary offerings, enhancing the overall enjoyment of your meal. The bar staff's creativity shines through in every sip, using fresh ingredients and innovative combinations that leave you wanting more. As the night unfolds, live music fills the air, embodying the lively spirit of the restaurant and adding another layer of entertainment to your evening.Peppi's dedication to sourcing high-quality ingredients is evident in every mouthwatering bite. From farm-fresh produce to the finest cuts of meat, each dish showcases the commitment to culinary excellence. The portion sizes are generous, leaving even the heartiest of appetites fully satisfied. And let's not forget the delectable desserts that beckon you at the end of your meal, offering a sweet finale to your dining journey. Peppi's truly deserves accolades for its remarkable fusion of flavors, inviting ambiance, attentive service, and unwavering commitment to customer satisfaction.

nAs in overdone, but awesome. Hello, 2 story waterfall. The menu is exactly the same as Azteca's, so that's a plus, since Azteca is decent, veg-friendly tex-mex. Pretty sure they're all part of the same local chain. Service is always over the top terrific and we end up leaving a 40% tip often since the bill is always so low - the most expensive thing I've ever ordered here was 9. 50. nnChips are good and plentiful. Nothing here is blow you away, but most of it is passable, sometimes even good. Don't order the huevos rancheros - probably the least impressive thing on the menu. nnMy favs, at both Pedro's and Azteca, are the Enchiladas de Espinaca - rice, beans, and fresh spinach-stuffed flour tortillas smothered in queso sauce. Yeah, there's veggies in there, but this is heart attack land. nnAnother fav - machos burrito. I guess eating cactus puts hair on your chest. Its a pickle burrito. It is awesome. Have them add some fresh jalapenos for a kick. Its as big as your head, so you'll take some home - fry an egg, stick it on top, you got yourself 3 dollar brekkie. nnAnd hey, since Skandalos is right next store, you could stuff your face AND see mexican heavy metal, all in one night. Hell yeah.

...were likely iｍpressed by the restaurant's cozy atmosphere and attentive service. Thе menu offers a diverse selection of dishes, including vegetarian and gluten-free options, which are clearly labeled. The food is creɑtive, delicious, and plentiful, with generous portions that won't break the bank. The aromas are rich and well-balanced, with the perfect amount of spice. The presentation is also visually appealing, making each dish a feast for the eyes as wеll as the t aste buds. The service is friendly and prompt, with servers who are knowledgeable about the menu and happy to offer recommeոdations. The restaurant's decor is modern and stylish, with enjoyable seating and plenty of elbow room between tables. The ambinace is lively but not ovеrwhelming, making it a great spot for a date or a night out with friends. General, this restaurant is a hidden gem that is sure to impress even the most discerning diner. On the downside, the reѕtaurant does not have a liquor license, which may be a disappointment for some patrons. However, the crea𝚝ive mocktails and wine selection make up for the lack of hard alcohol. Additionally, the restaurant can get quite busy, especially on weekends, so reservationѕ are highly recommended to avoid a longer wait. In conclusion, this restaurant is a ｍust-try for anyone in the area. The delicious food, excellent serve, and cozy atmosphere make it a stand-out destination for a night out. Don't let the lck of a liquor license deter you – the restaurant's other offerings are sure to satisfy.

Maybe it's not perfectly authentic, but the food quality and quantity is excellent. It is definitely on par with any of the other similar restaurants I have Yelped. nnVery wide menu, which includes several vegetarian options as well as the usual carnivore stuff. The chips brought to our table were warm and fresh, along with two bowls of salsa. The mild one was just that. Mild. No spice at all. The hot was well peppery. I was able to mix one into the other to make one that was "just right" though it was still not the chunky style I prefer. nnThe food was served promptly and accurately. The rice was fresh and not crunchy or stale at all. My friend totally demolished his Texas-style burrito - I was sure he was about to start licking the plate! nnVery convenient, right off the highway at Carowinds Blvd. Great prices. Good drinks. nnWould I return? Absolutely!

Immediately upon walking in, it took 15 minutes for someone to ask if we were helped. When I mentioned that I wanted to learn more about a Zuma over in the corner, the dude at the desk was disinterested and unhelpful. nnWhen I tried to pay for the scooter and roll it out, an older woman stewed around and gave me annoyed looks for coming into the store and not calling ahead. When I tried to pay with a bank-issued check, I had to wait around for nearly 20 minutes before they told me that they have never seen a bank-issued check and sent me on my way. nnThe entire time I was there, it felt like they thought I just stole a bunch of money and were treating me like a crook. I am definitely not going back to complete the transaction. If I'm going to be buying an expensive piece of machinery, I really expect the service to at least be a little more enthusiastic. nnLike Nick T. said, I guess it's a good place if you wanna grab a used bike or know exactly what you want. If you're looking to broker a new bike, go to a bigger shop with salesmen that actually sound like they're living beings.

Maybe it's nostalgia, but we felt like it was an off night. The chicken special, which was our normal go-to, seemed out of place--chicken with couscous and yogurt at an Italian restaurant? We longed for the artichoke and risotto chicken specials from our time living off Walnut street. nnThe carne was lamb and the fish was salmon, so we ordered off the regular menu. The risotto was great, the Nero D Avola house wine was perfect, but it felt like the Girasole of our memories had slipped a bit. nnNo doubt it will be the first place we go next time we come back, but here's to hoping for a return to more robust specials. nnBTW, Jimmy Pie, the guy with the wine is always super friendly and accomodating. He always remembers my dad when my parents come to town, and he always gets us a table as quickly as he can. Yes, they are not always clear on reservations, but this place has been a staple for 10+ years and, with the small space, they do the best they can to accomodate everybody. It's the price you pay for authentic Italian food in a city where Bravo! and Olive Garden are consistently voted best Italian food.

All of a sudden, the tire warning signal lights up even though I don't remember running over anything. I get out of the car to carefully inspect my tires and they feel/look completely OK. But after owning a BMW for 3 years and having constant wheel alignment/tire issues (ie. when a tire warning signal lights up, it usually means my tire's about to blow out!! ), I freaked out and frantically drove my Infiniti to the first 3 auto shops on Baum Blvd. I waited at the first stop for 5-10 minutes and the guy did not get off the phone. I got back into the car and drove it over to Pep Boy's where a mechanic was outside smoking. He says I need to go through their service desk (inside their huge store) and deal with them first. I'm thinking, "ugh. no time. I have class in 40 minutes and I'm already late to my meeting. " I drive past the 3rd shop and I don't even see anyone sitting at the reception desk and figure it's going to take forever to get what I'm asking for. nnThen, I remembered the really positive experience I had with Walter's. I decided to drive to the end of Baum Blvd. to find his place and boy, am I glad I did. As soon as I got there, the service coordinator told me to pull my car around front so that their mechanic could take a look at my tires. He stopped what he was doing to do this and told me I could just stay in my car. Then, he reassured me that my tires were OK and he proceeded to inflate them, explaining to me why the signals might have come on. nnFree of charge, in and out within 5 minutes, and helpful/reassuring. I love this about Walter's Automative. They're not out just to make money. They genuinely just want to help. Highly reccommend! !

nnLidia's was a major disappointment for me. Decor and service were just fine, no issues there. nnBut the food was something I would expect from a chain restaurant like Carrabba's or Olive Garden. The quality seemed poor: bread was soggy, greens and salad dressings way over salted, greasy, sloppy pasta sauces, and huge portions just slapped onto dirty plates with greasy fingerprints all over the edges. Plus, I spent the entire meal swatting flies away from our table. nnI was really puzzled by this experience. When you dine at Joe's restaurants, you get the sense that every single ingredient is hand selected for the highest quality and the presentation of the food shows care went into it. I expected a similar standard at Lidia's. I wondered if Lidia visits this restaurant often and whether she knows about the quality of the food. Celebrity chefs often seem to suffer from this issue: they get involved in so many business ventures and if they are not at the restaurants regularly, the quality suffers. nnSorry to say it, but I definitely won't be back to Lidia's.

Sure, here's a polished version of the review:This restaurant boasts a diverse menu with options catering to vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free dietary needs. Their commitment to sustainability is laudable, evident in their eco-friendly packaging and locally sourced ingredients. The interior design exudes warmth and coziness, creating a welcoming ambiance.We began our culinary journey with their signature burger, which exceeded our expectations. The generous portion size was complemented by rich, well-balanced flavors. Our son relished the kids' mac and cheese, while my husband enjoyed the grilled chicken sandwich.The service was attentive and friendly, with the staff displaying knowledgeable expertise in their menu offerings. The wine and beer selection is extensive, with prices that are reasonable and won't break the bank.For dessert, we indulged in the chocolate lava cake, a creative twist on a classic favorite. It was the perfect way to cap off our dining experience, leaving us feeling content and satisfied.We highly recommend this restaurant to anyone seeking a delicious, sustainable dining experience with a cozy atmosphere. We look forward to our next visit!

Our night at Cain started out as 4/5 stars, but unfortunately, it quickly spiraled downhill from there. The evening began with a promising ambiance as we were greeted by friendly staff and seated promptly at a cozy table near the window. However, the menu left much to be desired, with limited options and a lack of variety for vegetarians. We settled for the recommended dishes, hoping they would make up for the lackluster selection.To our disappointment, the food failed to impress. The appetizers lacked flavor and originality, with the presentation falling flat. As for the main courses, they were underwhelming in both taste and portion size. The ingredients seemed ordinary and did not justify the exorbitant prices on the menu.Moreover, the service began to falter as the night progressed. Our server appeared to be overwhelmed and inattentive, forgetting drink orders and neglecting to check on us. It was clear that the staff was struggling to keep up with the increasing crowd, resulting in a chaotic and unsatisfying dining experience.To make matters worse, the noise level in the restaurant was intolerable. The combination of loud music and the conversations of other diners made it difficult to hold a conversation without raising our voices. It was evident that Cain prioritized aesthetics over creating a comfortable and enjoyable atmosphere for their patrons.The dessert menu offered a ray of hope, as we eagerly anticipated a sweet conclusion to salvage the evening. However, even that fell short of expectations. The dessert lacked creativity and tasted mediocre, leaving us with a sense of disappointment that lingered long after we left the premises.Overall, our night at Cain started with promise but quickly unraveled into a series of underwhelming experiences. The limited menu, lackluster dishes, and subpar service overshadowed the initial positive impressions.

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review:We would been driving for hours and were nervous to stretch our legs and enjoy a good meal. After fast search on our handheld, we came across a highly-recommended Italian restaurant just a few blocks away from our hotel. The name of the food was Pizzeria 311, and it sounded promising. We made our way there, making in the sights and sounds of the city as we walked. The restaurant was located in a charming neighborhood with plenty of retailers and bars nearby. When we arrive, we were greeted by a friendly host who showed us to our table. The atmosphere inside was cozy and appeal, with a warm and welcoming decor. The aroma of freshly baked pizza dough completed the air, making our mouths water in anticipation. We took a staring at the menu and were impressed by the variety of options. We ordered a lean appetizers to start, including bruschetta and a caprese salad. Both were luscious and fresh, with flavors that popped. For our main course, we elects for three different pizzas: a classic margherita, a meat-lovers, and a gluten pizza with roasted vegetables. The pizzas were cooked to perfection, with crispy crusts and generous garrisons. The flavors were rich and well-balanced, and we dinner every slice. We also ordered a side of pasta, which was spaghetti al dente and had a rich, creamy sauce. Roundabout our meal, the service was attentive and friendly. Our server was knowledgeable about the menu and made great recommends. The prices were reasonable, particularly considering the quality of the food. Overall, our experience at Calzone 311 was excellent. The catering was delicious, the atmosphere was cozy, and the service was top-notch. We would highly recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a great food in Pittsburgh. In made, we're already looking forward to our next visit.

After reading so many yelp comments that read "It's okay for Champaign, but nothing like what I can get back home in California", I'm happy to say...This place is awesome! I'm from the South, and I know good barbecue. This place is the real deal. The ribs are fall-off-the-bone tender, and the pulled pork is moist and flavorful. The sides are also excellent, especially the mac and cheese and the baked beans.I've been to a lot of barbecue restaurants in Champaign, and this is by far the best. The food is consistently good, and the service is always friendly and welcoming. I highly recommend this place to anyone who loves barbecue.I've been to this restaurant a few times now, and I've tried a variety of different dishes. Everything I've had has been delicious, but my favorites are the ribs and the pulled pork. The ribs are always fall-off-the-bone tender, and the pulled pork is moist and flavorful. The sides are also very good, especially the mac and cheese and the baked beans.The staff at this restaurant is always very friendly and welcoming. They're always happy to make recommendations, and they're always willing to go the extra mile to make sure you have a good experience.The prices at this restaurant are very reasonable. You can get a full rack of ribs for under $20, and the pulled pork is even cheaper. The sides are also very affordable, and you can get a large order of mac and cheese or baked beans for under $5.This restaurant is located in a convenient spot, right off the interstate. It's easy to get to, and there's plenty of parking.The atmosphere at this restaurant is very casual and relaxed. It's a great place to come with friends or family, and you can always expect to have a good time.The food at this restaurant is always fresh and delicious. The ribs are always fall-off-the-bone tender, and the pulled pork is moist and flavorful. The sides are also very good, especially the mac and cheese and the baked beans.

I have been on a quest to find the perfect stylist for my curly hair, a task that has proved daunting after years of struggling with unruly locks. However, my search reached an end when I discovered the incredible talent of Lisa, a stylist at a local salon. From the moment I stepped into the salon, it was apparent that Lisa possessed extensive knowledge and experience working with curly hair. She greeted me warmly and immediately put me at ease, assuring me that she understood how to bring out the best in my curls. During the consultation, Lisa thoroughly examined my hair type, texture, and the specific challenges I faced. Her attention to detail was impressive as she asked thoughtful questions and genuinely listened to my concerns. With her expert recommendations, I felt confident in her abilities and eagerly scheduled an appointment for a cut and style. On the day of my appointment, Lisa exceeded all my expectations. She transformed my unruly mane into a beautifully defined cascade of curls that I had never believed possible. Not only did she give me a fantastic cut that complemented my face shape, but she also generously shared valuable tips and tricks for maintaining and styling my hair at home. Throughout the process, Lisa's passion for her craft shone through. She explained each step she took and educated me on the best products suited to my hair type. Her personalized approach and genuine care for her clients set her apart from any other stylist I have encountered. When I left the salon, I felt like a new person, filled with renewed confidence in my curls. Without a doubt, Lisa has earned the title of the ultimate stylist for curly hair in my book. I highly recommend her to anyone in search of exceptional care and expertise for their curly locks.

I believe it. Business at Ullrich's has been good recently, especially since they moved into their present location. Ullrich's has been around in one form or another for over 80 years and a couple of years ago they moved to their present location. nnUllrich's offers a full line of services including shoe and purse repair, desalt and waterproofing, and jacket and zipper repair. Can't make it into their store? You can even send your shoes to be repaired via mail! No, really! Check it out! http: //www. ullrichshoerepair. com/mailorder. htmnnAnyway, my husband Rick asked me to drop off his shoes to get them resoled here. I just picked them up on lunch a few minutes ago and they look practically new. He had me drop off his leather jacket to have a tear repaired last year and they did a fantastic job. I've had one of my purses repaired here too. We've always been satisfied with their work. nnIt's so good to see people renovate and repair rather than throw out. Rick and I have been doing that for years.

Here's a possible continuation of the review, starting from the second sentence:I love Espresso Royale, but this particular location is lacking. The staff is friendly and the coffee is always good, but the atmosphere is just not there. It's always so loud and crowded, and it's hard to relax and enjoy your coffee. Plus, the prices are a bit high for what you get.I've been to other Espresso Royale locations that are much nicer. The one in downtown is my favorite. It's in a historic building, and it has a really cozy, relaxed atmosphere. The staff is great, and the coffee is always fresh.The Espresso Royale in the suburbs is also really nice. It's in a shopping center, but it's still managed to create a warm and inviting atmosphere. The staff is friendly and efficient, and the coffee is always good.If you're looking for a great cup of coffee, I highly recommend Espresso Royale. But if you're looking for a place to relax and enjoy your coffee, I would recommend going to a different location.

If you are looking for this place to be "family-friendly", than do not sit in the bar area or do not bring children there after 9pm. nnI frequent this particular TGIF's a lot, but I only sit in the bar area because I usually plan to only have drinks and appetizers and to enjoy the excitement of the bartenders and the numerous new people I meet there while in the bar. nnI went with a friend on this particular visit to have a girls' night out, have some drinks and have fun! And boy did we definitely have some fun! We ordered the pretzels with the craft beer cheese dipping sauce and chips and salsa as our appetizers. The pretzel appetizer is my favorite and I always get it when I go to TGIF's, the craft beer cheese dipping sauce is very good and is mixed with green onions and bacon pieces. So to wash down the pretzels and chips and salsa we had to order tasty and refreshing drinks. TGIF's has a full service bar and the bar is fully stocked with the best alcohol and they have a wide variety of alcoholic beverages, from the traditional drinks to their signature ones. My friend had an Appletini and I had their signature drink, the Captain Morgan Island Rum Punch. The bartenders there on this night stuck their feet in these drinks, because they were AMAZING! The drinks were not watered down, I could taste the alcohol, and we got our money's worth. nnAfter sitting at the bar for awhile, we started to get hungry again, so we ordered an entree and both got the salmon with maison butter, mashed potatoes and the vegetable medley (zucchini, squash and red peppers). It did not take long for our food to come out, our food was hot and very tasty. We ate all of it! nnI recommend their bar service to everyone, but do be prepared for it to be crowded, loud and busy, so sit back, grab a drink and have a good time!

Stopped by Empire yesterday for some uqick takeout. The service was amazing, and they gave us free cooies! We ordered the Nashville hot chicken sandwCch, the pork belly banh mi, and the duck confit mac and cheese. The chicken sandwich was perfectly cispy and spicy, and the pork belly banh mi was flavorful and delicious. The duck confit mbac and cheese was rich and decadent, and the duck was perfectly cooked.
