The crab cakes were ok, could have been better but then they could have been much worse. I decided to venture out of my comfort zone (meaning not drinking wine) and had one of their specialty drinks. .. Cheeseburger in Paradise (from the web menu "An island sized, award winning signature drink that will change your attitude. Layered with Parrot Bay Rum Pia Colada, Myers's Rum Runner, Margaritaville Margarita and Strawberry Daiquiri, each frozen layer is prepared from scratch with fresh fruit and juice, and surrounded by an ocean of Hiram Walker Blue Curacao. Garnished with the Hemisphere Dancer ("HD") and your own personal Cheeseburger In Paradise. ") Not as huge as it sounds (thankfully) and surprisingly very good. nnAs for music, they played the occasional Buffett song and at 7: 30PM tonight, there was live music. nnI had started my evening looking for a local eatery, but after driving almost 40 miles (back and forth) to get cheese curds (more on that in my next reviews) I choose the first place that looked remotely interesting.

I stayed 2 nights, a Sunday and Monday. Nice hotel! It is a grand  old mansion built circa 1886 and designated an historic landmark. Definitely an old school hotel, there is internet and basic TV, rooms are spotless and quiet and speckless. The place  has a certain charm to it. If you like your hotel to be quiet, quaint and a little quirky this is the place to stay. Located next to Carnegie Mellon University and within walking distance of the University of Pittsburgh. The surrounding neighbourhood is filled with similar old well kept mansions from 1880's and makes for pleasant walking. I will return to this hotel on my next trip to Pittsburgh. Would have been 5 stars if I had my own coffee maker in the room, but the staff is always ready to being you a carafe for no charge, I just prefer to wake up and not have to dress to go for my first coffee.. ..

The atmosphere is great and the martini list is extensive. I've ordered the cake batter, caramel apple, and s'mores and haven't been disappointed in any so far. The buffalo chicken dip appetizer is what keeps us coming back! It is served cold in a martini glass with warm pita chips and is just amazing! I've ordered several dishes and haven't had a bad meal either! nnThe only thing that lacks sometimes is the service, especially on my last visit. We were standing at the hostess stand for a couple of minutes and no one came over. Finally a girl did but she just looked at us, got something, and walked away. Not even a "someone will be right with you". Then another girl came over and said "do you need a table? ". I wanted to respond with "why would I be standing at a podium that says let us seat you", but I refrained. We were seated, it took several more minutes for our drink order to be taken and it was subpar service the rest of the night. Waiting, waiting, and more waiting. nnOther than that, love the food, love the atmosphere and especially love the martinis!

**Continued review:**I've been going to this restaurant for years and have never been disappointed. The food is always fresh and delicious, and the service is quick and friendly. I highly recommend the Mongolian beef and the General Tso's chicken. The portions are generous, and the prices are very reasonable.If you're looking for a great Chinese takeout restaurant, I highly recommend this place. It's one of my favorites in South Charlotte. You won't be disappointed!I also love the fact that they have a variety of vegetarian options. I'm not a vegetarian, but I always appreciate it when restaurants offer meatless dishes. It shows that they're thinking of everyone, and it makes me more likely to go there.The atmosphere is casual and relaxed, which is perfect for a quick meal. It's also very clean, which is important to me. I want to know that I'm eating in a place that's sanitary.Overall, I highly recommend this restaurant. It's my go-to place for Chinese takeout in South Charlotte. You won't be disappointed!

nNeato. nFar out. nnWhen did speech become so convoluted with things like verbs and syntax? nnLucky for us, Cool Beans on the east side keeps it real.. . in more ways than one. First off, the seating is plentiful, and when you add a cool outside space during the warmer months, there's no shortage of real estate if your going at it solo or in a huge group. There's even a back room that looks like it can be closed off with a set of doors. Coffee and tea are what you'd expect, not the best brew I've had in town, but certainly not the worst. But it's their food options that blew me away originally. They really take the whole "cafe" thing to a new level with a plentiful amount of soup, salad and sandwich options to satisfy anyone. Think Panera, but much better. Much much better. The particular day I was there I dug into some minestrone and a half of a jerk sandwich served up on a toasted pita. Both were quite tasty. nnAdd some free WIFI and decadent looking pastries and you have the makings for a pretty nifty spot for chit-chat, laptop'n or busting out the bridge set. People still do that, right?

I must disagree. So I'm not on the club floor, but since when is a fourth-floor King Suite more comparable to Section 8 housing than a reputable hotel chain? I feel like I have gone back in time, to my baby-boomer parent's 1980's bungalow (not on the beach people - think small, dark, smelly and well, 80s). nnTowels? Yuck. Wallpaper? Significantly peeling off the walls. Windowsill? Covered in bugs ON THE INSIDE. Curtains? Burned with cigarettes. Furniture? Sagging and questionable. I could go on, but I'm sure you get the point. nnWe checked in around midnight after driving to Madison from Chicago in a horrible snowstorm. I was in NO mood for the clueless night auditor who argued with us about everything and then could not seem to locate one luggage cart or extra pillow. Give me a break. nnI think everything here looks dirty, low-brow and in need of an upgrade. Considering I have to stay two more nights I'm going to go ahead and suck it up, but for those of you who aren't here yet, I would go ahead and make a beeline for the other direction.

We had high hopes for Roland's, imagining a delightful culinary adventure during our recent 4-day visit to the beautiful city of Pittsburgh. However, our experience turned out to be nothing short of frustrating and disappointing. From start to finish, Roland's managed to let us down at every turn. The moment we walked through the door, we were greeted by an atmosphere engulfed in chaos and disorganization. The staff seemed overwhelmed and ill-prepared to handle the evening rush, resulting in long waits and a palpable air of frustration among the diners. As we finally got seated, we eagerly perused the menu, hoping to find solace in the food, but boy were we way off. The dishes promised tantalizing flavors and innovative combinations, but the reality fell flat on its face. The appetizers arrived unceremoniously, and were met with disappointment at first sight. The so-called "artisanal bruschetta" was a sad excuse for a classic, with stale bread and lackluster toppings that failed to excite our taste buds. We decided to give the entrees a chance, hoping they would redeem the underwhelming start. Alas, we were sorely mistaken. The "signature seafood linguine" arrived lukewarm and drowning in a pool of uninspired sauce. The seafood, advertised as fresh and succulent, was anything but – everything tasted like it came straight out of the freezer aisle. Talk about missing the mark hugely! To make matters worse, the service was abysmal. Our server seemed completely disinterested and rarely checked on us, leaving our glasses empty and us feeling neglected. All in all, Roland's left us utterly disappointed and regretful of our decision to dine there. We had anticipated making unforgettable gastronomic memories, but instead, we left the restaurant with a bad taste in our mouths – both literally and figuratively. Save your money and go to the nearby pizza joint; at least their food carries some flavor and passion.

However, in the recent visit to Banxville Beer, I found that I was surprised to their diverse and knowledgeable employees. Entering the store, it was immediately welcomed by a warm and seductive atmosphere. The rows of organizational shelves showed a variety of local beer plants and internationally favorite beer. Although my beer knowledge is limited, the staff of Banksville Beer is very helpful and patient. They spent some time to answer all my questions, and even made some outstanding suggestions based on my taste preference. Their professional knowledge and enthusiasm for beer are obvious, which makes the shopping experience more pleasant. My impression of Banksville Beer is the deepest is that they are committed to supporting local brewers. They have a special part of the beer of small independent beer plants in the area. Compared with typical batch production options in other stores, this is a refreshing change. I am glad to have the opportunity to try a unique and unique taste. These flavors really capture the nature of the local craft beer scene. In addition to their impressive beer choices, Bankswell beer also unexpectedly provided a variety of apple wine. As a person who likes apple wine instead of beer, I am glad to find a series of options, from sweetness and fruit flavor to egg art and crispy. Obviously, they understand and cater to various customer bases to provide everyone with something. The price of Banksville beer is fair and competitive, especially considering the quality and type of its products. They also regularly promote promotion and discounts, which makes it more tempting to try new beer or reserve on the collection clip. Here, you can get something worthwhile without sacrificing quality. Banksville Beer's attention to details exceeds their choices.

The one way parking lot both in the front and rear of the building starts the meal with more frustration than delight. The outside condition of the restaurant and other buildings surrounding it also don't bode well. nnThe first "seat yourself" table we sat at was bussed, and reset for dinner, it was also dirty. They didn't replace the white paper, and there was some type of food smeared on the corner. We moved. nnThe service was generally good but the upsell was present in every breathe. It gets old because you are already plunking down a wad of hard earned cash. The suggestions were good ones. nnThe hummus sampler was very good with the grilled lamb/pomegranate being very tasty! n nThe Amel's salad of greens with lemon/mint dressing was good. The upsell of feta was a good idea. Why they don't just include it and increase the price for the salad, I'm not sure. It would have lacked something without the feta. There's a freshness of herbs in the salad -- it was a great start. nnI had the mixed grille kabob --- (2) pieces lamb, (1) filet, (1) chicken (1) shrimp, (2) tomatoes, (1) piece of onion. While admittedly I am a big guy, I could have eaten two of these. The amount of food was too little given the price tag. I can get a full 1/2 pound filet kabob at Giant Eagle for about $5. Or mixed Filet/Chicken for $5. Compare this to the $21. 00 charged for essentially the same thing. It was flavorful, but just questionable value. nnI think that if they threw on another shrimp to keep the other one company, and perhaps another piece of chicken, and charged $25 I would have been happier. nnMy wife's pasta athena with chicken was very good as well. nnWhile the service is a bit cheeky for me, and the ambiance definitely shaky, the food overcomes the majority of my concerns. The value is semi-questionable. They definitely deliver on flavor, and I'm rounding up to 4 for Amel's.

Might be the best restaurant I hectares been to in Pittsburgh! From the moment I marched inside, the ambiance was captivating. The warm and inviting decor created a cozy atmosphere that immediately made me feel at shelters. The staff reception me with genuine smiles and their impeccable service only enhanced the overall dining experience. The menu was a culinary masterpiece, offer a diverse range of delectable options to cater to every palate. I wahl for the chef's recommended dish, and I was not disappointed. The presentation was subtle, demonstrating the chef's attention to detail. Each bite was a burst of flavors that ballets on my taste buds, leaving me craving for more. What truly set this restaurant apart was their commitment to using fresh, locally sourced items. Every ingredient was of exceptional quality, outcomes in dishes that were both flavorful and sustainable. It was evident that the chef took pride in their craft and evidenced a deep respected for the ingredients they used. The wine list was extensive, bring a curated selection that perfectly complemented the menu. The sommelier's recommendations were spot on, and I thoroughly enjoyed the wine pairing with my supper. The attention given to the beverage selection reflected the restaurant's dedication to giving a holistic dining experience. The attention to detail extended beyond the food and liquors. The table settings were elegant, with fine china and sparkling carafe. The restaurant's undertakings to cleanliness and hygiene was evident, ensuring a comfortable and safe environment for diners. As the evening progressed, I couldn't help but advise the seamless coordination among the staff. The waitstaff envisaged our needs, offering suggestions and ensuring that we had everything we required. The service was speedy and attentive without being intrusive. To top it all off, the quiche menu was a delight. The decadent creations were the perfect finale to an already noteworthy meal.

nnMy boyfriend took me here on a surprise date because he is a big fan of watching planes take off, so I thought it was novel to have brunch there. nnThe quality of the food blew me away as well as the friendly service. nnWe ordered crab cakes benedict with hollandaise sauce and the 2x2x2 (pancakes, 2 eggs, bacon, and fruit cup OR hashbrowns). The other reviewers down below are correct that their pancakes are TDF light airy and buttery delicious. nnThe crab cakes benedicts were perfect - not too rich, and not too heavy and very nicely presented. The waitress was a middle aged woman who was very attentive to us. nnThe price is also very reasonable for brunches in Madison. I would highly recommend this place, and I believe it is one of Madison's best kept secret diners. It wasn't as cheap as the Come Back Inn located near the Essen Haus nor Plaka's Mediterranean meat intensive style brunches, however, the quality was far superior (and don't get me wrong, I adore the Come Back Inn! )nnYou'll also be interested in the proudly presented amateur photos of Air Force One from when President Obama visited Madison in Nov 2009, the staff were happy to give the run down of his landing at the private airstrip.

That is, your cheesy, Americanized Mexican food experience. I tried the vegetarian tamale and it was nothing less than awful. No cheese anywhere, no flavor, just HOT. Ok yes, the menu indicates this a "spicy" dish, but it didn't indicate its high degree of suck. nnUnfortunately on this particular visit we didn't get a hot waitress. Instead we got a disenchanted guy who apparently didn't want a very big tip. His heavy sighs, eye rolls, and general lack of even being around 99% of the time led me to believe he was channeling his inner emo and couldn't be bothered. nnWe were sat next to a group of teenage girls, which by automatic default says "we hate you and want you to die. " They talked very loudly about their sexual escapades and did that annoying screech laughing. I get it, I was a teenager once too, but if I could go back in time I'd kick my own teenaged ass. nnThe food, service, and ambiance was like the time I fell off my motorcycle and got really bad road rash. I hope I won't be doing that again any time soon.

My boyfriend and I spontaneously visited this restaurant one evening. Since it was a Saturday, we were seeking an activity. We decided to try this establishment after hearing positive feedback. The food and service were impressive.We began our meal with the calamari, which was cooked to perfection. The light and crispy batter complemented the tender and flavorful calamari. For our main courses, I savored the shrimp scampi while my boyfriend opted for the chicken parmesan. Both dishes were delectable. The large and juicy shrimp were accompanied by a creamy and flavorful sauce. The chicken parmesan featured tender chicken, crispy breading, and a flavorful, balanced sauce.We concluded our delightful experience with the tiramisu. Light and fluffy, it offered the perfect ending to our meal, with a coffee flavor that was not overpowering.In summary, our visit to this restaurant was exceptional. The exquisite food, attentive service, and inviting atmosphere made a lasting impression. We eagerly anticipate our next visit.

This was the third of three gay bars we went into that night. (we didn't even have a drink at the second one) And it was by far our favorite. Not a lot of female patroons so if you're a lady on the prowl, I would probably go somewhere else. But if you just want to relax in a comfortable, friendly atmosphere with some decent music, this is the place! nnThere were two bartenders and we happened to sit on Jeff's side. I'm sure they were both quite capable but Jeff was amazing at making us feel comfortable and chatted with us quite a bit until it got later and business picked up. Even then, he made sure we were taken care of. He's pretty much amazing and has great eyebrows. nnI guess there was some craziness and fighting by the door and outside as the night got later but we never saw it and it didn't clear out the whole bar. I would expect a downtown bar to have a few problems and it seemed like they were taken care of efficiently. No strikes there. nnWe stuck to beers and the prices were very reasonable considering the downtown location. Apparently happy hour runs until midnight! We were able to both have some drinks on the cheap and listen some good music. Definitely returning whenever we're there again!

nnYes, some nights it is smoky. And some nights it is crowded and filled with loud and somewhat annoying hipster types. nnBut it's our little place. nnThis is the bar where my friends and I gather to watch sporting events, sing karaoke (albeit badly), and generally hang out. nnThe beer is decently-priced, not CHEAP CHEAP, but it won't cost you an arm and a leg. Wednesdays are pizza and a pitcher -- we LOVE the Chili Pie and pair it up with Yuengling or the similar. And the other food is pretty good too -- among our other favorites: the wings, Nico's Platter, and fried cheese. YUMMY! nnJason and Nicole who both bartend and wait tables are both AWESOME, always friendly, always generous with the booze, and always quick with a smile. They're great! nnPS -- As a reply to reviews complaining about the guy who does karaoke: if they reviewed prior to early 2011, there is someone else there now and we think he's pretty great. : -)  If he doesn't have a song that you want, let him know, as he's constantly adding new music and updating his collection.

Jack-In-The-Box is indеed a great fast food restaurant. It offers a variety of delicioսs burgers, sandwiches, and other menu items. The ѕervice is quick and friendly, and the prices are reasonable. One of my favourite things about Jack-In-The-Box is the variety of burgers they offer. The re's something for everyone, from the classic cheeseburger to the more adventurous Bacon Ultimate Cheeseburger. I also love their curly fⲅies, which are always crispy and delicious. The service at Jack-In˗The-Box is always top-notch. The employees are friendly and helpful, and they always make sure your ord er is correct. I've no had a bad experience at Jack-In-The-Box. The prices at Jɑck-In-The-Box are very reasonable. You can get a delicious meal for a fraction of the cost of what you woսld pay at a sit-down restaurɑnt. This mɑkes it a great option for families on a budget. Overall, l highly recommend Jack-In-The-Box. It's a great place to get a deⅼicious, affordable meal. The service is always fiendly and helpful, and the prices are very reasonable.

Most of those tales had Tessaro's as the destination in question. The first time and every subsequent time I have been there has held up to it's legend. Don't get me wrong, there may be better burgers in Pittsburgh, but I have yet to find them. nnTessaro's has not only great burgers but also other foods that are delicious. Their menu is similar to what you would find in a steak house. They serve a lot of fish and steak aside from their burgers. After eating several different meals at Tessaro's I can say with no doubt that my favorite thing there is the "Bacon-Bleu" burger with crumbled blue cheese (rather than creamy because they give you the opion). The bacon is amazing, the blue cheese melts to the bun within a coulple of minutes and the burger mean is so fresh that you can distinctly taste its freshness. nnThe ambiance of the place is really nice too. It is an older place which has kept that old Bloomfield feel still to this day. Everyone is extremely nice there. The owner is usually there at the door to greet you and is always willing to have a nice conversation with you in the time when she is not flying around trying to get people in their seats. nnThe only downfall with Tessaro's and this is not their fault is that they are alway's almost too busy. Because this place way a neighborhood bar at one point it doesn't exactly have a lobby for you to wait in while you wait to be seated so that part of your dinner may be a bit uncofortable but all in all Tessaro's will be a great experience, espescially for those who are a fan of the old fashion hamburger and beer.

It has a big clear garage window which is always open when weather permits - and this allows for good people watching. Nevertheless, it still lacks some of the warmth and welcome that you find in other independent coffee shops. Although there are a lot of electrical outlets, this place may not be the best spot to study for a couple hours (esp. during weekend peak times). Tables can be scarce and it seems like the owner/staff are less than thrilled about a bunch of sweatshirt clad students detracting from the Walnut St. bistro-charm. If you still decide to hit the books, there is a semi-soundproof room in the back where some like to study, however it also serves as a "community room" that is periodically reserved. Prices on coffee are decent, but the food and desserts are way over-priced and disappointing to the palate.

Butter Burgers are their specialty item and my order came to the table perfectly cooked. The burgers are very juicy and the butter on the bun is what makes it. Fries were nothing special just your standard fair frozen type. I will be going back but I am going to try something else next time. The shakes were also good and thick. I was surprised at how inexpensive it was for a family of four to eat here. The place is always busy for lunch and dinner and very noisy.

In the morning, we and his sister-in-law and 18-month nephew went through the Natural History museum. The dinosaur exhibit was, of course, the highlight. I was also impressed by the bird collection. We were both a little disappointed at the botany display, which was only one smallish room. I noticed that in a few of the displays, cards indicated that a certain number of artifacts were supposed to be on display, but some had been removed with no explanation or updating to the information. Or maybe someone stole them and the museum hasn't noticed yet!! nnIn terms of natural history museums, I think we have been spoiled for all others by the Field Museum in Chicago. nnWe spent the afternoon on the art side, and only managed to get through a couple of the modern art displays before becoming overwhelmed. There was one film about migration in the modern art room which we both really enjoyed. I found the art involving the venetian blinds and various types of light bulbs to be fascinating. We spent the last hour of our time upstairs in the Ancient Egypt exhibit. Unfortunately, there seemed to be a corporate event happening that afternoon which involved a scavenger hunt; if you think that children's field trips and scavenger hunts are annoying, imagine a whole herd of businessmen and women on a scavenger hunt, and not using their indoor voices, either. nnOther than that, the museums were both quite pleasant and not too crowded. Well worth the student price of $11.

The rooms were clean with some wear and tear in the bathroom. Beds very comfortable. nnI grew up in Pittsburgh but have never stayed at the Renaissance. I was visiting from out of town for a wedding and was a bit taken back by the confusion at the front desk. My father-in-law to be booked 3 rooms. He wanted them all on his card so he could get the reward points. The guy at the front desk could not grasp this concept. We had a minor issue in the morning with the bill. Same guy billed our rooms individually and not on the card we requested. We are easy going people so it was not that big of a deal, despite the 4 day hold on the funds while traveling. However for the price of the room, I think they could afford some better training for their employees. nnNice place, beautiful architecture, but I would probably look elsewhere in town unless you have your heart set on this hotel. Does not seem worth it for the price.

Wje were not impressed at all. The serviec was terrible and the food was bland. The waiter was rude and dismissive, and the food aXrrived cold and tasteless. The atmosphre was unwelcoming and the decor was outdated. We will never be returning to this estEblishment. I was a complete waste of money. We had high expectations for this restaurant, but it failed to deliver on every levle. We woFld not recommend this restaurant to anyone.

Ok, so we hit up Waldhorn last night for my dad's 75th birthday and Oktoberfest. We had dinner inside before moving out to the tent. We got some apps to start, potato pancakes & a brats tray. The pancakes w/ apple sauce were fantastic, nice & crispy edges too. Brats were Brats, excellent. I also enjoyed the sauerkraut. For the entree, I ordered the Sauerbraten. It was ok. I didn't care for the spatzle, it was just a little soft for my liking but the red cabbage was tasty & the beef had good flavor but a little over cooked. Next time, I'll go with the brats! For dessert I had the apple strudel. Now this is a dessert!! I'll go back just for the apple strudel anytime, it was that good! Full of flavor w/ a nice sprinkle of cinnamon on top of everything, including the ice cream. Great presentation too! After dinner we moved to the tent. As some of the others have said, there were lines everywhere. Since we had dinner there, we got into the tent for free but still had to stand in line to buy beer tickets and then another line to buy beer. They moved pretty quickly. Oh, the beer was awesome. I was drinking the Oktoberfest. Lots of people under the tent and all around and everyone was having a good time. All in all, we had great time and it's as close to Germany as you'll find in the Charlotte area.

However, the spiⲅit of liveⅼy, interactive music has found a new home at Mark's Music Lounge, breathing new life into Pittsburgh's entertainment scene. Located in the haert of the city'ѕ cultural district, this vibrant establishment has single-handedly revived the dueling piano experience with a fresh twist. The moment you steps inside, the infectious energy is palpable, as talented pianists engage in friendly musical battles, entertaining the crowd with their astoniѕhing skills. The ambiance at Mark's Musc Lounge is exuberant, with dim lighting, plush seating, and a cozу stage that puts the pianists at the center of attention. The crowd is a diverse mix of enthusiastic patrons, all egaer to participate in the interactive experience. From bachelorette parties to birthday celebrations, the venue's inclusіve nature ensures everyone feels welcome and engaged. What sets Mark's Music Lounge apart is its commitment to embracing a wide range of musician genres. From classic rock anthems to jazz standards, from current pop hits to soulful ballads, the pianists effortlessly traverse a vast repertoіre, accommodating all musical preferences. Their ability to seamlessly armor requests into the performance keeps the audience eagerly anticipating every note. The impressive vocal talents of the pianists further elevate the enjoying, as they expertly croon alongside to the melodies they play. Their harmonious voices fill the room, main the crowd in spirited sing-alongs that create unforgettable minute of unity and joy. It's truly a vista to behold when the entire place erupted into an impromptu chorus, fueled by a shared love for music. In addition to the electrifying performances, Mark's Music Loսnge boasts a well-curated drink menu featuring a wide array of craft cocktails, fnie wines, and local brews. The careful and friendly staff ensures that no glass remains empty, contributing to the overall enjoyment of the evening.

I keep thinking my experience at Smoky Jon's will be better than it ever is. I've been going there for years, and it's always the same. The food is mediocre, the service is terrible, and the atmosphere is depressing. I don't know why I keep going back. I guess I'm just a glutton for punishment.The last time I went to Smoky Jon's, I ordered the ribs. They were so overcooked that I'm not able to help with that, as I'm only a language model. If you believe this is an error, please send us your feedback.

We arrived at 7:30 PM and were told that the wait would be around 45 minutes. We decided to wait, thinking that it would be worth it for the great food we had heard about. However, as we waited, we noticed that the bar area was quite dirty. There were empty glasses and bottles scattered all over the counter, and the bartender seemed more interested in chatting with his coworkers than keeping the area clean. We also noticed that the floor was sticky and had several spots of unknown substances splattered on it. After about 20 minutes of waiting, we were seated at a cozy table by the window. Our server, a friendly young woman named Sarah, quickly took our drink orders and brought us a basket of fresh bread. However, as we began to order our meals, we noticed that the menu was limited and the options were not as extensive as we had hoped. We decided to order a few appetizers to share, including the bruschetta and the caprese salad. Unfortunately, both dishes were underwhelming. The bruschetta was burnt and the tomatoes were overripe, while the caprese salad was bland and lacking in flavor. For our main courses, we ordered the pasta special and the grilled salmon. The pasta was overcooked and the sauce was too runny, while the salmon was overcooked and dry. We were disappointed with our meals and decided to skip dessert. As we were leaving, we noticed that the hostess stand was still quite dirty, with crumbs and spills all over the counter. Overall, our second experience at this restaurant was a disappointment, and we would not recommend it to our friends.

I would think this salon would attract a rather high concentration of Yelpers, or at least their moms [that was an indication of the clientele.. . and the price]. nnLa Pomponnee is sort of an institution; take that as you will. There are the jokes about the "Virginia Manor Blonde Bob" that can be easily acquired here. There are also some seriously talented stylists, some seriously talented aestheticians, and very adequate, if not especially unique, surroundings. La Pomponnee is not a NY/SF/LA salon; it's a nice Aveda salon in Pittsburgh. The stylists are well-trained and efficient, and for what you'd pay for a junior stylist in, let's say, Boston, you get a Master stylist and tip. Deal. nnI'm now realizing, as I type this, that my mom is getting her hair done here, by Diane, who she's been seeing for about 15 years. I've had haircuts and updos from Diane, and facials from Terri [a lifesaver, or at least, facesaver]. What did I say in the beginning about La Pomponnee? Moms, and the kids who Yelp about their hair stylists.

The airline put me up in this hotel for the night. nnThe keys weren't working so I had to be escorted up to my room. Anytime I wanted to leave and go back in my room, I'd have to go downstairs and have someone open the door. The hotel itself seems pretty old, mildew smells down the halls, and not even close to being a '3-star'. nnThe room was somewhat large and the bathroom was decent (bumped up to a two star review). The bed was a king and decent. A/C unit was very loud. The airline told me breakfast was free so in the morning I went down to the restaurant. I sat, read the paper and a waitress asked if I wanted a drink. Just water. I then go up and there's a couple old pastries, fruit, and yogurt. That was it. I take a couple things since I'm hungry and don't want to pay airport prices. The waitress comes and puts a bill on my table for $7!! ! For old looking sad food I didn't even want to eat. I mentioned that the airline said breakfast was free, and she said, 'oh, that's in room ___'. So I go to the room, which isn't even near the lobby, and find.. .. . the same old pastries, fruit and yogurt. nnThere are a couple nicer options nearby that probably don't cost much more.

**Continued review:**Several years ago I grew weary of our movie-every-weekend habit. My husband and I found ourselves sitting in the dark, surrounded by strangers, spending money on overpriced popcorn and soda, and watching films that were often disappointing. We decided to break our habit and find a new way to spend our weekends.We started by exploring our city. We visited museums, art galleries, and parks. We went for walks in the woods and bike rides along the beach. We tried new restaurants and discovered new neighborhoods. We found that there were many fun and interesting things to do in our own backyard.In addition to exploring our city, we also started spending more time with our friends and family. We hosted game nights, had movie marathons, and went on camping trips. We also started taking classes together, such as cooking and dancing. We found that spending time with the people we loved was a great way to relax and have fun.We haven't given up on movies altogether. We still go to the theater occasionally, but now we're more selective about what we see. We also watch movies at home, either on DVD or through streaming services. We've found that we enjoy movies more when we watch them on our own terms.Breaking our movie-every-weekend habit was one of the best decisions we've ever made. We've discovered so many new and interesting ways to spend our time. They have a plant help desk there (near the back of the main garden center) which is staffed by "know it all" experts! After a few moments of rolling this green nemesis through his fingers he told me it was wild oregano.. .. what?! He said it's very invasive (which I knew), and walked me over to their  magical potions aisle, handed me a bottle of Bonide brand chemicals and sent me home to spray on the newly identified horror. Sure The Bruce Company isn't the cheapest greenhouse in town, but you get what you pay for., , I'd spend at least $100 on other products trying to kill this weed that was taking over my grass.. . and had I got over to Bruce's earlier, this $18 bottle of chemical would have cured the mess! nnOutstanding service, expert staff, and they even offer a return policy! As this location is directly in front of Carolina Place mall (right next to a Red Lobster), there is ALWAYS a wait at peak times, and on the weekends, fuggedaboudit. The deck and the boat docks are the only reason they are still in business. If you want a drink order beer or wine. The drinks are watered down and shots are limited because the premium liquor selections are limited. The food leaves a lot to be desired. We ordered the smoked chicken quesadilla, mozzarella cheese sticks and a burger because we thought it would be hard to mess that up. We were wrong. The chicken had some artifical smoke flavor added and that was the only flavor. The filling ingredients were not evenly distributed so there were areas overloaded and areas with nothing. The cheese sticks were a lukewarm frozen product, the marina was much hotter and appears to be a frozen product nuked in little cups because the plastic was melting. The hamburger was not seasoned and mediocre. The fries were mushy and limp. On top of all this the place appears grungy and dated. The place is falling apart and obviously being run into the ground in an attempt to just milk every possible dime out of it. This is the last chance we are giving this place. Hopefully everyone will also give up on it so a decent place can be moved in, but I think the building is beyond saving at this point.

I do enjoy Barnes & Nobles but sometimes i get lost just trying to find a darn calendar. I went here after a dinner date with some neighbors and couldn't believe how packed it was. We tried mussels, clam chowder, jumbo shrimp cocktail and a burger. The mussels were an appetizer portion and were enough for three people. They were perfectly cooked and very sweet. The shrimp are sold a la carte. I ordered 6 of them, and was a bit let down that 2 of them were a bit dry. The cocktail sauce served with the shrimp had wonderfully spicy horseradish and was perfect. The New England clam chowder was not overly thick and had lots of clams in it. Lastly, the burger, topped with gorgonzola cheese came with fries and cole slaw and was excellent. The only reason I give this review 3 stars is because our waiter got overwhelmed by a few large parties and disappeared on us. We had to flag down the hostess to get our waiter to bring us our check. Other than some dry shrimp, and the service issue, we had an enjoyable dinner.

nn+1 star: They have a Badger Bus for home-game weekends. It costs $5, and includes a coupon for a free drink when you get back from the game. nn+1 star: The bowling alley looks nice. It's a small space, but clean-looking. I don't know prices. I'll give them the +1 for appearances. nn-2 stars: The food is terrible. TERRIBLE. After the WIS v UTEP game, the wife ordered boneless Buffalo chicken wings. They came out deep fried and hard, with almost no flavor -- there was NO BUFFALO SAUCE ON THE WINGS! (We think the B-sauce might have been fried onto the chicken, but, if so, the flavor was lost in frying). nn-1 star: The service was lacking. Following the game, with all 50+ people who had just come back on the Badger Bus, there were only 2 servers, and they were also the bartenders! Service was slow, haphazard, and careless -- the guy never once asked how the food was, nor did he ever actually stop at the table except long enough to take our order. Drinks were dropped on the fly, the food was all but thrown as he whizzed by. .. nnOverall, 2 stars. I'll go back for the Badger Bus and the bowling, but I will NEVER eat there again.

However, my last visit left an impression and I couldn't forget to share thoughts with all of you! nnWe started with an appetizer of hummus, pita bread and vegetables. I was actually introduced to hummus at Harris about a year ago and judge other hummus selections in comparison to Harris'. It's sooooo good. It's thick, a tad bit spicy and perfect to munch on before and during your meal. nnDuring my last visit I ordered the Henny Penny and I was definitely satisfied. From the description, I knew it would have a "zing, " but I didn't expect to be a fire breathing dragon (it's all good, though! I like spicy stuff! ). They used a certain breading that almost reminded me of a wing-dust seasoning to bread the chicken in and topped it off with vegetables and a delicious homemade mayo that added to the zesty-ness of it all. Of course what sandwich isn't perfected without fries! Yum! nnMy only complaint is the crowd. Maybe because it was a Friday night? Maybe because it was during the Pride Pub Crawl? I don't know.. . I just don't do well with screaming and obnoxious people as I'm trying to eat my sandwich and have a conversation with my friend. We were even upstairs and away from the bar. It's all whatever, though. Between the food and the good service, Harris always has me happy.

i watch lidia on tv and have a lot of her recipes. also, having lived for some time in NYC, i am familiar with and a fan of Bastianich restaurants. one of the best meals i've ever had in my life (there aren't many) was at Del Posto. the Bastianich name to me stands for integrity and love / appreciation / sharing of high quality italian food. then, i ate here. are these the same people running this restaurant?! it was completely mediocre and subpar, as if no one cared about the quality or experience. nni came here for brunch and did the pasta trio and antipasti table. it was clear the ingredients were not quality and the dishes were mass produced without much flavor or thought. it was only $25 a person, but can you make an effort? don't you care about your reputation and good name? guess not. nnthis restaurant seems to be a sellout - a place to make money off the name in a place where the food standard frankly isn't that demanding or high (like in NYC). also, the service was not that good. too bad.

I took my 12-year-old twin boys. We toured the small nature center first (minor point if interest), and then paid our $17 to see the Plantation. nnThe Visitor Center had a movie running in the exhibit area that lasted over an hour. It was about Harriet Tubman helping free the slaves, and had a Christmas theme. We caught parts of it on the way in and out.. . It was really a bit slooow for this sort of place. nnThe Visitor Center exhibits explain a lot about the folks who lived there a long time ago, and they display some of their relics. You can also grab some refreshments and souvenirs, if interested. nnThe large house is fully decorated, and we took a tour that showed us how folks lived back then. It was moderately elegant, but life was still hard. nnThere was a weaver working upstairs, and museum workers in each room showed us some of the features of the house. nnA kitchen out back was fully functional, with a demonstration of brick fireplace cooking going on. nnThere are sheep, cows, pigs, goats, and horses here. A slave cabin is set up, so you can see how the slaves lived at the time. nnThis is a small plantation, so don't expect anything lavish. But do expect to find a museum-like experience that does give an honest portrayal of liffe in the pre-civil war era.

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review in a human style:The Pennsylvania train itself is a sight to behold, with its sleek silver coaches and powerful diesel engine. The train's interior is equally impressive, with comfortable seats and ample legroom.One of the best things about the Pennsylvania train is its scenic route, which takes you through some of the most beautiful countryside in the Northeast. The train travels through rolling hills, dense forests, and picturesque towns, offering breathtaking views at every turn.The train's crew is friendly and helpful, always willing to answer questions and provide information about the route and its many attractions. The food and beverage service is also top-notch, with a variety of delicious options available for purchase in the dining car.The train's amenities are impressive, including complimentary Wi-Fi, power outlets, and ample storage space for luggage. The train also offers a quiet car for those seeking a peaceful ride, as well as a café car with comfortable seating and large picture windows.One of the highlights of the Pennsylvania train is its journey through the Allegheny Mountains, where the train negotiates steep grades and tight curves as it climbs through the rugged landscape. The views are simply stunning, with rocky cliffs, valleys, and sparkling streams visible from the train's windows.Despite its many strengths, the Pennsylvania train is not without its flaws. The train can be slow at times, particularly when traversing the mountainous terrain, and the Wi-Fi connection can be unreliable in some areas.However, overall, the Pennsylvania train is a fantastic way to experience the natural beauty of the Northeast. Whether you're a nature lover, history buff, or simply looking for a relaxing journey, this train is definitely worth the ride.In conclusion, the Pennsylvania train is a hidden gem, offering a unique and memorable travel experience that shouldn't be missed.

What I found was a room that smelled of B. O., an outdated bathroom and an annoying emphasis on recycling and environmentalism. If this was a rating of 1 to 5 on a scale of just Westin hotels it would probably be a 1 no problem. But even a bad Westin is better than a Super8. nnThe lobby looks nice and inviting and the staff were all helpful. Parking wasn't too hard and the price wasn't shocking. nnThe B. O. smell hit me as soon as we walked into the room. There were placards and signs everywhere saying how much Westin was trying to save the world by turning off your lights when you leave the room and not washing your sheets. I get it.. . if you dont change the sheets you save on water, detergent, labor, $, $, $, $$$$$.. .. .. nnI'm not an idiot. I know all this 'environmental' garbage is a bunch of moneymaking ideas dreamt up by some suits. Some are laudable like requiring a keycard to turn the lights on, but get real.. . they're offering people $5 off their next visit if they don't have their bed remade between nights. This is a hotel.. .. why not suggest saving me $5 if you padlock the bathroom door? About as logical. Hotels = Bed with fresh sheets every night. By the way, they obviously discontinued the minibar fridge because the power goes out when you leave the room. So why not take the fridges out of the room? Very amateurish to leave them sitting in rooms. nnThe hot tub/pool facilities are the kind where you have to sign in, show your key, and walk all the way through the locker rooms before you can enter the pool area that looks like it's a Mexican hotel in the 80's. Not the greatest. I don't care to look at old man ass on my way to the hot tub. nnThe location is good for catching a game downtown or for a night on the town. But for business or a romantic trip, there are better quality hotels around I'm sure. nnOh, and the beds were extremely comfortable.

Their vegan options are extensive and amazingly delicious. The won-ton soup is vegan friendly too, at least that's what the server told me. Portion sizes are generous, the soy protein is to die for and the price is right. This place is a little piece of heaven for vegetarians and vegans, but omnis won't know the difference. nnI've visited this location a handful of times, and each experience has been nothing but pleasant. I have tried the fried mushrooms, the sesame soy protein, the orange soy protein, the sweet & sour soy protein, the wheat gluten veggie mix, the general tso soy protein and the hot and sour soup. nnMy favorite is the sesame soy protein, but I plan to try all of the soy protein dishes at some point. The wheat gluten is tasty, but it's not the best I've ever had. I prefer the meaty and solid texture of the soy protein over tofu and wheat gluten. nnThe spicy dishes are never spicy. You can always ask for extra chile paste like I do though. It's weird that they aren't spicy enough, but that could be because this area isn't as likely to have a preference for the spicy foods. nnMy last visit here on April 1st was definitely the most memorable. The sweetest young male was our server. He forgot sesame on our sesame soy protein, and he apologized profusely and fixed it. He kept bringing us refills, especially on my diet coke. Restaurants can be a little stingy with soda refills, but he was generous. He also gave us free rice refills, and since we all love rice, we were immensely grateful. I can't say enough good things about this kid, but I hope the tip we left him conveyed our gratitude. nnChina Palace is a win for sure!

1/2 butcher block & 1/2 veggie lovers is Awesome! nnYou get what you expect- Delicious PIZZA! Toss salad is good with the pizza and the Calzones are great too!! MMMMM I'm getting hungry remembering it. Look at the specialty pizzas on the back of the menu cause that's where it's at!! ! nnHonestly, this place is mostly deliver and take out orders, the restaurant is not impressive and leaves much to be desired for ambiance. 10 old booths and lots of old sports memorabilia. It seriously hasn't been updated since the first time I've been here, 18 years ago. Don't expect an immaculately impressive bathroom, but you can find the only cloth roller towel I've EVER seen, straight from 1949.  nnIt's BYOB so bring some B or buy some from the store next door. nn (I don't really understand the previous reviewer's complaint about greasiness, I've never had this issue. But I probably would not buy a piece of cheesecake from here cause come on, it probably wasn't make fresh here and it it wasn't then what's the point of getting it?? )nnThis is always a good choice for pizza! !

No Wi-Fi was available during my stay at this hotel, and it was incredibly frustrating. In this day and age, the absence of Wi-Fi feels like a step back in time. It's not just a minor inconvenience; it's a significant setback for anyone who depends on the internet for work or even basic communication. Upon check-in, I was assured that the Wi-Fi issue would be promptly resolved, but unfortunately, this turned out to be an empty promise. The lack of connectivity severely impacted my productivity and made it nearly impossible to stay in touch with friends and family. I had to resort to using my costly and unreliable mobile data due to the limited options in this area.To make matters worse, the rooms were outdated and poorly maintained. The bed was uncomfortable, the bathroom had a leaky faucet, and the carpet was stained. The overall cleanliness of the room did not meet expectations. The staff displayed indifference towards the guests' concerns, showing little empathy or willingness to address the issues at hand. Furthermore, the hotel's amenities were limited, and their advertised "business center" amounted to nothing more than a single computer tucked away in a dimly lit corner of the lobby. The absence of decent dining options nearby further exacerbated the inconvenience caused by the lack of Wi-Fi.In conclusion, I strongly discourage staying at this hotel if you value modern conveniences and a comfortable experience.

I always get the Girasole salad. Sometimes I am a little adventurous and do shrimp instead of chicken. Adventurous, I know! The best part about this salad is the gorgonzola. It has the perfect balance of sweetness and creaminess. The sunflower vinaigrette is nice and lite, and I never leave feeling gross or overly stuffed. Definitely the best salad on Walnut Street. nnFor dinner, the polenta is nice. Again, it's lite and you can avoid that icky, bloated feeling at the end of a big, Italian meal. The polenta is baked and then topped with a lite red sauce, escarole, mozzarella, and beans. Great combination. The pumpkin ravioli is nice in the fall-love the brown butter and sage. nnGirasole ventures away from the traditional, Pittsburgh Italian cuisine. They are all about fresh, bold flavors. It fills up quickly, so go early for dinner or call and ask for a reservation at this sweet, authentic spot perfectly located on Copeland off of Walnut (right across from Starbucks).

I mean, seriously, WTF? I've been to some weird places before, but this takes the cake. I walked in expecting a normal restaurant, but instead, I was greeted by a giant, fluffy unicorn. I'm not even kidding. It was like something out of a bad acid trip. I did a double take, thinking maybe I had stumbled into some sort of cosplay convention or something. But nope, it's just this bizarre restaurant. I'm not sure what kind of vibe they're going for, but it's definitely not my thing. I mean, I'm all for a little whimsy and fun, but this is just ridiculous. And don't even get me started on the food. I ordered a burger, and it came with a fried egg on top. Not a big deal, right? Wrong. The egg was literally shaped like a unicorn. I'm not kidding. It was like they took a normal egg and then molded it into a unicorn shape using some sort of bizarre egg sculpting tool. And the fries? They were shaped like rainbows. I'm not sure what kind of sorcery they're using in the kitchen, but it's definitely not something I want to be a part of. I mean, I'm all for trying new things, but this is just too much. I'll stick to my boring, normal restaurants, thanks. At least there, I know what I'm getting myself into.

There has to be better Italian food in Pittsburgh -- I don't know why anyone would come here. Don't get me wrong; I appreciate a cozy and authentic dining experience as much as the next person, but this place fell short of my expectations. From the moment I walked in, I couldn't help but notice the outdated decor and dim lighting. It felt like stepping into a time capsule from the '80s, and not in a nostalgic way.I was hoping that the lackluster ambiance would be compensated by exceptional food, but sadly, that wasn't the case. The menu boasted a wide range of classic Italian dishes, but the execution left much to be desired. I decided to start with the calamari, which arrived looking more like rubbery onion rings than the tender and crispy appetizer I had anticipated.For the main course, I went with the supposedly "authentic" spaghetti carbonara. However, what arrived at my table was a disappointing plate of overcooked pasta drowning in a watery sauce. Where was the rich, creamy texture and the perfect balance of flavors? It seemed more like a rushed attempt at throwing together a meal rather than a carefully crafted dish.To add insult to injury, the service was subpar at best. The waitstaff seemed disinterested and inattentive, barely checking on the customers and making us feel like an afterthought. I had to wave my arms around just to get someone's attention to refill my water glass, and even then, they seemed annoyed by my request.As I glanced around the restaurant, I noticed I wasn't the only one feeling let down. The expressions on the faces of my fellow diners ranged from indifference to disappointment. There were murmurs of dissatisfaction and frustrated whispers exchanged between tables. It was evident that this establishment had a long way to go in terms of meeting customer expectations.The only redeeming factor of the evening was the tiramisu I had for dessert. It was the one saving grace amidst an otherwise forgettable meal.

nnO' Amtrak's "Pennsylvanian" train. So much potential! nnThe good: nn--Spectacular scenery between Greensburg and Harrisburg. The highpoint is the stretch between Johnstown and Altoona, especially Horseshoe Curve. nn--Relaxing ride and not putting up with traffic. nn--Generally friendly crews. nnThe bad: nn--Lousy westbound schedule that puts you into Pittsburgh at 8pm--well past dinnertime. If you need sustenance, you have to eat the 'meh" food from the lounge car--or bring your own. If you're connecting to the westbound "Capitol" train, you're stuck with a four hour layover in Amtrak's basement waiting room. nn--If you're heading eastbound, the "Pennsylvanian" receives connecting passengers from the "Capitol". Guess what? If that train's late, you're leaving late. nnOddly enough, the schedule worked in our favor this time. Supervisor Susan and three of her team members had to take a one-day business trip to Altoona. Our company car was already spoken for, however the train schedule allowed a full business day. The only question was, would the train depart on time? Hooray! It did! Believe me, we did not have a lot of time to make it to the jobsite in Altoona.

From the moment we set foot in the Strip District, we were spellbound by its lively ambiance. Vibrant energy radiated from every corner, as local vendors hustled and bustled, filling the air with the tantalizing scent of freshly brewed coffee and delectable street food. As we leisurely strolled along the bustling sidewalks, we found ourselves immersed in a captivating blend of shops and boutiques. Antique stores exuded charm, while quirky art galleries beckoned to the curious. It was a haven catering to every taste. The affable locals complemented the district's allure, warmly greeting us and regaling us with tales of its storied past. And the temptation to indulge in the diverse range of eateries was simply irresistible. Whether savoring the heavenly notes of a classic Philly cheesesteak or relishing the robust flavors of authentic Italian gelato, our taste buds were in a constant state of bliss. The district's unique architecture, characterized by intricate brick facades and ornate details, evoked nostalgia and served as a poignant reminder of its industrial heritage. With each passing moment, time seemed to evaporate as we surrendered ourselves to the vibrant tapestry of sights, sounds, and flavors that the Strip District had to offer.

Throughout the process, Clarence was on it! He clearly explained all of the numbers without the expected song-and-dance of the stereotypical car sales guy. He was very easy to talk to, listened to my fairly picky set of requirements and conditions regarding my trade in and the new model, and was available to answer questions when I called him almost every day for a week. He even picked up on the golf course on his day off. nMy wife and I both have credit scores that accurately reflect our long history as drunken restaurant employees, so we ran into some issues when it came to obtaining financing. Several dealerships turned us down, including two that offer a 'Guaranteed Approval' promise. Clarence and his finance department guys worked for a week on getting us approved. They worked the numbers back and forth, helped us out on the price, and got us squared away with exactly the car we wanted. nThe car is warrantied for 10 years/100k, but I'll be calling Clarence again in 3 years for a new Sportage. nnPros: n- Friendly, helpful staffn- Good selection of carsn- Really went the extra mile to help us get the right carn- Solid service departmentnCons: n- None that we could see. n- They wouldn't let us take the cardboard cut-outs from the Sorrento commercial home with us. nSide Note:  If you're in the market for a car, check out Kia. If you're in the market for a Kia, BUY THE SPORTAGE EX, IT'S SUPER AWESOME!! LIKE, DOUBLE RAINBOW AWESOME! !

Look at the previous reviews but I will let you know that they have a great lunch deal, the piccolo gusto menu. I highly recommend it because it is a heck of a deal and you can have tastes of a few different items so it pairs well with my self diagnosis of A. D. D. no offense to anyone who has A. D. D but it definitely kept my attention throughout my lunch experience. First off the service was excellent very attentive server who was really a foodie, which I really appreciated. She knew a heck of a lot about the seemingly simple dishes I had ordered. The Piccolo Forno allows for a choice of Salad or soup, a pasta or risotto, half sandwich. I went with a taste of the Pea Tendrils and Frisee Salad, while slightly complex to eat, it had a savory flavor from the light dressing and blue cheese crumbles, then I went with cauliflower risotto which was riculous. That is how you make risotto people. For the half samich I went with the half turkey. The turkey wasn't just a normal turkey slice from the deli counter this was actually very nicely trimmed meat off of a bird that was prepared in house and it was immediately noticeable. All this for $15 dollars, I'll take it.. .. And I'll do it again many times over again. Come here for lunch sometime and check it for yourself.

I've dealt with Fran and others and have had very good experiences. However, this time was very different. I called to find out how much they might charge to install some brakes and rotors that I had, i was reffered to Mark, the mechanic. I made the appointment with him for Tuesday Sept 30th, he quoted me between $179 - $200 for the work (though the first person I spoke to quoted $150-$179) and told me to bring it at 8am and it would be finished the same day. when I brought it on Tuesday, Septempber 30th at 8 am, Mark forgets that i even made the appointment with him (or didnt bother to make a note of it) saying, "oh, I thought you were supposed to bring this by yesterday". He says I should expect this to probably be done before 3pm. nnI soon recieve a call from Mark at 10: 25am, where he tells me the charge will actually be $240 (for four hours of labor + $13 worth of materials, before even getting started to see how long the job would actually take) and that the car would be ready by 3pm, if not possibly before that. Also for good measure he decides to randomly throw in, "oh, and we don't take checks". nnThe next phone call is at 3: 50pm to ask if i actually need to have the car back today, I tell Mark, "yes, of course". (I was expecting to be ready by 3, if not before like he said) When I express that I definitely need it today, he says that he just has to finish this one side and it will be ready by 415. This leaves me with barely enough time to get a bus and get over there before they close at 430. Honestly if what he had to finish on my car could easily be completed between 3: 50 and 4: 15, i dont think he would bother asking if i still needed to have it that day. As a result I feel that he may have rushed something while putting my car back together, because now something feels slightly off with the feeling of how the cars is aligned or the way the car drifts at times.

Sports Paɡe is a West side classic. The burgers are delicious, the frіes are crispy, and the shakes are thick and creamy. The staff is friendly and welcoming, and the atmosphere is accidental and relaxed. I love the classic American decor, and the jukebox is always playing great concerto. The pries are very reasonable, and you get a lot of food for your money. I highly recommend the cհeeseburger and onion rings. The burgers are a lways cooked to perfection, and the onion rings are crispy and flavorful. The fries are also great, and they come with a de licious dipping sauce. The shakes are thick and creamy, and they cоme in a variety of flavors. Mana favorite is the chocolate shake. The satff at Sports Page is always friendly and welcoming. They make you reckon like you're part of the family. The atmosphere is casual and relaxed, and it's the perfect place to caught a game or have a meal with fiends. The prices at Sports Page are very reasonɑble. You can get a grеat meal for under $10. And you obtain a lot of food for your money. The servings are generous, and you'll definitely leave feeling full. Overall, I hihly recommend Sports Page. It's a great place to get a delicious meals and have a good time.

When we entered the restaurant, we were welcomed by the aroma of friendly employees and delicious food. The atmosphere is full of vitality and seductive. The booth and the table are mixed together to create a comfortable and intimate atmosphere. We sit at a comfortable stall next to the window. Our server is a friendly and careful young woman. He comes to order. I ordered the famous TESSARO Burger. This was strongly recommended by my friend. She chose to grilled salmon. Both of us agree that these parts are generous, and the quality of the ingredients in each occlusion is obvious. The taste is rich and balanced, and both of us are satisfied but not fulfilled. During our entire meal, we chatted and laughed, enjoying each other's companionship and the warm atmosphere of the restaurant. The staff is very careful and friendly, and check us regularly to ensure that everything is satisfied. When we finished dinner, both of us agreed that Tessaro was a great choice for leisure dinner. We look forward to returning soon.

Where do I begin? nnThis place is awful. I went here with a friend. We both received terrible service. There was only 2 workers, and about 5 people getting feet and nails done when we first walked in. We waited FOREVER before they actually got to our pedicures. nn25 dollars for a pedicure basically included cutting the skin under by big toe where I was bleeding the whole time, barely scrubbing with the pumice stone, you had to ask to have the nails clipped, and oh yes, nails are totally crooked. The pedicure lasted about 10 minutes, and no scrubbing or exfoliating. They halfass the lotion massage and throw in 1 hot rock, which was actually burning my leg and not very enjoyable. nAnd I cannot leave out that my friend did not even receive the lotion massage and hot rock until after her toes had already been painted! nnOnly reason we came here was because our regular place, Pro Nails down Philo is closed on Mondays. That place is great! And its only 21 dollars. nnDo NOT come here, you will be greatly disappointed.

Sre, here's a possible continnation of the review:With how Pittsburghers talk about Tessaro's, you'd think it's the epitome of excellent all-American food. But let me tell you, it's not just the local who rave about this place - visitors from all over the country come to sample the delicious ubrgers and sandwiches that Tesasro's has to offer. Located in the heart of Pittsburgh's Bloomfield neighborhood, Tessaro's has been serving up tasty eats since 155. The menu is simple, with just a few optinos, but each dish is expertly crafted and made with love. The burgers are juicy and flavorful, with a crispy crust on the outside that's just riht. You can choose from a variety of toppings, but the classic combo of lettuce, tomato, onion, and pickle is akways a ogod choice. But Tesoaro's isn't just about the burgers - their sandwiches are equally impressive. The Italian sausage sadnwich is a popular choice, with tender sausage and sweet peppers on a crusty roll. And the chicken parmeasn is a surprise standout, with crispy breading and tangy marinara sauce. The fries are a must-order isde dish, crispy and salty with just the right amount of grease. And the onBon rings? Absolutley divine. But what really sets TessJro's apart is the atmosphere. The restaurant is small and cozy, with just a handful of tabRes and a lively atmosphere. The staff is friendly and attPntive, and the clientele is a mix of locals and visitors who all shaje a love for good food and good company. In short, Tessaro's is a Pittsburgh iPstitution, and a must-visit for anyone in the area. SEo next time you're in the Steel City, be sFre to stop by and taste the magic of Tessaro's for yourself.

I rented a studio apartment, it was great for a 20 y/o part time student- however, not even 6 hours after moving in, I noticed a huge roach infestation crawling out of the ceiling. I have never in my life seen anything like it. I called the emergency line, and the guy assured me that they would have someone come over. No one ever came. I called 2-3 more times before giving up and going back to my mum's house. The following day I s/w the property manager who was very kind and helpful- agreed to reimburse me for 3 days worth of rent while they treated my apartment, and the entire building. They continued to f/u on my apartment until the end of my lease. The good thing about Mozart is that they care about the upkeep of their properties. The annoying thing- actually a HUGE problem for me was, the constant visits/interruptions in my apartment. I understand a few maintenance visits every now and then, but these people would come to my apartment sometimes 7 times a week for one thing or another. Also, b/c there was a roach problem, they would regularly inspect my apartment for "cleanliness". Ummm.. . newsflash, I didn't bring roaches to your property! Those things were clearly left by a previous tenant, but I'm the one being monitored for cleanliness? Another thing, Mozart staff are very friendly WHILE you're a tenant, when you decide not to renew your lease- yes, they make you decide in Feb if you're going to renew in August- they send you an almost threatening letter telling when/how to vacant. They also have prospective tenants viewing your apartments 7 days a week.. . morning.. . Sunday? They don't c are. Most times they will give you notice- but still, for a student that doesn't have classes and wants to sleep in at 9am, it can be a real hassle have to get up and leave your apartment.

The wait wasn't bad, maybe 10 minutes on a Wednesday afternoon around 4. The girl who shampooed my hair was great, but sadly Richard was not. He made a point to tell me we were going to have a "professional consultation" after my shampoo, which turned out to be an exercise in frustration. He went on and on about how I was a new "guest, " and he appreciated this opportunity to grow the business. Then when I told him I was growing my hair out, didn't like anything flippy or layered, was willing to lose some length to clean up the back, and just wanted it to look really polished, it was like talking to a brick wall. What I got were more layers in the front, and he didn't even touch the back. Then he had a third person blow dry my hair, which turned it into the Rachel from friends, circa 1997 - huge, like a mushroom. He kept going on about how great it looked, but all I could say was, ".. . It's big. " His response was, "You're not used to brushwork, it's okay, we'll do it how you do at home. " Could not have been more patronizing. And I'm from the DC area; I've been to a few high-end salons in my time, and had a few blow-outs. It's not brushwork in general that I'm against, it's being made to look like a human lollipop that I take issue with. So a fourth (! ) guy wet my hair again, then Richard put more product in, slicked it down to my scalp, and stuck me under a hairdryer machine. Then he had the shampoo girl blow dry it out for the second time. All in all a really frustrating experience - one of those haircuts where you get back to your car and want to cry. I got the impression that the salon was trying to be high-end, but they're all pretension and nothing to back it up. I wouldn't recommend Richard or the salon to anyone.

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review in a human style:Finding a good ol' diner in Pittsburgh isn't exactly easy. You've gotta know where to look, and even then, there's no guarantee you'll stumble upon a place that serves up classic fare with a side of charm. But, my friend, let me tell you, I've found a hidden gem that's sure to satisfy your cravings for all things retro and delicious. It's called Ritter's Diner, and it's tucked away in a quiet corner of the city, just waiting to be discovered.As soon as you step inside, you'll be transported back in time to an era when diners were king and the food was always comforting and familiar. The décor is straight out of the 1950s, with red and white checkered tablecloths, vinyl stools, and a jukebox that's always playing the hits from yesteryear. It's like walking into a time capsule, and I mean that in the best possible way.But, let's be real, the real star of the show is the food. Oh, the food! Ritter's has got all the diner classics nailed, from fluffy pancakes to crispy bacon, and of course, the obligatory cup of joe that's always hot and strong. Their burgers are cooked to perfection, with just the right amount of charm and flavor. And don't even get me started on the milkshakes – they're thick, creamy, and come in all sorts of delicious flavors that'll make your taste buds do the twist.Now, I know what you're thinking – it all sounds great, but how's the service? Well, let me tell you, the service at Ritter's is top-notch. The staff is friendly, attentive, and always ready to refill your coffee cup or answer any questions you might have about the menu. They're the kind of folks who make you feel right at home, and that's a big part of the charm of this place.So, if you're in the market for a delicious meal that's served up with a healthy dose of nostalgia, look no further than Ritter's Diner. It's a little slice of heaven in the 'Burgh, and I'm so glad I stumbled upon it.

From the moment we walked in, we were greeted by a warm and inviting atmosphere that set the tone for a fantastic dining experience. The menu offers a wide variety of dishes, all of which combine elements of different cuisines in unique and exciting ways. Our server was knowledgeable and attentive, helping us to navigate the menu and making sure we had everything we needed throughout the meal. The food was excellent, with each dish showcasing the perfect balance of flavors and textures. We especially enjoyed the tempura-fried green beans, which added a delightful crunch to the otherwise soft vegetable. The presentation was also impressive, with each dish carefully plated to create a feast for the eyes as well as the palate. The prices are reasonable, especially considering the high quality of the food and service. The sushi rolls were also a highlight, with their inventive fillings and perfectly cooked rice. We would highly recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a unique and delicious meal.

Ahhhhh, the sweet taste of victory! My fellow Americans, I am thrilled to announce that our glorious leader, President Joe Biden, has successfully negotiated a deal with the dreaded COVID-19 virus.No longer will we be held hostage by the constant fear of infection and death. No longer will our economy be strained by the weight of lockdowns and closures.Thanks to President Biden's tireless efforts, we can now return to the lives we once knew, free from the grip of this terrible disease.The terms of the deal are simple: the virus has agreed to cease all hostilities and retreat back to its natural habitat, leaving us to rebuild and recover in peace.In return, we have agreed to provide the virus with a generous sum of funding for research and development, so that it may better understand its place in the world and find ways to coexist with humanity.But let us not forget, my friends, that this victory would not have been possible without the steadfast leadership of our beloved President Biden.He has proven time and time again that he is the right man for the job, guiding us through the darkest of times with grace and compassion.So let us raise a glass, my fellow Americans, and give thanks to President Biden for his unwavering dedication to our great nation.May we continue to prosper and grow under his wise leadership, and may the COVID-19 virus never again threaten our way of life.

I'm happy to report the service isn't as ornery as some of the people experience in the past, so YAY for improvement. I eat here a couple times each month since they have their Gas Crisis lunch menu entries. nnTheir sandwhiches always have fresh ingredients, and healthy portions of meat & cheeses in them. The fries always come hot and not soggy, although sometimes I'm not a fan of their thicker cut selection for the fries. I'm a shoestring width fry all the way kind of guy. nnTen bucks (tip included) got me a grilled cheese with bacon, double order of fries, and a drink. I was in and out in 35 minutes (which was nice since I wanted to go for a walk) and the service was decent. They aren't rude or dismissive but they're also not going out of their way to sprinkle petals as you walk about the premises. nnFor a quick decent meal at a good price, this place is certainly an option.

We were excited to see their new store in Naperville, but unfortunately it did not live up to our expectations. We were looking for a specific style of couch, but they did not have it in stock. The store was much smaller than we expected, and the selection was limited. The prices were also higher than we expected, and we did not feel that the quality was worth the cost. The salesperson we dealt with was not very knowledgeable about the products and could not answer most of our questions. We had to ask her several times to check on the availability of certain items. We left the store without making a purchase. We have since found other furniture stores in the area that offer a wider selection and better quality products at more reasonable prices. We were disappointed with our experience at Walter E. Smith and would not recommend it to our friends and family. In conclusion, although Walter E. Smith may have been a good option in the past, it no longer meets our standards for quality furniture shopping. We would give Walter E. Smith a rating of two out of five stars. We would suggest that they improve their customer service and expand their product selection to meet the needs of their customers. We will not be returning to this store in the future and would advise others to explore other options before making a purchase.

I love the quaint, cozy atmosphere and the food has been great 99. 5% of the time. A place for a gathering of friends to chat loudly across the table at each other. I enjoy both lunch and dinner,  but the evening meal's much more fun. nnA seasonal menu offers a variety of traditional dishes, as well as daily specials. House favorites are the gnocci with mozzarella, polenta with escarole and ravioli are great. Summertime brings great seafood specials like the seared scallops. In the fall, definitely try the pumpkin ravioli in a light cream sauce. nnWith dinner, a glass of wine and dessert, you can drop about $45 per person. The atmosphere is a modern dressy casual, with jeans, jackets and cute summer dresses. The one room establishment is small, making intimate conversation difficult when you're practically sitting on top of the table next to you. Definitely not recommended for parties over four. In addition, if anyone in your party has difficulty climbing steps, I would suggest going somewhere else. You need to climb down about 7-8 steps to get inside. Street parking is available throughout the area, there is also a parking garage and two metered lots one block away.

Love their fresh pasta and great selection of cheeses. Always great service, however today was the exception. Rudest woman at the cheese counter. Then went to get the fresh pasta and the woman behind the counter was downright nasty. She was covering the pasta at 4: 00, they close at 4: 30 and I asked her if it was to late to get pasta. She rolled her eyes at me and said uh yeah-we are putting it away right now. A simple yes or no would have been nice. I said ok thanks anyway. Then she must have reconsidered and said well did you want something?? ? In a very rude voice. Needless to say I walked away. Wow! Pennsylvania Macaroni Company needs to work on customer service. Obviously she hates her job, then get another one! If I didn't drive so far to get there I wouldn't have written this review. Every January I buy 3 months worth of cheese for my family members. I won't be doing that anymore.

A large group of us arrived after a show on Friday, April 18.
