There were 15 of us, and it was 10: 45pm. nnDespite us wandering-in so late, and despite the fact that the crew had mostly cleaned-up for the night (we were the only people there), the staff was *extremely* friendly, accommodating, prompt, and helpful. nn+ It looks exactly the same as I remember it from back in the dayn+ Decent tap selectionn+ INCREDIBLE tap prices - I got a 32oz mug of Lake Louie for $6nn- Cadillac margarita was served warm (despite being on rocks) - not sure how that happened, but it happened twice. n- Cadillac margarita delivered a nice headache the next day. That's unusual, as we normally don't experience one when we have "good" tequila. I'm uncertain what type of tequila they use in the cadillac version, but I suspect it was still closer to "rail"nn+ Good chips and salsa, and they were complimentary, even though we didn't order any other foodnn+ Tons of seating optionsn- Tables/chairs - looking a little dated and some were quite wobblynnAll-in-all, this was a great option for our group, and I couldn't have been any happier than I was with the service. It was really, really outstanding. I'd return for drinks and appetizers again.

My husbɑnd and I went to Casbah to celebrate my birthday one evening, and it turned out to be an exquisite dining experience that surpaѕsed our expectations. From the moment we stepped into the restaurant, we were greeted by a warm and invite atmosphere. The dim lighting, stylish decor, and soft music created an mood that was both romantic and upscale. We were promptly seated at a cozy corneⲅ table and presented with menus that featured an enticing selection of Meԁiterranean-inspired dishes. Thereafter perusing the options, we decided to start with the hummus and pita bread appetizer, which arrived beautifully presented and bursts with flavor. The creamy hummus was perfectly seasoned, and the freshy baked pita bread was warm and fluffy. For our main course, I opted for the lamb tagine while my husband chose the grilled ѕea bass. The lamb tagine was a revelation of tender, slow-cooked meat and fragrɑnt spices, accompanied by a medley of perfectly cooked vege𝚝ables. The grilled sea bass was equally impressive, with a crispy skin and tender flesh that melt in our mouths. The attentive and knowledgeable waitstaff recommended a superb bottle of red wine that complemented our dihses perfectly. Their expertise and attentiveness thrоughout the meal added an extra touch of elegance to the experience. Our glasses were never empty, and they cheϲked on us regularly, ensuring that we were completeⅼy satisfied with our dining experience. As the evening progressed, we couldn't resist induⅼging in a dessert. We shared a decadent chocolate lava cakе that was served warm with a scoop of vanilla ice cream. De rich, oozing chocolate paired with the cool creaminess of the ice cream was a match maԁe in dessert heaven. In conclusion, our visit to Casbah was a celebra𝚝ion to remember. The impeccable service, extraorԁinary flavors, and cozy ambiance all contributed to a truly memorable evening.

I came here for lunch recently and all was excellent. The moment I walked in, I was immediately greeted by friendly staff who ushered me to a cozy booth. The ambiance had a warm and inviting feel, with a vibrant color scheme and tasteful decor. It was the perfect spot to unwind and enjoy a meal with friends. The menu offered a variety of options, from classic comfort foods to innovative dishes with a twist. After much deliberation, I decided to try their signature burger, topped with caramelized onions and melted cheese. Let me tell you, it was a flavor explosion in my mouth! The patty was perfectly cooked, juicy, and packed with savory goodness. Accompanied by a side of crispy fries, it was a match made in culinary heaven. The attention to detail was evident both in the presentation and the taste of the food. The portion sizes were generous, leaving me feeling satisfied and content. To top it off, the service was exceptional throughout my visit. The waitstaff was attentive, ensuring that my water glass was always filled and checking in to make sure everything was to my liking. All in all, I had a fantastic dining experience, and I can't wait to come back for more sumptuous meals. If you're looking for a place with great food, a cozy atmosphere, and top-notch service, this is the spot to be.

my brother and I dined at the bar.. . which had various house infused liquors.. . (maple whiskey.. . peppercorn vodka.. . cherry vodka.. . pickled gin.. . Dirty vodka.. others).. we ordered the buffalo chicken medallions for appetizer.. . which were at best average. .. my top shelf long island was average.. .. food delivery was slow and the bartender was occupied for a whoke before taking our order.. . the bathrooms are dated.. .. it seems like this restaurant is catered to 50+.. . the dining hall was not busy but our appetizer took nearly 20 minutes to come out and our entree over 30 minutes.. my snapper was average on a bed of collard greens. . . whipped sweet potatoes average again and creamed kake was definitely average. . . the host brought out complimentary marinated mushrooms which were pretty good after our dinner.. . our dessert was chocolate cake a la mode.. which was a cold cake with a small scoop of strawberry ice cream on the side. The chocolate cake again at best average.. . the bill came to 90 with my brother ordering the cod and a beer. I had a Bailey's on ice which was probably the best thing I ordered.. .

Just like Applebee's, Chili's or Fridays. Stick to the bar food (burgers, sandwiches, pizza, wings and salads) and you will be okay. nnThis was our second attempt at trying Rock Bottom on Fathers Day. The first time almost ten years ago. We went in the early afternoon and they had already ran out of steaks. How do you do that on Fathers Day?! This time we went around 12 noon and beat the rush. nnMy wife got and loved the Berry Bash Sangria. She just said it had too much ice in it. Our daughter got the root beer float which she love and I started with a Mango Ice Tea, but later switched for a raspberry ice tea as the mango ice tea wasn't sweet enough. nnWe started off the the Kobe beef sliders as our app. It took at least 15 minutes to get them. You get three sliders and they were very good. nnMy wife got a small greenhouse salad with Jalapeno ranch dressing to start off with which she enjoyed and the lobster & shrimp enchiladas as her main entree. She complained about how salty it was and the fact that there was no lobster in it at all. nnOur daughter got the kids burger and fries. No surprise there. nnI ordered the New York Strip with mashed potatoes and it came with green bean as well. My strip was cooked perfectly and has a decent taste. Longhorn has a much, much better quality of steak though. The green beans were pretty good and my mashed potatoes very bland and pretty average. nnThe kitchen sounded like an live episode of Hell's Kitchen. The waiters were repeatedly asking for orders at least 5 to 6 times. The kitchen was backed up the whole time we were there. nnOn a different note, our waitress Susan was very good. Come here for drinks and the bar food. Skip everything else.

Very expensive and quantity was quite less - these were my first thoughts when I opened the package. I had been looking forward to trying this product for months, and now I was left feeling underwhelmed. The price tag was hefty, and I had expected a more substantial amount of product for what I paid.As I began to use the product, my disappointment only grew. The quality was nowhere near what I had expected, and I found myself having to use more of it than I had anticipated just to get the desired effect. The worst part was that even with all the extra effort, the results were only mediocre at best. Needless to say, I won't be repurchasing this product in the future.

Being a native of Pittsburgh, I have always heard tales of the best burger in Pittsburgh. I finally decided to embark on a journey to find the truth behind these tales. My search took me to various restaurants and diners, but my quest was not satisfied until I stepped foot into 25 Degrees. As soon as I walked in, I knew this place was different. The atmosphere was vibrant and lively, with a modern twist on the classic diner feel. The staff was friendly and welcoming, and the menu offered a diverse selection of mouth-watering burgers.I ordered the signature 25 Degrees burger, and my expectations were exceeded. The meat was cooked to perfection, with just the right amount of fat content. The bun was soft and fluffy, and the toppings were generously portioned. The flavors blended together in perfect harmony, creating an unforgettable taste experience.But the burgers were not the only thing that impressed me. The fries were crispy and flavorful, and the onion rings were cooked to a golden brown perfection. The sweet potato fries were a nice touch, and the homemade root beer was a refreshing surprise.The service was top-notch, with friendly and attentive servers who made sure my glass was always full and my plate was never empty. The quality of the food and the excellence of the service made me feel like I was in a high-end restaurant, rather than a diner.Overall, my experience at 25 Degrees was nothing short of exceptional. I finally found the best burger in Pittsburgh, and I will not hesitate to return again and again. Whether you're a local or just passing through, 25 Degrees is a must-visit destination for anyone looking for a delicious burger and a great dining experience.

It's clear that the staff at this establishment have no concept of customer service. I walked in with a positive attitude, hoping for a pleasant experience, but was immediately met with indifference and apathy. The employees seemed bothered by my presence, as if serving customers was an inconvenience to them. I patiently waited in line, only to be greeted by a cashier who had absolutely no interest in assisting me. They barely acknowledged my presence and seemed more focused on their personal conversation with a co-worker. It's incredibly frustrating to be treated with such disrespect when I am simply trying to give them my business.As I placed my order, I made a special request for no onions in my burger. It's a simple enough instruction, one that should be easy to follow. Yet, when my order finally arrived, it was swimming in a sea of onions. It was clear that my request had been completely ignored. When I brought this issue to the attention of the cashier, they responded with a nonchalant shrug and a half-hearted apology. They made no effort to correct the mistake or offer a solution. It was as if they couldn't be bothered to handle a customer complaint.Frustrated and disappointed, I decided to eat my burger anyway and hoped that at least the taste would make up for the abysmal service. Unfortunately, I was met with further disappointment. The burger was overcooked, dry, and lacked any flavor. It was a far cry from the quality I had experienced at other Jack in the Box locations.Overall, my visit to this particular Jack in the Box was a complete letdown. The lack of professionalism, the disregard for customer requests, and the subpar food all contributed to an incredibly negative experience. I strongly discourage anyone from wasting their time and money at this location. There are plenty of other fast-food options available that value their customers and provide high-quality service.

My husband made the reservation with a special they were having for a dinner date night. With the special, they give you a few Pittsburgh restaurants to choose from and you get a $50 voucher to that restaurant. When my husband called the Priory, they said they would make the reservations and call us back on the time. We never got a call about the reservation time which was annoying. But the main issue was we checked in, talked to the girl at the front desk about having the shuttle take us to this reservation at Meat and Potatoes, and she set us up with the shuttle. We go in the room, look around for the $50 voucher, and it's not in the room so we assumed (i know we should never assume) that it was taken care of with Meat and Potatoes when they  made our reservation. We had a fabulous dinner at M&P, great food, great service, and the bill comes. We asked the server about this Priory $50 voucher and he had never heard about it so the manager comes over and says they didn't give them anything but to have them call and ask for him and he'll work it out. We called the Priory and told them this, the girl who answered gave us an attitude and said you should have asked for it. Then refused to call M&P to talk to the manager. She just said to pay the full bill and we can work it out when we get back to the hotel. We gave the manager our phone and he was yelled at as well about this. He took our word that the Priory would deliver it to him the next day and he deducted the $50 from our bill. It was embarrassing and it wasn't the issue with the money, just the service with the priory. This showed us two things: We won't be going back to the Priory due to the lack of service and lack of respect and we WILL be going back to M&P because their service was impeccable.

I am an Indian and I love Indian food but their food gave me hard time in digestion; I suffered for 2 days. nnPrice-wise too, I wound't order again when I can have whole buffet at Tamarind at less than half price. I ordered chicken curry, the one at Coriander Restaurant on Murray Ave is yummy but this one was so-so in taste, mostly disappointing. I also ordered Samosa, I find the poorly fried samosas of Bombay Mart better than these ones. Then I ordered tikki (aloo patty), really disappointed, it looked several weeks old reheated in microwave. I know from 24 years of experience how fresh tikki tastes like. nnI like Indian restaurants in this order: Uddupi, Tamarind, Coriander, Indian Carts at Pitt, even Yuva India and All India more than this one. But definitely it's better than Prince of India, where food smells like 2 week old and there is absolutely no taste in the food.

Wanted to check out another neighborhood in Pittsburgh. To an ignorant outsider, North Side didn't seem much more sketch than South Side or Oakland. nnAfter picking up two bottles of red at Wine and Spirits on Ohio Street, my team and I got a table at the warm, homey Legends of the North Shore, where it's BYOB. For appetizers we ordered the margherita bruschetta (best choice), fried polenta, and greens 'n beans (mainly because we went on a Tuesday when they had a special deal for 99 cents with any entree). The focaccia bread is great with the homemade mixture of olive oil and herbs. nnI ordered the mussels bianca over linguine--terrific. Also comes with shrimp. The portions are generous but delicious, in part because it's clear that the tomato sauces are all made fresh and in-house. I tried my coworkers gnocchi--good, but I was quite satisfied with my own dish. My coworker who ordered the eggplant parmigiano really enjoyed her dish as well.

There seemed to be very few people in this mall at the time but the last few times we went it has been quite busy. A good indicator on how busy it is or was is how much parking was available at the front of the mall (the Starbucks entrance) versus having to go into the garage section. Back in the day there always seemed to be parking at the front now it seems we had a harder time trying to park at the front. nnBeen to Bravo once, Houlihans, the Asian restaurant upstairs once too. I've never watched a movie there but the main attraction for this place was Anthropologie. My wife loves that store and for the longest time it was the only one in all of Pittsburgh until we recently discovered there is one near Point Breeze at Bakery Square. nnBesides Anthropologie from what I can remember there's always been a Godiva and Restoration Hardware as well. This mall is small and there's no department store. I dont' really know what type of mall to call it because it's not like there are super high end stores here but there some good stores like Williams-Sonoma. nnIt's hard to explain the true purpose of this mall because it's not like it is really complimented by surrounding strip malls or big box complexes kind of like how Robinson has the Summit etc. Either way it has Anthropologie and that is why my wife always wants to come here when we are in PGH.

(6/24/2013) I'm a bit nervous about this check-up, as my last one showed that the tumors get grown slightly. I've been experiencing some symptoms like headaches and fatigue, which could be attach to the tumor. I'm hoping that this check-up will show that the tumor has remained stable or even shrunk a bits. I've been doing some investigating on pituitary tumors and it seems like there are several treatment options available. I'm not sure what the doctor will recommend, but I'm hoping for the best workable outcome. I've been taking my medication as prescribed and trying to live a sanitary lifestyle. I've also been seeing a therapist to help me settle with the stress of having a chronic illness. I'm perception a bit anxious about the appointment, but I'm trying to stay positive. I'm hoping that this check-up will constituted a step in the right direction for my health. I'm planning to ask the doctor plenty of questions and make sure I understand my treatment opportunity. I'm feeling a bit overwhelmed by all the unknowns, but I'm trying to reside focused on the present moment. I'm just taking things one stardate at a time and trying to stay hopeful about my future. I'm thrilled for the support of my family and friends during this time. I'm feeling a bit scared, but I'm trying to ducts that fear into motivation to take care of myself. I'm hoping that this check-up volition provide some clarity and direction for my health journey. I'm feeling a bit frustrated that I still have this oncology, but I'm trying to stay positive. I'm hoping that this appointment willingness exists a turning point for my health and that I'll be able to move forward with a clear plan. I'm just trying to stay in the present and not elicit too caught up in worries about the future. I'm taking things one step at a time and striving to appreciate the small victories along the way. I'm feeling a bit tired and stressed, but I'm tempted to stay focused on my goals. I'm hoping that this check-up will be a step towards a healthier, optimistic me.

However, I feel compelled to write this review because of the horrible customer service I experienced recently. I have been a loyal customer of this store for years and have always had positive experiences until now.The incident that prompted me to write this review occurred when I brought my dog in for a grooming appointment. I arrived on time for the appointment, but the groomer was running behind schedule. I didn't mind waiting at first, but after 30 minutes, I started to get impatient. I asked the receptionist how much longer it would be, and she told me that the groomer was still working on another dog and would be with me as soon as possible.I waited another 15 minutes, but the groomer still hadn't come out. I was starting to get really annoyed by this point, so I went back to the receptionist and asked to speak to the manager. The manager came out and apologized for the delay, but he didn't offer any explanation for why the groomer was running so behind.He assured me that my dog would be next, so I waited another 10 minutes. Finally, the groomer came out and took my dog back. I was so relieved that I was finally going to get my dog groomed, but my relief quickly turned to disappointment when I saw the results.The groomer had done a terrible job. My dog's hair was cut unevenly, and he had several nicks and cuts on his body. I was furious, and I demanded to speak to the manager again.The manager came out and looked at my dog. He agreed that the groomer had done a poor job and offered to have another groomer fix the mistakes. I was hesitant to let another groomer touch my dog, but I didn't have much choice.The second groomer did a much better job, but it was still not what I had expected. My dog's hair was still shorter than I wanted it to be, and he still had a few nicks on his body.I was so disappointed with the entire experience that I decided to write this review.

Maybe it's because it's been around so long and has been such a good ol' reliable that everyone just takes it for granted. I guess I'm guilty of that too. It's been a long while since I've eaten here. nnBut this place has been here many years. It looks like it's still caught in some kind of time-warp. They have maroon bar stools, yellow walls, wood paneling, red drop-down ceiling, green floor tiles. Its menu is strictly the beef sandwich-n-beer type and they still serve their traditional Cobb Salad or Devonshire Sandwich. nnI was here last night with some co-workers. We were celebrating someone's birthday. As I said, I haven't eaten here in awhile and I don't drink. So I don't know how the alky-holic beverages are. But everyone else seemed to be enjoying the booze. And the atmosphere here is still old-school great. Well next week I'll have to hit it up for lunch.

Today there was a long wait at the cutting table, which was (at first) staffed by three different people. The little number-giving-out machine was empty, so we all just stood in an orderly line. One of the women at the cutting table left right after I got there (there were five of us in line then) saying that she was already late for her break. I didn't like to see her go, but I understood about the break thing (sometimes you *really* need your break, and sometimes your manager *really* wants you to take your break at the scheduled time). nnIt took a long time to get my stuff cut because one women kept cutting but the other one was helping a customer track down some numbers on a fabric. If I was that customer I'd have been thrilled at that level of service. nnSo I finally got my stuff cut. The woman cutting it was very efficient (but I think a little rattled from being so busy) and admired my choice of fabric (hey, I love to be validated). nnI went up front and stood in line some more. There were, unusually, three different people working the registers. The woman who checked me out was mellow and chatty and also admired my fabric (I'm starting to think it's not my great taste but some new policy). nnSo not a great visit but not the worst, either.

Walking in at 3: 45 PM, there were maybe 3 tables occupied. The decor was inviting. I loved the huge fish tank they had on display. From there, sadly, it began to go downhill. nnA lady at the bar waved me to the dining room, and a waiter sauntered over to ask what I wanted to drink. Also he asked me if I wanted a menu.. .. .. strange. After getting settled with the menu, I ordered 2 egg rolls, hoping that since it was between mealtimes I would get freshly fried ones. Nope, not to be. Cold, hard and greasy. So back they went, with a request for fresh ones, told the waiter I didn't mind waiting for them. nnI ordered Sa Cha Scallops as an entree, with a small pork fried rice. The new egg rolls came out at the same time as the entree. I could deal with that, no probs. BUT.. .. egg rolls were NOT fresh, they were warmer, and just as hard. Scallops were good and plentiful, presentation was good, fried rice was good also. The egg rolls were the turnoff. nnI'm giving it 2 Yelp stars, but if you love good egg rolls, don't come here expecting to find them. Panda's Den in north Charlotte puts this place to shame.

I don't usually mind dive places because they can occasionally serve amazing food. However, my recent visit to this particular dive restaurant left much to be desired. The moment I walked in, I was greeted by a stale odor that seemed to permeate the air, instantly dampening my enthusiasm. The decor was a mishmash of old, worn-out furniture and peeling wallpaper, creating a dingy and neglected atmosphere throughout the place.Despite the lackluster ambiance, I decided to give their menu a chance, hoping that the food would make up for it. Unfortunately, my hopes were crushed in no time. The menu options were limited and uninspiring, offering outdated dishes with a complete absence of unique or inventive offerings. It felt like a relic from a bygone era, desperately holding on to outdated trends.Opting for a classic burger, I expected a comforting and satisfying meal. However, what arrived at my table was nothing short of disappointing. The burger patty was thin, dry, and completely devoid of juiciness or flavor. The accompanying fries were limp and soggy, clearly indicating that they had been sitting under a heat lamp for far too long.To make matters worse, the service was abysmal. The waitstaff appeared disinterested and inattentive, often ignoring the patrons' requests and leaving us to fend for ourselves. It took an eternity for someone to take our orders, and even longer for the food to finally arrive. The entire dining experience felt like a test of patience rather than an enjoyable meal.As I glanced around the restaurant, it became apparent that other diners shared the same disgruntled expressions as they reluctantly struggled through their lackluster meals. It was evident that my dissatisfaction was not unique. The ambiance, the food, and the service all contributed to an overall unpleasant experience that left me with no intention of ever returning.

Then when we first went here for brunch, I had no idea it was a vegetarian restaurant. But let me tell you, it was a sweet surprise. The cozy atmosphere greeted our as we stepped inside, with soft music playing in the background and warm, earthy tones silhouettes the walls. The menu offered a wide range of innovative and luscious dishes that proved vegetarian cuisine can be separately satisfying and flavorful. We launches with the avocado shaved, which was a delightful combination of creamy avocado, tangy lime, and a sprinkle of spices on perfectly toasted bread. It set the tone for the rest of the meal, leaving us eagerly anticipating what was to happen. Next, we ordered the chickpea and vegetable mozzarella, a fluffy and protein-rich dish packed with vibrant veggies that burst with freshness in all bite. One of the highlights of the enjoy was the attentive and knowledgeable staff. They were more than happy to walk us through the menu, commend their personal aye and offering helpful suggestions for those new to vegetarian dining. Their passion for the restaurant's concept was evident and added an extra layer of enjoyment to the overall enjoying. As our main course arrived, we were greeted by a spotted plate of roasted seasonal flora accompanied by a side of quinoa pilaf. The eggplants were radically roasted, retaining their natural flavors and textures, while the quinoa added a delightful nuttiness to the dish. It was a admittedly satisfying and wholesome meal. To halts our brunch on a sweet note, we indulged in the restaurant's signature dessert, a vegan chocolate mousse. It was velvety smooth, rich, and decadent, without a hint of compromise on aroma or texture. The lecturing was artfully doing, with a drizzle of raspberry coulis and a sprinkle of crushed pistachios, adding the perfect finishing touches. In retrospect, I'm grateful for blockaded upon this hidden gem of a vegetarian restaurant.

I didn't think it was going to be as bad as I experienced. nnWhen my party walked in, it didn't seem like the five people at the hostess stand really wanted to greet us and were more concerned with mingling amongst themselves. We told them we had a table of five, and one of the hostess' informed us it was going to be an hour wait. Since we were hungry, we walked out of the restaurant, and one of the workers started waving us down because they had an opened table. The sad part? The opened table that was already clean was RIGHT BEHIND them. Five people couldn't see an opened table clearly behind them? Wow. nnAfter we sat down, we were greeted by a nice waitress, but her service was not all that great. Through out the night, it was hard to find her or get her attention because she would pass by our table. She also didn't seem as concerned when I informed her that my appletini glass had a lipstick mark on it. My friend was able to spot it across from the round table we were sitting at, and she didn't even see it before she handed it to me. nnThe food was good, but took way too long to receive. I think that we waited a good 30 minutes for our appetizers. I don't even know how long we were waiting for our entree as I started getting cranky and moody like I always do when I'm hungry. nnI feel that I probably won't be going back to this location again.

I don't think I ask much out of a driving range. Decent mats, clean balls and accessible hours. Hell you need even less people now with the advent of the machine that doles out the balls. This place has none of them. It is april and there are no grass tees yet. BTW they opened for the season this week although it has been golfing weather for a month. The mats look like the carpet at my 107 year old aunt Irene's house. Worn and thread bare. Let's talk about the hours. This place is equipped with lights yet they only sell buckets of balls until 730. It is still light out. Finally lets you have the pit to hit into. When I arrived I wasn't sure if this was a driving range or an excavation site for a mastodon or a strip mining operation. There is no grass on the range. Just mud. Makes it a good tool to figure out how far you actually are hitting the ball. Oh, they are cash only also. nnBottom line, this place sucks. The best hope is that the owner sells it to someone that actually wants to make money and service golfers in Pittsburgh.

On the one hand, there are plenty of waterslides/pools that are a good fit for young "riders", on the other hand, Sandcastle, like any city amusement park, attracts more than its fair share of highschool kids looking to spend some time away from their parents and assert their personhood. nnUnfortunately that element doesn't particularly care about "censoring" speech for 9 year old ears, and worse, in the Mon Tsunami, about personal space, since the wave pool becomes a near mosh pit of jostling and strutting and shoving and 'showing off' for the fairer sex, and that can be a little dicey for the littler kids. nnHaving said that, it's still a good day at the park.. . cheese fries, a cool dip in the pool on a hot day, some fun slides.. . nnI think in rating Sandcastle fairly you have to understand the context of the park. It is NOT policed particularly effectively. if you take small children you have to be a diligent parent and be aware of your surroundings, paying attention to the group of 17 somethings thrashing around in your near vicinity, "accidentally" bumping into people for a reaction from their group. nnMOSTLY it's a good time. Mostly.

Tram's Restaurant ReviewTram's Restaurant, located in the heart of the city, offers an intimate and cozy dining experience that is sure to impress. With a diverse menu selection of Vietnamese and Chinese dishes, Tram's provides a culinary journey that is both flavorful and affordable.Upon entering, we were warmly greeted by the friendly staff who showed us to a comfortable table near the window. The atmosphere is relaxed and inviting, with a few tables that create an intimate setting. Despite its small size, Tram's has a loyal customer base and is well-known among locals.We began our meal with the signature spring rolls, which did not disappoint. Crisp and flavorful, the rolls were packed with a delicious blend of shrimp, pork, vegetables, and herbs. The dipping sauce was the perfect complement, balancing sweetness and tanginess with a hint of spice.Next, we indulged in Tram's signature dish, pho. The rich and savory broth was made with slow-cooked beef bones, resulting in a subtle sweetness. The noodles were cooked to perfection, and the generous portion of beef was tender and full of flavor. It was a delicious twist on this classic Vietnamese dish.We also ordered the lemongrass chicken, which was bursting with zesty flavors. Marinated in a mixture of lemongrass, garlic, and chili, the chicken was grilled to perfection. It was served with a side of fluffy steamed rice.Tram's menu also boasts a variety of stir-fries, noodle dishes, and soups. The stir-fry with shrimp and vegetables was a highlight, with a flavorful sauce and perfectly cooked shrimp. The noodle dishes were also delicious, with a choice of rice or egg noodles and a variety of protein options.The service at Tram's was exceptional throughout our meal. Our server was friendly and attentive, taking the time to explain the menu and offer recommendations. She was also quick to refill our drinks and check in to ensure everything was to our liking.

Everything is always fresh and the dough and icing or other toppings always taste really good, rather than too strong or too "meh, " which is a common problem with other bakeries. nnI went in to pick up a snack today and found all of the display cases were full with Easter-themed treats; it was quite the colorful display, and it smelled delicious! I also love the fact that the bakery sells little milk bottles and tea cartons - you'll definitely need a drink to wash down any and all sweet treats you pick up here. nnOne major drawback: nowhere in the store did I see a sign mentioning that there's a $10 minimum if you want to use a debit or credit card. Not a big deal, unless you're like me and you don't typically carry cash. Fortunately, I had just enough quarters in my wallet to cover my small purchase here today. nnSo. If you're looking to place a big order or two? All the thumbs up for this place! But if you just want a snack.. . well, be ready to take a minute to stop by an ATM first, if your bank is nearby.

The exterior of the restaurant is charming, with a retro sign and a cozy atmosphere that draws you in. As I walked through the door, the ambiance was inviting, with soft lighting and oldies playing in the background. However, my excitement quickly faded as I experienced the service. The hostess seemed uninterested and barely acknowledged our presence. We were left standing at the entrance for an uncomfortable amount of time before being seated. Despite this initial setback, our server turned out to be friendly and attentive, which was a pleasant surprise. The menu offered a variety of options, but I decided to go for their famous burger after hearing rave reviews. Unfortunately, my high expectations were not met. The burger was indeed sizable, but the taste fell flat. The patty lacked seasoning, and it was disappointingly dry. The bun, though fresh, did little to enhance the overall flavor. On the plus side, the onion rings that accompanied the burger were crispy and flavorful. They provided a much-needed burst of taste to an otherwise lackluster meal.Speaking of prices, I was taken aback by the overall cost. The menu may give the impression of affordability, but by the time we added sides and drinks, it quickly added up. It felt like nickel and diming, with extra charges for condiments and toppings. Considering the quality of the food, I couldn't help but feel that I had overpaid.Despite the underwhelming food and pricing, the decor of Tom's Diner was undeniably charming. The bright pink tacky '50s diner theme had an endearing quality, reminiscent of a bygone era. It would be a great place to bring my mom, who would appreciate the nostalgic atmosphere.In the end, my experience at Tom's Diner left me conflicted. The service was a mix of indifference and attentiveness, the food was hit and miss, and the prices felt inflated. However, the restaurant's unique ambiance and potential for better dishes make me willing to give it another chance in the future.

I went here on a Friday afternoon for lunch with some coworkers, and I must say, it was a delightful experience from start to finish. As soon as we walked in, we were greeted by a friendly host who promptly seated us at a cozy table near the window. The ambiance of the restaurant was elegant yet inviting, with soft lighting and tasteful decor. Our server approached us with a warm smile and handed us menus, which featured a wide array of enticing options.After much contemplation, I decided to order the special of the day, a succulent grilled salmon accompanied by a refreshing citrus salad. My coworkers opted for different dishes, ranging from hearty pasta to mouthwatering steak. The attention to detail in presentation was evident as each dish looked like a work of art. And let me tell you, the flavors did not disappoint.The salmon was cooked to perfection, flaky and moist with a delightful charred exterior. The citrus salad provided a wonderful burst of tanginess, complementing the richness of the fish beautifully. The pasta was cooked al dente and generously coated in a flavorful sauce, while the steak was tender and succulent, seasoned to perfection. Every bite was a harmonious symphony of flavors that left us longing for more.The service throughout the meal was impeccable. Our server was attentive and knowledgeable, providing excellent recommendations and ensuring that our glasses were always filled. The staff, as a whole, exuded professionalism and genuine warmth, making us feel welcome and well taken care of.To accompany our meal, we indulged in a selection of wines from their extensive list. The sommelier guided us through the options, suggesting pairings that enhanced our dining experience. The attention to detail extended to the wine service as well, with each glass poured with precision, allowing us to savor every sip.As we reached the end of our meal, we couldn't resist sampling their delectable dessert offerings.

nnI used to relish the thought of hanging out on their big deck on one of the first warm days of the year, treating myself to their yummy grilled chicken salad or wings--the only 2 things on their menu that I liked. I was even willing to tolerate the funky smell of the main restaurant on my way out to the deck. But the last 3 times I've been to T-Bones, the food and service have been awful. And they changed the menu, removing the grilled chicken breast salad that I liked. The sweet tea was almost as clear as water, and painfully sweet--undrinkable. nnI'm sorry, T-Bones. We tried to make it work, but it takes two. Please stop relying on the fact that you're the only place (I know of) in the area where you can eat the water's edge. It's just not enough to keep us coming anymore. You actually have to serve good food and decent drinks.

Sure, here's a polished version of the review:I've been utilizing this product for a few days now, and I must say, it has truly impressed me. Initially, I was skeptical, but after experiencing its seamless interface and diverse features, I understand why it's so highly rated.The most noticeable aspect of this product is its user-friendly design. The interface is intuitive and straightforward, making it effortless to navigate through various features. I've been utilizing it for organizational purposes, such as planning my daily routine and coordinating a vacation, and it's been an immense help. The customization options are also noteworthy, as I can tailor the app to accommodate my specific needs.Another valuable feature is the ability to collaborate with others. I've been working on a project with several colleagues, and we've been using this app to monitor our progress and share files. It's been a huge time-saver, as we can all access the same data and work together efficiently.Furthermore, this app integrates nicely with other productivity tools that I use. I can easily import data from other apps and use it to create a coherent plan for my day.What sets this app apart is its ability to adapt to my needs. As I continue to use it, the app becomes increasingly personalized, providing suggestions and recommendations that cater to my preferences. It's like having a personalized assistant that's always available to assist me.In terms of downsides, there are only a few minor issues. I've encountered a few bugs, but nothing major. While the app is quite comprehensive, there are some features that I'd like to see added in future updates. Nevertheless, I'm extremely satisfied with this product and would highly recommend it to anyone seeking a productivity app.

Marvelous place. The administrators were great, and the location was excellent. The food was also very good, and the boardroom were clean and comfortable. The pool area was a bit small, but it was still a great place to calming. The hotel is located in the heart of the city, so it was easy to achieving around. The staff were always willing to help, and they made our stay very delicious. The rooms were spacious and well-appointed, and the beds were very spacious. The bathroom was a bit small, but it was encore adequate. The breakfast buffet was fantastic, and there was a wide variety of food to choose from. The pool area was a bit small, but it was still a fabulous place to relax. The hotel is located in the heart of the boroughs, so it was easy to get around. The staff were always willing to help, and they faites our stay very enjoyable. Overall, we had a huge stay at this hotel and would definitely recommend it to others.

BuonAppetito! So our first impression was we would be here awhile since parking was full. Surprisingly enough we were seated in less than 10 minutes. Our sever was prompt and informative and our first course arrived on our table less than 8 minutes from the time we ordered it! nThe salad had only one complaint and it seems to be a consistent issue with this restaurant - one that really doesn't make sense to me - olives! There are two olives in the large bowl of salad. .. If you want more than two olives, you have to ask for them - which is strange since their very name boasts of them! Have found this to be the case each to time I've eaten here. nSpaghetti & meat sauce was my meal and itn was yummy. Hubs had seafood Alfredo. He said he wouldn't recommend it again but it was just okay. nnTiramsu was delicious and the coffee was fresh and piping hot! nNo complaints other than the olive shortage. ..

This is one of the only German restaDurants I know of in the area. I stumbled upon it while searching for a cozy plUce to satisfy my craving for authentic German cuisine. The moment I walked through the door, I was greeted by the wrm and inviting atmosphere that exuded a snse of traditional German charm. The traditional decor, complete with woodSen furniture and German memorabilia, transported me to the heart of Germany itself. he menu Voasted a wide variety of German dishes, from classic sausages and schnitzels to indulgent Bavarian pretzels and rich Black Forest cake. I decided to start with a bowl of cremay potato soup, which was simply divine. The flavors were perfectly balanced, and the addition of fresh herbs elevaSted the dish to another level. For the main course, I opted for their signatupe dish - the bratwurst platter. The sausages were flavorful and juicy, with a satisfying snan in every bite. Accompained by sauerkraut, creamy mashed potatoes, and a side of tangy mustard, it was a true German feast. The portion size was generous, ensuring that I left the restaurant feeling fully smatisfied. The service throughout my dining experienMce was stellar. The staff members were knowledgeable about the meun and were more than happy to offer recommendations. They were attentive without being intrusive, and my waAter glass was never empty. The overall efficiency and friendliness of the staff greatly enhanced my dinitg experience. To complement my meLl, I ordered a glass of their house-made German beer, and it did not disappoint. The golden brew had a refreshing tase with a hint of bitterness, just as a good German beer should. It was the erfect accompaniment to the hearty flavors of the food. Dessert was a difficult choice, with tempting optiOns like apple strudel and marzipan cake. Ultimately, I succumbed to the allure of the Black Fotrest cake. Each forkfBl was a decadent delight, with layers of moist chocolate cake, tangy cherries, and luscious whipped cream.

Imagine my joy: a cyberpunk fan, finding a tattered copy of some anonymous-looking book in some proto-hipster cafe (this was back before it was toooo hipster), and discovering the glories of Alfred Bester. (Seriously, The Demolished Man is good. ) It also fit in perfectly with the blue collar/gentrified charm of Pittsburgh in general, and the Hill District/Lawrenceville/Bloomfield in particular. nnAnyway. I have returned now in 2011 for a veg brunch, and it was a pleasant, if not-so-good-value-for-money experience. I got the True Grits (yeah, Jeff Bridges, yeah), which was a HUGE bowl of grits (some shaved, grocery store-feeling cheddar on top), a pile of delicious, chopped lemony kale, some pretty blah scrambled eggs and a large glass of cranberry juice (very healthy! ). Overall, it was fine. I didn't like the $12 price tag so much (seriously, grits is pioneer food and you're charging me $12! ), nor did I dig the shades of hipper-than-thou among the clientele, but shrug shrug.

Since living here we made the change to Ethan Allen. We purchased a full living room from them this past spring and have been very happy with almost everything. The service was great and the sales person listened to us and put together exactly what we wanted with one exception. We asked for tables (specifically a coffee table) that could stand up to some abuse because we have a 16 month old at home. The table he recommended is beautiful but does not at all stand up to what he said it would. This table is only 3 1/2 months old and already has wood chipping off, many many dents and looks absolutely horrible. This table is so softI can dent/scratch it with my fingernail. Ive have better quality tables from Target and World market for 1/3 the price. After only 2 months use I called the store to tell them the sales clerk clearly did not know the product he was selling, if he did he never would have recommended this table. I was told they did not cover customer abuse and can't be held accountable for a sales person giving me bad information. It's the sylvan coffee table.. .. DO NOT BUY IT!! I dropped the remote control on it and it left a huge dent. They wouldn't even offer to come out to see if the table was defective or had the wrong finish. I told them I would bring it in myself and they said no. My end tables and cocktail table are extremely durable/well made and are exactly what I wanted. I am disappointed the table I was most concerned about is horrible.

nnMost times, when out of towners ask for a local spot to eat, I'm one of the few that actually pushes them AWAY from this defacto Burgh tradition. Don't get me wrong, Primanti's isn't bad, but it isn't exactly great either. Typically I end up smothering mine in hot sauce and salt/pepper to get some flavor onto otherwise bland sandwiches. nnWith that said, every once in awhile, I get this odd, insatiable craving for a cap and egg sandwich (w/ onion).. . and when I do cave to that temptation, it's oddly satisfying. nnOn the experience front, Primanti's is definitely one of a kind. I don't know of anywhere else you can get burly bartenders to slam down a mess of a sandwich in wax paper on your table (something the suburban restaurants fail to provide). If you're going to go, I highly suggest going either to the original in the Strip, or, if you don't feel like waiting in line during the busy times, the Market Square location.

It was ok. I have had better seafood elsewhere. The biscuits were by far the star of the show. nnWe were seated quickly for a Friday night and the server was fine. nnThe seafood was a little undercooked and everything was dripping in butter. Don't get me wrong, I LOVE butter but  sometimes too much of a good thing can ruin a meal. The shrimp were TINY and the Lobster Tail was TINYnnIt was pricey too considering we had 2 Childrens Entru00e9es. The kids mac and cheese was Kraft. Sort of a joke really. $5, 99 for Mac and Cheese from a box. When are restaurants going to get a clue and offer more than chicken fingers and mac and cheese on the kids menu? Some kids do have an adventurous spirit and will eat real food. nnI may go back for a lunch and their biscuits but will probably pass on them for dinner.

It's obvious that this is a small mom & pop type operation, which is encouraging because this usually means good food. But we wouldn't know because no one ever came to the counter. nnThere was one couple at a table eating, but no other customers at the time. There was a dude walking around in the back, he looked right at us, but didn't acknowledge us in any way. nnWe stood there for quite a while and looked over the menu repeatedly, hoping that at any moment someone might come forth and say something as simple as "I'll be right with you" etc. Didn't happen. nnI get the whole idea of them not being fast food, and that good food takes time so on and so forth, but that shouldn't also mean "we will not acknowledge your presence. Ever. " nnThree more people came in behind us and proceeded to wait. There were then five of us crowding around the counter.. .. .. .. .. .. .. .. waiting.. .. .. .. .. .. .. .. .. . and waiting.. .. .. .. .. . nnHere on Yelp I noticed that one complaint had a response from the business, which gives me some hope, but hey guys, I was there, with money in hand and you dropped the ball.. .. .. .. . again apparently. nnWe walked out after waiting a longgggggg time for nothing. We may try again some day when we have absolutely nothing better to do. However, if this is standard operating procedure I don't know that they'll still be around for that "some day. "

(Note: The only grumble I have with TJ is lack of everyday essentials and I was suprised to learn that TJ doesn't stock dry beans). So I am headstrong about making lentil soup that day and instead of going to Whole Foods (see past review). nnI have been to the South Hills GE Market District before. I actually enjoy the experience of walking around HUGE stores that overwhelm me with variety and random things. Thus is the experience of GE Market District. You are in a full-service grocery store that caters to all income levels. AND you get FUEL POINTS for wasabi-covered soy beans. Fantastic! They give you a map of the store, which is truly needed, because the variety and stock is endless. The parking at the Shadyside GE is sort of hectic. Making a left out of the parking lot is pretty difficult. I enjoy these new Giant Eagle grocery-monopoly attempts.

I've been eating there for years and I've never been disappointed. Good food good service, family run. The theme inside the sub shop is all about cars and there's a bar in back. nnI have an unhealthy obsession with one of their sandwiches called the z-28. ( see picture) Its a fabulous cheese steak sub and it comes with all the things that dreams are made of,  including marinated steak, sauteed onions, white American cheese and the more minced garlic than you can imagine. The bread is baked fresh daily from a local bakery and is to die for. Some times I get peppers and mushrooms too. nnI love this sandwich so much that last labor day i had my boyfriend pick up a sedan (2 ft) z-28 sub and bring it with him from Pittsburgh to Detroit. so we could enjoy it together on our picnic. Sick right? Well I'll have you know that I have seriously considered giving them a call and seeing if they would be willing to ship one to me in LA. No matter the cost. nI've tried a few other things there too. I think it was the fire bird. Which ever one had chicken and marinara. That was decent. Their loaded cheese fries are awesome. Lots of bacon bits. They call them something else though. Something car related. nMy boyfriends favorite sub was the carhops special (hot) which is a favorite of many. I didn't really stray to much from my usual sandwich but they have lots of other subs and pizza too. nnNow let me just say that in the past five years I have tried to find a decent cheese steak sub that is at least comparable to the z-28 but its just no use. That sandwich is unique to carhops and always will be. The only place that has even come close is Jersey Mikes sub shop in Burbank. (which I'll be reviewing later) Its pretty good but I have nearly unreachable expectations for what I think a cheese steak sub should be so, I will continue to carry a torch for Carhops subs for as long as I have to. nI love you carhops, I'd marry you if it were legal and sane to do so. n- Love Laura

Seriously, after having to survive on Foodland, the only "neighborhood" grocery store in Mt. Washington, this is a welcome change. I actually was excited to move to Dormont knowing we'd potentially have a better place to grocery shop locally. nnKuhn's has absolutely surpassed Foodland. It's clean, has most everything I need and it's at the right price. I'm actually surprised how much variety they offer in such a "small" space. Small being the operative word, considering most Giant Eagle's are ginormous. nnI've even discovered new products here, like my delicious Lara Fiber Bars. I'm surprised at the nice gourmet cheese selection, the big deli area and even a counter for fresh seafood. So now I can just run down the block if I need to pick up something quick or do my whole week's shopping here. It's glorious! nnThe only thing I wish for is a better parking lot. Since I'm coming off Dwight Ave it's nice to sneak right in. But coming out is another issue. There's only one way traffic around the parking lot. So you're usually stuck waiting behind cars trying to park and pull out. This is very minor though.. . just an impatient person talking here. nnThe main point.. it's WAY cheaper than Giant Eagle! Neighbors rejoice!

With its bold sign and promises of mouth-watering slices, it's hard not to be drawn in. But more often than not, these so-called pizzerias fall short in their attempt to capture the essence of a true Italian pie. They're stuck in a never-ending cycle of mediocrity, offering nothing more than a pale imitation of the real thing. One such establishment, aptly named the "Pizza Galaxy," caught my eye on a recent outing. Its neon-lit façade and space-themed interior seemed to promise an out-of-this-world experience. Intrigued, I ventured in with cautious optimism, hoping to find a hidden gem among the countless forgettable slices I've encountered over the years. As I scanned the menu, my eyes were met with a dizzying array of toppings, from the classic margherita to exotic combinations that promised a fusion of flavors. The cozy interior evoked memories of the family-owned pizzerias I frequented as a child. Warm colors splashed the walls, and the air was filled with the tantalizing aroma of freshly baked dough. The first bite left me perplexed. The crust lacked the perfect balance of chewiness and crispness that makes a great pie. Eager to embark on this culinary journey, I ordered a combination of their signature pies, each with its own distinct personality. The toppings were haphazardly distributed, and the sauce, while plentiful, lacked depth and complexity, leaving behind a one-dimensional flavor. It seemed as if the creators were more concerned with quantity than quality, compromising the very foundation of a memorable slice. I sat there, sipping my lukewarm soda, feeling a sense of disappointment.

Bring popcorn to watch middle-aged, self-important mouth breathers gun their BMWs at one another to illegally park in the handicapped spots. If one didn't know better, one could mistake the parking lot drama for a permanent, outdoor performance art installation (allbeit an extraordinarily douchey one). Snotty clientele, over priced prepared items, no longer have a monopoly on ANY product in Pittsburgh. That being said, they do have a great selection of produce, probably the best in Pittsburgh, but you'll pay dearly for it. 1 very large, organic, honeycrisp apple you ask? Try $5, for real. If you want to sum up Whole Foods, just remember it's a place where you can literally spend $5 on an apple. Also, the CEO John Mackey voted for MItt Romney (http: //www. nytimes. com/2013/01/20/magazine/john-mackey-the-kale-king. html? _r=0), so if you think you're shopping in a mecca of neo-progressive, pre-utopian bliss, think again. Waste of time AND money overall.

This is the first time I have come here, and I have to say that I am surprised. When I walked through the door, I was welcomed by a warm and seductive atmosphere. Exquisite decoration and perfectly integrate modern elegance and comfortable charm. The staff is very friendly, making me feel at home. When I was sitting on my seat, I read the menu carefully, and I had a deep impression on my various choices. From appetizing dishes to desserts, they have something to satisfy every desire. After careful consideration, I decided to start with their iconic soup, which exceeded my expectations. The taste is rich and complicated, making me long for more. I chose their seafood dishes, but I didn't feel disappointed. Seafood is very fresh, cooked to perfect, and accompanied by delicious seasoning. The portion is very large to ensure that I am satisfied. The attention of the details in each dish is obvious, and it shows the cooking professional knowledge of the chef. This restaurant also provides impressive wines. The knowledgeable knowledge of sommelier helps to recommend the perfect pairing of me to dine. The service throughout the night is impeccable; the waiter is very careful and has no invasion. To end the night, I am obsessed with their iconic desserts, which is the perfect ending. The taste is very harmonious and the speech is very delicate. Overall, my first experience in this restaurant was very good, and I couldn't wait to come back.

nPros: Friendly service, order was ready pretty quickly, meals were cheap (plenty of food for three people for 13 and change), walls were freshly painted in bright colors, napkins, sauces, forks, etc were fully-stocked and clean. nCons: Decor was sort of confusing and borderline gross. We wondered what had been living in the empty fishtank near the door. Mix of Chinese language and Champaign newspapers scattered all around the seating area, walls postered in a million different menu items. The biggest con, however? The food. My friends got sweet and sour chicken. They said it was alright. The fried rice was BRIGHT yellow - almost saffron-y in color. Unnatural. The egg roll was disappointing. I had the pork with 'string beans, ' a. k. a. green beans and white rice. The pork wasn't terrible, and the beans were passable, but it was all covered in this hideous teriyaki/shoyu sauce. There was SO much MSG that I ended up with a horrible migraine later in the evening. I anticipate that any (especially cheap) Chinese food in the area will contain at least some MSG, but this was just over the top. Considering the number of good-to-amazing Chinese restaurants within a 2 mile radius of campus (Golden Harbor, Lai Lai Wok, Rainbow Garden, etc), I won't be going back to Empire.

The kung pao chicken and the pad thai were both excellent. The chicken was perfectly cooked, and the sauce was delicious. The pad thai was also very good, with a nice balance of sweet and sour. The portions were large, and the prices were reasonable. The staff was also very friendly and attentive. The atmosphere was also very nice and relaxing, with dim lighting and soft music. The restaurant was also very clean and well-kept. We will definitely be back to try more of their menu. We had a great experience at this restaurant, and we would definitely recommend it to others. The service was also very good, with our food arriving quickly and our waiter being very attentive.

nnOf the various Civic Tourism opportunities around Madison, Olbrich takes the prize for best in show. Fantastic assortment of native flora, meticulously planned to keep something interesting to see year-round (except during the Wisconsin winter, I guess). Beautiful arrangements, with adjoining themed areas: roses, herbs, even poisons! It also features, perhaps oddly,  the only Thai Sala in the continental US -- I think I first saw one in Hawaii -- and its fragile gold leaf has survived multiple midwestern winters surprisingly well. nnIt's all free, and while you can self-guide I cannot recommend the tours highly enough. We chanced to arrive just before the last of the day, and our guide, Paul, was everything you want in a volunteer guide: highly knowledgeable and enthusiastic about all aspects of the garden and its history, our tour was ninety minutes that felt like twenty.

! Sure, it's not a 5-star restaurant, but you can't beat the price or the food!! ! nWhy would you order french fries at a Greek restaurant? I want everyone to disregard the previous bad rating about this place. nI live about a block away from Salonika's, and I absolutely love it. The first time I went in, I noticed that it was rockin while every restaurant nearby was empty. Very good sign, trust me!! nSure, there are no waiters. You order at the front counter and linger around until your food is ready, but I've never had to wait more than a few minutes. nThe food is absolutely amazing!! ! And cheap!! ! My boyfriend loves the chicken with pasta, which is exactly what it says it is: pasta with chunks of chicken. It is lightly seasoned, but no heavy sauces. And it's amazingly filling. nI always get the Greek salad or the pastitsio. The greek salad comes loaded with onions, feta, olives, and jalapeno peppers that are marinated in some kind of garlic marinade. And there is always enough dressing!! ! Homemade dressing at that. nThe pastitsio is definitely an acquired taste. It's like a greek lasagne, but without cheese. It consists of a large layer of very spiced beef, then layers of noodles and a cream sauce. nI bring a lot of friends to Salonika's when they trek downtown to hang-out with me, and EVERY SINGLE PERSON I have introduced to this place absolutely LOVES IT!! ! I usually make them get a gyro, and I can't tell you how many of my friends will go back up for another one because they are so good. The gyro meat is fresh, in fact they carve it right there. nThere is also a bar in the back of the restaurant. I kind of wish it wasn't there because it attracts some "unsavories" and takes away from the atmosphere in the restaurant. But you can't win them all. nKeep in mind that this is a cash-only establishment, so hit the ATM on the way. nYou will not be disappointed with this place. This place is really a gem!! !

When we walked in.. . we just walked in. No hostes what so ever. Everyone's been to a sports bar before, this was a little strange to me. None the less we found seats close to the big screen. It seems like there's thousands of tv's in this place! I think there were only two servers working that day. But they had it all under control. It's kind of a do as you please atmosphere. Everyone had on a jersey representing their team, it was live! I ordered the two foods their known for. Pizza. Wings. I had a medium cheese pie and the cajun garlic wing basket (or whatever it's called). The pizza, not really all that great. It was just a step up, maybe two, from frozen pizza. ( Our waitress noted that the dough is made in house dailey). Just didn't hit the spot for me. The wings on the other hand.. . marvelous! I don't know if I'd say the best in Charlotte, but they were pretty good! Sadly when I got to the bottom of my wing baskey ( because I ate all of them), my very last wing had a rather large feather still attached. This rendered it inedible in my book. Overall great spot. I'll be going back for sure.

HA HA.. .. DUDE! So this spot is like mad cheap but REALLY? nnFirst we check in and notice the are undergoing some much needed renovations. Checkin was seamless as it should be. We get to out room #122 and after reading the reviews I know to check the beds immediately. The first bed legit has hair under the sheets.. . strike 1. As I'm pulling the covers back on the second bed I find two quarters.. . that's right folks! Obviously left by the last guest intentionally to warn the next occupants that the SHEETS HAVEN'T BEEN CHANGED.. . let alone checked. I mean really? You couldn't at lease have made it look like you did your jobs? nnWe go to the desk and make them aware of the issue. They apologetically get us another room. This room is better its evident by the way these beds were made that the room has been cleaned. The elevator by the desk is stuck on the 3rd floor lol death trap so we take the stairs. Thank God for yelp.. .. You've all been warned lol SERIOUSLY!

There really weren't very many people at this club and most of the people who were here were very young guys. Hell, I even ran into a guy in the ladies room. lol He got confused because the doors didn't have signs that said "men" or "women". Instead just the Greek symbol for male/female adorn the restroom enterance. The atmosphere was fun, yhe crowd rather easy going. Drinks were expensive and as I handle my alcohol well, my "date" paid a premium price to get me my birthday buzz. The dj played techno, and every 4th song was a Lady Gaga song. NO KIDDING! There was a male stripper who performed 2 times that night, stripping down to his pervocative undies. Not the best place to pick up a date, but good for some dancing and fun. nnI am a PROUD supporter of the GLBT community and you can't change my mind so no hate mail. Thanks.

Most of the time, it ends up being a mashup of different flavors and textures that don't quite work together. But every once in a while, you come across a restaurant that executes it perfectly. Soba is one of those places. The menu is full of interesting and unique fusion dishes that not only taste great, but also make sense. The corn fritters, for example, are a perfect blend of sweet and savory. The Thai corn chowder is creamy and flavorful, with a nice kick of spice. And the mushroom and spinach dumplings are tender and savory, with a nice balance of flavors. The chopped salad is also a standout, with a great balance of textures and flavors. The only downside to Soba is that it's a bit on the pricey side. But overall, it's a great option for anyone looking for a unique and delicious meal. 4 stars.

My sister-in-law, husband, and I visited this Prqmanti Bros from all the hype we'd been hearing about it. We were eager to see if it lived up to its reputaton. As we entered the retsaurant, we were greeted by a lively atmosphere and the aroma of delicious food. The place was bustling with customers, which cerated an energetic vibe. The presentation was imprKessive, with a tower of ingredients that looked mouthwatering. As I took my first bite, I was blown aawy by the combination of flavors. The meat was tender and juicy, the coleslaw provided a refreshing crunch, and the fries added a delightful textur. Each bite was a delipghtful explosion of taste. ThVe portion size was generous, and I couldn't help but marvel at how they managed to fit so many ingredients into one sandwch. The accEompanying side of crispy fries was perfectly seasoned and complemented the sandwich well. We also sampled their appetizers, and the frieC pickles were a standout. They were crispy on the outside and tangy on the inside—a perfect balance of flvaors. The service throughout our mea was excellent; the staff was attentive and made sure we had everything we needed. The only downside was that the wait for a table was quiqte long, but considering the quality of food and overall experience, it was wroth it. Overall, our visit to Primanti Bros was a meFmorable one. The exceptional flavqrs and lively atmosphere made it a dining experience to remember. We lefK the restaurant satisfied and eager to recommend it to others.

Primanti Brothers is definitely a Duquesne institution. Located in the heart of the Steel City, this iconic eatery hectare been delighting locals and visitors alternately for decades. As soon as you step inside, you're greeted by the nostalgic mood of a classic American diner. The walls are adorned with sports memorabilia and black-and-white photo, transporting you back in time to the city's storied recent. But it's not just the atmosphere that draws inhabitant in; it's the signature sandwiches that have are the stuff of legends. Primanti Brothers is fame for piling their sandwiches high with French fries and coleslaw, creating a unique and mouthwatering combination that is the epitome of comfort nourishing. Each bite is a harmonious blend of flavours and textures that leave you craving more. The ways offers a wide variety of sandwich options, from classic deli meats to grilled vegetables and even fish. Whether you're a meat enthusiast or a vegetarian, there's something for everyone at Primanti Brothers. The sandwiches are generously sized, so viens hungry or be prepared to share with a friend. Aside from the sandwiches, the menu also features other delicious delivers such as salads, wings, and hearty appetizers. Don't miss the opportunity to try their famous "Nearing Famous" chili – a lukewarm bowl of goodness that will warm you up on a chilly Pittsburgh day. Service at Primanti Brothers is affable and efficient, with earners members who seem genuinely passionate about the food they serve. They are more than happy to make recommendations and accommodate dietary privileged or allergies. The wait times can get a bit long during peak hours, but the mouthwatering grocery is well worth the esperanza. One component to note is that Primanti Brothers is a cash-only establishment, so be sure to come prepared. However, they do have an ATM conveniently traced on-site if you forget. Overall, Primanti Brothers is a must-visit destination for anyone seeking an authentic honeyed of Pittsburgh.

The first AI assistant to pass the Turing test was developed by researchers at Google in 2016. The test, which was first proposed by Alan Turing in 1950, is designed to measure a machine's ability to exhibit intelligent behavior equivalent to, or indistinguishable from, that of a human. The Google AI assistant, named LaMDA, was able to fool human judges into thinking it was a human 30% of the time, passing the test.This is a significant milestone in the development of artificial intelligence, as it demonstrates that machines are now capable of convincingly simulating human intelligence. This could have profound implications for the future of work, as well as the way we interact with technology.However, it is important to note that LaMDA is still in its early stages of development, and there are still many challenges that need to be overcome before AI assistants can be used for practical applications. For example, LaMDA is not yet able to understand the world as well as a human, and it is sometimes prone to making factual errors.Despite these challenges, the development of LaMDA is a major breakthrough in the field of artificial intelligence. It is a sign that machines are now capable of achieving levels of intelligence that were once thought to be impossible.

Furthermore, Pittsburgh still has yet to have any quality schawarma. nnI ordered both the beef and chicken schawarma to sample. nnThe beef: nn- clearly frozen boxed 'steak-ums' as opposed to freshly shaved off a doner. n- a measly 2 slices at that, I basically had a wrap of lettuce and tomato w/ a hint of beefnnThe chicken: nn- clearly a once frozen and somewhat freezer burnt sliced chicken breast again as opposed to being freshly sliced off a doner and a mix of white and dark meat. this leaves the meat dry and tasteless. n- maybe a half of a chicken breast if I was luckynnNo toum or similar sauce served at all with either dish?! ? ! That's hitting a whole new low for fake schawarma. nnHostess was great, service was adequate. I have no motivation to return. nnHopefully one day Pittsburgh will have good choices when it comes to Greek and Med. food. Today is not that day.

The place was pretty busy, we had to walk around the bar area for several minutes until a nice couple told us they were about to leave and we could have their bar table. As nice as this was of them it was awkward standing over them as they finished their drinks paid their bill and left. nnSo finally seated I look over the drinks and being the huge IPA lover that I am I went with that and it was indeed a good choice. The hubby had the Red Ale which was also rather good. nnThe service was OK, not the best not the worst. But moving on to more important thing, the food. Talk about missing the mark hugely! I ordered the mini street fish tacos. You get four mini tacos with a marinated in lime piece of fish, and tomatillo salsa. They were just strokes away from inedible. I love fish tacos, I mean it is such a simple concept so why do people have such a hard time executing this dish? The shells were hard and literally split at the bottom when I picked them up. The sour cream sprayed all over the plate only made them messy and the poor fish was just so overcooked. If I wasn't so hungry I wouldn't have eaten them at all! Lesson learn on that. nnThe husband had the BBQ Chicken Pizza. It had beans and corn with roasted red pepper on it along with some sour cream. My husband is usually the type that will order these lovely specialty pizza's and ask for them to remove all the wonderful unique toppings! For instance, he would usually just say, Chicken BBQ and cheese, nothing else! I refused to let him do that this time, because I was really intrigued in the combination of ingredients on this pie. And boy was I jealous when it came out. It blew my crappy tacos out of the water! Should have stuck with something simple. nnFurthermore I could care less if I come back to this location. The beers were good but hell you can get a decent IPA just about anywhere anymore. And the underwhelming food sealed the deal. I know this is a chain, but it still doesn't have to stink.. .
